documentLocal: Memorandum of Association (MOA) — notarised, lists partners and shareholdings; partner names also appear on the Trade Licence
summary: A mainland LLC has no standalone statutory shareholder-list filing. Current partners are evidenced by the notarised Memorandum of Association and its amendments, which list each partner and share percentage; in most Emirates partner names (often percentages) are also printed on the Trade Licence. On a share transfer the MOA is amended and re-notarised, so the current MOA reflects current partners. These documents are NOT available from an open public registry — held by the company / notary / DED.

evidence.basis: UAE Commercial Companies Law (Federal Decree-Law No. 32 of 2021) — LLC MOA must state partners and their shares
evidence.note: UNVERIFIED verbatim (403). Under the CCL the LLC MOA is a mandatory notarised constitution stating each partner's name and shareholding; amendments on transfer must be notarised and registered with the DED.

evidence.basis: Emirate DED practice — trade licence lists owners/partners
evidence.note: UNVERIFIED verbatim — u.ae mainland pages 404. In Dubai and most Emirates the trade licence prints partner names (and frequently share %).

caveats: No dedicated 'shareholder list' document; shareholders are proved via the MOA (authoritative for share %) and, for names, via the trade licence.
NOT publicly registry-accessible: obtained from the company / notary / DED, not an open register — a key uncertainty for third-party KYC.
FRAGMENTATION: what the licence shows varies by Emirate.

documentLocal: Trade Licence (Rukhsa Tijariya) issued by the Emirate's Department of Economic Development
summary: For a UAE mainland LLC the register-extract equivalent is the Trade Licence issued by the relevant Emirate's DED. It states entity name, licence/commercial-register number, legal form, activities, manager, status and expiry, and in most Emirates the owners/partners. Existence and basic data can be verified via the federal National Economic Register (NER). Crucially, the UAE mainland has NO open EU-style public register: full extracts are held by the company or obtained from the DED, not freely downloadable by third parties.

evidence.basis: UAE Commercial Companies Law (Federal Decree-Law No. 32 of 2021) + Emirate DED trade-licence regime

summary: For an S.A. the shareholders (accionistas) are recorded in the company-held Libro de Registro de Acciones, which is kept with the formalities of commercial books and is open only to the shareholders — it is not a registry document (art. 213); nominative-share transfers are notified to and inscribed by the issuing company, not the public registry (art. 215). The registered estatuto names only the founders. Therefore the full/current shareholder body is not provable from the public registry.

evidence.basis: Ley General de Sociedades 19.550, Art. 213 (share register)
evidence.quote: Se llevará un libro de registro de acciones con las formalidades de los libros de comercio, de libre consulta por los accionistas
evidence.note: The record of shareholders is a company-kept libro de registro de acciones, not a filing with the commercial registry (IGJ/RPC).

evidence.basis: Ley General de Sociedades 19.550, Art. 215 (transfer of registered shares)
evidence.quote: La transmisión de las acciones nominativas o escriturales y de los derechos reales que las graven debe notificarse por escrito a la sociedad emisora
evidence.note: Transfers take effect against the company only from their inscription in the company's own book, confirming the registry does not hold the shareholder list.

caveats: Confirms the negative finding: the shareholder list for an S.A. lives in the company-held libro de registro de acciones, not in the public commercial registry.

documentLabel: Registered articles of association plus registered cuota (quota) transfers
documentLocal: Contrato social inscripto y cesiones de cuotas inscriptas
summary: For an S.R.L. the members (socios) and their cuotas are named in the contrato social, because the constitutive instrument must state the capital and each socio's contribution (art. 11 inc. 4). Cuota transfers become enforceable against third parties only upon registration in the Registro Público (art. 152), so current membership is reconstructable from registry-held documents. There is no single standalone 'shareholder list' document; the proof is the registered contrato social together with any registered cesiones de cuotas.

evidence.basis: Ley 19.550, art. 11 inc. 4
evidence.quote: 4) El capital social, que deberá ser expresado en moneda argentina, y la mención del aporte de cada socio.
evidence.note: The constitution names each socio and their contribution/cuotas.

evidence.quote: La transmisión de las cuotas es oponible a los terceros desde su inscripción en el Registro Público de Comercio, la que puede ser requerida por la sociedad; tambien podrán peticionarla el cedente o el adquirente exhibiendo el título de la transferencia y constancia fehaciente de su comunicación a la gerencia.
evidence.note: Cuota transfers are registered, so successive membership changes are provable from the registry.

caveats: No standalone consolidated shareholder-list document is issued; current membership must be assembled from the registered contrato social plus each registered cesión de cuotas.

Whether a routine IGJ/Registro Público extract itself already lists current socios varies by registry; the conservative basis is the registered constitution + registered transfers, hence 'additional_document' rather than 'base_document'.

summary: The S.A.'s constitution is the estatuto (acto constitutivo), which must be registered in the Registro Público (art. 5). A certified copy of the registered estatuto and its registered amendments is obtainable from the registry and is a distinct document from the register extract, so the constitution is proved by that additional registered document.

evidence.basis: Ley 19.550, art. 5
evidence.quote: El acto constitutivo, su modificación y el reglamento, si lo hubiese, se inscribirán en el Registro Público del domicilio social...

caveats: Distinct registry document from the register extract; the registered estatuto evidences the founders and by-laws but not the current shareholder body (see GESELLSCHAFTERLISTE / S.A.).

summary: The S.R.L.'s constitution is the contrato social, which must be registered in the Registro Público (art. 5). A certified copy of the registered contrato social (and its registered amendments) is obtainable from the registry and is a separate document from the register extract, so the constitution is proved by that additional registered document.

evidence.basis: Ley 19.550, art. 5
evidence.quote: El acto constitutivo, su modificación y el reglamento, si lo hubiese, se inscribirán en el Registro Público del domicilio social...
evidence.note: The constitutive instrument and its modifications are filed in the registry.

caveats: It is a distinct registry document from the register extract; availability of a certified copy and turnaround varies by IGJ vs provincial registry.

documentLocal: Certificado / constancia de inscripción del Registro Público (IGJ para CABA, o Registro Público de Comercio provincial)
summary: An S.A.'s acto constitutivo (estatuto) is registered in the Registro Público (art. 5), and the registry issues an extract/certificate evidencing the registered company. The register extract is itself the base document for the register-extract kind.

evidence.basis: Ley 19.550, art. 5
evidence.quote: El acto constitutivo, su modificación y el reglamento, si lo hubiese, se inscribirán en el Registro Público del domicilio social...

caveats: As with the S.R.L., there is no single nationwide standardized extract; document type and content differ between the IGJ (CABA) and provincial registries.

documentLocal: Certificado / constancia de inscripción del Registro Público (IGJ para CABA, o Registro Público de Comercio provincial)
summary: The acto constitutivo of an S.R.L. is registered in the Registro Público del domicilio social (Ley 19.550 art. 5), and the registry (IGJ in CABA, or the provincial Registro Público) issues an extract/certificate evidencing the registered company. The register extract is itself the base document that proves the register-extract kind.

evidence.basis: Ley General de Sociedades 19.550, art. 5
evidence.quote: El acto constitutivo, su modificación y el reglamento, si lo hubiese, se inscribirán en el Registro Público del domicilio social...
evidence.note: Registration in the Registro Público is the basis for a register extract.

caveats: Argentina has no single standardized nationwide register extract like the German Handelsregisterauszug; the exact document (constancia de inscripción, certificado de vigencia, informe) and its content vary between the IGJ (CABA) and each provincial Registro Público.

summary: Shareholders are not registered. Only a sole shareholder (Alleinaktionär) is recorded; otherwise the Aktienbuch is internal.

evidence.basis: § 5 FBG i.V.m. § 35 AktG (AT)
evidence.quote: alle Anteile an einer Aktiengesellschaft alleine oder neben der Gesellschaft einem Aktionär
evidence.note: Only in this sole-shareholder case is the Alleinaktionär registered (name, DOB/FN).

caveats: Sole-shareholder case is the only exception; partial holdings never appear.

summary: No separate shareholder list exists. The shareholders are statutory content of the register extract itself, which is a base document.

evidence.basis: § 5 Z 6 Firmenbuchgesetz (FBG)
evidence.quote: Name und Geburtsdatum der Gesellschafter, gegebenenfalls ihre Firmenbuchnummer, ihre Stammeinlagen und die darauf geleisteten Einzahlungen

evidence.basis: § 26 GmbHG (AT)
evidence.quote: diese Tatsachen unverzüglich zum Firmenbuch anzumelden
evidence.note: Registers each change as an event; this is the structural difference from the German §40 GmbHG, which maintains a list.

caveats: FlexKapGG 2023: holders of Unternehmenswert-Anteile are NOT named individually — only a note that they exist plus the aggregate sum.

WKO/USP checklists mention a founding 'Gesellschafterliste' attachment. It is court practice, not statutory (§ 9 Abs 2 GmbHG names only the Gesellschaftsvertrag and appointment documents), is never updated, and is NOT a §40 equivalent. The Hauptbuch entry is authoritative.

legalFormLocal: Australian public company limited by shares (Ltd)
summary: For a public company the member (shareholder) list is not held by ASIC. The obligation to notify ASIC of member-register particulars falls only on proprietary companies (Part 2C.2, ss 178A-178C); public companies are outside that regime and notify ASIC of no member changes. The complete statutory list is the company-held register of members (ss 168-169), open to inspection at the company (s 173), and - for listed companies - maintained by the share registry. It is not obtainable as an ASIC registry document, so a member list cannot be proved from the register.

evidence.basis: Corporations Act 2001 Part 2C.2 heading + s 178A(1) (Compilation No. 145, vol. 1 p.349)
evidence.quote: A proprietary company must notify ASIC within the time determined under section 178D and in the prescribed form, if: (a) it is required to add or alter a particular in the register it maintains under section 169 ...
evidence.note: Part 2C.2 is titled 'Notice by proprietary companies of changes to member register'; there is no equivalent duty on public companies, so ASIC carries no current member data for them.

evidence.basis: Corporations Act 2001 s 168(1) and s 169(1),(3) (Compilation No. 145, vol. 1 p.337-338)
evidence.quote: A company or registered scheme must set up and maintain: (a) a register of members (see section 169); ... The register of members must contain the following information about each member: (a) the member's name and address ... If the company has a share capital, the register must also show: ... (c) the shares held by each member; and (d) the class of shares ...
evidence.note: The complete member list is the company-held register, not an ASIC-held document.

evidence.basis: ASIC 'Guide to reading ASIC company extracts', Members note
evidence.quote: For each class of shares issued by a proprietary company, ASIC records the details of the top twenty members of the class (based on shareholdings).
evidence.note: Note is expressly limited to proprietary companies, confirming ASIC does not record members for public companies via this mechanism.

caveats: The register of members is inspectable/obtainable at the company itself (s 173) and, for listed companies, via the share registry / substantial-holder (Ch 6C) filings - but that is not the ASIC registry and not a certified full member list.
Historical member data may appear in old annual returns lodged before the 2003 annual-review reforms; this is not current and not a reliable member list.

INFERENCE: we treat the absence of a public-company member-notification duty (Part 2C.2 being proprietary-only) as meaning the ASIC extract's Members section is empty/limited for public companies; not tested against a live public-company extract.

legalFormLocal: Australian proprietary company limited by shares (Pty Ltd)
summary: For a proprietary company the same ASIC company extract carries a 'Members' section listing each member's name and address with class, number held, beneficial-holding flag and paid status. This exists because only proprietary companies must notify ASIC of member-register changes (Part 2C.2, s 178A). So the shareholder list is proved by the same base document as the Registerauszug. However, ASIC records only the top 20 members per class (s 178B), so for a proprietary company with more than 20 members in a class the extract is not the complete list.

evidence.basis: ASIC 'Guide to reading ASIC company extracts', Members section of the Current Company Extract
evidence.quote: For each class of shares issued by a proprietary company, ASIC records the details of the top twenty members of the class (based on shareholdings).
evidence.note: The Current Company Extract has a dedicated Members section (Name, Address, Class, Number Held, Beneficially Held, Paid) derived from the ASIC database under s.1274A Corporations Act 2001.

caveats: For a proprietary (Pty Ltd) company ASIC records only the top twenty members per class, so for companies with more than 20 members the extract's member list is not exhaustive.

legalFormLocal: Australian public company limited by shares (Ltd)
documentLabel: Company constitution (lodged copy), obtained via ASIC 'copies of documents'
summary: A public company that has a constitution must lodge a copy with ASIC within 14 days of the special resolution adopting it (and lodge any later modification), under s 136(5). The lodged constitution is therefore held by ASIC and can be ordered as a separate document (it does not appear on the company extract itself, which only lists it among lodged documents). This makes the constitution provable via an additional registry document for public companies.

evidence.basis: Corporations Act 2001 s 136(5) (Compilation No. 145, vol. 1 p.298)
evidence.quote: A public company must lodge with ASIC a copy of a special resolution adopting, modifying or repealing its constitution within 14 days after it is passed. The company must also lodge with ASIC within that period: (a) if the company adopts a constitution--a copy of that constitution; or (b) if the company modifies its constitution--a copy of that modification.

evidence.basis: Corporations Act 2001 s 136(1) (Compilation No. 145, vol. 1 p.297)
evidence.quote: A company adopts a constitution: (a) on registration--if each person specified in the application for the company's registration as a person who consents to become a member agrees in writing to the terms of a constitution before the application is lodged; or (b) after registration--if the company passes a special resolution adopting a constitution or a court order is made under section 233 that requires the company to adopt the constitution.

evidence.basis: ASIC company extract 'Documents' section (Guide, p.10)
evidence.quote: A list of all documents lodged for this organisation or business name.
evidence.note: The extract lists lodged documents (with document numbers); the constitution copy itself is obtained through ASIC's separate 'copies of documents' service, not printed on the extract.

caveats: Provable only where the public company has actually adopted a constitution. A public company may be governed solely by replaceable rules (s 135), in which case no constitution exists or is lodged.

The constitution is an 'additional' ordered document, not part of the base extract; retrieval depends on ASIC having a copy on file and the copy being available (the extract flags where a lodged document copy 'is not yet available').
UNVERIFIED: exact current ASIC product name/fee for ordering a lodged constitution copy was not fetched; the lodgement duty (s 136(5)) is the load-bearing, verified fact.

legalFormLocal: Australian proprietary company limited by shares (Pty Ltd)
summary: A proprietary company is not required to have or lodge a constitution: its internal management may be governed by the replaceable rules in the Corporations Act (s 135), by a constitution, or a combination (s 134); the sole-director/sole-shareholder Pty Ltd is governed by tailored rules with replaceable rules disapplied. The duty to lodge a constitution with ASIC under s 136(5) falls only on public companies (and Pty companies applying to convert to public). Therefore ASIC generally does not hold a proprietary company's constitution and it cannot be obtained as a registry document.

evidence.basis: Corporations Act 2001 s 136(5) (Compilation No. 145, vol. 1 p.298)
evidence.quote: A public company must lodge with ASIC a copy of a special resolution adopting, modifying or repealing its constitution within 14 days after it is passed. The company must also lodge with ASIC within that period: (a) if the company adopts a constitution--a copy of that constitution; or (b) if the company modifies its constitution--a copy of that modification.
evidence.note: Lodgement duty is expressed for 'a public company' only (plus Pty companies mid-conversion to public); no equivalent duty on ordinary proprietary companies.

evidence.basis: Corporations Act 2001 s 135(1) (Compilation No. 145, vol. 1 p.296-297)
evidence.quote: The section or subsection does not apply to a proprietary company while the same person is both its sole director and sole shareholder.

evidence.basis: ASIC 'Constitution and replaceable rules' page
evidence.quote: The replaceable rules do not apply to a proprietary company with a sole director who is also the sole shareholder.
evidence.note: Also states a company 'must be governed by either a constitution or a set of rules called the replaceable rules'.

caveats: If a Pty Ltd adopts a constitution it holds it privately and must supply a copy to a member on request within 7 days (s 139) - company-held, not a registry document.

Edge cases where ASIC may hold a Pty Ltd constitution: ASIC can direct lodgement of a consolidated constitution (s 138), and a Pty company applying to convert to public must lodge under the s 136(5) tail - these are exceptions, not the norm.
Where the company relies wholly on replaceable rules there is no 'constitution' document at all; the governing terms are the Act's provisions themselves.

legalFormLocal: Australian public company limited by shares (Ltd)
documentLocal: Current Company Extract / Current & Historical Company Extract
summary: The same ASIC 'Current Company Extract' (s 1274A) is issued for public companies and is the register extract of current registered particulars: organisation details, status, company type/class (public, limited by shares), registered office, officeholders and share structure. It is the direct equivalent of a Registerauszug for a public company and is a single base document.

evidence.basis: ASIC 'Guide to reading ASIC company extracts' (extract issued under s 1274A)
evidence.quote: This extract contains information derived from the Australian Securities and Investment Commission's (ASIC) database under section 1274A of the Corporations Act 2001.
evidence.note: Same extract product applies to all companies; company type field distinguishes 'Australian public company'.

evidence.basis: ASIC register search page / product listing
evidence.note: Company and organisation register; current company extract fee $10.

caveats: Same product-naming caveat as the Pty Ltd row: labelled 'Current Company Extract', not 'Registerauszug'.

UNVERIFIED against a public-company sample extract specifically: our public-vs-proprietary distinction is confirmed for the Members section (below) but the sample PDF ASIC publishes is a Pty Ltd; the organisation-details/officeholder/share-structure sections are identical products.

legalFormLocal: Australian proprietary company limited by shares (Pty Ltd)
documentLocal: Current Company Extract / Current & Historical Company Extract
summary: ASIC maintains the national companies register and issues a paid 'Current Company Extract' (and 'Current & Historical Company Extract') derived from its database under s 1274A of the Corporations Act 2001. This extract is the register extract of current registered particulars: organisation details, status, company type/class, registered office, officeholders and share structure. It is the direct Australian equivalent of a Registerauszug and is a single base document.

evidence.basis: ASIC 'Guide to reading ASIC company extracts', cover/Organisation Details pages
evidence.quote: This extract contains information derived from the Australian Securities and Investment Commission's (ASIC) database under section 1274A of the Corporations Act 2001.
evidence.note: Sample extract header for 'ASIC Company Pty Ltd'; shows Company type 'Australian proprietary company', Class 'Limited by shares'.

evidence.basis: ASIC register search page / product listing
evidence.note: Lists 'current company extract - fee $10' and links the sample PDF (current-company-extract-paid-feb-2026.pdf).

caveats: Product name is 'Current Company Extract' / 'Current & Historical Company Extract'; ASIC issues no document literally titled 'Registerauszug'.
The extract is a database-derived report under s 1274A, not a certified register 'page'; ASIC also issues a separate 'Certificate of Registration', which is not the particulars extract.

summary: Only the founders at incorporation are public, via the Staatsblad extract. Later transfers are never published. The shareholder register is private.

evidence.basis: art. 2:8 WVV
evidence.quote: naam, voornaam en woonplaats van de … oprichters
evidence.note: The extract of the oprichtingsakte published in the Bijlagen bij het Belgisch Staatsblad must state the founders, and shareholders who have not fully paid up their contribution. Founders at incorporation are public; later transfers are not.

evidence.basis: art. 5:24 WVV (BV) / 7:28 WVV (NV) — aandeelhoudersregister
evidence.note: Kept at the registered office or electronically (eStox). Inspection right belongs to shareholders only. Not filed with KBO/BCE or the Staatsblad. KBO Public Search publishes no shareholders.

caveats: Sole shareholder (art. 2:8 §4 WVV): the identity is FILED in the company dossier at the Enterprise Court registry, but only the FACT is published in the Staatsblad. The dossier is consultable at the registry, so the identity is obtainable but not published. A company incorporated as single-shareholder from the outset has no such duty.

SOURCE CONFLICT: an aggregated summary claimed §4 requires the identity to be published. The primary text and law-firm sources (Monard, Cazimir/Practicali) say filed, not published. The latter is trusted.

UNVERIFIED: annual accounts (NBB VOL model) carry a 'Structuur van het aandeelhouderschap' note, but it reports only holdings notified under transparency rules (art. 7:225 WVV) — typically empty for an unlisted BV. Exact scope not extracted from the NBB PDF.

summary: Shareholders of an AD are NOT a registered particular. Ownership is recorded in the company's own book of shareholders (книга на акционерите) for registered shares, kept internally by the company and not public. The Commercial Register shows only the founders named in the constitutive act at incorporation and the share capital — not the current shareholder body. Consequently no register document evidences the current list of shareholders. (Exception: for a single-member ЕАД the sole owner of the capital is a registered particular.)

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice Portal — Business registers in Bulgaria (official)
evidence.quote: basic information, stored in the form of structured data, about the name, legal form, head office and address of the company, its managing bodies, objects (if applicable), partners (if applicable), and shareholding capital (if applicable).
evidence.note: For an AD the register lists 'shareholding capital' but not the shareholders — 'partners (if applicable)' applies to OOD, not AD shareholders.

caveats: For a single-member ЕАД the sole shareholder IS registered, so for that sub-form the extract does evidence sole ownership — treat ЕАД separately.
Founders appear in the constitutive act/минути on file, but that evidences ownership only at incorporation, not the current shareholder list.
Book of shareholders (книга на акционерите, ~Art. 179 Търговски закон) is a private company record — statutory reference not verified verbatim; INFERENCE.

summary: For an OOD the partners (съдружници) are a registered particular published in the Commercial Register, and every transfer of a company share is itself entered in the register. The current register extract therefore lists the current members and their participations, so the base register document evidences the equivalent of a shareholder/member list. This is a genuine registered particular, not merely a snapshot of founders — unlike an AD.

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice Portal — Business registers in Bulgaria (official)
evidence.quote: basic information, stored in the form of structured data, about the name, legal form, head office and address of the company, its managing bodies, objects (if applicable), partners (if applicable), and shareholding capital (if applicable).
evidence.note: The register expressly holds 'partners (if applicable)' — applicable to OOD partners (съдружници). For AD it lists only shareholding capital, not shareholders.

caveats: The 'list' is proved via the register extract's partners field plus the дружествен договор, not a standalone 'Gesellschafterliste' document as in Germany.
Underlying statutory basis (Art. 119(2) and Art. 129/140 Търговски закон requiring entry of partners and registration of share transfers) not verified verbatim — INFERENCE; source pages were blocked.

summary: The constitution of an AD is the устав (statute). It is filed with the Commercial Register and kept in the company's public electronic file, downloadable free by anyone. It is the base constitutive document evidencing the constitution; it names the founders and the capital but does not establish the current shareholder body.

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice Portal — Business registers in Bulgaria (official)
evidence.quote: particulars that are required by law to be registered and the documents that are required by law to be kept available for public inspection
evidence.note: The устав is among the documents kept for public inspection in the AD's company file.

caveats: Statutory basis (Art. 165/174 Търговски закон on the content and filing of the устав) not verified verbatim — INFERENCE; lex.bg blocked.

summary: The constitution of an OOD is the дружествен договор (for a single-member ЕООД, the учредителен акт). It is filed with the Commercial Register and kept in the company's public electronic file, downloadable by anyone free of charge. It states the partners, capital and each partner's share, so it is the base constitutive document evidencing the constitution.

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice Portal — Business registers in Bulgaria (official)
evidence.quote: particulars that are required by law to be registered and the documents that are required by law to be kept available for public inspection
evidence.note: The дружествен договор is among the documents kept available for public inspection in the company file.

caveats: Statutory basis (Art. 114/115 Търговски закон on the content and filing of the дружествен договор) not verified verbatim — INFERENCE; lex.bg blocked.

documentLabel: Commercial Register extract / current status certificate
documentLocal: Удостоверение за актуално състояние (извлечение от Търговския регистър)
summary: As for all Bulgarian traders, an AD has a public account in the free online Commercial Register from which a current status extract can be obtained. The extract evidences name, legal form, seat, object, capital, and the management/representation bodies (board of directors or management/supervisory boards). It is the base register document.

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice Portal — Business registers in Bulgaria (official)
evidence.quote: The business register is accessible 24 hours a day at https://portal.registryagency.bg/en/. Anyone may search the business register and the non-profit legal entities register. Access to the registers' database is open to everyone free of charge.
evidence.note: Same free public register applies to AD.

caveats: Article numbers of the Търговски закон not verified verbatim — INFERENCE; source pages blocked.

documentLabel: Commercial Register extract / current status certificate
documentLocal: Удостоверение за актуално състояние (извлечение от Търговския регистър)
summary: The Bulgarian Commercial Register (Търговски регистър, Registry Agency) is a free, public, online register from which anyone can obtain a company extract / current status certificate. For an OOD this extract is the base register document evidencing the registered particulars: name, legal form, seat, object, capital, managers and partners. It is generated directly from the register account and requires no additional filing.

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice Portal — Business registers in Bulgaria (official)
evidence.quote: The business register is accessible 24 hours a day at https://portal.registryagency.bg/en/. Anyone may search the business register and the non-profit legal entities register. Access to the registers' database is open to everyone free of charge.
evidence.note: Confirms free, public online access and that the register issues extracts.

caveats: Article numbers of the Търговски закон (Commercial Act) not independently verified verbatim; lex.bg and registryagency.bg PDFs returned HTTP 403/302 and could not be fetched — INFERENCE from established Bulgarian company law.

summary: For a BSC the capital is divided into negotiable shares, and shareholding is established by entry in the company's own register of shareholders held at its head office; for listed public companies the shares are dematerialised and held through the central depository (Bahrain Clear). The Commercial Register / Sijilat shows only the founders and the board/authorized signatories, not the current shareholder body. The current shareholder list is therefore not provable from the MOIC registry — it must come from the company's share register or Bahrain Clear.

evidence.basis: Commercial Companies Law, Legislative Decree No. 21 of 2001, provisions on joint-stock companies keeping a register of nominal shares/shareholders and transfer by entry therein; Bahrain Clear (CSD) for listed issuers
evidence.note: Share-register provisions sit in the CCL joint-stock chapter (approx. Arts. 116-118) — UNVERIFIED. Verbatim text not fetched.

caveats: INFERENCE/UNVERIFIED: no primary-source text fetched.
The registered memorandum lists only founders/initial subscribers, not current shareholders.
Current shareholders are evidenced by the company's own share register or, for listed companies, Bahrain Clear — neither is a MOIC/Sijilat registry document.

summary: A WLL is not a public-issue company; its partners and their shareholdings are fixed in the registered memorandum of association and reflected on the Commercial Register. The Sijilat CR record shows the company's owners/partners, so the members are provable directly from the registry base document. The authoritative statement of each partner's quota/share is the registered memorandum of association, which corroborates the split.

evidence.basis: Commercial Companies Law, Legislative Decree No. 21 of 2001, provisions on the With Limited Liability Company (memorandum must state partners and their shares; shares not offered to the public); MOIC/Sijilat CR record of partners
evidence.note: Relevant provisions sit in the CCL chapter on WLLs (approx. Arts. 262 et seq.) — article numbers UNVERIFIED. Verbatim text not fetched.

caveats: INFERENCE/UNVERIFIED: no primary-source text fetched.
Bahrain has no separate statutory 'shareholder list' filing like Germany's Gesellschafterliste; proof is the CR partners section, backed by the registered memorandum of association.
Whether the public CR search exposes each partner's percentage (vs. only names) is UNVERIFIED; the definitive split is in the memorandum.

summary: A BSC is constituted by its founders through a memorandum of association and articles of association (statute), which are filed with MOIC as part of incorporation. Together these form the constitution equivalent to a German Gesellschaftsvertrag and are documents distinct from the CR extract. They are obtained as additional filed documents rather than being contained in the base register extract.

evidence.basis: Commercial Companies Law, Legislative Decree No. 21 of 2001, provisions requiring a memorandum and articles of association for a joint-stock company and their filing with MOIC
evidence.note: CCL joint-stock chapter (approx. Arts. 63 et seq.) — UNVERIFIED. Verbatim text not fetched.

caveats: INFERENCE/UNVERIFIED: no primary-source text fetched.
Third-party obtainability of the filed memorandum and articles from MOIC/Sijilat is uncertain; listed BSCs additionally publish their articles via Bahrain Bourse disclosures.
Amendments require registration; the current consolidated version is the relevant document.

summary: A WLL is constituted by a written, authenticated memorandum of association (contract of establishment) that is filed with MOIC as part of registration. This is the constitution equivalent to a German Gesellschaftsvertrag and is a document distinct from the CR extract. It is obtained as an additional filed document rather than being contained in the base register extract.

evidence.basis: Commercial Companies Law, Legislative Decree No. 21 of 2001, provisions requiring a written/authenticated memorandum of association for a WLL and its filing with MOIC
evidence.note: CCL WLL chapter (approx. Arts. 262 et seq.) — UNVERIFIED. Verbatim text not fetched.

caveats: INFERENCE/UNVERIFIED: no primary-source text fetched.
Third-party obtainability of the filed memorandum from MOIC/Sijilat is uncertain — it may require being a stakeholder or a formal request rather than open public download.
Amendments to the memorandum must also be registered; the current consolidated version is the relevant document.

summary: A Bahraini Shareholding Company (public B.S.C. or closed B.S.C.(c)) is entered on the Commercial Register maintained by MOIC via Sijilat. The CR extract is the base register document showing company particulars — CR number, legal form, trade name, issued/paid-up capital, registered address, objects and authorized signatories/board. It is the direct analogue of a German Registerauszug.

evidence.basis: Commercial Companies Law, Legislative Decree No. 21 of 2001 (joint-stock company registration in the Commercial Register); MOIC/Sijilat commercial registration system
evidence.note: Verbatim text not fetched (source fetches blocked). UNVERIFIED against primary text.

caveats: INFERENCE/UNVERIFIED: no primary-source text fetched.
Public Sijilat search shows summary particulars; a certified CR extract is issued by MOIC/Sijilat.

summary: The Ministry of Industry and Commerce (MOIC) maintains the Commercial Register via the Sijilat platform, and a WLL is entered on it. The CR extract is the base register document evidencing company particulars — CR number, legal form, trade name, capital, registered address, activities and the registered owners/partners. This is the direct analogue of a German Registerauszug.

evidence.basis: Commercial Companies Law, Legislative Decree No. 21 of 2001 (registration in the Commercial Register); MOIC/Sijilat commercial registration system
evidence.note: Verbatim text not fetched (all source fetches blocked). WLL registration and CR-extract contents asserted from knowledge of the CCL and Sijilat; UNVERIFIED against primary text.

caveats: INFERENCE/UNVERIFIED: no primary-source text fetched; based on knowledge of the CCL 21/2001 and Sijilat.
Public Sijilat CR search shows summary particulars; a full certified CR extract is issued by MOIC/Sijilat.

summary: Under the Companies Act 1981 every Bermuda company must keep a register of members at its registered office, open to inspection there (members free; public on payment). Crucially the register of members is COMPANY-HELD and is NOT filed with, kept by, or issued by the Registrar. So there is no Registrar-issued document evidencing current shareholders — current ownership cannot be proved FROM the registry; the proof exists only at the company's registered office (or a company-provided certified extract).

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1981 (Bermuda) s.65 (register of members; kept at the registered office) — company-held, not filed with the Registrar
evidence.note: Verbatim NOT retrieved (search budget exhausted; bermudalaws.bm deep-links 404'd). Established position: s.65 requires the company to keep the register of members; maintained at the registered office (s.62), not lodged with the Registrar.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1981 (Bermuda) — inspection of the register of members at the registered office (members free; public on payment)
evidence.note: Inspection right sits in the s.65-67 area; exact subsection unverified this session. Inspection is AT THE COMPANY, not a Registrar issuance.

caveats: KEY DISTINCTION: the register of members is inspectable AT THE COMPANY's registered office, NOT a Registrar-issued document — so for KYC-from-the-registry it is NOT provable.
No Gesellschafterliste equivalent is filed with the Registrar; beneficial-ownership data under the Beneficial Ownership Act is held by the BMA and is NOT a public register.
INFERENCE / UNVERIFIED this session: exact inspection subsection not confirmed against retrieved primary text.

summary: A Bermuda exempted company's constitution comprises the Memorandum of Association and the Bye-laws. The Memorandum IS filed with the Registrar on incorporation and forms part of the public record — a certified copy is obtainable, so the constitution's filed component is provable from the registry. The Bye-laws (governing directors, shares, meetings) are adopted by the company and NOT filed; they are private. So the constitution is provable from the registry only as to the Memorandum, not the bye-laws.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1981 (Bermuda) — Memorandum of Association filed with the Registrar on incorporation; part of the public register
evidence.note: Verbatim NOT retrieved this session. Established position: the memorandum is delivered to and registered by the Registrar (incorporation provisions) and is publicly obtainable; the Registrar issues the Certificate of Incorporation on registration.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1981 (Bermuda) s.13 area — Bye-laws adopted by the company, NOT filed with the Registrar (private)
evidence.note: Established position: bye-laws are not lodged with the Registrar and are not a public document; exact section unverified this session.

caveats: Only the MEMORANDUM OF ASSOCIATION is filed/public and thus provable; the BYE-LAWS (the substantive operating constitution) are NOT filed and are private — the constitution is only partially provable from the registry.
INFERENCE / UNVERIFIED this session: exact Companies Act 1981 section numbers not confirmed against retrieved primary text (gov.bm SPA; bermudalaws.bm deep-links 404'd).

summary: Bermuda's Registrar of Companies does not issue a line-item 'register extract' listing current directors/shareholders/capital. The Registrar-issued document evidencing current standing is the Certificate of Compliance (a good-standing certificate), confirming the company is incorporated, in existence and has met filing/fee obligations. Closest Registrar-issued base document to a REGISTERAUSZUG; obtained on request from the Registrar.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1981 (Bermuda), s.14(1) — Registrar maintains the register of companies
evidence.quote: Subject to subsection (4), the Registrar shall maintain a register of companies in such form as he shall determine.
evidence.note: Consolidated Companies Act 1981 (official PDF hosted by the Bermuda Monetary Authority); downloaded and verified verbatim this session.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1981 (Bermuda), s.14(2) — registration and certificate of incorporation
evidence.quote: shall register the memorandum, issue a certificate of incorporation showing the date of registration and attach to the certificate a facsimile of the memorandum delivered to him.
evidence.note: The Registrar's registration deliverable is the certificate of incorporation (one-time) attached to the memorandum — not a line-item extract.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1981 (Bermuda), s.14(4) — contents of the register of companies
evidence.quote: The Registrar shall, in respect of each company registered under this section, enter in the register— ... the name of the company; ... the certificate of incorporation of the company; ... the Memorandum of Association of the company; and ... the address of the registered office of the company.
evidence.note: Ellipses stand for the subparagraph letters (a)-(d). The statutory register content is limited to these four items — no current directors, members or capital line-items — which is why Bermuda has no itemised Registerauszug-style extract.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1981 (Bermuda), s.14 (final subsection) — public inspection of the register of companies
evidence.quote: The register of companies, containing the information entered under subsection (4) and such other information as the Registrar may determine, shall be open to public inspection at the office of the Registrar during normal business hours.
evidence.note: Public access to the Registrar's register is statutory; the register is a base registry source even though its content is thin (s.14(4)).

evidence.basis: Registrar of Companies (Bermuda) — online portal (registrarofcompanies.gov.bm)
evidence.quote: Request certificates
evidence.note: The Registrar's portal offers certificate ordering (in Registrar practice this includes the Certificate of Compliance / good standing). Full-text search of the consolidated Companies Act 1981 this session found NO occurrence of 'certificate of compliance' — it is an administrative product, not a statutory document.

caveats: Bermuda has NO true 'register extract': the statutory register of companies holds only the company name, certificate of incorporation, Memorandum of Association and registered-office address (s.14(4), verified verbatim) — the Registrar issues certificates (Incorporation, Compliance/good standing), not a listing of current directors/members/capital.
The Certificate of Compliance confirms EXISTENCE and GOOD STANDING; it does not itself list shareholders or (fully) directors.

'Certificate of Compliance' is a Registrar practice/product name — the Companies Act 1981 does not define it (verified by full-text search of the consolidated Act this session); Registrar-issued certificate wording itself could not be fetched (gov.bm/portal are login-gated SPAs).

documentLabel: Simplified certificate listing the sócios and their capital participation (member list embedded in the register extract)
documentLocal: Certidão Simplificada — relação de sócios e participação no capital
summary: For a Ltda the members (sócios) and the quota of each in the share capital are mandatory contents of the registered contrato social (CC art. 997, IV, applied to the Ltda by art. 1.054) and are carried into the Junta's certified extract, which lists the current sócios and their participation. The member list is therefore provable from the Junta register. If the extract's breakdown is insufficient, the full authoritative list of quotas is in the registered contrato social / última alteração (an additional archived document), which is independently provable.

evidence.basis: Código Civil, Art. 997, IV
evidence.quote: a quota de cada sócio no capital social, e o modo de realizá-la;
evidence.note: Art. 997 caput: the contract must mention, inter alia (inciso I) 'nome, nacionalidade, estado civil, profissão e residência dos sócios'.

evidence.basis: Código Civil, Art. 1.054
evidence.quote: O contrato mencionará, no que couber, as indicações do art. 997, e, se for o caso, a firma social.
evidence.note: Extends the art. 997 mandatory contents (sócios + quotas) to the sociedade limitada.

evidence.basis: Lei 8.934/1994, art. 29 — publicidade do Registro Público de Empresas Mercantis
evidence.quote: Qualquer pessoa, sem necessidade de provar interesse, poderá consultar os assentamentos existentes nas juntas comerciais e obter certidões, mediante pagamento do preço devido.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'Any person, without having to prove an interest, may consult the records held by the juntas comerciais and obtain certificates (certidões), on payment of the fee due.' Fetched from the Câmara dos Deputados official legislation portal (publicação original text); planalto.gov.br was unreachable this session (ECONNRESET x2).

evidence.basis: Lei 8.934/1994, art. 32, II, a — arquivamento dos atos constitutivos na Junta Comercial
evidence.quote: dos documentos relativos à constituição, alteração, dissolução e extinção de firmas mercantis individuais, sociedades mercantis e cooperativas
evidence.note: English gloss: the registry comprises the arquivamento (archiving) 'of the documents relating to the constitution, amendment, dissolution and extinction of individual merchant firms, commercial companies and cooperatives'. Original 1994 wording from the Câmara portal (later amendments modernised some terms). Together with CC arts. 997/1.054 and art. 29, this closes the chain: the contrato social carrying each sócio's quota is archived at the Junta and obtainable by anyone via certidão.

caveats: INFERENCE: That the Certidão Simplificada itself lists each sócio's capital participation was not fetched verbatim; it rests on established Junta practice. If a caller requires certified per-partner quota amounts, treat the registered contrato social / última alteração (additional document) as the definitive source — its status would be proved_by_additional_document.

Ownership updates via cessão de quotas take effect against the company and third parties only upon averbação of the instrument at the Junta (CC art. 1.057, parágrafo único), so the register reflects a member list only as current as the last archived alteração.

summary: Current shareholders of an S.A. are not held by the Junta Comercial. They are recorded in company-held corporate books — the Livro de Registro de Ações Nominativas (Lei 6.404/76 art. 100, I), which records the shareholder's name and number of shares. The registry evidences only the founding subscribers at incorporation (the relação completa dos subscritores archived under art. 95, II), not the ongoing shareholder base. Therefore the current shareholder list is not provable from the registry.

evidence.quote: do nome do acionista e do número das suas ações;
evidence.note: The 'Registro de Ações Nominativas' book — held by the company, not the Junta — records shareholder names and shareholdings.

evidence.quote: a relação completa, autenticada pelos fundadores ou pelo presidente da assembléia, dos subscritores do capital social, com a qualificação, número das ações e o total da entrada de cada subscritor
evidence.note: Only the incorporation-time subscribers list is archived at the registro do comércio — this is a snapshot of founders, not the current shareholder register.

caveats: Partial exception: the FOUNDING subscribers (art. 95, II) are provable from the archived constitutive acts (ata de constituição / assembleia), but these are not the current shareholders and go stale on any share transfer.
Companhias abertas (listed/CVM-registered) disclose relevant shareholders via CVM filings, but that is a separate securities-regulator source, not the Junta Comercial registry.

documentLabel: Full copy of the registered articles of association (and consolidated amendment)
documentLocal: Contrato Social (e última alteração contratual / consolidação) — inteiro teor arquivado na Junta Comercial
summary: The constitutive act of a Ltda is the contrato social, which must be filed at the Junta Comercial (CC art. 1.150; art. 998/999 on inscrição and averbação of every modification). It is a distinct archived document — obtained as a certified full copy (inteiro teor), not printed on the simplified extract — so it is provable from the registry as an additional document.

evidence.basis: Código Civil, Art. 997 (caput)
evidence.quote: A sociedade constitui-se mediante contrato escrito, particular ou público, que, além de cláusulas estipuladas pelas partes, mencionará:
evidence.note: The contrato social is the constitutive instrument.

evidence.basis: Código Civil, Art. 999, parágrafo único
evidence.quote: Qualquer modificação do contrato social será averbada, cumprindo-se as formalidades previstas no artigo antecedente.
evidence.note: Confirms amendments (alterações) are also registered — so the registered contrato + alterações are the full constitution.

caveats: Classed as additional because the full contrato social is retrieved as a separate certified copy (inteiro teor) rather than appearing on the base Certidão Simplificada.

documentLabel: Full copy of the registered by-laws (and constitutive minutes)
documentLocal: Estatuto Social (e ata de constituição / atas de assembleia) — inteiro teor arquivado na Junta Comercial
summary: The constitution of an S.A. is the estatuto social, which must be archived at the registro do comércio / Junta Comercial (Lei 6.404/76 art. 95, I when constituted by assembleia; art. 96 when by escritura pública). It is a distinct archived document obtained as a certified full copy (inteiro teor), not on the simplified extract, so it is provable from the registry as an additional document.

evidence.quote: um exemplar do estatuto social, assinado por todos os subscritores (artigo 88, § 1º) ou, se a subscrição houver sido pública, os originais do estatuto e do prospecto, assinados pelos fundadores
evidence.note: The estatuto social is filed at the registro do comércio (Junta Comercial).

evidence.quote: Se a companhia tiver sido constituída por escritura pública, bastará o arquivamento de certidão do instrumento.
evidence.note: Alternative constitution route — still archived at the registro do comércio.

caveats: Classed as additional because the full estatuto is retrieved as a separate certified copy (inteiro teor), not printed on the base Certidão Simplificada.
Statutory amendments (reforma estatutária) are approved by assembleia and filed at the Junta, so the current estatuto = last archived consolidated version.

documentLabel: Simplified certificate / register extract from the Junta Comercial
summary: A sociedade empresária Ltda is bound to the Registro Público de Empresas Mercantis kept by the state Junta Comercial (CC art. 1.150). The Junta issues a Certidão Simplificada — the certified register extract of the currently archived particulars (firm name, NIRE, CNPJ, seat, capital, sócios and administradores). This is the base register-extract document, equivalent to the German Registerauszug.

evidence.basis: Código Civil (Lei 10.406/2002), Art. 1.150
evidence.quote: O empresário e a sociedade empresária vinculam-se ao Registro Público de Empresas Mercantis a cargo das Juntas Comerciais, e a sociedade simples ao Registro Civil das Pessoas Jurídicas
evidence.note: Establishes the Junta Comercial as the mercantile register for the sociedade empresária; the certidão is its extract.

evidence.basis: DREI (Departamento Nacional de Registro Empresarial e Integração) — coordinator of the Juntas Comerciais
evidence.note: The Certidão Simplificada / ficha cadastral is the standard certified extract issued by the Juntas Comerciais under DREI normalization (IN DREI 81/2020). Verbatim content of the certidão model was not fetched.

caveats: INFERENCE: The specific field list of the Certidão Simplificada (that it enumerates sócios and capital) was not fetched verbatim from a DREI/Junta source; grounded on established Junta Comercial practice under IN DREI 81/2020, not on a fetched quote.

documentLabel: Simplified certificate / register extract from the Junta Comercial
summary: A companhia (S.A.) cannot operate without its constitutive acts being archived (Lei 6.404/76 art. 94) at the registro do comércio / Junta Comercial. The Junta issues the Certidão Simplificada as the certified extract of the currently registered particulars (firm name, NIRE, CNPJ, seat, capital, directors). This is the base register-extract document.

evidence.quote: Nenhuma companhia poderá funcionar sem que sejam arquivados e publicados seus atos constitutivos.
A certidão dos atos constitutivos da companhia, passada pelo registro do comércio em que foram arquivados, será o documento hábil
evidence.note: The registro do comércio (Junta Comercial) issues the certidão of the archived constitutive acts.

caveats: INFERENCE: Exact field list of the S.A. Certidão Simplificada not fetched verbatim; grounded on standard Junta/DREI practice. Unlike a Ltda, the S.A. extract does NOT list current shareholders (see GESELLSCHAFTERLISTE row).

legalFormLocal: Company Limited by Shares (Companies Act, Ch. 308)
documentLocal: Annual list of members and summary
summary: Unlike an IBC, a domestic company with share capital must at least once a year make a list of all members with names, addresses, occupations and shares held (Companies Act, CH.308, s.58(1)), and 'a copy shall be forwarded to the Registrar to be kept by him in his office with the original memorandum' (s.58(2)). So a member list DOES reach the registry as an additional (annual) filing. The company's own register of members is also open to inspection by any member of the public on payment of a fee (s.56(2)). Current membership is provable via the registry-held annual list — an annual snapshot, not a live register.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Bahamas) CH.308 s.58(1) — Annual list of members
evidence.quote: a list of all persons who ... are members of the company; and such list shall state the names and addresses and occupations of all the members ... and the number of shares held by each of them
evidence.note: Re-verified verbatim this session against the official consolidated PDF downloaded from laws.bahamas.gov.bs.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Bahamas) CH.308 s.58(2) — filing with Registrar
evidence.quote: a copy shall be forwarded to the Registrar to be kept by him in his office with the original memorandum.
evidence.note: Re-verified verbatim this session against the official consolidated PDF downloaded from laws.bahamas.gov.bs.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Bahamas) CH.308 s.56(2) — register of members open to public at registered office
evidence.quote: The register of members ... may be inspected by members of the company and any member of the general public upon payment of such fee as may be determined by the directors
evidence.note: Attaches to the company-held register at the registered office, subject to director-imposed restrictions — distinct from the Registrar-held annual list. Re-verified verbatim this session against the official consolidated PDF.

caveats: The registry-held member evidence is an ANNUAL list (up to ~12 months stale), not a live current-members extract — additional, not base.

Public accessibility of the Registrar-held annual list: s.58(2) requires the copy to be kept 'with the original memorandum', but a full-text search of CH.308 this session CONFIRMS the Act contains no general 'any person may inspect' clause for Registrar-held records (unlike IBC Act s.191); third-party release rests on Registrar practice — s.16(3) certified copies expressly cover only the memorandum/articles or extracts therefrom.
Many small/older domestic companies may be delinquent on the annual list, so the document may not exist for a given company.

summary: For an IBC the register of members ('Share Register') is kept at the registered office, maintained by the registered agent (IBC Act, CH.309, s.29 with s.37/s.38). It is NOT filed with the Registrar and is NOT public. The Registrar's Register of IBCs records only the M&A and (publicly) the directors and officers — it does NOT hold or publish a list of current shareholders. Current membership cannot be proved from the registry; only the registered agent holds the Share Register.

evidence.basis: IBC Act (Bahamas) CH.309 s.29(1) — Share Register
evidence.quote: A company shall cause to be kept at its registered office one or more registers to be known as Share Registers containing -- (a) the names and addresses of the persons who hold registered shares in the company

evidence.basis: IBC Act (Bahamas) CH.309 s.38(1) — Registered agent (holds registered-office records)
evidence.quote: A company shall at all times have a registered agent in The Bahamas.
evidence.note: The registered office (s.37) at which the Share Register is kept is provided by the licensed registered agent; the members register never reaches the Registrar.

caveats: BLUNT: the IBC register of members is registered-agent/registered-office held, not filed with the Registrar and not public.
The only optional filing of registers under the IBC Act (s.36B/36C) concerns mortgages and charges, NOT the share/member register.
The Register of Beneficial Ownership Act 2018 requires BO data held (via registered agents) for competent-authority access only — NOT public, no shareholder-list document to a third party.

legalFormLocal: Company Limited by Shares (Companies Act, Ch. 308)
summary: A domestic company's memorandum is filed with and registered by the Registrar, who issues the certificate of incorporation (Companies Act, CH.308, ss.9, 16), and the articles are filed within a set period after incorporation (s.10). A Registrar-certified copy or extract of the memorandum or articles is expressly receivable in evidence (s.16(3)). The constitution is evidenced by a base registry document.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Bahamas) CH.308 s.16(3) — certified copy of memorandum/articles
evidence.quote: A copy of a memorandum or articles filed and registered in accordance with this Act or any extract therefrom certified under the hand and seal of the Registrar as a true copy shall be received in evidence

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Bahamas) CH.308 s.19(f) — memorandum filing recorded by Registrar
evidence.quote: the date of the filing of the memorandum;
evidence.note: Confirms the memorandum is filed with the Registrar; articles filed under s.10.

caveats: Obtain the current version — s.29 permits alteration of the memorandum and articles; amendments are filed.
Companies Act verified verbatim against the official Bahamas laws portal consolidated edition.

summary: The Memorandum and Articles are registered by the Registrar in the Register of International Business Companies (IBC Act, CH.309, s.15(1)) and the Registrar issues a certificate of incorporation (s.15(2)). Any person may inspect documents kept by the Registrar and obtain a Registrar-certified copy or extract (s.191). The constitution is evidenced by a base registry document — a certified copy of the M&A.

evidence.basis: IBC Act (Bahamas) CH.309 s.15(1) — Registration
evidence.quote: The Memorandum and the Articles shall be registered by the Registrar in a register to be maintained by him and to be known as the Register of International Business Companies.

evidence.basis: IBC Act (Bahamas) CH.309 s.191(1) — Inspection / certified copies
evidence.quote: may ... inspect the documents kept by the Registrar pursuant to this Act
evidence.note: s.191(1)(b) allows any person to require a Registrar-certified copy or extract, which is prima facie evidence.

caveats: Amendments to the M&A are also filed; ensure the copy obtained is the current/consolidated version.
Quote verified verbatim against the consolidated LRO edition.

legalFormLocal: Company Limited by Shares (Companies Act, Ch. 308)
summary: For a domestic company the Registrar maintains a statutory Register with the company's name, registered office, capital/shares, subscribers and filing dates (Companies Act, CH.308, s.19). The Registrar issues, on request by any person, a Certificate of Good Standing (s.277) and a certified copy/extract of filed documents is admissible in evidence (s.16(3)). Registration/current status is evidenced by a base registry document.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Bahamas), CH.308 (Act 18 of 1992) s.19 — Register of companies
evidence.quote: The Registrar shall maintain a register in which shall be entered the following particulars — (a) the name of the company; (b) the location in The Bahamas of the registered office; (c) the amount of capital of the company, the number of shares into which it is divided and either the nominal value of each share or that the shares are shares of no par value; (d) the names, addresses and occupations of the subscribers to the memorandum and the number of shares taken by each subscriber;
evidence.note: Quote extended and re-verified verbatim this session against the official consolidated PDF (s.19 continues with (e) date of execution, (f) date of filing, (g) company number, (h) guarantee/unlimited statement).

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Bahamas) CH.308 s.277(1) — Certificate of good standing
evidence.quote: The Registrar shall, upon request by any person, issue a certificate of good standing under his hand and seal certifying that a company incorporated or registered under this Act is of good standing
evidence.note: Re-verified verbatim this session; s.277(1) continues with the conditions (name on the Register, fees/penalties paid, required filings made).

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Bahamas) CH.308 s.16(3) — certified copies/extracts
evidence.quote: A copy of a memorandum or articles filed and registered in accordance with this Act or any extract therefrom certified under the hand and seal of the Registrar as a true copy shall be received in evidence
evidence.note: Re-verified verbatim this session against the official consolidated PDF.

caveats: s.19 records the founding subscribers, not current members; a live consolidated 'extract' is not a defined product — the base proof is the Certificate of Good Standing plus certified copies of filed documents.
Directors are filed (s.80(4)) but that filing's public-inspection is not spelled out as explicitly as the IBC Act.
Companies Act re-verified verbatim this session against the official consolidated PDF; live registry search/fee UX not tested.

summary: The Registrar maintains the Register of International Business Companies (IBC Act, CH.309, s.15). The Registrar issues, on request by any person, a Certificate of Good Standing (s.190) and any person may inspect the documents kept by the Registrar and obtain certified copies/extracts (s.191). A copy of the register of directors and officers is filed with the Registrar and open to public inspection (s.44(4)). Registration and current status IS evidenced by a Registrar-issued document; there is no single consolidated live extract — the Certificate of Good Standing plus certified extracts are the registry proof.

evidence.basis: International Business Companies Act (Bahamas), CH.309 s.190(1)
evidence.quote: The Registrar shall, upon request by any person issue a certificate of good standing under his hand and seal certifying that a company incorporated under this Act is of good standing

evidence.basis: IBC Act (Bahamas) CH.309 s.191(1)(a) — Inspection of documents
evidence.quote: may ... inspect the documents kept by the Registrar pursuant to this Act
evidence.note: Any person; certified copies/extracts of certificate of incorporation, merger, continuation, dissolution or good standing obtainable under s.191(1)(b).

evidence.basis: IBC Act (Bahamas) CH.309 s.44(4) — Register of directors and officers
evidence.quote: A copy of the register of directors and officers shall be filed with the Registrar and shall be open to inspection by members of the public during official hours.
evidence.note: Public director-filing derives from the 2001 amendment (19 of 2001), predating the 2019 reforms.

caveats: No consolidated 'current register extract' equivalent to a Handelsregisterauszug; the base registry document is the Certificate of Good Standing (status only) plus certified copies/extracts of filed documents.
The publicly-filed IBC register of directors/officers can be stale: s.44(6)/(8) allow filing within 12 months.
Quotes from the official Bahamas On-line Legislation portal (consolidated LRO edition); live registry-search UX not tested.

legalFormLocal: Corporation incorporated under the Canada Business Corporations Act (federal corporation)
summary: In Canada shareholders are not filed with or held by the public registry. Under CBCA s.50 the corporation itself maintains a securities register listing its security holders, kept at its registered office (or by an agent) and open only to shareholders/creditors — it is never sent to Corporations Canada. The separate register of individuals with significant control (ISC) is also corporation-kept (s.21.1); although some ISC data became publicly searchable in 2024 (s.21.303), that is a beneficial-ownership data query about 25%+ individuals, not a document and not a shareholder list. No registry document evidences the shareholders.

evidence.basis: CBCA s.50(1) — securities register
evidence.quote: A corporation shall maintain a securities register in which it records the securities issued by it in registered form, showing with respect to each class or series of securities (a) the names, alphabetically arranged, and the latest known address of each person who is or has been a security holder; (b) the number of securities held by each security holder; and (c) the date and particulars of the issue and transfer of each security.
evidence.note: Register is company-held, not filed with the Director.

evidence.basis: CBCA s.21.1 — register of individuals with significant control
evidence.quote: The corporation shall prepare and maintain, at its registered office or at any other place in Canada designated by the directors, a register
evidence.note: ISC register is corporation-maintained, not a shareholder list.

evidence.basis: CBCA s.21.303(1) — public disclosure of ISC information
evidence.quote: The Director shall make available to the public the following information sent to the Director under section 21.21 for each individual with significant control
evidence.note: Confirms federal ISC data is now partly public — but it is structured data on beneficial owners, not a document and not a Gesellschafterliste.

caveats: The federal ISC register is now partly public — CBCA s.21.303 (in force since 22 Jan 2024) makes certain ISC particulars publicly searchable. This does NOT change the status: ISC data is a beneficial-ownership query on 25%+ individuals, not a document, and not equivalent to a full shareholder list.
The securities/shareholder register (s.50) is company-held and is not obtainable from any registry.

legalFormLocal: Corporation incorporated under the Business Corporations Act (Ontario), R.S.O. 1990, c. B.16
summary: As with federal corporations, Ontario shareholders are not filed with the registry. Under OBCA s.140 the corporation maintains its own securities/ownership register listing security holders at its registered office; it is never delivered to the Ontario Business Registry. The OBCA transparency register of individuals with significant control (s.140.2, in force since 1 Jan 2023) is likewise corporation-kept and — unlike the federal ISC register — is NOT public: it is accessible only to specified investigative, tax and regulatory authorities. No registry document evidences the shareholders.

evidence.basis: OBCA s.141(1) — securities register maintained by the corporation at its registered office
evidence.quote: A corporation shall prepare and maintain at its registered office, or at any other place in Ontario designated by the directors, a securities register in which it records the securities issued by it in registered form, showing with respect to each class or series of securities, (a) the names, alphabetically arranged of persons who, (i) are or have been within six years registered as shareholders of the corporation
evidence.note: The shareholder/securities register is a corporation-held record; the OBCA contains no requirement to file it with the Ontario Business Registry. Retrieved from the official e-Laws consolidation via the ontario.ca e-Laws API (doc-search endpoint for statute 90b16).

evidence.basis: OBCA s.145(1) — examination of company-held records limited to shareholders/creditors (public only for offering corporations)
evidence.quote: Registered holders of shares, beneficial owners of shares and creditors of a corporation, their agents and legal representatives may examine the records referred to in subsection 140 (1), other than a register of individuals with significant control described in clause 140 (1) (f), during the usual business hours of the corporation, and may take extracts from those records, free of charge, and, if the corporation is an offering corporation, any other person may do so upon payment of a reasonable fee.
evidence.note: Even the corporation-held records are not open to the general public for a non-offering (private) corporation, and the ISC register is expressly excluded from examination.

evidence.basis: OBCA s.140.2(1) — register of individuals with significant control kept by the corporation
evidence.quote: A corporation shall prepare and maintain, at its registered office or at another place in Ontario designated by the directors, a register of individuals with significant control over the corporation showing, (a) the name, date of birth and latest known address of each individual with significant control
evidence.note: The Ontario transparency register is corporation-kept, not filed with the registry.

evidence.basis: OBCA s.140.3(3) — disclosure of the ISC register restricted to law enforcement (with tax and regulatory officials in s.140.3(4) ff.); no public access
evidence.quote: A member of a police service as defined in subsection 2 (1) of the Community Safety and Policing Act, 2019, a First Nation Officer appointed under section 101 of that Act or a member of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police may request disclosure of the register for the purpose of, (a) conducting an investigation into an offence under a law of Ontario or Canada
evidence.note: s.140.3 enumerates who may request disclosure (police, tax officials, specified regulators); there is no public-access provision — unlike the federal CBCA ISC regime.

caveats: The securities/shareholder register and the ISC register are company-held records; nothing shareholder-related is filed with or obtainable from the Ontario Business Registry.

Unlike the federal ISC register, Ontario did NOT make its transparency register public — disclosure is restricted to police, tax officials and specified regulators (s.140.3) — this reinforces the not_provable status.
Section numbering precision: the securities register is s.141 (s.140(1) lists the corporate records, including the register of directors); the summary's 's.140' refers to this records regime.

legalFormLocal: Corporation incorporated under the Canada Business Corporations Act (federal corporation)
documentLocal: Articles of Incorporation (with Certificate of Incorporation)
summary: A federal corporation's constituting document is its Articles of Incorporation, which the incorporator must send to the Director; the Director then issues a Certificate of Incorporation (CBCA ss.7-8). The articles are on the public record at Corporations Canada and a copy/certified copy can be obtained. They are the Gesellschaftsvertrag equivalent. By-laws, by contrast, are internal and are NOT filed with Corporations Canada.

evidence.basis: CBCA s.7 — delivery of articles
evidence.quote: An incorporator shall send to the Director articles of incorporation and the documents required by sections 19 and 106.
evidence.note: Articles are filed with the Director.

evidence.basis: CBCA s.8(1) — certificate of incorporation
evidence.quote: on receipt of articles of incorporation, the Director shall issue a certificate of incorporation in accordance with section 262.

caveats: Scope note: 'constitution' here = the filed Articles of Incorporation (public). Corporate by-laws are internal and NOT part of the public registry record for CBCA corporations.
Articles of amendment (s.27) would be the 'additional document' route for later changes; the base articles alone establish the constitution.

legalFormLocal: Corporation incorporated under the Business Corporations Act (Ontario), R.S.O. 1990, c. B.16
documentLocal: Articles of Incorporation (with Certificate of Incorporation), Ontario Business Registry
summary: An OBCA corporation's constituting document is its Articles of Incorporation, sent to the Director under OBCA s.5, upon which a Certificate of Incorporation is endorsed/issued (s.6). The articles are on the public record and copies/certified copies can be obtained through the Ontario Business Registry. They are the Gesellschaftsvertrag equivalent; by-laws are internal and not filed.

evidence.basis: OBCA s.6 — articles of incorporation sent to the Director; certificate of incorporation endorsed
evidence.quote: An incorporator shall send to the Director articles of incorporation and any other required documents and information and, upon receipt of the articles, documents and information, the Director shall endorse the articles, in accordance with section 273, with a certificate which shall constitute the certificate of incorporation.
evidence.note: Retrieved from the official e-Laws consolidation via the ontario.ca e-Laws API (doc-search endpoint for statute 90b16).

evidence.basis: OBCA s.5(1) — contents of articles
evidence.quote: Articles of incorporation must be in the form approved by the Director and must set out the information required by this Act, the regulations or the Director.

evidence.basis: OBCA s.7 — certificate of incorporation as conclusive proof
evidence.quote: A certificate of incorporation is conclusive proof that the corporation has been incorporated under this Act on the date set out in the certificate

evidence.basis: Ontario Business Registry (ontario.ca) — filed information is public record; copies of filed documents orderable
evidence.quote: Most of the information filed on the OBR, such as registered office or other address information, will be placed on the public record and is publicly available for searches under the business statutes.
evidence.note: The same page lists the orderable search products: 'profile report – $8', 'document copies – $3', 'certificate of status – $26' — 'document copies' covers copies of the filed articles.

caveats: Scope note: 'constitution' = filed Articles of Incorporation (public); corporate by-laws are internal and not on the public registry record.
Later changes would be evidenced by Articles of Amendment (additional document); the base articles establish the constitution.

legalFormLocal: Corporation incorporated under the Canada Business Corporations Act (federal corporation)
documentLabel: Federal corporate record / Certificate of Compliance
documentLocal: Corporation Information (Corporations Canada online record) / Certificate of Compliance
summary: Corporations Canada (the Director under the CBCA) maintains the federal register and publishes each corporation's current registered particulars — legal name, corporation number, registered office, directors, status and annual-return history — which anyone can view and download. A formal register extract can be ordered as a Certificate of Compliance (or Certificate of Existence). This public corporate record is the register-extract equivalent; it does not, however, show shareholders.

evidence.basis: CBCA s.263.1(1) — Director's certificate (compliance / existence)
evidence.quote: The Director may provide any person with a certificate stating that a corporation (a) has sent to the Director a document or other information required to be sent under this Act; (b) has paid all required fees; or (c) exists as of a certain date.
evidence.note: Statutory basis for the Certificate of Compliance / Certificate of Existence issued by Corporations Canada to any person.

evidence.basis: CBCA s.266(1) — public examination of documents sent to the Director
evidence.quote: A person who has paid the required fee is entitled during usual business hours to examine and make copies of or take extracts from a document or other information
evidence.note: Continues: '... that is required to be sent to the Director under this Act' (with carve-outs for ISC information under s.21.21, s.21.303(3) applications and s.230(2) reports). Establishes public access to the federal corporate record.

evidence.basis: CBCA s.266(2) — certified copies and extracts from the Director
evidence.quote: The Director shall, on request, provide any person with a copy, extract, certified copy or certified extract of a document that may be examined under subsection (1).

caveats: INFERENCE: There is no single statutory document literally titled 'register extract' for federal corporations; the public 'Corporation Information' record plus an orderable Certificate of Compliance/Existence (s.263.1) and certified extracts (s.266(2)) together serve as the Registerauszug equivalent.
The federal corporate record shows directors/officers and registered office, NOT shareholders (ISC information is expressly carved out of public examination in s.266(1)).

legalFormLocal: Corporation incorporated under the Business Corporations Act (Ontario), R.S.O. 1990, c. B.16
documentLocal: Corporation Profile Report / Certified Corporation Profile Report (Ontario Business Registry)
summary: The Ontario Business Registry (administered by ServiceOntario) issues a Corporation Profile Report giving the current registered particulars of an OBCA corporation — legal name, Ontario Corporation Number, registered/head office, status, incorporation date and current directors/officers. A certified version is available. This is the register-extract equivalent; it does not list shareholders.

evidence.basis: Ontario Business Registry (ontario.ca) — public record and search
