evidence.note: The SARL statuts must state the allocation of the parts sociales among the associés, so the up-to-date statuts evidence the members and their holdings.

caveats: Proof is via the statuts à jour, not the Kbis; the Kbis does not list SARL associés.

summary: The registry does not evidence who the current shareholders of a SAS are. Actionnaires are recorded in the company's own private registre des mouvements de titres and comptes d'actionnaires, which are kept by the company, not filed at the RCS and not public. Share transfers do not amend the statuts, so the filed statuts do not track ownership either.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. R228-9 (registre des mouvements de titres — kept by the company)
evidence.quote: En cas de transfert de titres, l'inscription est faite (...) et le registre mentionne les nom, prénoms et domicile du cédant et du cessionnaire.
evidence.note: This register is maintained by the issuing company, not by the RCS; it is not filed and not public.

evidence.basis: Registre des bénéficiaires effectifs (RBE) — Code monétaire et financier, art. L561-46
evidence.quote: Depuis le 31 juillet 2024, l'accès aux informations du RBE est restreint (...) réservé aux personnes justifiant d'un intérêt légitime.
evidence.note: Even the RBE only records beneficial owners (>25% or effective control), not the full cap table, and public access has been closed since the CJEU ruling of 22 Nov 2022 — so it is not a substitute shareholder list.

caveats: Founding shareholders may appear in the initial constitutive statuts, but that is NOT proof of the CURRENT shareholders and must not be treated as such.
The RBE is filed but (a) access-restricted since 31 July 2024 and (b) covers beneficial owners, not the legal shareholder list.

documentLabel: Register extract identifying the partners (Kbis), plus statuts for the share allocation
summary: Unlike capital companies, an SNC's partners are provable from the registry. The associés (all traders, indefinitely and jointly liable) are recorded at the RCS and appear on the Kbis, and the répartition des parts is stated in the statuts. Parts are non-negotiable and cannot be transferred without unanimous consent, and any change of partner requires an amendment of the statuts filed at the RCS — so the on-file record tracks the current partners.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. L221-1 (Légifrance)
evidence.quote: Les associés en nom collectif ont tous la qualité de commerçant et répondent indéfiniment et solidairement des dettes sociales.
evidence.note: Because every SNC associé has merchant status and is indefinitely and jointly liable, each is individually identified in the RCS registration and named on the Kbis.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. R123-54, 1° (Légifrance) — déclaration aux fins d'immatriculation des personnes morales
evidence.quote: Les nom, nom d'usage, pseudonyme, prénoms et domicile personnel des associés tenus indéfiniment ou tenus indéfiniment et solidairement des dettes sociales
evidence.note: English gloss: the company's registration declaration to the RCS must state 'the surname, customary name, pseudonym, first names and personal domicile of the associés who are indefinitely, or indefinitely and jointly-and-severally, liable for the company's debts' (plus their date/place of birth and nationality). For an SNC all associés fall under this (art. L221-1), so the partners are RCS register data — directly supporting registry provability of the partner list.

caveats: The répartition des parts (share allocation among partners) is not an R123-54 register datum; it is evidenced by the statuts filed at the RCS — hence 'Kbis + statuts' as the document set.

The precise print layout of the Kbis for an SNC (that the associés block is reproduced on the extract) was not verbatim-confirmed (infogreffe returned HTTP 403); the Kbis is the certified reproduction of the RCS entries in which the associés are, per R123-54, recorded.

summary: The statuts are a filed act, publicly obtainable via Infogreffe / Data INPI, ordered separately from the Kbis.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. L210-2
evidence.quote: La forme, la durée qui ne peut excéder quatre-vingt-dix-neuf ans, la dénomination sociale, le siège social, l'objet social et le montant du capital social sont déterminés par les statuts de la société.

evidence.basis: INPI – Data INPI
evidence.quote: Les actes et statuts depuis 1993 (...) sont mis à disposition.

caveats: Ordered separately from the Kbis.

summary: The statuts are the constitution and are a filed act at the greffe/RNE, publicly obtainable via Infogreffe or Data INPI. They are a separately-ordered document (not part of the Kbis).

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. L210-2
evidence.quote: La forme, la durée qui ne peut excéder quatre-vingt-dix-neuf ans, la dénomination sociale, le siège social, l'objet social et le montant du capital social sont déterminés par les statuts de la société.

evidence.basis: INPI – Data INPI (public consultation of acts and statuts since 1993)
evidence.quote: Les actes et statuts depuis 1993 (...) sont mis à disposition.

caveats: Ordered separately from the Kbis (dépôt d'actes).

summary: The statuts are a filed act, publicly obtainable via Infogreffe / Data INPI, ordered separately from the Kbis.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. L210-2
evidence.quote: La forme, la durée qui ne peut excéder quatre-vingt-dix-neuf ans, la dénomination sociale, le siège social, l'objet social et le montant du capital social sont déterminés par les statuts de la société.

evidence.basis: INPI – Data INPI
evidence.quote: Les actes et statuts depuis 1993 (...) sont mis à disposition.

caveats: Ordered separately from the Kbis; a SAS's statuts do not record the actionnaires (only founders, and share transfers do not amend them).

summary: The statuts are a filed act, publicly obtainable via Infogreffe / Data INPI, ordered separately from the Kbis. For an SNC they also carry the répartition des parts among the named partners.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. L210-2
evidence.quote: La forme, la durée qui ne peut excéder quatre-vingt-dix-neuf ans, la dénomination sociale, le siège social, l'objet social et le montant du capital social sont déterminés par les statuts de la société.

evidence.basis: INPI – Data INPI
evidence.quote: Les actes et statuts depuis 1993 (...) sont mis à disposition.

caveats: Ordered separately from the Kbis.

summary: Standard up-to-date RCS extract from the greffe / Infogreffe. It shows the declared corporate officers (administrateurs / directeurs généraux or directoire/conseil de surveillance), not the actionnaires.

evidence.basis: Infogreffe – official document 'Extrait Kbis'
evidence.quote: L'extrait Kbis (...) constitue la véritable carte d'identité à jour d'une entreprise immatriculée au registre du commerce et des sociétés (RCS).

caveats: The Kbis does NOT identify the shareholders of an SA.

summary: The extrait Kbis is the standard, up-to-date register extract for any company registered at the RCS, delivered by the greffe / Infogreffe. It evidences the registered particulars (SIREN, form, denomination, seat, object, capital, duration, managers/dirigeants and insolvency judgments). It does not list ordinary shareholders.

evidence.basis: Infogreffe – official document 'Extrait Kbis'
evidence.quote: L'extrait Kbis (...) constitue la véritable carte d'identité à jour d'une entreprise immatriculée au registre du commerce et des sociétés (RCS).
evidence.note: Standard extract ordered by SIREN or company name; delivered paper or electronic.

evidence.basis: Service-Public – Comment obtenir un extrait K ou Kbis (F21000)
evidence.note: Confirms Kbis is the official extract of the RCS registration for a company (personne morale).

caveats: The Kbis for a SARL identifies the manager(s)/dirigeants, NOT the ordinary associés and their shareholdings — do not treat it as proof of the shareholder structure.
One secondary site claims the Kbis lists 'autres associés ou membres'; this is imprecise for capital companies and not relied on — UNVERIFIED against a primary source.

summary: Same as for any registered company: the Kbis is the standard up-to-date RCS extract delivered by the greffe / Infogreffe. It shows the président and other declared dirigeants, not the actionnaires.

evidence.basis: Infogreffe – official document 'Extrait Kbis'
evidence.quote: L'extrait Kbis (...) constitue la véritable carte d'identité à jour d'une entreprise immatriculée au registre du commerce et des sociétés (RCS).

caveats: The Kbis does NOT identify the shareholders of a SAS.

summary: Standard up-to-date RCS extract from the greffe / Infogreffe. For an SNC the extract also reveals the unlimited, joint-and-several liability of the partners via the legal form, and the associés en nom (who are traders/commerçants) are recorded in the RCS registration.

evidence.basis: Infogreffe – official document 'Extrait Kbis'
evidence.quote: L'extrait Kbis (...) constitue la véritable carte d'identité à jour d'une entreprise immatriculée au registre du commerce et des sociétés (RCS).
evidence.note: English gloss: the Kbis extract is the true, up-to-date identity card of a business registered in the RCS.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. L221-1 (SNC — associés indéfiniment et solidairement responsables)
evidence.quote: Les associés en nom collectif ont tous la qualité de commerçant et répondent indéfiniment et solidairement des dettes sociales.
evidence.note: English gloss: SNC partners all have merchant status and are indefinitely and jointly-and-severally liable for the company's debts.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. R123-54, 1° (Légifrance) — déclaration aux fins d'immatriculation des personnes morales
evidence.quote: Les nom, nom d'usage, pseudonyme, prénoms et domicile personnel des associés tenus indéfiniment ou tenus indéfiniment et solidairement des dettes sociales
evidence.note: English gloss: the RCS immatriculation declaration must identify every associé who is indefinitely (and jointly) liable for the company's debts. This closes the earlier inference gap: SNC associés (all of whom are indefinitely and jointly liable per L221-1) are mandatorily declared to and recorded in the RCS, whose up-to-date extract is the Kbis.

caveats: The Kbis print-out content itself was not verbatim-confirmed from infogreffe (HTTP 403); that the associés declared under R123-54 appear on the extract follows from the Kbis being the certified reproduction of the RCS entries.

summary: No share data at all. Members appear as officers; capital contributions are private.

evidence.basis: Companies House CS01 form
evidence.quote: Not required for companies without share capital.

summary: No Companies House document proves the current shareholder list. The CS01 reports only CHANGES since the last statement, is name-only, and carries no completeness marker.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006, s.853F(4)
evidence.quote: Subsection (3) does not apply if and to the extent that the information most recently delivered to the registrar under this section applies equally to the confirmation period concerned.
evidence.note: s.853F(5) appears to require a full list; subsection (4) disapplies it. There is no periodicity requirement at all.

evidence.basis: Companies House Form CS01, Part 4, v7.0 (02/2026)
evidence.quote: Only use this Part to tell us of a change to shareholder information since the company last delivered this information.
evidence.note: Also captures name only — '(Address not required)'.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006, old s.856A(4) (repealed)
evidence.quote: If either of the two immediately preceding returns has given the full particulars required by subsections (2) and (3), the return need only give such particulars as relate—(a) to persons who became, or ceased to be, members during the return period…
evidence.note: The three-year full-list refresh belonged to the AR01. The CS01 replaced the AR01 on 30 June 2016 and replaced this refresh with NOTHING. No compelled full-list checkpoint has existed since.

caveats: Share TRANSFERS are never separately notified to Companies House; they surface only inside CS01 deltas. SH01 does not name the allottee ('allottee' appears zero times in SH01 v8.0). So a reconstructed chain has no resync point and corrupts silently.
A filed CS01 is NOT self-describing: a full list and a delta look structurally identical, with no completeness marker. INFERENCE from the form, not verified against a corpus of filings.
Heuristic (INFERENCE, unsourced): if listed holdings sum to the total issued shares in the statement of capital, that is strong evidence of a full list. A confidence signal, not proof.
In fairness: filing software often auto-includes a full list when share transactions occurred (Inform Direct). That is software behaviour, not law — absent in no-change years, never compelled.
PSC register is NOT a substitute: Sch 1A triggers at >25% of shares/votes/board control in BANDS, recording beneficial control. A 24% shareholder is invisible.
No shareholder endpoint in the Companies House API; no certificate carries shareholders; no register extract exists at all — only filing history.

ECCTA WATCH ITEM: will require a ONE-OFF full shareholder list + full names. Legislated, NOT commenced, no target date (CH XML forum 31.03.2025; will not precede ID verification). This is the event that would make the CS01 chain reconstructible.
bestAvailable: The company's own statutory register of members, obtained as a director-certified copy — not a registry document. LEAD TO VERIFY, not a finding: s.113 mandates it, s.127 makes it prima facie evidence, and ECCTA's Nov-2025 abolition of local registers is believed to have retained the register of members.

summary: Identical to Ltd — a non-traded PLC is an s.853F company. PLC does not imply traded.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006, s.853F
evidence.note: Applies to non-traded companies regardless of Ltd/PLC status.

caveats: Do not assume PLC ⇒ traded.

summary: Only holders of 5% or more appear, with the same disapplication logic. Sub-5% ownership never appears.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006, s.853G
evidence.note: Traded companies: only >=5% holders (with address), subject to the same s.853F(4)-style disapplication.

caveats: A company on the Main Market or AIM (DTR5 issuer) is EXEMPT entirely — ownership lives in FCA DTR 5 notifications, not Companies House. See https://handbook.fca.org.uk/handbook/dtr5

documentLocal: LLP agreement / members' agreement (private, not filed)
summary: An LLP has NO articles of association. Its constitutional/governance document is the LLP agreement (members' agreement), a private contract NOT delivered to the registrar. Under LLP Act 2000 s.2 only the incorporation document and a compliance statement are filed to form the LLP; the members' agreement never reaches the public register. So the Gesellschaftsvertrag equivalent cannot be proved from the registry for an LLP — the key form difference from Ltd/PLC.

evidence.basis: Limited Liability Partnerships Act 2000 s.2 — Incorporation document etc.
evidence.quote: there shall be delivered to the registrar either the incorporation document or a copy authenticated in a manner approved by him, and there shall be delivered to the registrar a statement in a form approved by the registrar
evidence.note: Only the incorporation document and a compliance statement are filed; the LLP members' agreement is not delivered to the registrar. LLPs have no articles of association.

caveats: Best available filed document is the LLP incorporation document (form LL IN01), which records name, registered office and members but is NOT the LLP's constitution/agreement.
The governing LLP agreement is a private contract; where none exists the default LLP Regulations 2001 apply — but no document is obtainable from the registry either way.

documentLabel: Articles of Association (and Memorandum of Association)
documentLocal: Articles of Association / Memorandum of Association
summary: Every company must have articles of association (s.18(1)); unless the default model articles apply (s.20) it must register its articles (s.18(2)). Registered articles and the memorandum (s.8, delivered on formation) are held by Companies House and served as free downloadable document images via the filing history / Document API, inspectable/copyable under s.1085-1086. The articles are the UK constitution equivalent of the Gesellschaftsvertrag/Satzung, so this kind is provable by a base registry document.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006 s.18(1)-(2) — Articles of association
evidence.quote: A company must have articles of association prescribing regulations for the company. Unless it is a company to which model articles apply by virtue of section 20 ... it must register articles of association.

evidence.basis: Companies House official page (gov.uk) — free document images
evidence.quote: document images
evidence.note: Listed among information available free; articles and memorandum are filed images.

evidence.basis: Companies House Developer / Document API
evidence.note: Filed documents (incl. constitution) are downloadable programmatically for free.

caveats: INFERENCE / edge case: a company that adopts the unamended default model articles under s.20 may have NO bespoke articles document on the register — its constitution is the statutory model articles; only the memorandum is then distinctive. Amended/bespoke articles are always filed.

documentLabel: Articles of Association (and Memorandum of Association)
documentLocal: Articles of Association / Memorandum of Association
summary: A PLC, like any company, must have articles of association (s.18(1)) and files them with the memorandum (s.8). They are served as free downloadable document images and inspectable/copyable under s.1085-1086. PLCs register bespoke articles rather than relying on unamended model articles, so a filed articles document is reliably present. Provable by a base registry document.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006 s.18(1) — Articles of association
evidence.quote: A company must have articles of association prescribing regulations for the company.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006 s.8 — Memorandum of association
evidence.quote: A memorandum of association is a memorandum stating that the subscribers wish to form a company under this Act, and agree to become members of the company ... to take at least one share each.
evidence.note: Memorandum filed on formation for all company types including PLCs.

evidence.basis: Companies House official page (gov.uk) — free document images
evidence.quote: document images

caveats: INFERENCE that PLCs practically always file bespoke articles; the s.20 default-model-articles edge case is far less likely for a PLC than a small private Ltd but is not formally excluded.

documentLocal: filing history / company overview (no consolidated register extract)
summary: LLPs sit on the same Companies House register and, like companies, get no consolidated certified current register extract. Only the overview page, filing history, document images and certificates (incorporation / good standing) exist. No Registerauszug equivalent is obtainable.

evidence.basis: Companies House official page (gov.uk) — LLPs are searchable on the same register
evidence.quote: company information, for example registered address and date of incorporation ... document images

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006 s.1086 — Right to copy of material on the register
evidence.quote: Any person may require a copy of any material on the register that is available for public inspection.
evidence.note: Right is to copies of individual filed material, not a synthesized extract.

caveats: Best available partial alternative: certificate of good standing for the LLP; not a register extract (INFERENCE).

documentLocal: filing history / company overview (no consolidated register extract)
summary: The UK has NO single certified current-status register extract equivalent to a Handelsregisterauszug. Companies House exposes a company overview page and a filing history list (both free) plus downloadable document images, but none is a consolidated certified extract of current register contents. It issues certificates (certificate of incorporation under s.15; certificate of good standing) but a certificate of incorporation is only a formation snapshot and good standing only confirms continued existence — neither reproduces the current register (officers, capital, registered office). Best available is a certificate of good standing or incorporation, but neither is a true register extract; the overview is parsed data, not a document.

evidence.basis: Companies House official page (gov.uk) — what you can get about a company
evidence.quote: company information, for example registered address and date of incorporation ... document images ... previous company names
evidence.note: Free information is a web overview plus document images; no 'register extract' product exists.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006 s.15(4) — Issue of certificate of incorporation
evidence.quote: The certificate is conclusive evidence that the requirements of this Act as to registration have been complied with and that the company is duly registered under this Act.
evidence.note: The only 'certificate' that conclusively evidences status is a formation snapshot, not a current extract.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006 s.1085(1) — Inspection of the register
evidence.quote: Any person may inspect the register.
evidence.note: The statutory right is inspection/copying of individual material, not issuance of a consolidated extract.

caveats: Best available partial alternatives: certificate of good standing (orderable) and certificate of incorporation (s.15 formation snapshot); INFERENCE that neither qualifies as a register extract because they do not reproduce current officers/capital/address.
The 'company overview' is parsed structured data / a web page, not a downloadable certified document.

documentLocal: filing history / company overview (no consolidated register extract)
summary: Same as a private limited company: Companies House holds one unified register for all company types and issues no consolidated certified current register extract. Only a company-overview page, filing history, individual document images, and certificates (incorporation / good standing) are available — none is a Registerauszug equivalent. No form-specific difference from Ltd.

evidence.basis: Companies House official page (gov.uk) — what you can get about a company
evidence.quote: company information, for example registered address and date of incorporation ... document images
evidence.note: Applies to all company types on the single register; no register-extract product.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006 s.1085(1) — Inspection of the register
evidence.quote: Any person may inspect the register.

caveats: Best available partial alternatives identical to Ltd: certificate of good standing / certificate of incorporation; neither is a register extract (INFERENCE).

summary: There is NO obtainable current-shareholder document from the Guernsey Registry for a company limited by shares. Under the Companies (Guernsey) Law 2008 the register of members is kept by the COMPANY at its registered office, not filed with the Registry. The annual validation filed with the Registry does NOT disclose shareholders (it covers directors, resident agent, business category, audit-exemption status and issued share capital — but not who the members are). Consequently shareholder identities cannot be retrieved from a Registry search. Beneficial-ownership data sits on a SEPARATE, non-public register. This applies to both private and public/listed companies limited by shares.

evidence.basis: Appleby, Guide to Companies in Guernsey — register of members
evidence.quote: Every company must keep registers containing specified details of its members, its directors, its resident agent, and, if appropriate, its secretary, which must be kept at the company's registered office.
evidence.note: Register of members is a company-held register at the registered office, not a Registry filing.

evidence.basis: Mourant, A guide to Guernsey's Companies Law — annual validation
evidence.quote: Guernsey companies are required to file an annual validation with the Registry between 1 June and 31 July each year containing basic company information
evidence.note: 'Basic company information' does not include a shareholder list; the annual validation does not disclose members.

evidence.basis: Appleby / Guernsey Registry — beneficial ownership register
evidence.quote: The content of the Guernsey Register of Beneficial Ownership of Legal Persons is not publicly available.
evidence.note: The separate beneficial-ownership register is non-public; obliged entities supervised in the Bailiwick can access it, others cannot.

caveats: CONFIRMED: shareholder data is NOT public because it is NOT filed at all — the annual validation does not require disclosure of shareholders. The current-shareholders document (register of members) is held PRIVATELY by the company.
The only route to a current shareholder list is the company's own register of members obtained directly from the company/registered office — not a Registry product.
Beneficial ownership register is separate and non-public — not a substitute.

INFERENCE: no distinction found between private and public companies as to Registry-held membership data; a listed company's shareholding may be visible via listing/market disclosure rules, not the Guernsey Registry.
UNVERIFIED: raw statutory section numbers not retrieved verbatim; facts corroborated by multiple professional guides and the Registry.

summary: A company limited by shares is constituted by its Memorandum and Articles of Incorporation, filed with the Registrar on incorporation and publicly available from the Guernsey Registry (approx. GBP 2). The memorandum shows the company name, type and objects; the articles govern internal management. This is the constitution/Gesellschaftsvertrag equivalent and is obtainable as a document.

evidence.basis: Guernsey Registry / Companies (Guernsey) Law 2008 — filing of memorandum and articles
evidence.note: The memorandum and articles of incorporation must be filed with the Registrar together with the incorporation fee and an application for incorporation; the Memorandum and Articles of Incorporation are publicly obtainable from the Registry (approx. GBP 2).

evidence.basis: Appleby, Guide to Companies in Guernsey — public information at the Registry
evidence.quote: The following information is available to the public from the Registry
evidence.note: Publicly available items include memoranda and articles and later special resolutions amending them.

caveats: The filed memorandum/articles are the constitution; they are NOT a current shareholder list and must not be treated as a Gesellschafterliste substitute.
INFERENCE: memorandum/articles description (fields, fee) from the Registry's product/legislation pages and professional guides; availability corroborated across sources.

summary: The Guernsey Registry issues a certified 'Statement of the Register' — a full extract of the publicly held electronic register (company name, registration number, type, status, registered office, incorporation date, and current/previous directors with service addresses and dates). This is the functional equivalent of a current register extract and is obtainable for a small fee (approx. GBP 10). It does NOT contain shareholders/members — Guernsey's register does not hold ownership data.

evidence.basis: Official Guernsey Registry — description of the Statement of the Register product
evidence.note: Guernsey Registry describes the Statement of the Register as a full certified extract of all publicly available information on the electronic register, including current and previous directors with service addresses and dates; a sample is published by the Registry. Basic company data is searchable free of charge.

evidence.basis: Appleby, Guide to Companies in Guernsey (Companies (Guernsey) Law 2008)
evidence.quote: The following information is available to the public from the Registry
evidence.note: The public information list covers memoranda, articles, resolutions and director changes, but excludes membership/shareholder details.

caveats: The Statement of the Register proves corporate existence, status and directors but NOT ownership; not a substitute for a shareholder document.
INFERENCE: exact field list taken from the Registry's own product description and published sample; the raw statutory list of publicly filed items was not retrieved verbatim.

summary: An AE's shareholders are NOT held at GEMI. For registered shares the company itself keeps the book of shareholders (βιβλίο μετόχων) under N.4548/2018 art. 40; for listed companies holdings are dematerialised in the central securities depository (ΣΑΤ/ΕΛΚΑΤ), not GEMI. GEMI's public file shows only the founders (ιδρυτές) named in the original καταστατικό at incorporation, not the current shareholder body. Therefore a current shareholder list is not provable from the registry.

evidence.quote: Ονομαστικές μετοχές – Μετοχικοί τίτλοι
evidence.note: Article 40 title (verbatim); registered shares are recorded in the company-kept βιβλίο μετόχων, not in GEMI. Full text not fetched.

caveats: Only the founders and their initial subscription in the original καταστατικό are public (historical, incorporation-only) — this is not a current shareholder list.

Bearer shares were abolished; all AE shares are now registered (ονομαστικές), but the register of them is private (company book) or in the CSD for listed AEs. That the βιβλίο μετόχων is company-kept and not GEMI-filed is well established but the operative sentence of art. 40 was not fetched verbatim (paywalled) — UNVERIFIED at quote level.

documentLabel: Articles of association plus registered share-transfer announcements
documentLocal: Καταστατικό (κωδικοποιημένο) + ανακοινώσεις μεταβίβασης εταιρικών μεριδίων στο ΓΕΜΗ
summary: For an IKE the partners (εταίροι) and their shares (εταιρικά μερίδια) are provable from GEMI: the founding partners and their contributions are stated in the καταστατικό (N.4072/2012 art. 50), and every inter-vivos transfer of a share must be registered/published in GEMI (art. 84). Current holdings are therefore reconstructed from the codified articles plus the registered transfer announcements — an additional document set beyond the plain register extract. The company additionally keeps an internal βιβλίο εταίρων (art. 66), but that book is not the GEMI-sourced proof.

evidence.quote: Περιεχόμενο του καταστατικού
evidence.note: Article 50 title (verbatim); the καταστατικό states founding partners and their μερίδια. Full text not fetched.

evidence.basis: N.4072/2012 (IKE), art. 84 para. 1, as in force (text via Lawspot law database reproducing the codified statute)
evidence.quote: Η μεταβίβαση ή επιβάρυνση των εταιρικών μεριδίων εν ζωή γίνεται εγγράφως και επάγεται αποτελέσματα ως προς την εταιρεία και τους εταίρους από τη γνωστοποίηση σε αυτή της μεταβίβασης.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'The inter-vivos transfer or encumbrance of company shares is made in writing and takes effect vis-à-vis the company and the partners from the notification of the transfer to the company.' Verbatim operative sentence now obtained, replacing the earlier title-only citation.

evidence.basis: N.4072/2012 (IKE), art. 84, third-party effect sentence, as in force (text via Lawspot law database reproducing the codified statute)
evidence.quote: Ως προς τους τρίτους η μεταβίβαση θεωρείται ότι έγινε από την καταχώριση στο βιβλίο των εταίρων.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'Vis-à-vis third parties the transfer is deemed to have taken place from its entry in the book of partners.' Under the current wording of art. 84, third-party effect attaches to the INTERNAL βιβλίο εταίρων, not to a GEMI registration — an important limit on registry provability of current holdings.

evidence.quote: Τήρηση βιβλίων
evidence.note: Article 66 title (verbatim); the company keeps a register of partners (βιβλίο εταίρων) internally — not the registry proof.

caveats: There is no single German-style standalone 'Gesellschafterliste' document at GEMI for an IKE; the current member list is assembled from the codified καταστατικό and registered transfer announcements. Classified as additional (not base) document.

SUBSTANTIVE (verbatim-confirmed 2026-07-22): art. 84 as in force makes an inter-vivos share transfer effective vis-à-vis the company upon notification and vis-à-vis third parties upon entry in the internal βιβλίο εταίρων — the article itself does NOT hinge effectiveness on GEMI registration. The practice of announcing transfers/partner changes at ΓΕΜΗ rests on the GEMI-law publicity regime and chamber procedure, which was not verbatim-confirmed this session; registry-derived holdings can therefore lag or under-record the legally effective state in the βιβλίο εταίρων.
The internal βιβλίο εταίρων (art. 66) is not obtainable from the registry.
Art. 84 verbatim text was fetched from lawspot.gr, a Greek legal database reproducing the codified statute; the official et.gr/ΦΕΚ text was not fetched (official portals unreachable within budget).

summary: The AE's constitution (καταστατικό) is filed with and published by GEMI (N.4548/2018 art. 12 lists it among acts subject to publicity) and is retrievable as a distinct codified document separate from the register-extract certificate. Provable from the registry via this additional document.

evidence.quote: Πράξεις και στοιχεία που υποβάλλονται σε δημοσιότητα στο Γ.Ε.ΜΗ.
evidence.note: Article 12 title (verbatim); the καταστατικό and its amendments are subject to GEMI publicity. Full text not fetched.

evidence.quote: Εμπορική δημοσιότητα στο ΓΕ.ΜΗ.
evidence.note: GEMI commercial publicity; downloadable copy of the articles.

caveats: Treated as an 'additional document' (a distinct downloadable filing), not the register-extract certificate itself.
Whether a single fully-codified καταστατικό is on file vs. only the original plus amendment announcements depends on the company's GEMI maintenance. Verbatim article text not fetched (paywalled).

summary: The IKE's constitution (καταστατικό) is filed with and published by GEMI and is retrievable as a distinct document (the codified/current articles), separate from the register-extract certificate. Its required content is set by N.4072/2012 art. 50. Provable from the registry via this additional document.

evidence.quote: Περιεχόμενο του καταστατικού
evidence.note: Article 50 title (verbatim). Full text not fetched.

evidence.quote: Εμπορική δημοσιότητα στο ΓΕ.ΜΗ.
evidence.note: GEMI commercial publicity covers the καταστατικό and its amendments; downloadable copy.

caveats: Treated as an 'additional document' because it is a distinct downloadable filing, not the register-extract certificate itself.
Availability of a fully codified καταστατικό vs. only the successive amendment filings varies by how the company has maintained its GEMI file. Verbatim article text not fetched (paywalled).

documentLocal: Γενικό Πιστοποιητικό ΓΕΜΗ (also Πιστοποιητικό Ισχύουσας Εκπροσώπησης)
summary: GEMI is the constitutive public register for the AE (L.4919/2022; L.4548/2018 art. 12 lists the acts/data subject to GEMI publicity). Its general certificate is the register extract evidencing identity, seat, capital, board/representation and registered acts — the base register document for an AE.

evidence.quote: Πράξεις και στοιχεία που υποβάλλονται σε δημοσιότητα στο Γ.Ε.ΜΗ.
evidence.note: Article 12 title (verbatim); enumerates what an AE files with GEMI. Full text not fetched.

evidence.quote: Εμπορική δημοσιότητα στο ΓΕ.ΜΗ.
evidence.note: GEMI publicity/certificate regime.

caveats: Certificate label precision (Γενικό vs Ισχύουσας Εκπροσώπησης vs Αναλυτικό) is INFERENCE from GEMI practice; the certificate-catalogue article of L.4919/2022 was not fetched verbatim.
Verbatim statutory text was paywalled; only article titles retrieved.

documentLocal: Γενικό Πιστοποιητικό ΓΕΜΗ (also Πιστοποιητικό Ισχύουσας Εκπροσώπησης for representation)
summary: The General Commercial Registry (ΓΕΜΗ/GEMI) is the constitutive public register for the IKE under L.4919/2022. It issues a general certificate that functions as the register extract, evidencing the company's identity, seat, capital, managers/representation and registered acts. This is the base register document for an IKE.

evidence.basis: N.4919/2022 (GEMI law)
evidence.quote: Εμπορική δημοσιότητα στο ΓΕ.ΜΗ.
evidence.note: Article 17 title (verbatim, retrieved from TOC); full text paywalled. Art. 46 'Πρόσβαση στα στοιχεία του Γ.Ε.ΜΗ.' governs access to the register data.

caveats: Exact certificate label varies: 'Γενικό Πιστοποιητικό' (history of acts) vs 'Πιστοποιητικό Ισχύουσας Εκπροσώπησης' (current representation) vs 'Αναλυτικό Πιστοποιητικό'. INFERENCE from GEMI practice; the precise catalogue article of L.4919/2022 (certificates/copies) was not fetched verbatim.
Verbatim statutory text was behind paywalls; only article titles were retrieved.

documentLocal: Annual Return (Form NAR1), Schedule 1 'Particulars of Member(s) of a Non-listed Company'
summary: The public register does NOT keep a live register of members (kept by the company itself). The document evidencing members at the registry is the Annual Return (Form NAR1): Schedule 1 states members and shareholdings as at the return's made-up date. A publicly searchable image record; an additional filing, ordered separately.

evidence.basis: Companies Registry FAQ 'Annual Returns' (Local Limited Companies), citing Companies Ordinance (Cap. 622) s.662
evidence.quote: the particulars of members and their shareholdings ... Schedule 1 'Particulars of Member(s) of a Non-listed Company'
evidence.note: Annual return due within 42 days after the return date (s.662); a private company delivers one for every financial year.

evidence.basis: Companies Registry FAQ 'Documents relating to Share Capital / Shares'
evidence.quote: the transfer of shares should be reported in the annual return first made by the company after such a transfer took place
evidence.note: Share transfers are not filed in real time; the register of members is company-kept; the registry sees membership periodically.

caveats: STALENESS: NAR1 reflects members only as at the annual made-up date; transfers appear only in the next return, so up to ~a year (or more if overdue) stale. Not a live shareholder list.
A newly incorporated company has filed no NAR1 yet (first due within 42 days after the first anniversary).
NOMINEE RISK: NAR1 shows the registered member, which may be a nominee; beneficial/controller data lives in the company-kept Significant Controllers Register (verify current public-access rules).

summary: A public company with share capital also delivers an annual return (Form NAR1) under s.662 whose member particulars evidence shareholders as at the return date — a publicly searchable additional filing. DISTINCTION: NAR1 Schedule 1 is 'Particulars of Member(s) of a Non-listed Company'. For an UNLISTED public company this gives a full member list; for a LISTED public company the annual return does not carry a usable full current member list (members trade on the exchange, maintained by a share registrar), so a registry-based current shareholder list is effectively not obtainable.

evidence.basis: Companies Registry, Form NAR1 specimen (public company), Notes for Completion, note 1 + title ('ANNUAL RETURN — For the purposes of section 662 of Companies Ordinance (Cap. 622)')
evidence.quote: Every private company, public company or company limited by guarantee must make an annual return which should be delivered to the Registrar of Companies (the Registrar) for registration.
evidence.note: Official CR documentation confirms the s.662 annual-return obligation for public companies; NAR1 Section 14 ('Particulars of Member(s) of a Company Having a Share Capital') carries the member particulars, with 'Company having a share capital must complete this section'.

evidence.basis: Form NAR1 Notes for Completion, note 33(b) — Schedule 1 'Particulars of Member(s) of a Non-listed Company (Section 14)'
evidence.quote: Please provide the full name and address of each member and the number of shares held.
evidence.note: For a NON-LISTED company (including an unlisted public company) Schedule 1 requires every member's full name, address and shares held — a full shareholder list as at the return date.

evidence.basis: Form NAR1 Notes for Completion, note 34(a) — Schedule 2 (listed company)
evidence.quote: The particulars of members of a listed company to be provided in Schedule 2 are limited to those relating to members who held not less than 5% of the issued shares in any class of the company's shares as at the date of the return.
evidence.note: For a LISTED public company the annual return does NOT carry a full member list — only holders of >=5% per class; confirms the listed/unlisted distinction in the summary.

evidence.basis: Form NAR1, Section 14 (member particulars delivered on CD-ROM/DVD-ROM)
evidence.quote: Particulars of members recorded in the CD-ROM or DVD-ROM will be available for public search electronically in PDF format.
evidence.note: Member particulars delivered with the annual return are publicly searchable at the Companies Registry — supports registry-provability.

evidence.basis: Companies Registry FAQ, 'Documents on share capital and members'
evidence.quote: the transfer of shares should be reported in the annual return first made by the company after such a transfer took place
evidence.note: Members/shareholder particulars reach the Companies Registry via the annual return (Form NAR1); the register of members itself is kept by the company.

caveats: Shareholder changes are captured only through the periodic annual return (NAR1), so the Registry copy can lag the company-held register of members.

For a LISTED public company Schedule 2 discloses only members holding >=5% of a class — a full current member list is not obtainable from the registry; the full-list route (Schedule 1) applies to non-listed companies only.

The s.662/Schedule 6 statute text itself was not fetched (elegislation.gov.hk is JS-rendered); the verbatim evidence is the Companies Registry's official NAR1 specimen form and Notes for Completion, which state the s.662 basis on their face.

summary: Every HK company must have Articles of Association (its constitution). A copy is delivered to the Registrar at incorporation and registered; the image record is publicly searchable from the date of incorporation and a certified copy can be ordered. This registered Articles document is the GESELLSCHAFTSVERTRAG equivalent — a separate registered document.

evidence.basis: Companies Registry FAQ 'Incorporation', citing Companies Ordinance (Cap. 622) ss.81, 83(1), 85(1)
evidence.quote: you have to deliver the application documents ... including a copy of the company's articles of association
evidence.note: Articles state limited liability (s.83(1)) and, for share-capital companies, capital and initial shareholdings (s.85(1)). Image records searchable from incorporation.

evidence.basis: Companies Registry 'How to obtain company information' (certified copies of image records)
evidence.quote: place orders for certified copies of image records of registered documents

caveats: Only registered amendments are on the public file; an unfiled amended version would leave the registry copy outdated (UNVERIFIED per company).
Section numbers (81/83/85) relayed via Registry FAQ; exact Cap. 622 wording not pulled from e-Legislation (JS-only) — filing/registration fact high-confidence, section text INFERENCE-grade.

summary: A public company limited by shares must have Articles of Association delivered to and registered by the Registrar at incorporation; the image record is publicly searchable and a certified copy can be ordered. Model Articles for public companies are a distinct schedule. This registered Articles document is the GESELLSCHAFTSVERTRAG equivalent, a separate registered document.

evidence.basis: Companies Registry FAQ 'Incorporation', citing Cap. 622 and the Companies (Model Articles) Notice
evidence.quote: you have to deliver the application documents ... including a copy of the company's articles of association
evidence.note: Model Articles have separate schedules for private vs public companies limited by shares. Image records searchable from incorporation.

evidence.basis: Companies Registry 'How to obtain company information' (certified copies)
evidence.quote: place orders for certified copies of image records of registered documents

caveats: Only filed/registered articles are on the public record; unfiled amendments would make the registry copy stale (UNVERIFIED per company).
Section numbers relayed via Registry FAQ; exact Cap. 622 text not pulled from e-Legislation (JS-only) — filing fact high-confidence, section wording INFERENCE-grade.

documentLocal: Company Particulars Search report (e-Search / current data of registered companies)
summary: Hong Kong has no consolidated 'register extract' like a Handelsregisterauszug. The Companies Registry e-Search produces a search report on current registered particulars (name, number, incorporation date, company type, registered office, directors, secretary, status, share capital) — orderable and certifiable, the closest REGISTERAUSZUG equivalent. It does NOT list members/shareholders (those come only from the annual return).

evidence.basis: Companies Registry official page 'How to obtain company information'
evidence.quote: searches on the current data of registered companies and image records of documents registered and kept in the Companies Register
evidence.note: Registry lets searchers 'place orders for certified copies of image records of registered documents and search reports'.

caveats: INFERENCE: 'REGISTERAUSZUG' mapped to the e-Search current-particulars report; HK issues no single statutory register extract. Product naming UNVERIFIED verbatim.
The current-particulars report does NOT show shareholders/members.

The REGISTERAUSZUG equivalent; does not list members.

evidence.basis: Companies Registry official page 'How to obtain company information'
evidence.quote: searches on the current data of registered companies and image records of documents registered and kept in the Companies Register

caveats: INFERENCE: mapped to the e-Search current-particulars report; HK issues no single statutory register extract. Naming UNVERIFIED verbatim.
