summary: Shares of a Croatian d.d. are dematerialised securities recorded in the central depository SKDD (Središnje klirinško depozitarno društvo); the register of shareholders (dionička knjiga) is derived from SKDD records, not from the court register. The Sudski registar records share capital and share classes but NOT the identity of shareholders, and the izvadak has no members section for a d.d. Therefore no document proving the current shareholders is obtainable from the court register.

evidence.basis: ZTD – dionice as nematerijalizirani vrijednosni papiri; SKDD depository
evidence.note: Verbatim ZTD text not fetched (page truncation); the dematerialised-shares/SKDD regime and the absence of a shareholder field on the d.d. extract are the basis.

evidence.basis: Zakon o sudskom registru, čl. 4. st. 2 (what the register/zbirka contains)
evidence.quote: Svatko, bez dokazivanja pravnog interesa, ima pravo uvida u podatke upisane u glavnoj knjizi, u isprave na temelju kojih je obavljen upis i u druge isprave i podatke pohranjene u zbirci isprava...
evidence.note: Public access covers register data and the document collection — neither of which holds a d.d. shareholder list.

caveats: Shareholder identity for a d.d. sits with SKDD (and, for beneficial owners, the Registar stvarnih vlasnika / UBO register) — separate systems, not the court register.
A single-shareholder d.d. must notify the sole-shareholder fact for the register/zbirka, but that is a one-shareholder disclosure, not a general shareholder list.

documentLabel: Court register extract – members section (Članovi društva)
documentLocal: Izvadak iz sudskog registra – Članovi društva
summary: Croatian company law (Austrian-derived) records the members of a d.o.o. as register data, so the izvadak shows a 'Članovi društva' section with each member and the nominal amount of their business share (temeljni ulog / poslovni udjel). The dedicated membership record is the knjiga poslovnih udjela, but under ZTD (čl. 410-411) that book is an INTERNAL company register kept by the uprava — inspected on the company's premises, not filed with the court register — so it is not itself obtainable from the registry. Because membership is legally determined by entry in that internal book, the register's member data can lag actual ownership.

evidence.basis: Zakon o sudskom registru, članak 4. — javnost registra (consolidated text, zakon.hr)
evidence.quote: Registar je javan.
evidence.note: The register is public. Čl. 4 st. 2 continues verbatim: 'Svatko, bez dokazivanja pravnog interesa, ima pravo uvida u podatke upisane u glavnoj knjizi, u isprave na temelju kojih je obavljen upis i u druge isprave i podatke pohranjene u zbirci isprava' — anyone, without proving legal interest, may inspect the registered data and underlying documents.

evidence.basis: Zakon o sudskom registru, članak 31.c st. 1 t. 3 — podaci koji se upisuju za d.o.o.
evidence.quote: ime i prezime, prebivalište, OIB članova društva, a za pravne osobe tvrtka ili naziv, sjedište, MBS, OIB i EUID, odnosno odgovarajući podaci za stranu pravnu osobu
evidence.note: Introductory sentence verbatim: 'Osim podataka iz članka 24. ovoga Zakona za društvo s ograničenom odgovornošću upisuju se slijedeći podaci:'. The MEMBERS of a d.o.o. (name, residence, OIB; for legal persons firm, seat, MBS/OIB/EUID) are thus statutory register data — they appear on the izvadak (base document). Točka 1 of the same provision registers 'ukupni iznos temeljnog kapitala' (total share capital).

evidence.basis: ZTD, čl. 410-411 (knjiga poslovnih udjela; imatelj poslovnog udjela)
evidence.quote: Član društva je samo ona osoba koja je upisana u knjizi poslovnih udjela društva kao imatelj poslovnog udjela
evidence.note: Quote as reproduced by a secondary Croatian legal source (codex-sortium.hr); the knjiga poslovnih udjela is described as an internal register inspected during working hours, not filed with the public court register.

caveats: The authoritative membership record is the internal knjiga poslovnih udjela (ZTD čl. 410), which is NOT filed with the court register and is not publicly downloadable — so registry member data may be secondary/lagging.

Nominal amounts of individual poslovni udjeli were not verbatim-confirmed among the čl. 31.c register data (confirmed verbatim: total temeljni kapital in t. 1 and member identities in t. 3); per-member share amounts on the izvadak rest on registry practice.
The ZTD čl. 410 quote is still sourced from a secondary reproduction (zakon.hr serves the full ZTD as one truncating page); it grounds a caveat, not the status claim itself.

summary: A d.d.'s constitution is the statut, adopted on formation and amended by the general assembly. It is submitted to the registry court on incorporation and on each amendment and stored in the zbirka isprava, which is publicly accessible without proof of legal interest.

evidence.basis: Zakon o sudskom registru, čl. 2. t. 3 (zbirka isprava)
evidence.quote: zbirka isprava je dio registra u koji se pohranjuju isprave dostavljene za upis podataka o subjektima upisa u glavnu knjigu, isprave dostavljene ili sastavljene u postupku upisa, odluke registarskog suda...
evidence.note: The statut is among the isprave submitted for registration and stored here.

evidence.basis: Zakon o sudskom registru, čl. 4. st. 2 (javnost zbirke isprava)
evidence.quote: Svatko, bez dokazivanja pravnog interesa, ima pravo uvida u podatke upisane u glavnoj knjizi, u isprave na temelju kojih je obavljen upis i u druge isprave i podatke pohranjene u zbirci isprava...
evidence.note: Public right to inspect the statut.

caveats: Older filings may not be fully digitised in the online zbirka isprava and may require a court request.

documentLocal: Društveni ugovor (odnosno izjava o osnivanju za jednočlano društvo)
summary: A d.o.o.'s constitution — the društveni ugovor (or, for a single-member company, the izjava o osnivanju) — is submitted to the registry court on incorporation and on each amendment. It is stored in the zbirka isprava (collection of documents) and is publicly accessible: anyone may inspect and obtain copies of documents in the collection without proving legal interest.

evidence.basis: Zakon o sudskom registru, čl. 2. t. 3 (zbirka isprava)
evidence.quote: zbirka isprava je dio registra u koji se pohranjuju isprave dostavljene za upis podataka o subjektima upisa u glavnu knjigu, isprave dostavljene ili sastavljene u postupku upisa, odluke registarskog suda...
evidence.note: Founding acts submitted for registration are stored here.

evidence.basis: Zakon o sudskom registru, čl. 4. st. 2 (javnost zbirke isprava)
evidence.quote: Svatko, bez dokazivanja pravnog interesa, ima pravo uvida u podatke upisane u glavnoj knjizi, u isprave na temelju kojih je obavljen upis i u druge isprave i podatke pohranjene u zbirci isprava...
evidence.note: Public right to inspect documents on which registration was based (i.e., the društveni ugovor).

caveats: Availability/format online (PDF via esudreg zbirka isprava) is the norm; not every legacy filing is digitised, so some older documents may require a court request.

summary: As for any subjekt upisa, a d.d. has a certified extract (izvadak) from the free public Sudski registar showing firm, seat, OIB/MBS, share capital, share classes, and the management/supervisory board. Anyone may obtain it without proving legal interest.

evidence.basis: Zakon o sudskom registru, čl. 2. t. 14 (definicija izvatka)
evidence.quote: ovjerena isprava koja se izdaje iz registra na propisan način, a sadrži upisane podatke o jednom subjektu upisa koji važe ili koji su važili...
evidence.note: Verbatim fragment; ellipsis is truncation.

evidence.basis: Sudski registar online
evidence.note: Free public search portal.

caveats: The extract does not list shareholders for a d.d. (see GESELLSCHAFTERLISTE).

summary: The Sudski registar (court register, sudreg.pravosudje.hr) is free and public online. The certified extract (izvadak) is the base register document for any subjekt upisa, including a d.o.o., and shows firm, seat, OIB/MBS, share capital, management and members. Anyone may obtain it without proving legal interest.

evidence.basis: Zakon o sudskom registru, čl. 2. t. 14 (definicija izvatka)
evidence.quote: ovjerena isprava koja se izdaje iz registra na propisan način, a sadrži upisane podatke o jednom subjektu upisa koji važe ili koji su važili...
evidence.note: Verbatim fragment as returned by the consolidated text; ellipsis is truncation.

evidence.basis: Zakon o sudskom registru, čl. 4. st. 2 (javnost registra)
evidence.quote: Svatko, bez dokazivanja pravnog interesa, ima pravo uvida u podatke upisane u glavnoj knjizi, u isprave na temelju kojih je obavljen upis i u druge isprave i podatke pohranjene u zbirci isprava...
evidence.note: Confirms free public access to register data.

evidence.basis: Sudski registar online
evidence.note: Free public search portal for all subjekti upisa.

caveats: Register is free/public online; extract is issued certified (ovjereni izvadak) or as an uncertified printout.

summary: For a Kft the members (tagok) are a registered, public datum of the cégjegyzék under Ctv. 27. § (3), so the cégkivonat itself evidences the member identities (name, residence/seat, birth data). A separate members' list, the tagjegyzék, is maintained internally by the managing director (ügyvezető) under Ptk. 3:182 § and is company-held, but it is not needed to prove membership because the register extract already records the members. Member identity is therefore provable from the base register extract.

evidence.basis: Ctv. 2006. évi V. törvény 27. § (3) a) — Kft member data recorded in the register
evidence.quote: a tagok nevét, természetes személy esetén lakóhelyét, születési idejét, anyja születési nevét, jogi személy esetén székhelyét
evidence.note: Members are registered cégjegyzék data, hence they appear on the cégkivonat.

evidence.basis: Ptk. 2013. évi V. törvény 3:182 § — tagjegyzék (members' list) kept by the ügyvezető
evidence.note: Company-held members' list; verbatim text could not be fetched and is left empty. It is a separate internal document, not the proof relied on here.

caveats: The cégkivonat records member identity; the internal tagjegyzék additionally records each member's capital contribution (törzsbetét) and business quota (üzletrész). If per-member contribution amounts (not just identities) are required, the base extract may be insufficient and the tagjegyzék (proved_by_additional_document) would be needed — this nuance is INFERENCE and UNVERIFIED as to current extract layout.
Subsection lettering extracted by automated read; re-verify.

summary: For a Zrt the shareholders (részvényesek) are recorded in the company's own share register, the részvénykönyv (Ptk. 3:245 ff.), which is maintained by the board/company and is NOT part of the public cégjegyzék. The cégjegyzék records a shareholder only in narrow cases — the sole shareholder of an egyszemélyes rt, and a shareholder whose voting rights exceed 50% (majority/qualified influence) — so an ordinary multi-shareholder Zrt's full ownership cannot be proved from the register. There is no publicly filed shareholder-list document for a private Rt.

evidence.basis: Ctv. 2006. évi V. törvény 27. § (4) b) bd) — sole shareholder of egyszemélyes rt recorded
evidence.quote: a részvényes nevét, adóazonosító számát, természetes személy esetén születési idejét, lakóhelyét
evidence.note: Only the SOLE shareholder is registered for a one-person Rt.

evidence.basis: Ctv. 2006. évi V. törvény 27. § (4) b) bc) — shareholder recorded only above 50% voting rights
evidence.quote: amennyiben a részvényes szavazati jogának mértéke az 50 százalékot meghaladja
evidence.note: Ordinary shareholders below 50% are not in the register.

evidence.basis: Ptk. 2013. évi V. törvény 3:245–3:247 § — részvénykönyv (share register) kept by the company
evidence.note: Company-held share register, not public; verbatim text could not be fetched and is left empty.

caveats: A sole shareholder (egyszemélyes Zrt) and any shareholder above 50% DO appear on the cégkivonat, so for those specific cases ownership is partially provable from the base extract — but the general/complete shareholder list is not.
The részvénykönyv is company-held and generally not disclosed publicly; access is limited under Ptk.
Subsection lettering extracted by automated read; re-verify.

summary: A Kft is founded by a társasági szerződés (Ptk. 3:159 §), which is filed with the company court as part of the company's document collection (cégiratok). Under Ctv. 10. § (1) the cégiratok are fully public, so the constitution is obtainable as a separate filed document (not carried on the cégkivonat itself). Hence proved by an additional filed document.

evidence.basis: Ctv. 2006. évi V. törvény 10. § (1) — cégiratok are fully public
evidence.quote: A cégjegyzék fennálló, illetve törölt adatai, valamint a cégiratok...teljeskörűen nyilvánosak.
evidence.note: The társasági szerződés is part of the public cégiratok.

evidence.basis: Ptk. 2013. évi V. törvény 3:159 § — Kft founded by társasági szerződés
evidence.note: Verbatim text could not be fetched; left empty.

caveats: Single-member Kft uses an alapító okirat (deed of foundation) instead of a társasági szerződés; both are the constitution and both are filed public cégiratok.
Subsection lettering extracted by automated read; re-verify.

summary: A részvénytársaság is founded by an alapszabály (Ptk. 3:250 §), filed with the company court as part of the public cégiratok. Under Ctv. 10. § (1) the cégiratok are fully public, so the Zrt's constitution is obtainable as a separate filed document, distinct from the cégkivonat. Hence proved by an additional filed document.

evidence.basis: Ctv. 2006. évi V. törvény 10. § (1) — cégiratok are fully public
evidence.quote: A cégjegyzék fennálló, illetve törölt adatai, valamint a cégiratok...teljeskörűen nyilvánosak.
evidence.note: The alapszabály is part of the public cégiratok.

evidence.basis: Ptk. 2013. évi V. törvény 3:250 § — Rt founded by alapszabály
evidence.note: Verbatim text could not be fetched; left empty.

caveats: Subsection lettering extracted by automated read; re-verify.

summary: The cégjegyzék (company register) is kept by the company courts (cégbíróság) under Ctv. (2006. évi V. törvény). The cégkivonat is the official extract of the currently effective registered data and is the base register-extract document. It is issued per company and is fully public.

evidence.basis: Ctv. 2006. évi V. törvény 10. § (1) — publicity of register data and documents
evidence.quote: A cégjegyzék fennálló, illetve törölt adatai, valamint a cégiratok...teljeskörűen nyilvánosak.
evidence.note: Establishes that register data (from which the cégkivonat is drawn) are public.

caveats: Subsection lettering was extracted by an automated read of net.jogtar.hu and should be re-verified against the consolidated text.

summary: Like all Hungarian companies, a Zrt is entered in the cégjegyzék kept by the company courts under Ctv. The cégkivonat is the official public extract of its currently registered data and is the base register-extract document.

evidence.basis: Ctv. 2006. évi V. törvény 10. § (1) — publicity of register data and documents
evidence.quote: A cégjegyzék fennálló, illetve törölt adatai, valamint a cégiratok...teljeskörűen nyilvánosak.

caveats: Subsection lettering extracted by automated read; re-verify.

documentLabel: Deed of Establishment (names founding shareholders) + subsequent notarial share-transfer deeds notified to Kemenkumham
documentLocal: Akta Pendirian (menyebut pendiri/pemegang saham); Akta Jual Beli Saham / Akta RUPS + penerimaan pemberitahuan; current statutory list: Daftar Pemegang Saham (Pasal 50, dipelihara Direksi)
summary: The founding shareholders are named as a document fact in the Akta Pendirian (Pasal 8 UUPT), and share transfers must be effected by notarial deed and notified to the Minister for recording in the Daftar Perseroan (Pasal 56). Thus a shareholder list is provable through documents, but as a point-in-time / founders proof rather than a guaranteed current cap table. The statutory CURRENT shareholder register is the company's Daftar Pemegang Saham kept by the Board of Directors (Pasal 50), which is company-held and open only to shareholders, not the general public; the AHU Profil Perseroan may display current shareholders but is parsed data with restricted access.

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007 (UUPT), Pasal 8 ayat (2) huruf c — the akta pendirian's 'keterangan lain' must name the initial shareholders and their shareholdings
evidence.quote: nama pemegang saham yang telah mengambil bagian saham, rincian jumlah saham, dan nilai nominal saham yang telah ditempatkan dan disetor.
evidence.note: English gloss: '(the deed of establishment shall state) the names of the shareholders who have taken up shares, details of the number of shares, and the nominal value of the shares issued and paid up.' Confirms the founding shareholders are a document fact in the Akta Pendirian. Quoted verbatim from the official PDF (peraturan.bpk.go.id download of UU 40/2007).

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 50 ayat (1) — the current statutory shareholder register (Daftar Pemegang Saham) is kept by the Board of Directors
evidence.quote: Direksi Perseroan wajib mengadakan dan menyimpan daftar pemegang saham
evidence.note: English gloss: 'The Board of Directors of the Company is obliged to make and keep a register of shareholders.' The current register is company-held, not registry-held.

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 50 ayat (4) — access to the Daftar Pemegang Saham is for shareholders, at the company's seat
evidence.quote: disediakan di tempat kedudukan Perseroan agar dapat dilihat oleh para pemegang saham.
evidence.note: English gloss: '(the register of shareholders and the special register) shall be made available at the Company's domicile so that they can be viewed by the shareholders.' Confirms the DPS is not open to the general public.

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 56 ayat (1) dan (3) — share transfers by deed, changes in the shareholder composition notified to the Minister for recording in the Daftar Perseroan
evidence.quote: Pemindahan hak atas saham dilakukan dengan akta pemindahan hak.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'Transfer of rights over shares shall be effected by a deed of transfer of rights.' Pasal 56(3) continues (also verbatim in the same source): 'memberitahukan perubahan susunan pemegang saham kepada Menteri untuk dicatat dalam daftar Perseroan paling lambat 30 (tiga puluh) hari' — 'notify the change in the composition of shareholders to the Minister for recording in the register of companies within at most 30 days'. Together these make post-incorporation shareholdings provable via notarial transfer deeds plus ministerial notification receipts.

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 29 ayat (2) huruf g dan ayat (5) — the Minister's Daftar Perseroan contains shareholder names/addresses and is open to the public
evidence.quote: nama lengkap dan alamat pemegang saham, anggota Direksi, dan anggota Dewan Komisaris Perseroan;
evidence.note: English gloss: '(the register of companies contains) the full names and addresses of the shareholders, members of the Board of Directors, and members of the Board of Commissioners of the Company.' Pasal 29(5), also verbatim in the same source: 'Daftar Perseroan sebagaimana dimaksud pada ayat (1) terbuka untuk umum.' — 'The register of companies referred to in paragraph (1) is open to the public.' This is the statutory basis of the AHU Profil Perseroan data view: shareholder names are registered DATA, not an issued document.

caveats: The akta proves FOUNDERS / a historical snapshot (Pasal 8(2)(c)); it does not by itself prove the CURRENT shareholder body.

The current statutory register (Daftar Pemegang Saham, Pasal 50(1)) is maintained by the company's Direksi and, per Pasal 50(4), is made available at the company's seat for inspection by shareholders — not the general public.

Current shareholder names do reach the Minister's Daftar Perseroan (Pasal 29(2)(g), public per Pasal 29(5)) through Pasal 56(3) notifications, but that is registered data — the AHU Profil Perseroan snapshot is parsed data, not a document; reconstructing a current list as a DOCUMENT generally requires the latest akta (perubahan/pemindahan hak) plus notification receipts, or the company's own DPS.

summary: For a listed PT Tbk, shares are dematerialised (scripless) and held collectively through the central securities depository KSEI, with the shareholder register maintained by a securities administration bureau (Biro Administrasi Efek), not by the company-law registry. No AHU/Kemenkumham document lists the current full body of public shareholders. The founding shareholders remain named in the Akta Pendirian, and holders of significant stakes (e.g. >=5%) are disclosed to OJK/IDX and in the annual report/prospectus, but a complete current shareholder list is not obtainable as a registry document.

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 29 ayat (4) — carve-out: shareholder name/address data of a Perseroan Terbuka in the Daftar Perseroan follows capital-markets law, not the normal company-register rule
evidence.quote: Ketentuan sebagaimana dimaksud pada ayat (2) huruf g mengenai nama lengkap dan alamat pemegang saham Perseroan Terbuka sesuai dengan ketentuan peraturan perundang-undangan di bidang pasar modal.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'The provision referred to in paragraph (2) letter g concerning the full names and addresses of shareholders of a Publicly Listed Company shall be in accordance with the laws and regulations in the capital markets field.' The statute itself removes Tbk shareholders from the ordinary Daftar Perseroan shareholder-data regime — the company-law registry does not carry the public shareholder body.

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 50 ayat (5) — the company-held shareholder-register provisions apply to a Perseroan Terbuka only insofar as capital-markets law does not provide otherwise
evidence.quote: Dalam hal peraturan perundang-undangan di bidang pasar modal tidak mengatur lain, ketentuan sebagaimana dimaksud pada ayat (1), ayat (3), dan ayat (4) berlaku juga bagi Perseroan Terbuka.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'To the extent that the laws and regulations in the capital markets field do not provide otherwise, the provisions of paragraphs (1), (3) and (4) also apply to a Publicly Listed Company.' Shareholder administration of a listed PT is deferred to the capital-markets regime (KSEI depository / share registrar).

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 56 ayat (5) — transfers of exchange-traded shares governed by capital-markets law (so the Pasal 56(3) minister-notification chain does not track listed trading)
evidence.quote: Ketentuan mengenai tata cara pemindahan hak atas saham yang diperdagangkan di pasar modal diatur dalam peraturan perundang-undangan di bidang pasar modal.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'Provisions on the procedure for the transfer of rights over shares traded on the capital market are regulated in the laws and regulations in the capital markets field.'

caveats: Founding shareholders are still documented in the Akta Pendirian, and major (>=5%) holders are disclosed via OJK/IDX filings and the annual report/prospectus.

The current full shareholder list sits in the KSEI depository / share registrar (BAE), which are not the company-law registry and do not issue a public list; KSEI/OJK primary sources were not fetched this session (statutory deference is proven, the operational depository detail is established practice).
INFERENCE on the exact substantial-shareholding threshold and disclosure vehicle.

documentLabel: Deed of Establishment containing the Articles of Association (and later deeds of amendment approved by the Ministry)
documentLocal: Akta Pendirian yang memuat Anggaran Dasar (dan Akta Perubahan Anggaran Dasar + SK Persetujuan Menteri)
summary: The constitution of a PT is the Anggaran Dasar contained in the notarial Akta Pendirian (Pasal 8 and 15 UUPT), which is ratified by the Minister (SK Pengesahan) and announced in the Berita Negara / Tambahan Berita Negara (Pasal 30). It exists as a concrete document; the current constitution is the original akta as amended by any Akta Perubahan Anggaran Dasar approved by the Minister. Copies are held by the officiating notary and evidenced by the ministerial decree and the BNRI announcement, rather than by a self-service registry download.

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 8 ayat (1) — the deed of establishment contains the articles of association
evidence.quote: Akta pendirian memuat anggaran dasar dan keterangan lain berkaitan dengan pendirian Perseroan.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'The deed of establishment contains the articles of association and other information relating to the establishment of the Company.'

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 15 ayat (1) — mandatory minimum contents of the anggaran dasar
evidence.quote: Anggaran dasar sebagaimana dimaksud dalam Pasal 8 ayat (1) memuat sekurang-kurangnya:
evidence.note: English gloss: 'The articles of association referred to in Article 8 paragraph (1) shall contain at least:' (name/seat, objects, duration, capital, shares, organs, etc.). Confirms the anggaran dasar is a defined constitutional document inside the akta.

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 21 ayat (1) — certain amendments of the articles require ministerial approval (others are notified, ayat (3)); all are made in a notarial deed (ayat (4))
evidence.quote: Perubahan anggaran dasar tertentu harus mendapat persetujuan Menteri.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'Certain amendments to the articles of association must obtain the approval of the Minister.' The currently effective constitution = akta pendirian as amended by ministerially approved/notified Akta Perubahan Anggaran Dasar.

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 30 ayat (1) huruf a — the Minister announces the deed of establishment (with the ratification decree) in the State Gazette supplement
evidence.quote: Menteri mengumumkan dalam Tambahan Berita Negara Republik Indonesia: a. akta pendirian Perseroan beserta Keputusan Menteri sebagaimana dimaksud dalam Pasal 7 ayat (4);
evidence.note: English gloss: 'The Minister announces in the Supplement to the State Gazette of the Republic of Indonesia: a. the deed of establishment of the Company together with the Ministerial Decree referred to in Article 7 paragraph (4).' Confirms official publication of the constitutional document (and, per letters b-c, of amendment deeds).

caveats: The akta/anggaran dasar is primarily obtained from the officiating notary; the registry evidences it via the SK and the BNRI/TBNRI announcement rather than issuing the deed itself.
Only the anggaran dasar (articles) and its amendments are public; the full deed may contain details accessed via the notary.

documentLabel: Deed of Establishment containing the Articles of Association (and later ministerially-approved deeds of amendment)
documentLocal: Akta Pendirian yang memuat Anggaran Dasar (dan Akta Perubahan Anggaran Dasar + SK Persetujuan Menteri)
summary: As for any PT, the constitution of a PT Tbk is the Anggaran Dasar contained in the notarial Akta Pendirian (Pasal 8/15 UUPT), ratified by the Minister and announced in the Berita Negara (Pasal 30); amendments are made by ministerially-approved deeds. It exists as a document, obtained from the notary and evidenced via the SK and BNRI announcement. For a listed company the current consolidated anggaran dasar is additionally reproduced in public OJK/IDX filings (prospectus, annual report), reinforcing document-based provability.

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 1 angka 7 — a Perseroan Terbuka is (a species of) Perseroan, so the same akta/anggaran dasar regime applies
evidence.quote: Perseroan Terbuka adalah Perseroan Publik atau Perseroan yang melakukan penawaran umum saham, sesuai dengan ketentuan peraturan perundang-undangan di bidang pasar modal.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'A Publicly Listed Company is a Public Company or a Company that conducts a public offering of shares, in accordance with the laws and regulations in the capital markets field.'

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 8 ayat (1) — the deed of establishment contains the articles of association
evidence.quote: Akta pendirian memuat anggaran dasar dan keterangan lain berkaitan dengan pendirian Perseroan.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'The deed of establishment contains the articles of association and other information relating to the establishment of the Company.'

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 21 ayat (1) (amendments require ministerial approval/notification) and Pasal 30 ayat (1) huruf a (BNRI announcement)
evidence.quote: Perubahan anggaran dasar tertentu harus mendapat persetujuan Menteri.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'Certain amendments to the articles of association must obtain the approval of the Minister.' Pasal 21(2)(f) expressly includes the change of status from a closed company to a Perseroan Terbuka among the amendments requiring ministerial approval, and Pasal 30(1) provides for announcement of the deed and amendment deeds in the Tambahan Berita Negara.

caveats: The deed/anggaran dasar is primarily obtained from the notary; the registry evidences it via the SK and BNRI announcement.
For a Tbk, the current anggaran dasar is also publicly available through OJK/IDX disclosure documents (prospectus, annual report).

documentLabel: Ministerial Decree ratifying legal-entity status (and decrees approving amendments)
documentLocal: Surat Keputusan Menteri Hukum dan HAM tentang Pengesahan Badan Hukum Perseroan (SK Kemenkumham); data snapshot: Profil Perseroan (AHU/SABH)
summary: The registry (Ministry of Law and Human Rights via AHU Online) maintains the Daftar Perseroan and issues the SK Pengesahan Badan Hukum that confers legal-entity status on the PT, plus further SKs approving/receiving each anggaran-dasar or data change (Pasal 7(4), 29, 30 UUPT). Those decrees are the registry-issued documents evidencing the company's existence and current legal data. Indonesia has no single certified 'current extract' document in the German Handelsregisterauszug sense; the current-state snapshot is the AHU 'Profil Perseroan', which is a data printout (parsed data) with restricted access.

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 7 ayat (4) — legal-entity status is conferred by the Ministerial Decree (SK Pengesahan), making the SK the registry-issued base document
evidence.quote: Perseroan memperoleh status badan hukum pada tanggal diterbitkannya Keputusan Menteri mengenai pengesahan badan hukum Perseroan.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'The Company obtains legal-entity status on the date of issuance of the Ministerial Decree concerning the ratification of the Company's legal-entity status.'

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 29 ayat (1) dan (5) — the Daftar Perseroan is administered by the Minister and open to the public
evidence.quote: Daftar Perseroan diselenggarakan oleh Menteri.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'The register of companies is administered by the Minister.' Pasal 29(5), also verbatim in the same source: 'Daftar Perseroan sebagaimana dimaksud pada ayat (1) terbuka untuk umum.' — 'The register of companies referred to in paragraph (1) is open to the public.' Pasal 29(2) enumerates its data content (name/seat, objects, capital, deed and decree numbers/dates, shareholders, directors, commissioners, etc.) — a data register, not an issued extract document.

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 30 ayat (1) huruf a — official announcement of the deed plus decree in the Tambahan Berita Negara
evidence.quote: Menteri mengumumkan dalam Tambahan Berita Negara Republik Indonesia: a. akta pendirian Perseroan beserta Keputusan Menteri sebagaimana dimaksud dalam Pasal 7 ayat (4);
evidence.note: English gloss: 'The Minister announces in the Supplement to the State Gazette of the Republic of Indonesia: a. the deed of establishment of the Company together with the Ministerial Decree referred to in Article 7 paragraph (4).'

caveats: INFERENCE: SK Kemenkumham is treated as the register-extract equivalent because no standalone certified 'current extract' document exists in ID; the statutory register (Daftar Perseroan, Pasal 29) is a public data register, not an extract-issuing product.
The AHU 'Profil Perseroan' shows current consolidated data but is parsed data, not a document, and public self-service access is limited (notary/paid access).

UUPT was amended by the Job Creation Law (UU 11/2020, re-enacted as UU 6/2023); core incorporation/registration articles remain substantively intact (quotes above are from the original 2007 text as published by peraturan.bpk.go.id).

documentLabel: Ministerial Decree ratifying legal-entity status (and decrees approving amendments)
documentLocal: Surat Keputusan Menteri Hukum dan HAM tentang Pengesahan Badan Hukum Perseroan (SK Kemenkumham); data snapshot: Profil Perseroan (AHU/SABH)
summary: A PT Tbk is still a Perseroan Terbatas registered at AHU and holds the same registry footprint as a private PT: the SK Pengesahan Badan Hukum and subsequent SKs recorded in the Daftar Perseroan are the registry-issued documents evidencing existence and legal data (Pasal 7(4), 29, 30 UUPT). As with a private PT, there is no single certified 'current extract' document; the AHU Profil Perseroan is the current-state data snapshot (parsed data). For a listed company, additional current status is also visible via OJK/IDX disclosures, but the core company-law register is the same as for a PT.

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 1 angka 7 — definition: a Perseroan Terbuka is a Perseroan, hence same AHU registry footprint
evidence.quote: Perseroan Terbuka adalah Perseroan Publik atau Perseroan yang melakukan penawaran umum saham, sesuai dengan ketentuan peraturan perundang-undangan di bidang pasar modal.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'A Publicly Listed Company is a Public Company or a Company that conducts a public offering of shares, in accordance with the laws and regulations in the capital markets field.' A Tbk remains an AHU-registered PT.

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 7 ayat (4) — legal-entity status via the Ministerial Decree (SK Pengesahan)
evidence.quote: Perseroan memperoleh status badan hukum pada tanggal diterbitkannya Keputusan Menteri mengenai pengesahan badan hukum Perseroan.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'The Company obtains legal-entity status on the date of issuance of the Ministerial Decree concerning the ratification of the Company's legal-entity status.'

evidence.basis: UU No. 40 Tahun 2007, Pasal 29 ayat (1) dan (5) — the Daftar Perseroan (Minister-administered, public) records the company data for every Perseroan including a Tbk
evidence.quote: Daftar Perseroan sebagaimana dimaksud pada ayat (1) terbuka untuk umum.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'The register of companies referred to in paragraph (1) is open to the public.'

caveats: INFERENCE: SK Kemenkumham treated as register-extract equivalent; no standalone certified current extract exists.
Listed-company current data is additionally disclosed via OJK and IDX, but that is a securities-disclosure channel, not the company register.
AHU Profil Perseroan is parsed data with restricted public access.

documentLocal: Form B1 — List of past and present members
summary: The B1 annual return carries a full list of past and present members with shareholdings, EVERY year. Ireland is the strongest non-DE case found so far.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2014, s.343(4) + Companies Act 2014 (Forms) Regulations, S.I. 627/2020 reg 4, Part 2 of Schedule (in force 16.12.2020)
evidence.quote: A full list of members (i.e. shareholders) is required with the return.
evidence.note: Form B1 note twenty-one. The substance lives in the PRESCRIBED FORM under s.343(4)(a), never in the Act itself. The form's members block: 'Persons holding shares on the date to which the annual return has been made up … and of persons who have held shares therein at any time since the date of the last return'. Columns: Name and Address / Share class / Numbers held at date of last return / Number transferred & date / Particulars of transferee / Total number held at date of this return.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1963, Fifth Schedule Part II Note (1) — REPEALED
evidence.note: A five-year (not three-year) partial-list rule existed pre-2015 and was NOT carried into the CA 2014 Form B1. The UK s.856A three-year analogy fails for Ireland on both count and currency.

caveats: RETRIEVAL TRAP: the CRO permits the shareholder list to be uploaded as a SEPARATE attachment to the B1. CRO verbatim: 'When a Form B1 is purchased, if a separate shareholders list is associated to this, it can be provided on request to info@cro.ie.' Where that route was used, the list is NOT in the purchased B1 image and is email-request-only — a hard blocker for automation, and it fails SILENTLY (the B1 simply has no members section). See https://cro.ie/annual-return/filing-an-annual-return/

Detail gradient by form (B1 notes twenty-two/twenty-three): private companies (LTD, DAC, ULC) must give shares transferred since the last ARD; other share-capital companies need only give name, address, share class and number held at the return date. This is a detail gradient, NOT an exemption — the full member list is required from all companies with share capital.

UNVERIFIED: S.I. 627/2020's schedule pages are scanned images with no text layer. The verbatim note wording is taken from the preceding prescribed B1 (S.I. 95/2018, text layer intact) and corroborated by a filed post-2020 B1. No evidence the 2020 substitution changed the members notes, but the 2020 image text was not read directly.
UNVERIFIED: the CRO company printout's shareholder content (sources conflict; the official 'Printout of basic company details' label leans toward no shareholders). Needs a direct query to CRO.
UNVERIFIED: the CLG (company limited by guarantee) members position — inferred, not confirmed.

legalFormLocal: Private company limited by shares (חברה פרטית)
summary: Current shareholders of a private company are provable from the Registrar. The company itself keeps the statutory Shareholders Register (s.127/s.130), but a private company must file with the Registrar every share allocation within 14 days (s.140(5), s.292), every share transfer within 14 days (s.140(6), s.299), and an annual report (s.140, s.141) whose prescribed form lists the shareholders and their holdings. The company extract (נסח חברה) therefore reflects the current shareholders and is the base document evidencing them.

evidence.basis: Companies Law 5759-1999 (Israel) s.140 (Reports of a private company)
evidence.quote: A private company shall send the Registrar an annual report, as said in section 141, and it shall also report to the Registrar as specified in this Law and on the following matters: ... (5) share allocations, as said in section 292; (6) share transfers, as said in section 299, within 14 days after the transfer;
evidence.note: Establishes that allotments and transfers of a private company are Registrar-filed, so the current shareholder position is provable from registry filings.

evidence.basis: Companies Law 5759-1999 (Israel) s.292 (Report of allocation) and s.141 (Annual report)
evidence.quote: 292. A private company must deliver the following documents to the Registrar within 14 days after the allocation of shares: (1) a report on a form to be prescribed by the Minister, specifying the particulars of the allocation;
evidence.note: s.141: 'Once a year a private company shall draw up and submit to the Registrar an annual report ... within 14 days after the General Meeting.' The prescribed annual-report form (Companies Regulations (Annual Report and Registration) 5760-2000) itemises shareholders and holdings.

evidence.basis: Companies Law 5759-1999 (Israel) s.127 & s.130 (Shareholders Register)
evidence.quote: 127. A company shall keep a Shareholders Register.
evidence.note: s.130 lists register contents (name, ID, address, number/category of shares, dates of allocation/transfer). This statutory register is kept by the COMPANY, not the Registrar; the Registrar's extract is derived from the s.292/s.299/s.141 filings.

caveats: Currency depends on the company having filed its 14-day allotment/transfer notices and annual report; the definitive statutory Shareholders Register is company-held (s.127), the extract is the Registrar-derived copy.
A shareholder may be a trustee (s.131) — registered holder is not necessarily beneficial owner.

summary: Current shareholders of a public/listed company are NOT provable from the Registrar of Companies. A public company does not report allotments, transfers or shareholders to the Registrar (s.145 exhaustively lists its Registrar reports, excluding these). Listed shares are held on the TASE through a registration company (nominee) which is expressly not deemed a shareholder (s.132, s.177(1)). Only substantial (5%+) holdings are disclosed — to the Securities Authority under the Securities Law, recorded in the company-held Register of Substantial Shareholders (s.128, s.137) — not a complete current shareholder list at the Registrar.

evidence.basis: Companies Law 5759-1999 (Israel) s.145 (Public company's report to the Registrar)
evidence.quote: a public company shall report to the Registrar under this Law only on the matters specified below: (1) a decision to change the name ...; (2) a change in the address of the registered office ...; (3) a merger ...; (4) its conversion into a public company ...
evidence.note: Share allocations (s.292) and transfers (s.299) — mandatory for private companies under s.140 — are absent, so shareholder data does not reach the Registrar for public companies.

evidence.basis: Companies Law 5759-1999 (Israel) s.132 (Registration company) & s.177(1)
evidence.quote: a registration company shall not be deemed a shareholder in the company and the shares in its name are owned by the persons entitled to them, as said in section 177(1).
evidence.note: Listed shares sit in the name of a nominee/registration company; the registered holder is not the beneficial owner, so the register does not reveal current beneficial shareholders.

evidence.basis: Companies Law 5759-1999 (Israel) s.128 & s.137 (Register of Substantial Shareholders)
evidence.quote: 137. In the Register of Substantial Shareholders shall be kept all the reports received by the company under the Securities Law on the holdings of company shares by substantial shareholders.
evidence.note: Only 5%+ ('substantial') holders are disclosed, via Securities Law reporting to the Israel Securities Authority (MAGNA), and this register is company-held — not the Registrar of Companies. Not a full shareholder list.

caveats: Substantial (5%+) shareholders ARE discoverable via the Israel Securities Authority / MAGNA and the company's Register of Substantial Shareholders — but that is not the Registrar of Companies and not a complete current shareholder list.
Listed-company ordinary shares are held through TASE nominee / registration company (s.132), masking beneficial owners.
documentLocal set to the extract for schema consistency, but the extract does not evidence current public-company shareholders.

legalFormLocal: Private company limited by shares (חברה פרטית)
summary: Every company must have articles (תקנון; termed 'by-laws' in the official English translation), and a copy is filed with the Registrar as an attachment to the incorporation application (s.8(1), s.15). Any change of the articles must be delivered to the Registrar (s.21(b), reported under s.140(1)). The current articles are therefore obtainable from the company's file at the Registrar as a separate document (not part of the summary extract), under the public-inspection right in s.43.

evidence.basis: Companies Law 5759-1999 (Israel) s.8 (Application for registration) & s.15
evidence.quote: 8. If a person wishes to register a company, then he shall submit to the Registrar an application on a form prescribed by the Minister, and he shall attach to it: (1) a copy of the by-laws;
evidence.note: s.15: 'Every company shall have by-laws, as specified in this Article.' 'By-laws' is the translation of תקנון (articles of association / constitution).

evidence.basis: Companies Law 5759-1999 (Israel) s.21 & s.140(1) (change of by-laws reported to Registrar)
evidence.quote: If a company adopted a resolution on a change of its by-laws, then it shall deliver the text of the decision to the Registrar within ...
evidence.note: s.140(1) lists 'changes in the by-laws, as said in section 21' among the private company's mandatory Registrar reports, so the amended articles reach the Registrar's file.

caveats: The full articles text is retrieved from the company's document file at the Registrar, not from the short company extract; ordering the underlying filed document may be a separate request.
Currency depends on the company having filed its s.21/s.140(1) amendment; a company incorporated before the 1999 Law may rely on transitional memorandum/articles (s.24).

summary: A public company must have articles (תקנון) and a copy was filed with the Registrar on incorporation (s.8(1), s.15), obtainable from its Registrar file under the s.43 inspection right. However, s.145 does NOT include a change of by-laws among a public company's mandatory Registrar reports, so amendments to the articles of a public/listed company are filed with the Israel Securities Authority (MAGNA / prospectus regime) rather than the Registrar. The Registrar's file therefore proves the constitution as a filed document but may hold only the founding (or pre-listing) version.

evidence.basis: Companies Law 5759-1999, Section 8 (application for registration) — ICNL unofficial English translation
evidence.quote: If a person wishes to register a company, then he shall submit to the Registrar an application on a form prescribed by the Minister, and he shall attach to it: (1) a copy of the by-laws;
evidence.note: The articles/by-laws (תקנון) are the company's constitution and a copy is filed with the Registrar of Companies at registration.

evidence.basis: Companies Law 5759-1999, Section 15 (company by-laws)
evidence.quote: Every company shall have by-laws, as specified in this Article.
evidence.note: The by-laws are the mandatory constitutional document whose contents are prescribed in ss.18-35.

caveats: English text is an unofficial ICNL translation; the binding version is the official Hebrew.
Confirms the articles/by-laws exist and are filed with the Registrar as an additional constitutional document.

legalFormLocal: Private company limited by shares (חברה פרטית)
summary: The Registrar of Companies keeps a register for every company and the registers are open to public inspection with certified copies obtainable (s.38, s.43). Any person can obtain a full company extract (נסח חברה) for a fee; for a private company it shows current registered particulars including status, registered office, directors/officers and shareholders with their holdings. This extract is the current register extract and the base evidentiary document.

evidence.basis: Companies Law 5759-1999 (Israel) s.43 (Inspection)
evidence.quote: The registers kept by the Registrar in the registers office shall be open to inspection by the public, and every person may inspect them and receive certified copies of their contents, whether through the Registrar or through others whom the Registrar authorized for this purpose, all as the Minister prescribed.
evidence.note: s.38 requires the Registrar to keep registers for each company and a Register of Companies; s.41 makes a Registrar-certified copy conclusive proof.

evidence.basis: Israeli Corporations Authority / Registrar of Companies — company extract service (gov.il / ica.justice.gov.il)
evidence.quote: This service allows any person to obtain basic information free of charge on companies and partnerships, or a full extract for a fee.
evidence.note: Wording per the gov.il 'company extract' service page as surfaced in search; gov.il blocks automated fetch so exact wording not independently re-fetched. Companion 'reverse search' service states it 'provides details on office holders and shareholders, including tenure dates and shareholding periods.'

caveats: The full (paid) extract, not the free basic lookup, carries the detailed particulars.
INFERENCE that the extract heading = REGISTERAUSZUG equivalent; grounded in s.38/s.43 register-inspection rights plus the gov.il service description.

summary: A public company is registered in the Register of Companies and a company extract (נסח חברה) is obtainable for it, evidencing legal existence, registration number, name, registered office and status. However, under s.145 a public company reports to the Registrar only on name change, registered-office change, merger and conversion — director changes (s.223 applies only to private companies) and shareholdings are NOT filed with the Registrar — so the public-company extract is materially thinner than a private one.

evidence.basis: Companies Law 5759-1999 (Israel) s.145 (Public company's report to the Registrar)
evidence.quote: Without derogating from the provisions of any statute, a public company shall report to the Registrar under this Law only on the matters specified below: (1) a decision to change the name, as said in section 31; (2) a change in the address of the registered office, as said in section 123; (3) a merger, as said in section 317; (4) its conversion into a public company, as said in section 343.
evidence.note: Confirms the extract's registry-sourced content for a public company is limited to identity/status matters; directors and shareholders are reported elsewhere (Securities Authority / TASE).

evidence.basis: Israeli Corporations Authority / Registrar of Companies — company extract service (gov.il / ica.justice.gov.il)
evidence.quote: This service allows any person to obtain basic information free of charge on companies and partnerships, or a full extract for a fee.
evidence.note: Same extract service applies to public companies; wording per gov.il service page as surfaced in search (gov.il blocks automated fetch).

caveats: Public-company extract carries limited data: per s.145 (and s.223 being private-company-only) directors and shareholders are not filed with the Registrar.
INFERENCE that the same נסח חברה product exists for public companies; grounded in the single Register of Companies (s.38) and universal extract service.

summary: A 1931 Act company with a share capital must, in its annual return, list all members and the shares each holds, and must forward a signed copy to the Registrar within one month of its return date. The annual return is therefore a public, filed document evidencing shareholders. It is a point-in-time snapshot (up to ~12 months old), not a live register.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1931 (IoM) s107(1)
evidence.quote: The annual return of a company having a share capital shall contain a list of all persons who, at the date of the return, are members of the company, and of all persons who have ceased to be members since the date of the last return
evidence.note: s107(2) adds names, addresses and number of shares held by each existing member.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1931 (IoM) s109(1)
evidence.quote: the company must, within one month after that date, forward to the Department a copy signed by a director or by the manager or by the secretary of the company
evidence.note: Confirms the members-listing annual return is filed with the Registrar and thus publicly obtainable.

caveats: The 1931 annual return lists members and IS public and filed — the opposite of the 2006 Act regime.
Point-in-time: reflects members at the return date, not necessarily current holders.
Does not apply to a company limited by guarantee (s107(6)); the members-list requirement is for share-capital companies.

documentLocal: register of members (kept by registered agent, not filed)
summary: This is the decisive distinction. Under the 2006 Act the register of members is kept at the office of the registered agent (s62, s78), is NOT filed with the Registrar, and the annual return (s85) contains only 'such particulars as may be prescribed' — it does NOT list members. Filing a copy of the register of members with the Registrar is purely OPTIONAL under s203. Therefore, by default, a current shareholder document is NOT obtainable from the registry and shareholders are NOT public. A document is only obtainable if the specific company has voluntarily elected under s203 to file its register of members.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006 (IoM) s62(1)
evidence.quote: A company shall cause to be kept a register of members containing ... the names and business or residential addresses of the persons who hold shares in the company
evidence.note: Kept by the company, not filed with the Registrar.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006 (IoM) s78(1)
evidence.quote: A company shall keep the following documents at the office of its registered agent -- ... the register of members maintained under section 62 or a copy of the register of members
evidence.note: Confirms register of members is held privately by the registered agent, not the Registrar.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006 (IoM) s85(1)
evidence.quote: An annual return shall be made by a company containing such particulars as may be prescribed.
evidence.note: Unlike the 1931 Act s107, the 2006 annual return does NOT statutorily list members.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006 (IoM) s203(1)
evidence.quote: A company may elect to file a copy of its register of members for registration by the Registrar.
evidence.note: Filing is voluntary ('may elect'); absent this election, shareholders are not on the public register.

caveats: CRUX: 2006 Act shareholders are NOT public by default — register of members kept by the registered agent and NOT filed; the annual return does not list members. This is the exact opposite of the 1931 Act regime.

CONDITIONAL EXCEPTION: if the specific company made an optional s203 election to file (and keep current) its register of members, a shareholder document IS then obtainable — rare, must be checked per company.
Optional director filing (s204) is separate and does not evidence shareholders.

summary: A 1931 Act company files its memorandum and articles of association at registration; these are held by the Companies Registry and are publicly orderable as documents.

evidence.basis: IoM Companies Registry - 1931 Act Companies (official gov.im)
evidence.note: Registry document set for 1931 Act companies includes Memorandum and Articles of Association, which are filed and publicly orderable.

caveats: Verbatim statutory filing clause not quoted here; confirmed via the official registry document list (memorandum and articles are on the public file).

summary: Incorporation of a 2006 Act company requires filing a memorandum (s2(1)(a), s5), which is on the public register. Articles are filed only if they differ from the relevant model articles (or for PCCs / unlimited-without-shares companies) per s2(1)(b); where the company adopts model articles, no bespoke articles are on file (the model articles apply by operation of law and are public). The constitution is therefore obtainable from the registry.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006 (IoM) s2(1)
evidence.quote: application may be made to the Registrar for the incorporation of a company by filing -- (a) a memorandum complying with section 5; and (b) if the articles of the company are to differ from the relevant model articles ... articles.
evidence.note: Memorandum always filed and public; bespoke articles filed only where they differ from model articles.

caveats: If the company uses the statutory model articles, no separate articles document is on the registry file — the model articles apply by law rather than as a filed instrument.
Amendments to memorandum/articles must be filed (s9), so the current constitution should be reconstructable from the file.

summary: The IoM Companies Registry maintains a public register for 1931 Act companies. A current register record (name, number, type, status, incorporation date, registered office, directors/secretary) is publicly searchable and a Certificate of Incorporation and filed documents can be ordered. There is no single German-style certified consolidated extract, but the official public register record is the functional equivalent.

evidence.basis: IoM Companies Registry (official gov.im portal)
evidence.note: Free basic company search plus orderable documents (Registration Certificate, First Directors/Secretary & Registered Office, Memorandum/Articles). Equivalence inferred from the public register service.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1931 (Isle of Man), s.284A(1) — public inspection and certified copies/extracts of documents kept by the Department (registry)
evidence.quote: any person may require a certificate of the incorporation of any company, or a copy or extract of any other document or any part of any other document, to be certified by the Department, on payment for the certificate, certified copy or extract, of such fees as may be prescribed under section 283A.
evidence.note: s.284A(1) opens with 'Any person may inspect' — '(a) a copy of any document, kept by the Department' (or the document itself if the copy is illegible/unavailable) on payment of prescribed fees. So the register record and all filings of a 1931 Act company are publicly inspectable and obtainable as Department-certified copies/extracts.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1931 (Isle of Man), s.284A(3) — evidential status of certified registry copies/extracts
evidence.quote: A copy of or extract from any document kept and registered at any of the offices for the registration of companies, certified to be a true copy by the Department (whose official position it shall not be necessary to prove) shall in all legal proceedings be admissible in evidence as of equal validity with the original document.

caveats: INFERENCE (retained): IoM issues a company search / certificates and certified copies or extracts of individual filed documents (s.284A), not a single certified consolidated extract like a German Handelsregisterauszug; the public register record is treated as the equivalent.
In s.284A 'the Department' is the Department for Enterprise, which operates the Companies Registry.

summary: The IoM Companies Registry maintains a public register for 2006 Act companies. A current register record (name, number, type, status, incorporation date, registered office, registered agent) is publicly searchable, and a Certificate of Incorporation and filed documents can be ordered. Same equivalence caveat as for 1931 Act: no single certified consolidated German-style extract.

evidence.basis: Isle of Man Companies Registry — 2006 Act companies page
evidence.quote: Disclosure is, in the main, by means of filing information with the Companies Registry
evidence.note: Confirms 2006 Act company information is filed with and disclosed via the Registry; the page also lists Certificates of Fact and Good Standing and a Company Search as Registry services, supporting availability of a register extract/search.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006 (Isle of Man), s.209(1) — inspection and certified copies/extracts of Registrar-held documents
evidence.quote: require a certificate of incorporation, merger, consolidation, continuation, discontinuance, dissolution, re-registration, restoration or good standing of a company incorporated under this Act, or a copy or an extract of any document or any part of a document of which the Registrar has custody, to be certified by the Registrar
evidence.note: s.209(1)(a) provides that a person may 'inspect the documents kept by the Registrar pursuant to this Act'; the section closes: a certificate 'or a certified copy or extract is conclusive evidence of the matters contained therein' (all literal text from s.209(1)).

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2006 (Isle of Man), s.211(1) — certificate of good standing available to any person
evidence.quote: The Registrar shall, upon request by any person, issue a certificate of good standing certifying that a company is of good standing
evidence.note: Conditions: the company is on the register and has paid all fees, annual fees and penalties due; s.211(2) requires the certificate to state whether winding-up/dissolution/receivership documents are on file or strike-off proceedings have been instituted.

caveats: No single consolidated German-style extract product is defined in the Act; the public register record plus certified copies/extracts of filed documents (s.209) and certificates of fact/good standing (s.210-211) together serve as the register-extract equivalent.

2006 Act companies file less content than 1931 Act companies (e.g. the 2006 Act annual return does not carry member details), so the register record centres on registered agent/office, directors and status.

documentLocal: Annual Return (Form MGT-7) — Companies Act 2013 s.92
summary: Members/shareholders are provable via the annual return. Under s.92(1) every company files an annual return containing 'its members ... along with changes therein', filed with the ROC; per Rule 11 the form is MGT-7 (a public company is never a 'small company', so always MGT-7). Publicly obtainable (paid) via MCA21 'View Public Documents' under s.399. Additional because it is a discrete filed document, not a base register extract (India issues none).

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2013 s.92(1) — reproduced text
evidence.quote: Every company shall prepare a return (hereinafter referred to as the annual return) in the prescribed form containing the particulars as they stood on the close of the financial year regarding— ... its members and changes therein since the close of the previous financial year
evidence.note: s.92(1)(b) covers shares and shareholding pattern.

evidence.basis: Companies (Management and Administration) Rules 2014, Rule 11 — reproduced text
evidence.quote: Every company shall prepare its annual return in Form No. MGT.7.
evidence.note: A public company cannot be a 'small company' (s.2(85)), so MGT-7 (not MGT-7A) applies.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2013 s.399(1) — public inspection/copies on payment of fees
evidence.quote: inspect by electronic means any documents kept by the Registrar ... being documents filed or registered by him in pursuance of this Act
evidence.note: Statutory basis for obtaining the filed MGT-7 via MCA21 'View Public Documents' (paid).

caveats: STALENESS: reflects members as at last financial-year close, filed up to 60 days post-AGM — 12-18+ months old; the statutory register of members (s.88) is company-held, not at the ROC.
For listed public companies a more current shareholding pattern is filed with SEBI/stock exchanges (Reg. 31 LODR) — outside the MCA registry.
Base-vs-additional labelling is a judgement call.

documentLocal: Annual Return (Form MGT-7; MGT-7A for OPC/small companies) — Companies Act 2013 s.92
summary: Shareholders/members ARE provable from the registry, but via a separate filed document, not any base extract. Under s.92(1) every company files an annual return containing 'its members ... along with changes therein', filed with the ROC. Per Rule 11 the form is MGT-7 (a small company/OPC files the abridged MGT-7A). The annual return is a publicly filed document obtainable (paid) via MCA21 'View Public Documents' under s.399. Additional (not base) because India has no register extract; members are evidenced only by ordering this specific annual filing.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2013 s.92(1) — reproduced text
evidence.quote: Every company shall prepare a return (hereinafter referred to as the annual return) in the prescribed form containing the particulars as they stood on the close of the financial year regarding— ... its members and changes therein since the close of the previous financial year
evidence.note: s.92(1)(b) also covers shares/securities and shareholding pattern.

evidence.basis: Companies (Management and Administration) Rules 2014, Rule 11 — reproduced text
evidence.quote: Every company shall prepare its annual return in Form No. MGT.7.
evidence.note: MGT-7A applies to OPCs and small companies (from FY 2020-21); a small Pvt Ltd files MGT-7A.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2013 s.399(1) — public inspection/copies on payment of fees
evidence.quote: inspect by electronic means any documents kept by the Registrar ... being documents filed or registered by him in pursuance of this Act
evidence.note: Statutory basis for obtaining the filed annual return via MCA21 'View Public Documents' (paid).

caveats: STALENESS: the annual return reflects members as at the close of the last financial year and is filed up to 60 days after the AGM — data can be 12-18+ months old; the statutory register of members (s.88) is company-held, not at the ROC.
Form is MGT-7A (abridged) for small companies/OPC — confirm which form was filed.
Base-vs-additional labelling is a judgement call: members are provable, but only via a discrete filed document.

documentLocal: Memorandum of Association (MOA) & Articles of Association (AOA) — Companies Act 2013 s.7(1)(a)
summary: The constitution is the MOA and AOA, filed with the Registrar at incorporation under s.7(1)(a), signed by all subscribers, and obtainable (paid) via MCA21 'View Public Documents' under s.399. Direct documentary proof; identical treatment to a private company.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2013 s.7(1)(a) — reproduced text
evidence.quote: the memorandum and articles of the company duly signed by all the subscribers to the memorandum in such manner as may be prescribed
evidence.note: Filed at incorporation with the Registrar.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2013 s.399(1) — public inspection/copies on payment of fees
evidence.quote: require ... a copy or extract of any other document or any part of any other document to be certified by the Registrar, on payment in advance of such fees as may be prescribed.
evidence.note: Basis for obtaining certified MOA/AOA via MCA21 'View Public Documents' (paid).

caveats: AOA/MOA amendments (special resolution + Form MGT-14) are filed later; order the latest filed version.
UNVERIFIED via official portal: mca.gov.in returned 403; obtainability is INFERENCE from s.399 + established MCA21 practice.

documentLocal: Master Data (free data view; no consolidated register extract issued)
summary: Same as for a private company: the MCA registry issues no consolidated current register extract document. Current particulars are exposed only as free 'Master Data' (structured data, not a document). The Certificate of Incorporation is static. Certified copies of specific filed forms are obtainable under s.399, but there is no single current-state extract document.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2013 (India), s.399(1)(a)-(b) — official India Code consolidated Act text
evidence.note: The Registrar's public products are (a) electronic inspection of filed documents and (b) a certificate of incorporation or certified copies/extracts of specific filed documents. No provision creates a single consolidated current register extract (Registerauszug) — consistent with MCA exposing current particulars only as the free 'Master Data' data-view. Verbatim from the official India Code PDF of the Act.

caveats: Finding is a negative (no consolidated extract issued); s.399 confirms only per-document certified copies and a certificate of incorporation, which supports but cannot conclusively prove the absence of a Registerauszug.

documentLocal: Master Data (free data view; no consolidated register extract issued)
summary: India's MCA registry does not issue a consolidated current register extract document. Current particulars (status, directors, registered office, paid-up capital) are exposed only via free 'Master Data', a structured data view, not an issued/certified document — parsed data is not a substitute. The Certificate of Incorporation is a document but static. Certified copies of specific filed forms can be obtained under s.399, but there is no single current-state extract document.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2013 (India), s.399(1)(a)-(b) — official India Code consolidated Act text
evidence.note: Same statutory basis as the Ltd row: the Registrar's public products are electronic inspection and per-document certified copies/certificate of incorporation, not a consolidated register extract; MCA offers only a free Master Data data-view. Verbatim from the official India Code PDF of the Act.

caveats: Negative finding; s.399 evidences per-document certified copies rather than a single Registerauszug, but cannot definitively prove non-existence of such a product.

summary: For an ehf the board must immediately draw up the share register (hlutaskrá), which is kept at the company's office and is accessible to shareholders and public authorities, not filed with Fyrirtækjaskrá. The register of enterprises therefore does not issue the shareholder list as a document. Iceland is comparatively transparent about ownership: a public beneficial-owner register lets anyone see a company's registered beneficial owner, but that is structured data (only owners above threshold), and the signed hlutaskrá is only lodged as supporting evidence, not released as a registry document.

evidence.basis: Act on Private Limited Companies No. 138/1994, art. 19 (hlutaskrá)
evidence.note: On formation the board must at once make a share register (hlutaskrá); it is kept at the company's office and shareholders and public authorities have access to it. Not sent to the register.

evidence.basis: Register of beneficial owners (raunverulegir eigendur), Skatturinn/Fyrirtækjaskrá
evidence.quote: með því að fletta félaginu upp í fyrirtækjaskrá á www.skatturinn.is er hægt að sjá hver er skráður raunverulegur eigandi félagsins á hverjum tíma
evidence.note: Beneficial owner is publicly viewable as data; a dated and signed hlutaskrá is listed among supporting documents submitted to prove ownership, not issued back publicly.

caveats: The public beneficial-owner register is DATA, not a document, and covers only owners above the 25% threshold — it is not a substitute for a full shareholder list.
Icelandic ownership transparency also derives from publicly accessible tax records, but those are not a registry-issued shareholder-list document.
art. 19 quote left empty (not confirmed verbatim); wording paraphrased from a summarizing fetch of the Althingi statute.

summary: For an hf the board must immediately make the share register (hlutaskrá), which is always kept at the company's office and is accessible to shareholders and public authorities; it is not filed with Fyrirtækjaskrá. The register of enterprises therefore does not issue the shareholder list as a document, and for listed hf ownership sits in the securities depository. As with ehf, Iceland's public beneficial-owner register shows a company's registered beneficial owner as data (owners above threshold only), not the full shareholder-list document.

evidence.basis: Act on Public Limited Companies No. 2/1995, art. 30 (hlutaskrá)
evidence.note: On formation the board must at once make a share register; the share register shall always be kept at the company's office, and all shareholders and public authorities have access to it. Not sent to the register.

evidence.basis: Register of beneficial owners (raunverulegir eigendur), Skatturinn/Fyrirtækjaskrá
evidence.quote: með því að fletta félaginu upp í fyrirtækjaskrá á www.skatturinn.is er hægt að sjá hver er skráður raunverulegur eigandi félagsins á hverjum tíma
evidence.note: Beneficial owner viewable publicly as data; signed hlutaskrá is only supporting evidence, not a publicly issued document.

caveats: Beneficial-owner register is DATA and threshold-limited — not a substitute for a full shareholder list.
For listed hf, the share register is maintained via the central securities depository, still not via Fyrirtækjaskrá.
art. 30 quote left empty (not confirmed verbatim); paraphrased from a summarizing fetch of the Althingi statute.

summary: An ehf's articles of association (samþykktir) form part of the registration file lodged with Fyrirtækjaskrá, and amendments to the articles must be notified to the register. The current articles are held by the register and can be obtained as a separate document, distinct from the register extract. This is an additional registry document rather than the base extract.

evidence.basis: Lög nr. 138/1994 um einkahlutafélög, 7. gr.
evidence.quote: Stofnendur skulu gera tillögur að samþykktum einkahlutafélags.
evidence.note: 7. gr. mgr. 2 lists mandatory contents: Í samþykktum einkahlutafélags skal greina eftirtalin atriði: 1. heiti félagsins... 3. tilgang félagsins; 4. hlutaféð... — confirms Samþykktir are the ehf's constitutive instrument.

evidence.basis: Lög nr. 138/1994, 9. gr. (registration)
evidence.quote: Stjórn félags skal tilkynna það til skráningar innan tveggja mánaða frá dagsetningu stofnsamnings.
evidence.note: The statute mandates notifying the company for registration but does not, in the fetched text, expressly require separate filing of the Samþykktir with fyrirtækjaskrá.

caveats: Statute confirms the Samþykktir exist as the mandatory constitutive document; it does not expressly mandate separate registry-filing of the articles (only company registration), so registry availability as an additional document rests on registry practice (skatturinn/fyrirtækjaskrá).

summary: An hf's articles of association (samþykktir) form part of the registration file lodged with Fyrirtækjaskrá, and amendments must be notified to the register. The current articles are held by the register and obtainable as a separate document, distinct from the register extract. This is an additional registry document rather than the base extract.

evidence.basis: Lög nr. 2/1995 um hlutafélög, 9. gr.
evidence.quote: Stofnendur skulu gera tillögur að samþykktum félags.
evidence.note: 9. gr. mgr. 2: Í samþykktum skal m.a. greina eftirtalin atriði: followed by 12 enumerated mandatory items (name, purpose, capital, shares, board, meetings, financial year, etc.).

evidence.basis: Lög nr. 2/1995, 3. gr. (founding agreement)
evidence.quote: Í stofnsamningi skulu vera drög að samþykktum félagsins
evidence.note: The founding agreement must include the draft Samþykktir; 14. gr. requires the board to notify the company for registration within six months.

caveats: Statute confirms Samþykktir as the mandatory constitutive document; separate registry-filing of the articles is not expressly stated in the fetched text (only company registration under 14. gr.).

documentLocal: visura camerale ordinaria (soci accomandatari e accomandanti)

evidence.note: Official description of the visura camerale contents: 'holders and partners with their respective quotas'. For a registered Sas the soci — accomandatari and accomandanti alike — appear on the visura (base document).

evidence.note: 'Partners and holders of rights over quotas and shares' listed as visura content.

evidence.note: Statutory basis: the Sas atto costitutivo must indicate both classes of partners and is registered. Article text NOT fetched verbatim this session — normattiva.it serves the whole code as a single truncating page.

The registry's visura documentation uses the generic term 'soci' and does not separately name accomandanti; that limited partners are individually shown on the Sas visura is standard registry practice grounded in art. 2316's requirement that they be indicated in the registered atto costitutivo.

documentLocal: elenco dei soci (deposito ex art. 2435, comma 2, c.c.)
summary: SpA shareholders are NOT on the visura: the authoritative shareholder register is the private libro soci kept by the directors (art. 2421 n.1). However, a non-listed SpA must deposit an annual elenco dei soci at the Registro delle Imprese together with the bilancio (art. 2435, comma 2, c.c.), and that filing is a retrievable public document evidencing the shareholders as at the last accounts-approval date.

evidence.basis: Codice civile art. 2421, comma 1, n.1 — the libro dei soci is a mandatory SpA book kept internally (private, not filed)
evidence.quote: il libro dei soci ... a cura degli amministratori o dei componenti del consiglio di gestione

evidence.basis: Codice civile art. 2435, comma 2 — annual deposit of the elenco dei soci for non-listed companies
evidence.quote: Entro trenta giorni dall'approvazione del bilancio le società non aventi azioni quotate in mercati regolamentati sono tenute altresì a depositare per l'iscrizione nel registro delle imprese l'elenco dei soci riferito alla data di approvazione del bilancio, con l'indicazione del numero delle azioni possedute
evidence.note: The L. 2/2009 abolition of the elenco-soci filing applied ONLY to Srl, not to SpA — the SpA obligation under art. 2435 remains in force.

caveats: The register filing is a SNAPSHOT as at the last bilancio-approval date and can be up to ~15 months stale; it is NOT real-time and is NOT the legally authoritative record — rights are exercised on the private libro soci.
Applies only to non-listed SpA. For listed SpA there is NO elenco soci in the register — ownership runs through Monte Titoli/Consob transparency (effectively not_provable_from_registry).
A socio unico is separately registered and public under art. 2362 c.c., so a single-shareholder SpA's owner IS in the register regardless of the bilancio filing.

Quota transfers take effect toward the company only from deposit in the register (art. 2470 c.c.), so the register is the authoritative source of the current membership.

evidence.basis: L. 2/2009 art. 16, comma 12-septies — soppressione del libro soci per le Srl

evidence.note: Official Camera di Commercio (Pisa) explanatory page. Publicity of the compagine sociale moved into the Registro delle Imprese, appearing on the visura.

evidence.note: Deposit in the registro delle imprese makes a transfer effective toward the company — confirms the register holds the current soci.

evidence.quote: 1) [numero abrogato dal d.l. 29 novembre 2008, n. 185]

caveats: The ordinary visura shows the current soci; for the fully itemised per-member quota breakdown a dedicated 'visura elenco soci / partecipazioni' report from the same Registro Imprese data is typically pulled — same registry source, more detail.
