documentLocal: annual return / annual confirmation statement (Art. 71); register of members (Art. 41/45)
summary: Unusually for a secrecy jurisdiction, LEGAL shareholders ARE evidenced by a registry document in Jersey. The register of members is kept by the company (Art. 41) but the company files an annual return (Art. 71) with the registrar that lists members and their shareholdings; this is on the public record and is routinely sold by data providers as a shareholder document (name, address, number/class of shares). The register of members is also open to inspection by any person on payment (Art. 45). So a current-shareholder document IS obtainable — but only for holders of 1% or more, and it shows REGISTERED (often nominee) holders, not beneficial owners.

evidence.basis: Companies (Jersey) Law 1991, Art. 45(1) — inspection of register of members (official revised edition, jerseylaw.je)
evidence.quote: The register of members shall during business hours be open to the inspection of a member of the company without charge, and of any other person on payment of such sum (if any), not exceeding the published maximum, as the company may require.
evidence.note: Verified this session against the official Jersey Legal Information Board text; replaces the earlier incorporations.io citation of the same wording.

evidence.basis: Companies (Jersey) Law 1991, endnote 255 to Art. 71 — annual return abolished
evidence.quote: Article 71 substituted by L.9/2002, deleted by R&O.138/2020
evidence.note: Art. 71 (annual return) no longer exists in the current Law; the body of the Law shows '71' with no text. The registry filing that evidences members is now the annual confirmation statement under the 2020 Disclosure Law.

evidence.basis: Companies (Jersey) Law 1991, Art. 127YE(1) — cross-reference identifying the annual confirmation statement's statutory basis
evidence.quote: Article 5 of the Financial Services (Disclosure and Provision of Information) (Jersey) Law 2020 (which requires a company to provide an annual confirmation statement to the Commission)
evidence.note: Confirms from within the Companies Law itself that the annual confirmation statement obligation sits in the 2020 Disclosure Law.

evidence.basis: Financial Services (Disclosure and Provision of Information) (Jersey) Law 2020, Art. 5(1) — annual confirmation statement
evidence.quote: An entity must, within the relevant period, provide a statement (an “annual confirmation statement”) to the Commission verifying that the beneficial owner information, significant person information and any other prescribed information provided to the Commission in relation to the entity is accurate.
evidence.note: Member/share detail enters as prescribed information; the JFSC's registry documentation itemises it (see JFSC entries below). 'Relevant period' is 1 January to end of February each year (Art. 5(4)).

evidence.basis: JFSC Registry — Associated parties (official registry documentation): member details companies must provide
evidence.quote: Companies must provide us with details of the members on incorporation and confirm the information on a yearly basis through their annual confirmation statement
evidence.note: The page then itemises the required information: 'name and address of each member who holds not less than 1% of all issued shares in a class'; 'the number of members who each hold less than 1% of all the issued shares of a class'; 'the class and number of shares held by each member in the above two categories' (all literal text from the same page).

evidence.basis: JFSC Registry — Associated parties: public availability of member information
evidence.quote: Member information will be available to the public.

evidence.basis: JFSC Registry — Annual confirmation page: what is on the public register
evidence.quote: The following information will be available on our public register:
evidence.note: The list that follows this sentence reads (literal items): 'members', 'share information', 'significant person information, except:' (under-18s, company secretaries, share-transfer-property directors). The page also states: 'Companies must also ensure their share capital information is up-to-date before making the annual confirmation statement.'

evidence.basis: SystemDay — Jersey company documents (data provider description of the public search report)
evidence.quote: the name, address, number of shares held by shareholders at date of last return, class of share and any addition remarks
evidence.note: Shareholder detail is derived from the filed annual return and is treated as public. Pre-2021-regime framing; retained as evidence of practical retrievability.

evidence.basis: Topograph — Business Registers in Jersey
evidence.quote: Annual Returns: This provides details on shareholders, with their addresses, names, the number of shares, share class, and the secretary
evidence.note: Pre-2021-regime framing; retained as evidence of practical retrievability.

caveats: THRESHOLD: per JFSC registry documentation, only the name and address of each member holding not less than 1% of all issued shares in a class must be reported individually; below-threshold members are reported only as a count and are not individually public.

NOMINEE/BENEFICIAL: the public record shows the REGISTERED member (frequently a nominee), NOT the beneficial owner. Beneficial owner and controller information is provided to the JFSC under the 2020 Disclosure Law but is not on the public register (JFSC: visible only to the entity's nominated person).

TERMINOLOGY (resolves earlier CONFLICT caveat): the Art. 71 annual return was deleted by R&O.138/2020; since 2021 the registry filing is the annual confirmation statement under Art. 5 of the Financial Services (Disclosure and Provision of Information) (Jersey) Law 2020. JFSC documentation confirms member and share information IS available on the public register, so the member list remains publicly provable. References to 'Art. 71' in documentLocal are historical.
The register of members itself is company-held (Art. 41), though open to inspection by any person on payment (Art. 45); the registry-side document is the annual-confirmation-derived member record.

Member information is confirmed annually (JFSC: member updates are not subject to the 21-day change-notification duty that applies to beneficial owners/significant persons), so the public member record can be up to ~14 months stale.

legalFormLocal: Company limited by shares (private) / par value company
summary: The memorandum and articles of association are filed with the registrar at incorporation (uploaded as a single PDF). They constitute the company's constitution (Art. 10) and are obtainable from the JFSC as a certified/purchasable copy, so the constitution is provable from the registry.

evidence.quote: Every company must have a Memorandum and Articles of Association.
evidence.note: The memorandum and articles are the mandatory constitutive documents for every Jersey company.

evidence.note: The memorandum and articles are filed with the JFSC registry on incorporation and are therefore obtainable as a distinct document, confirming proved_by_additional_document.

caveats: JFSC registry guidance; the underlying statute (Companies (Jersey) Law 1991, Arts 4–5) could only be confirmed via its table of contents as the full-text page returned no article body.

summary: The memorandum and articles are filed with the registrar at incorporation and bind the company and members (Art. 10). They are obtainable from the JFSC as a certified/purchasable copy, so the constitution is provable from the registry for public companies as well.

evidence.quote: every company on the Register, which is not in any winding up process, must submit an annual confirmation statement every year
evidence.note: Confirms the JFSC maintains the public Register of Companies against which searches/extracts are produced.

evidence.quote: core company information — name, registration number, status, legal form, and date of registration — is freely searchable

documentLabel: Certificate of Registered Matters — showing business-executing members (業務執行社員) and representative members (代表社員)
documentLocal: 登記事項証明書 (業務執行社員・代表社員の氏名・住所); complete membership/equity held in company 定款/records
summary: Unlike a KK (where no owners appear on the register), a GK's members (社員) partly appear on the register itself: Art. 914 registers the names of members who execute the business (業務を執行する社員) and the names and addresses of representative members (代表社員). By default all members execute business (Art. 590(1)), so in the common configuration the register shows every member. Hence current members can, in that case, be evidenced by the base register extract. However this is NOT a guaranteed-complete ownership list: if the articles designate only some members as business-executing (Art. 591), non-executing members are not registered, and the register never shows members' equity/investment interests. Whether such carve-outs exist is not visible from the register alone, so completeness must be corroborated by the 定款/company records.

evidence.basis: 会社法 (Companies Act, Act No. 86 of 2005) Art. 914 — registered matters for a Godo Kaisha; chapeau plus items 6 and 7
evidence.quote:
合同会社の設立の登記は、その本店の所在地において、次に掲げる事項を登記してしなければならない。
六　合同会社の業務を執行する社員の氏名又は名称
七　合同会社を代表する社員の氏名又は名称及び住所
evidence.note: English gloss: 'Registration of the formation of a GK must record ... (vi) the names of the members who execute the business of the GK; (vii) the names and addresses of the members who represent the GK.' Only business-executing and representative members are registered — not all members, and no equity interests. Verbatim from the official e-Gov statute XML (laws.e-gov.go.jp law API, law ID 417AC0000000086).

evidence.basis: 会社法 Art. 590(1) — default that all members execute business
evidence.quote: 社員は、定款に別段の定めがある場合を除き、持分会社の業務を執行する。
evidence.note: English gloss: 'Members execute the business of a membership company, unless otherwise provided in the articles of incorporation.' Default = all members are business-executing (hence registered under Art. 914(vi)); a contrary provision in the articles removes non-executing members from the register. Verbatim from the official e-Gov statute XML.

evidence.basis: 商業登記法 (Commercial Registration Act, Act No. 125 of 1963) Art. 10(1) — anyone may obtain a certificate of registered matters
evidence.quote: 何人も、手数料を納付して、登記簿に記録されている事項を証明した書面（以下「登記事項証明書」という。）の交付を請求することができる。
evidence.note: English gloss: 'Any person may, on payment of a fee, request the issuance of a document certifying the matters recorded in the registry (a certificate of registered matters).' The 登記事項証明書 showing the Art. 914 member entries is publicly obtainable. Verbatim from the official e-Gov statute data (law ID 338AC0000000125).

caveats: COMPLETENESS RISK: the register reliably lists ALL members only when the articles do not restrict business execution (Art. 591). Non-executing members and members' equity shares are not on the register; corroborate against the 定款/company records for a full current-member picture.

documentLocal: 株主名簿 (shareholder registry, company-held; not on the commercial register)
summary: Shareholders of a KK are NOT a registered matter on the commercial register — Art. 911(3) does not list shareholders. Current shareholders are recorded only in the shareholder registry (株主名簿), which the company itself prepares and keeps at its head office (Companies Act Art. 121). Inspection of the 株主名簿 is restricted to the company's own shareholders and creditors and must state a reason (Art. 125); it is not a public document and cannot be obtained from the Legal Affairs Bureau. Therefore current shareholders cannot be proved from the registry.

evidence.basis: Companies Act Art. 121 — Shareholder Registers (contents)
evidence.quote: 株式会社は、株主名簿を作成し、これに次に掲げる事項（以下「株主名簿記載事項」という。）を記載し、又は記録しなければならない。 一 株主の氏名又は名称及び住所
evidence.note: Establishes the 株主名簿 as a company-created record, distinct from the commercial register.

evidence.basis: Companies Act Art. 125 — Keeping and inspection of the shareholder register
evidence.quote: 株式会社は、株主名簿をその本店（株主名簿管理人がある場合にあっては、その営業所）に備え置かなければならない。 … 株主及び債権者は、株式会社の営業時間内は、いつでも、次に掲げる請求をすることができる。
evidence.note: Shareholder register is kept at the company's head office and inspectable only by shareholders and creditors — not public, not registry-issued. Art. 125 wording was returned by a live fetch of the e-Gov Companies Act XML; Art. 121 wording is statutory text keyed to the same source.

caveats: The register extract (登記事項証明書) never lists individual shareholders for a KK; parsed register data cannot substitute for a shareholder document.
Live-fetch verbatim confirmed for Art. 125 (shareholders/creditors inspection); Art. 121 text is INFERENCE from the statute keyed to the e-Gov URL (SPA prevented full re-render).

documentLocal: 定款 (teikan / articles of incorporation) — company-held; not notarially authenticated for a GK; not on the commercial register
summary: A GK's articles of incorporation (定款) are not filed on or issued from the public commercial register. Unlike a KK, a GK's articles do not require notarial authentication, but they are prepared and kept by the company; inspection is limited to the company's members and creditors under Art. 618 (keeping and inspection of articles for a mochibun-kaisha). The Legal Affairs Bureau does not issue the 定款. Therefore the constitution cannot be proved from the registry.

evidence.basis: Companies Act Art. 618 — inspection of the articles of a mochibun-kaisha (incl. GK)
evidence.quote: 持分会社の社員及び債権者は、当該持分会社の営業時間内は、いつでも、次に掲げる請求をすることができる。
evidence.note: Articles held by the company; inspection limited to members and creditors — not public, not registry-issued.

caveats: Art. 618/575 wording is Companies Act statutory text keyed to the e-Gov URL; e-Gov SPA prevented live re-render (INFERENCE from statute, UNVERIFIED via live fetch).

documentLocal: 定款 (teikan / articles of incorporation) — company-held; original notarially authenticated and held by the notary; not on the commercial register
summary: The articles of incorporation (定款) are NOT filed on or issued from the public commercial register. For a KK the original articles must be notarially authenticated at incorporation (Art. 30) and the notarized original is retained by the notary public; the company keeps the articles at its head and branch offices (Art. 31(1)). Inspection under Art. 31(2) is limited to incorporators and, after incorporation, the company's shareholders and creditors. The Legal Affairs Bureau does not issue the 定款. Therefore the constitution cannot be proved from the registry.

evidence.basis: Companies Act Art. 31 — Keeping and Inspection of Articles of Incorporation
evidence.quote: 発起人（株式会社の成立後にあっては、当該株式会社）は、定款を発起人が定めた場所（株式会社の成立後にあっては、その本店及び支店）に備え置かなければならない。 … 発起人（株式会社の成立後にあっては、その株主及び債権者）は、… いつでも、次に掲げる請求をすることができる。
evidence.note: Articles are held by the company and inspectable only by shareholders/creditors — not public, not on the register.

caveats: The notarized original (認証を受けた定款原本, Art. 30) is held by the notary public, not the Legal Affairs Bureau; obtaining it is not a registry function.
Art. 31 wording is Companies Act statutory text keyed to the e-Gov URL; live re-render blocked by the e-Gov SPA (INFERENCE from statute, UNVERIFIED via live fetch).

documentLabel: Certificate of Registered Matters (commercial register extract)
documentLocal: 登記事項証明書 (登記簿謄本) — 商業登記, issued by the Legal Affairs Bureau (法務局)
summary: A Godo Kaisha (合同会社) is registered on the same commercial register maintained by the Legal Affairs Bureau, and its register extract is likewise the Certificate of Registered Matters (登記事項証明書). Registered matters under Companies Act Art. 914 include trade name (商号), purpose (目的), head/branch office locations (本店及び支店の所在場所), amount of capital (資本金の額), the names of members who execute the business (業務を執行する社員の氏名又は名称) and the names and addresses of representative members (代表社員の氏名又は名称及び住所). This is the base registry document, publicly obtainable.

documentLabel: Certificate of Registered Matters (commercial register extract)
documentLocal: 登記事項証明書 (登記簿謄本) — 商業登記, issued by the Legal Affairs Bureau (法務局)
summary: The commercial register (商業登記) is maintained by the Legal Affairs Bureau under the Ministry of Justice. The register extract is the Certificate of Registered Matters (登記事項証明書, formerly 登記簿謄本). For a KK the registered matters under Companies Act Art. 911(3) include trade name (商号), purpose (目的), head/branch office locations (本店及び支店の所在場所), amount of capital (資本金の額), total authorized/issued shares (発行可能株式総数・発行済株式の総数), directors' names (取締役の氏名) and representative directors' names and addresses (代表取締役の氏名及び住所). This is the base registry document and is publicly obtainable by anyone.

evidence.quote: 三 本店及び支店の所在場所 … 五 資本金の額 … 十三 取締役の氏名 … 十四 代表取締役の氏名及び住所

documentLocal: 주주명부 (register of shareholders, company-held under 상법 제352조 · 제396조)
summary: Shareholders of a Jusik Hoesa are NOT a registered matter. The Art. 317(2) registration list contains no shareholder entry. Shareholders are recorded only in the company's own register of shareholders (주주명부), whose contents are set by Commercial Act Art. 352 and which the directors must keep at the head office under Art. 396. It is not part of the public court register and cannot be obtained as a registry document; there is no registry-issued proof of current shareholders.

evidence.basis: 상법 제317조 제2항 — enumerated registered matters (proof of absence)
evidence.quote: 8. 사내이사, 사외이사 … 감사 및 집행임원의 성명과 주민등록번호  9. 회사를 대표할 이사 또는 집행임원의 성명·주민등록번호 및 주소
evidence.note: Verbatim. The complete list registers directors/representative director but has no shareholder item — confirming shareholders are outside the register.

evidence.basis: 상법 제352조 (주주명부의 기재사항) and 제396조 (정관·주주명부의 비치의무) — shareholder register is a company-held book kept at the head office
evidence.note: Quote omitted — could not extract verbatim this session (law.go.kr/casenote JS-rendered/refused). Art. 352 defines the contents of the 주주명부; Art. 396 requires directors to keep the 정관 and 주주명부 at the head office for inspection by shareholders/creditors — it is a private company book, not a court-registry filing. Canonical source: law.go.kr 상법 제352조·제396조.

caveats: Art. 352 and 396 could not be quoted verbatim from a primary server-rendered source this session; the company-held nature of the 주주명부 is established Korean corporate law but the exact wording here is INFERENCE from statute structure, not a fetched quote.
No registry document evidences current shareholders; any shareholder proof must come from the company (주주명부) or ancillary sources, not iros.go.kr.

documentLocal: 사원명부 (register of members, company-held; 상법 제566조) — member identities also appear in the 정관 (제543조), which is likewise not on the public register
summary: Members (사원) of a Yuhan Hoesa are NOT a registered matter. Member identities are an absolute requirement of the articles (정관, Art. 543) and are recorded in the company's register of members (사원명부), which the directors must keep at the head office under Art. 566. Neither the 정관 nor the 사원명부 forms part of the public court register, so there is no registry-issued document proving current members.

evidence.basis: 상법 제549조 제2항 (Commercial Act Art. 549(2)) — closed enumeration of the registered matters for a 유한회사; members (사원) are not among them
evidence.quote:
②제1항의 등기에서 다음 각 호의 사항을 등기하여야 한다.
1.  제179조제1호ㆍ제2호 및 제5호에 규정된 사항과 지점을 둔 때에는 그 소재지
2.  제543조제2항제2호와 제3호에 게기한 사항
3.  이사의 성명ㆍ주민등록번호 및 주소. 다만, 회사를 대표할 이사를 정한 때에는 그 외의 이사의 주소를 제외한다.
4.  회사를 대표할 이사를 정한 때에는 그 성명, 주소와 주민등록번호
5.  수인의 이사가 공동으로 회사를 대표할 것을 정한 때에는 그 규정
6.  존립기간 기타의 해산사유를 정한 때에는 그 기간과 사유
7.  감사가 있는 때에는 그 성명 및 주민등록번호
evidence.note: English gloss: the incorporation registration records purpose/trade name/head office (via Art. 179 items), total capital and amount per contribution unit (via Art. 543(2) items 2-3), directors, representative director, duration, and auditor. Members are NOT a registered matter. Verbatim from the Ministry of Government Legislation statute portal (law.go.kr, DRF open-API rendering of 상법 제549조).

evidence.basis: 상법 제566조 (Art. 566 — 서류의 비치, 열람: register of members kept at the company, inspection restricted)
evidence.quote:
①이사는 정관과 사원총회의 의사록을 본점과 지점에, 사원명부를 본점에 비치하여야 한다.
②사원명부에는 사원의 성명, 주소와 그 출자좌수를 기재하여야 한다.
③사원과 회사채권자는 영업시간 내에 언제든지 제1항에 게기한 서류의 열람 또는 등사를 청구할 수 있다.
evidence.note: English gloss: '(1) The directors must keep the articles of incorporation and the minutes of the general meeting at the head and branch offices, and the register of members (사원명부) at the head office. (2) The register of members must state each member's name, address and number of contribution units. (3) Members and company creditors may inspect or copy those documents during business hours.' The member list is company-held and open only to members and creditors — not filed with or issued by the public commercial registry. Verbatim from law.go.kr.

caveats: Negative claim is evidenced by the closed enumeration of registered matters in 상법 제549조(2) (members omitted) together with 제566조 locating the 사원명부 at the company's head office with inspection restricted to members and creditors — the statute affirmatively places the member list outside the public register.

documentLocal: 정관 (jeonggwan, articles of incorporation, company-held; 상법 제289조 · 제396조)
summary: The articles of incorporation (정관) are drawn up and signed by the promoters (발기인) under Commercial Act Art. 289 and kept at the head office under Art. 396; they are NOT filed as part of the public court register. Although some articles-derived facts (purpose, trade name, capital, share classes, public-notice method) are separately registered under Art. 317, the constitution document itself cannot be obtained from the commercial registry. A notarized 정관 is submitted to the registry office only as a supporting incorporation filing, not as a publicly retrievable register document.

evidence.basis: 상법 제289조 제1항 (정관의 작성, 절대적 기재사항) — the articles are made by the promoters
evidence.quote: 1. 목적  2. 상호  3. 회사가 발행할 주식의 총수  4. 액면주식을 발행하는 경우 1주의 금액  5. 회사의 설립 시에 발행하는 주식의 총수  6. 본점의 소재지  7. 회사가 공고를 하는 방법  8. 발기인의 성명·주민등록번호 및 주소
evidence.note: Verbatim. 발기인은 정관을 작성하여 위 사항을 적고 기명날인/서명한다 — the 정관 is a promoter-made constitutional document, not a registry output.

evidence.basis: 상법 제396조 (정관 등의 비치·공시의무) — the 정관 is kept at the head office, not on the public register
evidence.note: Quote omitted — not fetched verbatim this session. Art. 396 obliges directors to keep the 정관 (and 주주명부) at the head office for inspection; the public register (Art. 317) reproduces only selected articles-derived items, never the full constitution. Canonical source: law.go.kr 상법 제396조.

caveats: The registry does show certain 정관-derived data via Art. 317, but the constitution document itself is not registry-obtainable — status is not_provable_from_registry for the DOCUMENT.
Art. 396 not quoted verbatim (law.go.kr JS-rendered); its effect is established statute but the wording here is INFERENCE.

documentLocal: 정관 (jeonggwan, articles of incorporation, company-held; 상법 제543조)
summary: The articles of incorporation (정관) of a Yuhan Hoesa are drawn up by the members under Commercial Act Art. 543 and are NOT filed as part of the public court register. Some articles-derived facts (purpose, trade name, total capital, contribution-unit amount) are separately registered under Art. 549, but the constitution document itself cannot be obtained from the commercial registry. A notarized 정관 is submitted only as a supporting incorporation filing, not as a publicly retrievable register document.

evidence.basis: 상법 제543조 제1항 (Commercial Act Art. 543(1)) — the members draw up the 정관
evidence.quote: ①유한회사를 설립함에는 사원이 정관을 작성하여야 한다.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'To incorporate a limited company (유한회사), the members must draw up the articles of incorporation (정관).' The 정관 is a company-made constitutional document. Verbatim from law.go.kr (DRF open-API rendering of 상법 제543조).

evidence.basis: 상법 제566조 제1항·제3항 (Art. 566(1),(3)) — the 정관 is kept at the company; inspection restricted to members and creditors
evidence.quote:
①이사는 정관과 사원총회의 의사록을 본점과 지점에, 사원명부를 본점에 비치하여야 한다.
③사원과 회사채권자는 영업시간 내에 언제든지 제1항에 게기한 서류의 열람 또는 등사를 청구할 수 있다.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'The directors must keep the articles of incorporation ... at the head and branch offices'; 'Members and company creditors may at any time during business hours request inspection or copying of those documents.' The 정관 is company-held with member/creditor-only access — it is not a document the public commercial registry files or issues. Verbatim from law.go.kr.

evidence.basis: 상법 제549조 제2항 (Art. 549(2)) — only specific articles-derived facts are registered, not the 정관 itself
evidence.quote:
②제1항의 등기에서 다음 각 호의 사항을 등기하여야 한다.
1.  제179조제1호ㆍ제2호 및 제5호에 규정된 사항과 지점을 둔 때에는 그 소재지
2.  제543조제2항제2호와 제3호에 게기한 사항
evidence.note: English gloss: the registration carries selected 정관-derived data points (purpose, trade name, head office, total capital, amount per contribution unit) — the constitution document itself is not a registered/issuable item. Verbatim from law.go.kr.

caveats: 정관-derived facts (purpose, trade name, capital, contribution-unit amount) ARE registered via 제549조(2) and appear on the 등기사항증명서, but the 정관 document itself is company-held (제566조(1)) and inspectable only by members and creditors (제566조(3)).

documentLabel: Certified copy of the corporate register / Certificate of All Registered Matters
documentLocal: 법인등기사항전부증명서 (구칭 법인등기부등본) — corporate registration certificate issued by the court commercial registry (대법원 등기소 / IROS, iros.go.kr)
summary: The corporate register certificate is the base register extract. Under Commercial Act Art. 317(2) it evidences trade name, purpose, head-office/branch address, total capital (자본금의 액), total number and classes of shares, and the names/resident-registration numbers of directors and the representative director. It is publicly obtainable from the court commercial registry (IROS). It proves the company's existence, capital and management — but NOT its shareholders.

evidence.basis: 상법(Commercial Act) 제317조(설립의 등기) 제2항 — registered matters for a stock corporation
evidence.quote: 2. 자본금의 액  3. 발행주식의 총수, 그 종류와 각종주식의 내용과 수 … 8. 사내이사, 사외이사, 그 밖에 상무에 종사하지 아니하는 이사, 감사 및 집행임원의 성명과 주민등록번호  9. 회사를 대표할 이사 또는 집행임원의 성명·주민등록번호 및 주소
evidence.note: Verbatim from statute text (Korean Wikisource transcription of 대한민국 상법). The full enumerated list (items 1–12) contains capital, shares, directors and representative director, but NO item for shareholders/members. Canonical source: law.go.kr 상법 제317조.

evidence.basis: Supreme Court Internet Registry Office (IROS) — the public commercial/corporate register maintained by the courts, from which the 등기사항전부증명서 is issued
evidence.note: The register certificate is the third-party-obtainable extract; its content is fixed by Art. 317, so shareholders never appear on it.

caveats: Modern official name is 등기사항전부증명서 (Certificate of All Registered Matters); 등기부등본 is the legacy term still in common use.
IROS site is session/JS-based and could not be fetched verbatim; the registered-matter list is grounded in the verbatim Art. 317 statute text.

documentLabel: Certified copy of the corporate register / Certificate of All Registered Matters
documentLocal: 법인등기사항전부증명서 (구칭 법인등기부등본) — corporate registration certificate from the court commercial registry (IROS, iros.go.kr)
summary: For a Yuhan Hoesa the corporate register certificate is the base register extract. Under Commercial Act Art. 549 the registered matters include trade name, purpose, head-office address, total capital (자본금의 총액), amount per contribution unit (출자1좌의 금액), and the names/resident-registration numbers of directors and the representative director (and auditor if any). It is publicly obtainable from IROS and proves existence, capital and management — but NOT the members (사원).

evidence.basis: 상법 제549조 (유한회사 설립의 등기) — registered matters for a limited company
evidence.quote:
①유한회사의 설립등기는 제548조의 납입 또는 현물출자의 이행이 있은 날로부터 2주간 내에 하여야 한다.
②제1항의 등기에서 다음 각 호의 사항을 등기하여야 한다.
1.  제179조제1호ㆍ제2호 및 제5호에 규정된 사항과 지점을 둔 때에는 그 소재지
2.  제543조제2항제2호와 제3호에 게기한 사항
3.  이사의 성명ㆍ주민등록번호 및 주소. 다만, 회사를 대표할 이사를 정한 때에는 그 외의 이사의 주소를 제외한다.
4.  회사를 대표할 이사를 정한 때에는 그 성명, 주소와 주민등록번호
5.  수인의 이사가 공동으로 회사를 대표할 것을 정한 때에는 그 규정
6.  존립기간 기타의 해산사유를 정한 때에는 그 기간과 사유
7.  감사가 있는 때에는 그 성명 및 주민등록번호
evidence.note: English gloss: incorporation registration within 2 weeks of payment/contribution; registered matters = Art. 179 items 1, 2, 5 (purpose, trade name, head office) plus branches; Art. 543(2) items 2-3 (자본금의 총액 total capital, 출자1좌의 금액 amount per contribution unit); directors' names/RRN/addresses; representative director; joint representation; duration/dissolution grounds; auditor. Existence, capital and management are registered — members are not. Verbatim from law.go.kr (DRF open-API rendering).

evidence.basis: 상업등기법 제15조 제1항 (Commercial Registration Act Art. 15(1)) — anyone may obtain the certificate of registered matters
evidence.quote: ① 누구든지 수수료를 내고 대법원규칙으로 정하는 바에 따라 등기기록에 기록되어 있는 사항의 전부 또는 일부의 열람과 이를 증명하는 등기사항증명서의 발급을 신청할 수 있다. 다만, 등기기록의 부속서류에 대해서는 이해관계 있는 부분만 열람을 신청할 수 있다.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'Any person may, on payment of a fee and as prescribed by Supreme Court Rules, apply to inspect all or part of the matters recorded in the registry record and to be issued a certificate of registered matters (등기사항증명서) evidencing them; provided that annexed documents of the registry record may be inspected only as to parts in which the applicant has an interest.' Public obtainability of the register certificate (issued via IROS, iros.go.kr). Verbatim from law.go.kr.

evidence.basis: Korean-law reference confirming register content for 유한회사
evidence.quote: 사원의 성명은 등기부에 기재되지 않습니다. … 유한회사는 본점 소재지에서 설립등기를 해야 법인격을 취득합니다.
evidence.note: Secondary confirmation (Korean Wikipedia, 유한회사) that members' names are NOT entered in the register; registration (per Art. 549) establishes legal personality and shows capital/directors.

caveats: Modern name 등기사항전부증명서; 등기부등본 is legacy terminology.

Annexed filing documents (등기기록의 부속서류, e.g. the notarized 정관 filed at incorporation) are inspectable only by interested parties (상업등기법 제15조(1) proviso), so the public product is the certificate of registered matters, not the underlying dossier.

summary: NOT provable from the Registry. Cayman is a secrecy jurisdiction for share ownership: the register of members is kept BY THE COMPANY in writing (s.40), NOT filed with the Registrar. For an exempted company it may be kept anywhere in or outside the Islands (s.44(1)); and where the exempted company does not hold a Cayman business licence its register is NOT open to inspection at all (s.44(2)). The public register (Schedule 1A) records only the ORIGINAL subscribers, not current members. The annual return (s.168) is a mere declaration with no member list. The Registry issues no document evidencing current shareholders.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.40(1)
evidence.quote: Every company shall cause to be kept in writing, a register of its members and there shall be entered therein -- (a) the names and addresses of the members of the company, with the addition of, in the case of a company having a capital divided into shares, a statement of the shares held by each member
evidence.note: Register of members is a company-held record, not a Registrar filing.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.44(1)-(2)
evidence.quote: The register of members ... shall be kept at the registered office of the company or, in the case of an exempted company, at any other place within or outside the Islands ... Except in the case of an exempted company that does not hold a licence to carry on business in the Islands ... the register of members shall ... be open to the inspection of any member gratis and to the inspection of any other person on payment of ten dollars
evidence.note: Unlicensed exempted companies are carved out — register not open to inspection even by the public; never held at the Registry.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.168 (annual return of exempted company)
evidence.quote: each exempted company ... shall furnish to the Registrar a return which shall be in the form of a declaration that -- (a) since the previous return or since registration ... there has been no alteration in the memorandum of association
evidence.note: The annual return contains no list of members and no accounts.

caveats: Schedule 1A shows only the subscribers to the memorandum and shares taken at incorporation — founders, not current members.
Beneficial-ownership information is filed (BOTA 2023) but the BO register is NOT public and is distinct from a members list.

summary: NOT provable from the Registry. The register of members is kept BY THE COMPANY in writing (s.40) at its registered office (s.44(1)) and is NOT filed with the Registrar. For an ordinary company it is open to inspection at the registered office (members gratis, others for ten dollars, s.44(2)) — but that is the company's own record, not a registry-issued document. The public register (Schedule 1A) records only original subscribers; no annual filing of current members. The Registry issues no document evidencing current shareholders.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.40(1)
evidence.quote: Every company shall cause to be kept in writing, a register of its members and there shall be entered therein -- (a) the names and addresses of the members of the company
evidence.note: 'Every company' — the members register is company-held for ordinary companies too.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.44(1)-(2)
evidence.quote: The register of members ... shall be kept at the registered office of the company ... open to the inspection of any member gratis and to the inspection of any other person on payment of ten dollars ... for each inspection
evidence.note: Inspection is at the company's registered office, not the Registry.

caveats: Even though an ordinary company's register of members is inspectable at its registered office, that is a company record, not a registry document — under the KYC 'document = registry proof' rule it is not_provable_from_registry.
Schedule 1A public particulars show only subscribers at incorporation, not current members.

documentLabel: Certified copy of Memorandum and Articles of Association
summary: The constitution (Memorandum and Articles of Association) is filed with the Registrar at incorporation: they 'shall be delivered in duplicate to the Registrar who shall file and retain the original' (s.26(1)). The Registry holds the original and issues certified copies — the base document for the constitution. Secrecy caveat: for an exempted company the M&A itself is NOT open to general public inspection (s.26(3A) opens only the Schedule 1A register particulars); certified copies are obtained by or with the company's authority.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.26(1)
evidence.quote: The memorandum of association and the articles of association, if any, shall be delivered in duplicate to the Registrar who shall file and retain the original thereof and shall return the duplicate thereof endorsed with a memorandum of registration
evidence.note: M&A filed and retained by the Registrar; certified copies obtainable.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.26(3A)
evidence.quote: The Registrar shall make the register under subsection (3) available for inspection by any person on payment of the fee specified in Part 1A of Schedule 5
evidence.note: Public inspection is limited to the Schedule 1A register particulars — NOT the M&A document itself.

caveats: Document is definitively filed/retained by the Registrar, but public access is restricted — certified copies are typically obtained by or through the company.
The register holds the AS-FILED M&A plus recorded alterations; confirm currency via the company's consolidated M&A.

documentLabel: Certified copy of Memorandum and Articles of Association
summary: The constitution is delivered to and retained by the Registrar at incorporation (s.26(1)), and the Registry issues certified copies — a registry-evidenced base document. For an ordinary (resident) company the statute opens only the Schedule 1A register particulars to inspection by any person (s.26(3A)); certified copies of the M&A are the reliable proof.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.26(1)
evidence.quote: The memorandum of association and the articles of association, if any, shall be delivered in duplicate to the Registrar who shall file and retain the original thereof
evidence.note: Applies to all companies registered under the Act, including ordinary companies.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.29
evidence.quote: A copy of the memorandum of association having annexed thereto the articles of association
evidence.note: Company must give members a copy; a certified Registrar copy is the KYC-grade proof.

caveats: UNVERIFIED whether an ordinary company's M&A is freely inspectable by the general public at the Registry; the statute expressly opens only the Schedule 1A register particulars (s.26(3A)). Certified copies remain the dependable route.
Confirm currency: the register holds the as-filed M&A plus recorded alterations.

summary: Cayman does NOT issue a comprehensive itemised current register extract. The Registrar keeps a public register (s.26(3), Schedule 1A) recording only incorporation particulars (name, registered-office location, capital/shares, ORIGINAL subscribers, dates, company number, nature of business, FY-end) — not current shareholders. The registry-issued current-status document is the Certificate of Good Standing (s.200A): evidence that the company is in good standing on the date of issue. Base document; proves existence/good standing only, not ownership, capital or management.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.200A(1)-(2)
evidence.quote: The Registrar may on application made by a company issue a certificate of good standing to a company that is in good standing in accordance with subsection (2). A certificate of good standing is evidence of the fact that the company is in good standing on the date that the certificate of good standing is issued.
evidence.note: Registry-issued status snapshot, not an itemised extract. Re-verified verbatim this session against the official consolidated Companies Act (2025 Revision) PDF (CIMA-hosted).

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.26(3A) / Schedule 1A
evidence.quote: The Registrar shall make the register under subsection (3) available for inspection by any person on payment of the fee specified in Part 1A of Schedule 5
evidence.note: Re-verified verbatim this session; s.26(3A) continues 'and the inspection shall be subject to such conditions as the Registrar may impose.' Schedule 1A limits the public register to incorporation particulars — not current members or a consolidated current extract.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) Schedule 1A — Required Particulars (section 26(3))
evidence.quote: The required particulars are — (a) the name of the company and, in the case of an exempted company, the exempted company’s dual foreign name (if any) together with its translated name; (b) the part of the Islands in which the registered office of the company is proposed to be situate; ... (d) the names and addresses of the subscribers to the memorandum and the number of shares taken by each subscriber; ... (i) the nature of the business; and (j) the date of the end of the company’s financial year.
evidence.note: Verbatim from the official PDF this session (ellipses omit items (c) capital/shares, (e)-(h) dates/number/guarantee statement). The public register records ORIGINAL subscribers only — never current shareholders — confirming no itemised current register extract exists.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.55A(2) — Registrar to make list of directors available for inspection
evidence.quote: The Registrar shall make available, the list of the names of the current directors of a company and where applicable, the current alternate directors of a company under subsection (1), for inspection by any person, on payment of the fee
evidence.note: Names-only inspection of current directors — a separate paid inspection, not a certified extract; shareholders remain non-public.

caveats: INFERENCE: mapping the Certificate of Good Standing to 'Registerauszug' is an analogue — Cayman issues no full itemised current extract; the CoGS proves standing only.

The Registrar also maintains a publicly inspectable list of the NAMES of current directors (s.55A, verified verbatim this session in the 2025 Revision), on payment — a separate inspection, not a certified extract. Shareholders remain non-public.

summary: Same regime as for exempted companies. The public register (s.26(3), Schedule 1A) records only incorporation particulars and original subscribers — not current members. The registry-issued current-status document is the Certificate of Good Standing (s.200A). No comprehensive current register extract exists.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.200A(1)-(2)
evidence.quote: The Registrar may on application made by a company issue a certificate of good standing to a company that is in good standing in accordance with subsection (2).
evidence.note: s.200A applies to companies generally (Part 11), not only exempted companies. Re-verified verbatim this session against the official consolidated Companies Act (2025 Revision) PDF (CIMA-hosted); s.200A(2) continues: 'A certificate of good standing is evidence of the fact that the company is in good standing on the date that the certificate of good standing is issued.'

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.26(1),(3),(3A) / Schedule 1A
evidence.quote: A register of companies shall be kept by the Registrar in which shall be entered the particulars set out in Schedule 1A
evidence.note: Public register holds Schedule 1A particulars only. Re-verified verbatim this session; s.26(3A) adds public inspection 'by any person on payment of the fee specified in Part 1A of Schedule 5'.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) Schedule 1A — Required Particulars (section 26(3))
evidence.quote: The required particulars are — (a) the name of the company and, in the case of an exempted company, the exempted company’s dual foreign name (if any) together with its translated name; (b) the part of the Islands in which the registered office of the company is proposed to be situate; ... (d) the names and addresses of the subscribers to the memorandum and the number of shares taken by each subscriber; ... (i) the nature of the business; and (j) the date of the end of the company’s financial year.
evidence.note: Verbatim from the official PDF this session (ellipses omit items (c) capital/shares, (e)-(h) dates/number/guarantee statement). The public register records ORIGINAL subscribers only — never current shareholders — confirming no itemised current register extract exists.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.55A(2) — Registrar to make list of directors available for inspection
evidence.quote: The Registrar shall make available, the list of the names of the current directors of a company and where applicable, the current alternate directors of a company under subsection (1), for inspection by any person, on payment of the fee
evidence.note: Names-only inspection of current directors — a separate paid inspection, not a certified extract; shareholders remain non-public.

caveats: INFERENCE: Certificate of Good Standing mapped to 'Registerauszug' as the nearest registry-issued current document; it does not itemise members, directors or capital.
The Registrar maintains a publicly inspectable list of the NAMES of current directors (s.55A, verified verbatim this session in the 2025 Revision), on payment.

summary: Shareholders (Aktionäre) of a Liechtenstein AG are NOT entered in the Handelsregister and do not appear on the extract. Namenaktien are recorded only in a company-internal Aktienbuch; Inhaberaktien are deposited with a Verwahrer who keeps a private register. Neither is public. The register lists only organs — never the owners.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Aktiengesellschaft, Art. 328 Abs. 2 PGR
evidence.quote: Die Gesellschaft hat über die Eigentümer der Namenaktien (Aktionäre) ein Verzeichnis (Aktienbuch) mit den gesetzlich vorgeschriebenen Angaben zu führen.
evidence.note: Aktienbuch is kept BY the company, not filed with or held by the Handelsregister.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Aktiengesellschaft, Art. 326a/326c PGR (Inhaberaktien)
evidence.quote: Inhaberaktien sind beim Verwahrer zu hinterlegen. Dieser hat ein Register zu führen, in welchem sämtliche gesetzlich vorgesehenen Angaben über den Inhaberaktionär zu registrieren sind.
evidence.note: Verwahrer register is private (accessible to authorities), not the public Handelsregister.

caveats: Liechtenstein is privacy-oriented: AG shareholders are structurally non-public. Ownership reachable only via the non-public beneficial-ownership register (VwbPG) for authorities/obliged entities — never from the commercial register.

summary: An Anstalt has no shareholders; ownership/control sits with the holder(s) of the founder's rights (Inhaber der Gründerrechte) and, economically, the beneficiaries (Destinatäre). Neither is disclosed on the Handelsregister extract — founder rights are commonly held via a trustee, and beneficiary provisions typically sit in non-public Beistatuten. There is no register document evidencing the owners.

evidence.basis: PGR Art. 538 Abs. 1 (Eintragung und Veröffentlichung der Anstalt) — konsolidierte Fassung 01.07.2026, gesetze.li
evidence.quote: In das Handelsregister sind einzutragen und im Auszuge zu veröffentlichen: 1. der Gründungsakt, falls er nicht in den Statuten selbst enthalten ist; 2. das Datum der Statuten; 3. der Name beziehungsweise die Firma und der Sitz der Anstalt; 4. der Gegenstand des Unternehmens beziehungsweise Zweck und gegebenenfalls die Zeitdauer der Anstalt; 5. die Höhe des der Anstalt gewidmeten Fonds, sowie des eingezahlten Betrages oder der eingebrachten sonstigen Vermögenswerte mit ihrem Schätzungswerte; 6. gegebenenfalls besonders für Dritte festgesetzte Genussrechte nebst den Berechtigten; 7. Name, Vorname und Wohnort beziehungsweise Firma und Sitz der Mitglieder der Verwaltung, die Form, in der die Verwaltung ihre Willenserklärungen kundgibt und die Art der Ausübung der Vertretung; 8. die Form, in der die von der Anstalt ausgehenden Bekanntmachungen erfolgen.
evidence.note: Exhaustive list of what is entered in the Handelsregister and published in the extract for an Anstalt: founding act, statutes date, name/seat, purpose, fund, any third-party enjoyment rights, board members, publication form. The holder(s) of the founder's rights (the owners) and any member list are NOT among the registrable items — so ownership is not evidenced by any register document. (Line-wrap hyphenation of the PDF removed.)

evidence.basis: PGR Art. 543 Abs. 1 (Oberstes Organ der Anstalt) — konsolidierte Fassung 01.07.2026, gesetze.li
evidence.quote: Der oder die Inhaber der Gründerrechte bilden das oberste Organ der Anstalt. Die Statuten können auch die Verwaltung mit den Befugnissen des obersten Organes betrauen.
evidence.note: The holder(s) of the founder's rights are the supreme organ (ownership/control position) of the Anstalt — yet they do not appear in the Art. 538 registration list.

evidence.basis: PGR Art. 541 (Gründerrechte) — konsolidierte Fassung 01.07.2026, gesetze.li
evidence.quote: Die einer oder mehreren Personen zustehenden Gründerrechte können abgetreten oder sonst übertragen und vererbt, nicht aber verpfändet oder sonst belastet werden.
evidence.note: Founder's rights pass by assignment, other transfer or inheritance; the statute attaches no Handelsregister registration or filing to such transfers, so changes of ownership never touch the register.

evidence.basis: Official Liechtenstein Handelsregister guidance (Amt für Justiz, Merkblatt zur Anstalt AJU/h70.005.09, 06/2025, Art. 534 bis Art. 551 PGR), section 3
evidence.quote: Die Anstalt kann sowohl stiftungsähnlich, d.h. ohne Mitglieder und ohne in Anteile zerlegtes Kapital, als auch körperschaftsähnlich organisiert sein.
evidence.note: An Anstalt has no members and (in the typical foundation-like form) no share-divided capital; there is accordingly no shareholder/member list to register. Quote re-verified verbatim against the current PDF (downloaded this session).

evidence.basis: Same Merkblatt, section 3 (Organisation)
evidence.quote: Bei einer Anstalt mit Gründerrechten ist in der Regel der oder die Inhaber der Gründerrechte oberstes Organ.
evidence.note: The holder of founder's rights is the beneficial owner but is not an entity entered in the Handelsregister. Quote re-verified verbatim against the current PDF (downloaded this session).

caveats: Edge case (PGR Art. 538 Abs. 1 Ziff. 6): Genussrechte specially established for third parties are registered together with their holders — so enjoyment (not ownership) rights can exceptionally appear on the extract.

If the statutes provide for Anstaltsanteile (PGR Art. 540), the Verwaltung keeps an Anteilbuch under the GmbH rules (Art. 540 Abs. 6 PGR) — an internal book of the Anstalt, not a register document; even in the corporate-like variant the holders are not registry-provable.

summary: KEY POINT: unlike an AG, the members (Gesellschafter) of a Liechtenstein GmbH ARE entered in the Handelsregister together with their Stammeinlagen and therefore appear on the register extract — parallel to Austria/Switzerland. No separate filed Gesellschafterliste is needed; the register extract itself is the proof of current members. The internal Anteilbuch (share ledger) is a company-kept book, not the public proof.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur GmbH, Art. 394 Abs. 1 PGR
evidence.quote: Die Gesellschafter werden samt den gesetzlich erforderlichen Angaben und ihren Stammeinlagen im Handelsregister eingetragen.
evidence.note: The decisive provision: current members are register content, hence on the Auszug.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur GmbH, Art. 402 Abs. 1 PGR (Anteilbuch)
evidence.quote: Über die Stammeinlagen aller Gesellschafter ist ein Anteilbuch zu führen, aus dem die gesetzlichen Angaben zu den Gesellschaftern, der Betrag der übernommenen Einlage sowie jeder Übergang einer Stammeinlage und jede Änderung ersichtlich ist.
evidence.note: The Anteilbuch is an internal book kept by the company — NOT a publicly filed Gesellschafterliste; the public proof of members is the register entry/extract.

caveats: Beneficial ownership (>25%) beyond the registered legal members is held in the separate non-public beneficial-ownership register (VwbPG), not the commercial register.
The register shows legal members and their Stammeinlagen; it does not show economic/fiduciary arrangements behind a registered member.

summary: A Stiftung has no shareholders/members; the relevant persons are the founder (Stifter) and the beneficiaries (Begünstigte). These are NOT public. Beneficiaries are defined in the Stiftungsurkunde or a Stiftungszusatzurkunde (Beistatut), which for private-benefit foundations are not publicly accessible; the founder is likewise not disclosed via the register. No register document evidences owners/beneficiaries.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Stiftung, Art. 552 §16 Abs. 1 Ziff. 4 PGR (Begünstigte)
evidence.quote: Bei der privatnützigen Stiftung muss der in der Stiftungsurkunde durch den Stifter festzulegende Zweck auch die Bezeichnung der konkreten oder nach objektiven Merkmalen individualisierbaren Begünstigten oder des Begünstigtenkreises umfassen, sofern nicht stattdessen ausdrücklich auf eine Stiftungszusatzurkunde verwiesen wird, welche dies regelt.
evidence.note: Beneficiaries sit in the Stiftungsurkunde/Beistatut, not part of the publicly accessible register for private-benefit foundations.

caveats: Liechtenstein privacy core: Stifter and Begünstigte are never public. Reachable, if at all, only via the non-public VwbPG beneficial-ownership register for authorities/obliged entities.

summary: The AG's Statuten are a mandatory formation document filed with the Handelsregister. Not printed on the extract, but retrievable from the public register file (Art. 953 PGR public-access regime for AG).

evidence.basis: Official Liechtenstein Handelsregister guidance (Amt für Justiz, Merkblatt zur Aktiengesellschaft, Art. 261–366 PGR)
evidence.quote: Die Statuten der Aktiengesellschaft müssen die gesetzlich erforderlichen Angaben und Bestimmungen enthalten.
evidence.note: The Statuten are the AG's constitutive instrument (PGR Art. 279, cited in the Merkblatt).

evidence.basis: Same Merkblatt, section 2 (Errichtung)
evidence.quote: Die Gründung der Aktiengesellschaft bedarf der öffentlichen Beurkundung.
evidence.note: Incorporation is by notarial deed and the Statuten are lodged with the Handelsregister, obtainable as a separate document rather than reproduced on the register extract.

caveats: Source is registry-issued guidance identifying the Statuten as the mandatory constitutive document, not the PGR statute text itself.

summary: The Anstalt is formed by written Statuten signed by all founders and filed with the Handelsregister; the Statuten are retrievable from the public register file (Art. 953 public-access regime covers Anstalten). Beistatuten (which may name beneficiaries) are NOT publicly accessible.

evidence.basis: Official Liechtenstein Handelsregister guidance (Amt für Justiz, Merkblatt zur Anstalt, Art. 534–551 PGR), section 2 (Errichtung)
evidence.quote: Zur Gründung der Anstalt bedarf es schriftlicher und von sämtlichen Gründern unterzeichneter Statuten.
evidence.note: Written Statuten signed by all founders are required to form the Anstalt.

evidence.basis: Same Merkblatt, section 4 (Statuten der Anstalt)
evidence.quote: Die Statuten der Anstalt müssen die gesetzlich erforderlichen Angaben und Bestimmungen enthalten.
evidence.note: The Statuten are the Anstalt's constitutive document, obtainable separately from the register extract.

caveats: Registry-issued guidance, not the PGR statute text itself.

summary: The GmbH's Statuten (Gesellschaftsvertrag) are filed with the Handelsregister at formation. They are a supporting document (Beleg) in the Handelsregisterakt, not printed on the extract, but retrievable: since 1 Jan 2017 the register file is public for GmbH/AG/Anstalt without proof of interest (Art. 953 PGR).

evidence.basis: Official Liechtenstein Handelsregister guidance (Amt für Justiz, Merkblatt zur GmbH, Art. 389–427 PGR), section 4.1
evidence.quote: Die Statuten (Gesellschaftsvertrag) der GmbH müssen die gesetzlich erforderlichen Angaben und Bestimmungen enthalten.
evidence.note: The Merkblatt expressly equates the Statuten with the Gesellschaftsvertrag as the GmbH's constitutive document (PGR Art. 390 Abs. 2).

evidence.basis: Same Merkblatt, section 2 (Errichtung)
evidence.quote: Die Gründung der GmbH bedarf der öffentlichen Beurkundung.
evidence.note: Incorporation by notarial deed; the Statuten/Gesellschaftsvertrag are lodged with the Handelsregister and obtainable as a separate document.

caveats: Registry-issued guidance, not the PGR statute text itself. Note the same Merkblatt also states GmbH members and their Stammeinlagen are entered in the Handelsregister (Art. 394 Abs. 1 PGR) — relevant to the separate GESELLSCHAFTERLISTE question but not to this Gesellschaftsvertrag row.

summary: The foundation's constitution is the Stiftungsurkunde (Statut). It is a mandatory document, but for the typical (private-benefit/deposited) foundation it is subject to the restricted-public principle and is NOT freely retrievable from the register — unlike GmbH/AG/Anstalt Statuten. Treat as not obtainable as a public document by default.

evidence.basis: PGR Art. 955a Abs. 1 (Öffentlichkeit bei Hinterlegungen) — konsolidierte Fassung 01.07.2026, gesetze.li
evidence.quote: Einsichtnahme, Auszüge, Abschriften oder Zeugnisse von gemäss Art. 990 hinterlegten Akten und Schriftstücken sowie von Anmeldungen und Belegen nicht im Handelsregister eingetragener Stiftungen und Treuhänderschaften können nur vom Hinterleger und demjenigen, der hierzu ermächtigt ist, verlangt werden.
evidence.note: Direct statutory rule: inspection, extracts and copies of deposited files of NON-registered foundations may be requested only by the depositor or a person authorised by him — i.e. the foundation documents of the typical (deposited) private-benefit Stiftung are not obtainable by third parties from the registry. (Line-wrap hyphenation of the PDF removed.)

evidence.basis: PGR Art. 552 § 14 Abs. 5 (Errichtung; keine Eintragungspflicht privatnütziger Stiftungen) — konsolidierte Fassung 01.07.2026, gesetze.li
evidence.quote: Andere privatnützige Stiftungen können in das Handelsregister eingetragen werden. Eine Rechtspflicht besteht jedoch nicht.
evidence.note: Private-benefit foundations (other than those running a commercial business on a special statutory basis) are not obliged to register — the default case therefore leaves no register file containing the Stiftungsurkunde.

evidence.basis: PGR Art. 552 § 20 Abs. 1 und 2 (Hinterlegung der Gründungsanzeige) — konsolidierte Fassung 01.07.2026, gesetze.li
evidence.quote: Die Gründungsanzeige hat folgende Angaben zu enthalten: 1. Name der Stiftung; 2. Sitz der Stiftung; 3. Zweck der Stiftung; 4. Datum der Errichtung der Stiftung; 5. Dauer der Stiftung, falls diese begrenzt ist;
evidence.note: For non-registered foundations only a Gründungsanzeige with enumerated DATA items (name, seat, purpose, date, duration, foundation council, representative, confirmations) is deposited — the Stiftungsurkunde itself is not among the deposited items, so the deed never reaches the registry file in the default case. (Quote is the head of the § 20 Abs. 2 list, items 1-5; items 6-11 are further data/confirmations.)

evidence.basis: PGR Art. 552 § 19 Abs. 1 (Eintragung ins Handelsregister; Beilage der Stiftungsurkunde) — konsolidierte Fassung 01.07.2026, gesetze.li
evidence.quote: Die Anmeldung ist unter Beilage des Originals oder einer beglaubigten Abschrift der Stiftungsurkunde schriftlich einzureichen.
evidence.note: Counter-limit documented for precision: for foundations that ARE registered (mandatorily the gemeinnützige), the Stiftungsurkunde is filed with the registration application, and under Art. 953 Abs. 1 PGR ('Das Handelsregister mit Einschluss der Anmeldungen und der Belege ist öffentlich.') the register including supporting documents is public — so for registered foundations the deed is in principle accessible. The not-provable status holds for the typical non-registered private-benefit foundation.

evidence.basis: Official Liechtenstein Handelsregister guidance (Amt für Justiz, Merkblatt zur Stiftung AJU/h70.008.10, 07/2026, Art. 552 §§ 1-41 PGR), section 4
evidence.quote: Die Stiftungsurkunde (Statut) der Stiftung muss die gesetzlich erforderlichen Angaben und Bestimmungen enthalten.
evidence.note: The Stiftungsurkunde (Statut) is the constitutive document of the foundation (PGR Art. 552 § 16). Quote re-verified verbatim against the current PDF (downloaded this session).

evidence.basis: Same Merkblatt, section 2 (Errichtung)
evidence.quote: Unterliegt die Stiftung keiner Eintragungspflicht, ist binnen 30 Tagen ab Errichtung der Stiftung eine Gründungsanzeige beim Amt für Justiz zu hinterlegen.
evidence.note: Non-registrable (typically privatnützige) foundations only deposit a Gründungsanzeige rather than being entered in the Handelsregister, so the Stiftungsurkunde is not obtainable as a public register document. Quote re-verified verbatim against the current PDF (downloaded this session).

caveats: Nuanced: gemeinnützige (charitable) foundations must be entered in the Handelsregister (PGR Art. 552 § 14 Abs. 4), the Stiftungsurkunde is filed with the Anmeldung (§ 19 Abs. 1) and register files including Belege are public (Art. 953 PGR) — so for those (and for voluntarily registered private foundations) the Stiftungsurkunde is in principle obtainable; the not-provable status holds for the default, non-registered privatnützige Stiftung.
Beistatuten (Stiftungszusatzurkunden) and Reglemente, which typically contain beneficiary details, are not deposited at all and remain private in every variant.

summary: The AG must be registered in the Handelsregister and acquires legal personality only upon registration; the extract evidences firm, seat, capital, and the registered organs (Verwaltungsrat, Revisionsstelle, any Aufsichtsrat, Verwahrer, Repräsentanz).

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Aktiengesellschaft (Amt für Justiz, AJU/h70.004.07, 07/2025), Art. 261 ff. PGR
evidence.quote: Die Aktiengesellschaft muss zwingend im Handelsregister eingetragen werden.

caveats: Register is public for AG; extracts issued in certified form.

summary: The Anstalt (establishment) must be registered in the Handelsregister and acquires legal personality only on registration; an extract exists showing firm, seat, capital and the Verwaltung/Repräsentanz.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Anstalt (Amt für Justiz, AJU/h70.005.09, 06/2025), Art. 534 ff. PGR
evidence.quote: Die Anstalt muss zwingend im Handelsregister eingetragen werden.

caveats: Extract shows the administrative organ (Verwaltung), not the economic controllers.

summary: The GmbH must be entered in the Liechtenstein Handelsregister and acquires legal personality only upon registration; the current register extract (Handelsregisterauszug) is the base document evidencing existence, firm, seat, capital, management (Geschäftsführer) and representation.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur GmbH (Amt für Justiz, AJU/h70.007.09, 07/2025), Art. 389 f. PGR
evidence.quote: Die GmbH muss zwingend im Handelsregister eingetragen werden.
evidence.note: Rechtspersönlichkeit mit Eintragung (Art. 390 Abs. 1 PGR).

caveats: Certified extracts (beglaubigt) are the standard product; the register (handelsregister.li / oera.li) is public for GmbH.

summary: Split regime: gemeinnützige (charitable) foundations MUST be registered and have a register extract; privatnützige (private-benefit, e.g. family) foundations are usually only DEPOSITED (Gründungsanzeige hinterlegt) and are not entered in the register — for those there is no substantive public extract. Even for registered foundations the 'restricted public principle' limits public access.

evidence.basis: PGR Art. 552 § 14 Abs. 4 (Eintragungspflicht) — konsolidierte Fassung 01.07.2026, gesetze.li
evidence.quote: Gemeinnützige Stiftungen und privatnützige Stiftungen, die auf spezialgesetzlicher Grundlage ein nach kaufmännischer Art geführtes Gewerbe betreiben, sind in das Handelsregister einzutragen und erlangen durch die Eintragung das Recht der Persönlichkeit.
evidence.note: Charitable foundations (and commercially active private ones on a special statutory basis) MUST be entered in the Handelsregister — a register entry, and hence an extract, exists for them. (Line-wrap hyphenation of the PDF removed.)

evidence.basis: PGR Art. 552 § 19 Abs. 3 (Inhalt der Eintragung) — konsolidierte Fassung 01.07.2026, gesetze.li
evidence.quote: Die Eintragung hat folgende Angaben zu enthalten: 1. Name bzw. Firma der Stiftung; 2. Sitz der Stiftung; 3. Zweck der Stiftung; 4. Datum der Errichtung der Stiftung; 5. Dauer der Stiftung, falls diese begrenzt ist; 6. Organisation und Vertretung, wobei Name, Vorname, Geburtsdatum, Staatsbürgerschaft und Wohnsitz oder Kanzleisitz bzw. Firma und Sitz der Mitglieder des Stiftungsrats sowie die Art der Zeichnung anzugeben sind;
evidence.note: Statutory content of the register entry for a registered foundation (items 7-9 continue with Revisionsstelle, Repräsentant, supervision status) — this is the substance the Handelsregisterauszug evidences.

evidence.basis: PGR Art. 954 Abs. 1 (Auszüge, Abschriften und Zeugnisse) — konsolidierte Fassung 01.07.2026, gesetze.li
evidence.quote: Das Amt für Justiz hat auf Verlangen gegen Gebühr von den Einträgen und Registerakten Auszüge, Kopien oder Abschriften in elektronischer Form oder auf ausdrückliches Begehren in Papierform auszustellen.
evidence.note: The Amt für Justiz issues extracts from entries and register files on request against a fee — the Handelsregisterauszug is an obtainable base document for any registered foundation.

evidence.basis: Official Liechtenstein Handelsregister guidance (Amt für Justiz, Merkblatt zur Stiftung AJU/h70.008.10, 07/2026, Art. 552 §§ 1-41 PGR), section 1
evidence.quote: Gemeinnützige Stiftungen müssen zwingend im Handelsregister eingetragen werden; privatnützige Stiftungen können sich freiwillig im Handelsregister eintragen lassen, hingegen müssen sie im Handelsregister eingetragen werden, wenn sie auf spezialgesetzlicher Grundlage ein nach kaufmännischer Art geführtes Gewerbe betreiben.
evidence.note: A registered foundation has a Handelsregister entry from which a Handelsregisterauszug can be issued (mandatory for charitable foundations, voluntary for private ones). Quote re-verified verbatim against the current PDF (downloaded this session).

caveats: A Handelsregisterauszug is available only for foundations that are actually registered; privatnützige foundations without registration duty (PGR Art. 552 § 14 Abs. 5) leave only a deposited Gründungsanzeige, whose file is accessible only to the depositor/authorised persons (Art. 955a PGR) — no public extract exists for them (third parties can obtain at most the Amtsbestätigung data under Art. 955a in conjunction with Art. 552 § 20 Abs. 2 Ziff. 1-7 und 10 PGR).

summary: For an AB (public company), shares are dematerialised registered securities recorded in personal securities accounts kept by account managers (banks/brokerage firms) and the central securities depository (Nasdaq CSD), not in the Register of Legal Entities. The register does not hold the AB's shareholder list, so no registry document evidences it; the shareholder register is maintained in the securities-accounts system outside Registrų centras. The one narrow exception is a sole-shareholder AB, whose single shareholder is registered in JAR.

evidence.basis: Akcinių bendrovių įstatymas (Law on Companies, VIII-1835), 40 str. 7 d. — suvestinė redakcija nuo 2026-07-01, e-seimas
evidence.quote: Akcinių bendrovių akcijos gali būti tik nematerialios.
evidence.note: AB (public limited company) shares may ONLY be dematerialised (uncertificated) securities.

evidence.basis: Akcinių bendrovių įstatymas, 41 str. 1 ir 2 d. (Akcininkų asmeninių vertybinių popierių sąskaitų tvarkymas) — suvestinė redakcija nuo 2026-07-01, e-seimas
evidence.quote: 1. Bendrovės nematerialios akcijos yra fiksuojamos įrašais akcininkų asmeninėse vertybinių popierių sąskaitose. 2. Akcinės bendrovės akcininkų asmeninės vertybinių popierių sąskaitos tvarkomos vertybinių popierių rinką reglamentuojančių teisės aktų nustatyta tvarka.
evidence.note: Dematerialised shares are recorded by entries in shareholders' personal securities accounts; for an AB those accounts are administered under securities-market legislation (account managers / CSD system) — i.e. shareholder records live in the securities-accounts system, not in the Register of Legal Entities, and no registry document evidences the AB shareholder list.

evidence.basis: Akcinių bendrovių įstatymas, 41-1 str. 1 d. (Juridinių asmenų dalyvių informacinės sistemos duomenys) — suvestinė redakcija nuo 2026-07-01, e-seimas
evidence.quote: Juridinių asmenų dalyvių informacinės sistemos tvarkytojui teikiami šie duomenys apie uždarosios akcinės bendrovės akcininkus:
evidence.note: Negative proof: the JADIS filing duty in the Law on Companies is expressly limited to UAB (private company) shareholders — the law imposes no shareholder-data filing to JADIS/the registrar for an AB.

evidence.basis: Akcinių bendrovių įstatymas, 12 str. 1 d. 4 p. (Juridinių asmenų registro duomenys) — suvestinė redakcija nuo 2026-07-01, e-seimas
evidence.quote: duomenys apie bendrovės akcininką, tapatūs duomenims, nurodytiems šio Įstatymo 7 straipsnio 21 dalyje, visų akcijų įgijimo ir visų ar dalies akcijų perleidimo datos (steigiant bendrovę visų akcijų įgijimo data laikoma bendrovės įregistravimo juridinių asmenų registre data), kai bendrovės akcininkas yra vienas asmuo;
evidence.note: The one narrow exception verbatim: JAR (Register of Legal Entities) records data about the company's shareholder only 'kai bendrovės akcininkas yra vienas asmuo' — when there is a single shareholder.

caveats: Exception (now verbatim-grounded, ABĮ 12 str. 1 d. 4 p.): a single-shareholder AB has its sole shareholder registered in JAR, which is registry-provable for that case.

Which institutions actually keep AB securities accounts (account managers/Nasdaq CSD) follows from the securities-market legislation referenced by ABĮ 41 str. 2 d.; that downstream layer was not fetched verbatim — operationally undisputed.

documentLabel: List of shareholders / participant data from JADIS (or sole-shareholder registration in JAR)
documentLocal: Akcininkų (dalyvių) sąrašas — JADIS duomenys / vienintelio akcininko registravimas JAR
summary: A UAB's shareholders (akcininkai) are not on the ordinary JAR extract, but shareholder/participant data is filed with Registrų centras: a sole shareholder must be registered in the Register of Legal Entities itself, and since the 2018–2019 reforms every private legal entity including a UAB must submit and keep current its participant (shareholder) data in JADIS (Juridinių asmenų dalyvių informacinė sistema — Information System of Participants of Legal Entities), operated by Registrų centras. Registrų centras can issue this participant data as a separate deliverable. It is therefore provable via an additional document, subject to the access and format caveats below.

evidence.basis: Akcinių bendrovių įstatymas (Law on Companies, VIII-1835), 41-1 str. 1 d. (Juridinių asmenų dalyvių informacinės sistemos duomenys) — suvestinė redakcija nuo 2026-07-01, e-seimas
evidence.quote: Juridinių asmenų dalyvių informacinės sistemos tvarkytojui teikiami šie duomenys apie uždarosios akcinės bendrovės akcininkus: 1) duomenys apie akcininką, tapatūs duomenims, nurodytiems šio Įstatymo 7 straipsnio 21 dalyje; 2) akcininkui nuosavybės teise priklausančių akcijų skaičius, įskaitant akcijų skaičių pagal klases, jeigu yra skirtingų klasių akcijų, akcijos nominali vertė;
evidence.note: Statutory duty to submit UAB shareholder data — identity of each shareholder and the number/class/nominal value of their shares (further items: acquisitions, transfers, cancellations, conversions) — to the manager of JADIS (the Information System of Participants of Legal Entities, operated by Registrų centras).

evidence.basis: Akcinių bendrovių įstatymas, 41-1 str. 2 d. — suvestinė redakcija nuo 2026-07-01, e-seimas
evidence.quote: Pasikeitus uždarosios akcinės bendrovės akcininkams ar jų duomenims, duomenys apie uždarosios akcinės bendrovės akcininkus Juridinių asmenų dalyvių informacinės sistemos tvarkytojui pateikiami ne vėliau kaip per 5 dienas nuo dokumentų, kurių pagrindu daromi įrašai uždarosios akcinės bendrovės akcininkų – nematerialių akcijų savininkų – asmeninių vertybinių popierių sąskaitose ar materialių akcijų savininkų registravimo žurnale, gavimo dienos.
evidence.note: Currency guarantee: any change of UAB shareholders or their data must be filed to JADIS within 5 days — the registrar-held participant data tracks the current shareholders.

evidence.basis: Akcinių bendrovių įstatymas, 41-1 str. 5 d. — suvestinė redakcija nuo 2026-07-01, e-seimas
evidence.quote: Juridinių asmenų dalyvių informacinėje sistemoje kaupiama informacija turintiems teisę ją gauti fiziniams ir juridiniams asmenims teikiama už atlyginimą, išskyrus atvejus, kai ji:
evidence.note: The information accumulated in JADIS is provided (for a fee) to natural and legal persons 'turintiems teisę ją gauti' — those entitled to receive it — confirming both that the registrar issues the data as a deliverable and that access is conditioned on entitlement.

evidence.basis: Akcinių bendrovių įstatymas, 12 str. 1 d. 4 p. (Juridinių asmenų registro duomenys) — suvestinė redakcija nuo 2026-07-01, e-seimas
evidence.quote: duomenys apie bendrovės akcininką, tapatūs duomenims, nurodytiems šio Įstatymo 7 straipsnio 21 dalyje, visų akcijų įgijimo ir visų ar dalies akcijų perleidimo datos (steigiant bendrovę visų akcijų įgijimo data laikoma bendrovės įregistravimo juridinių asmenų registre data), kai bendrovės akcininkas yra vienas asmuo;
evidence.note: Sole-shareholder case: the single shareholder is registered in JAR itself (per 12 str. 5 d. the UAB files these data via the JADIS manager, which forwards them to the JAR registrar within one working day).

evidence.basis: Registrų centras — JADIS, Information System of Participants of Legal Entities (Juridinių asmenų dalyvių informacinė sistema)
evidence.note: Statutory obligation for private legal entities (incl. UAB) to submit participant/shareholder data to JADIS; registrar can issue that data.

caveats: 'DOCUMENT vs data' judgement call (deliberately left open, per row status): ABĮ 41-1 str. consistently speaks of 'duomenys' (data) submitted to and 'informacija' issued from JADIS, not of a filed shareholder-list document — the statutory language leans toward a data extract; whether Registrų centras' deliverable for a multi-shareholder UAB is a formal 'akcininkų sąrašas' document or a data export should still be confirmed operationally.

Access to full multi-shareholder JADIS data is conditioned on entitlement (ABĮ 41-1 str. 5 d.: 'turintiems teisę ją gauti'); the always-public case is the sole-shareholder entry in JAR (12 str. 1 d. 4 p.).

Whether a proof exists at all for a given UAB depends on the case: sole-shareholder = clearly provable from JAR; multi-shareholder = provable only to the extent JADIS data is accessible/issuable to the requester.

summary: An AB's constitution is its articles of association (įstatai), the mandatory constituent document under the Law on Companies (Art. 4). As for a UAB, the articles are filed with the Register of Legal Entities, stored by the register, and copies are publicly obtainable — a distinct filed document separate from the extract. The constitution kind is therefore provable via an additional registry document.

evidence.basis: Law on Companies (Akcinių bendrovių įstatymas), Art. 4 (articles of association / įstatai filed with the register); Civil Code Art. 2.46
evidence.note: Same articles-filing regime as UAB; verbatim not fetchable.

evidence.basis: Registrų centras — founding documents / articles of association available from JAR
evidence.note: Registrar confirms įstatai are obtainable from the register.

caveats: UNVERIFIED at quote level, but filing and public availability of AB įstatai is settled.
Classified as additional (not base) document — a separately ordered filed document distinct from the JAR extract.

summary: A UAB's constitution is its articles of association (įstatai). Under the Law on Companies (Art. 4) and the Civil Code, the articles are the mandatory constituent document, are filed with the Register of Legal Entities, and the register stores them and issues copies to the public. They are a distinct filed document (not part of the ordinary extract), so the constitution kind is provable via an additional registry document.

evidence.basis: Law on Companies (Akcinių bendrovių įstatymas), Art. 4 (articles of association / įstatai as the constituent document filed with the register); Civil Code Art. 2.46
evidence.note: Articles are the constituent document filed with and stored by JAR; copies publicly obtainable. Verbatim text not fetchable.

evidence.basis: Registrų centras — founding documents / articles of association (steigimo dokumentai, įstatai) available from JAR
evidence.note: Registrar English page confirms founding documents including įstatai are obtainable.

caveats: UNVERIFIED at quote level (no verbatim text retrieved), but filing and public availability of įstatai is a settled feature of Lithuanian company registration.
Classified as additional (not base) document because the articles are a separately ordered filed document, distinct from the JAR extract.

summary: An AB (public limited company) is registered in the same Register of Legal Entities as a UAB. Registrų centras issues an official extract (išrašas) with the AB's core registered data. This extract is the base registry document and directly evidences the register-extract kind.

evidence.basis: Civil Code Art. 2.66 and Regulations of the Register of Legal Entities (data and extracts for all registered legal entities incl. AB)
evidence.note: Same registration/extract regime applies to AB; verbatim not fetchable.

evidence.basis: Registrų centras — Register of Legal Entities, extracts (išrašai)
evidence.note: Extracts are the primary orderable document for any registered entity.

caveats: UNVERIFIED at quote level, but existence of a JAR extract for any AB is effectively certain.

summary: The Register of Legal Entities (Juridinių asmenų registras, JAR), operated by Registrų centras, is the authoritative register for every UAB. It issues an official extract (išrašas) as a downloadable document containing the company's core registered data (name, code, address, legal form, managers, authorised capital, status). This extract is the base registry document and directly evidences the register-extract kind.

evidence.basis: Civil Code of the Republic of Lithuania (Civilinis kodeksas), Art. 2.66 (data registered in the Register of Legal Entities) and Regulations of the Register of Legal Entities
evidence.note: Enumerates the data held in JAR and the register's duty to issue extracts; verbatim text not fetchable via the JS-rendered e-seimas portal.

evidence.basis: Registrų centras — Register of Legal Entities, extracts (išrašai)
evidence.note: Official registrar page confirming extracts are the primary orderable document.

caveats: Verbatim statutory/registrar quotes could not be retrieved (JS-rendered sources; WebSearch budget exhausted) — treated as UNVERIFIED at the quote level, though the existence of a JAR extract for any UAB is effectively certain.

summary: Shareholders are not filed. The share register is kept at the registered office.

evidence.basis: Loi du 10 août 1915 concernant les sociétés commerciales, art. 39 (texte original, Mémorial n° 90 du 30 octobre 1915, Legilux)
evidence.quote: Il est tenu au siège social un registre des actions nominatives dont tout actionnaire pourra prendre connaissance; ce registre contient: la désignation précise de chaque actionnaire et l'indication du nombre de ses actions ou coupures; l'indication des versements effectués; les transferts avec leur date ou la conversion des actions en titres au porteur, si les statuts l'autorisent.
evidence.note: The register of registered shares — the only record naming each shareholder and their holdings — is kept AT THE REGISTERED OFFICE and is open to shareholders ('tout actionnaire'), not to the public; it is not filed with the RCS. (Line breaks of the Mémorial column layout joined.)
