evidence.basis: Loi du 10 août 1915, art. 40 (texte original, Mémorial n° 90 du 30 octobre 1915, Legilux)
evidence.quote: La propriété de l'action nominative s'établit par une inscription sur le registre prescrit par l'article précédent.
evidence.note: Ownership of a registered share is established by entry in that internal company-kept register — ownership proof lives at the company seat, not in any registry document.

evidence.basis: Guichet.lu — société anonyme
evidence.quote: Un registre des actions nominatives qui établit la propriété est tenu au siège social.

caveats: Numbering: the verbatim quotes are from the original 1915 Mémorial text (art. 39-40); in the current coordinated numbering of the loi de 1915 (règlement grand-ducal du 5 décembre 2017) these provisions live in art. 430-3 ff. with the same substance (as the current-practice guichet.lu quote confirms). The current consolidated Legilux text could not be fetched this session (SPA-only site; consolidated-PDF probes returned 404).
UNVERIFIED: whether an SA's sole shareholder must be disclosed (EU 12th Directive / §42 AktG analogue). No source found. Treat as unclear.

summary: Members are mandatory public RCS content and appear in the extract itself. Kept current: share transfers must be filed and published.

evidence.basis: Loi du 19 décembre 2002 (RCS)
evidence.quote: l'identité des associés, leur adresse privée ou professionnelle précise et le nombre de parts sociales détenues par chacun

evidence.basis: Cession de parts — RCS/RESA publication
evidence.note: Share transfers must be notarised or by private deed, then filed with the RCS and published via RESA, so the associés data stays current. Contrasted explicitly with the SA, where transfers are not visible to third parties.

caveats: Delivered as extract content, not as a discrete list document.

summary: A joint-stock company's shareholders are NOT recorded in the Enterprise Register. Registered-share holders are kept in the company's own shareholders register maintained by its management board, and dematerialised/publicly-traded shares are recorded with Nasdaq CSD (the Latvian central securities depository). The Enterprise Register's public open data lists SIA members but contains no AS shareholder list, so no shareholder-list document is obtainable from the registry.

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice portal — Business registers in Latvia (open data list)
evidence.quote: data on members of private limited liability companies (first name, surname, part of the personal identity number, number of shares owned by them and the nominal value of those shares)
evidence.note: Open data is limited to SIA members; joint-stock company shareholders are conspicuously absent — negative evidence that the registry does not hold the AS shareholder list.

evidence.basis: Commercial Law (Komerclikums) — joint-stock company share/shareholder provisions (Sec. ~234–235) and Financial Instruments Market Law
evidence.note: AS shareholders register held by the company (registered shares) or Nasdaq CSD (dematerialised/listed shares); statute text not fetchable verbatim.

caveats: INFERENCE/UNVERIFIED verbatim: the specific Commercial Law sections placing the AS shareholder register with the company/Nasdaq CSD were not fetchable; conclusion rests on domain knowledge plus the negative open-data evidence.
A private per-issuer shareholder list may exist at the company or Nasdaq CSD, but it is not a public registry document.

summary: Latvia is notable for making the SIA member list public: since amendments to the Commercial Law in force from 1 July 2021, an SIA's register of members (dalībnieku reģistrs) is filed with and held by the Enterprise Register and is publicly available, listing each member, their number of shares and nominal value. It is a distinct filed document in the registration file (and the same member data is also exposed as free open data). It therefore evidences the German 'Gesellschafterliste' equivalent.

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice portal — Business registers in Latvia (open data list)
evidence.quote: data on members of private limited liability companies (first name, surname, part of the personal identity number, number of shares owned by them and the nominal value of those shares)
evidence.note: Member/shareholder data of the SIA is public in the Enterprise Register — the distinguishing Latvian feature.

evidence.basis: Commercial Law (Komerclikums), Section 10(2) — signatures required for Commercial Register filings
evidence.note: The fetched English text references notarised signatures 'on the division of the register of shareholders (stockholders) of a capital company', confirming the register of shareholders is a Commercial Register document. Section 187 (register of members) itself could not be fetched verbatim.

caveats: INFERENCE/UNVERIFIED verbatim: the exact statutory mechanics (Commercial Law Sec. 186–187, and the 1 July 2021 amendment moving the dalībnieku reģistrs to the Enterprise Register) are asserted from domain knowledge plus the open-data evidence; the section text itself was not fetchable.

Base-vs-additional is arguable: because current member data now also appears on the register extract/open data, this could alternatively be scored 'proved_by_base_document'. Scored 'additional' because the dedicated dalībnieku reģistrs is a separate document in the file.

summary: The AS's articles of association (statūti) are filed with the Enterprise Register on incorporation and any amendment (with the full amended text) must be submitted to the Commercial Register. Copies from the public section of the registration file are obtainable online free of charge. The statūti is a separate document from the register extract, proving the constitution via an additional document.

evidence.basis: Commercial Law (Komerclikums), Section 9(2)
evidence.quote: After making amendments to the documents of incorporation of a capital company (memorandum of association, articles of association), the text of the amendments and also the full text of the document as amended must be submitted to the Commercial Register.
evidence.note: 'Capital company' covers the AS (akciju sabiedrība); articles are a filed Commercial Register document.

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice portal — Business registers in Latvia
evidence.quote: The Register also issues copies of documents from the registration file of a legal entity
evidence.note: Articles are part of the public registration file.

caveats: AS-specific articles section (Sec. ~144 applied via Sec. 226 et seq.) not fetched verbatim; grounded in the general Section 9(2) capital-company rule.

summary: The SIA's articles of association (statūti) are filed with the Enterprise Register on incorporation and any amendment (with the full amended text) must be submitted to the Commercial Register. Copies of documents from the public section of the registration file are obtainable online free of charge. The statūti is a separate document from the register extract, so it proves the constitution via an additional document.

evidence.basis: Commercial Law (Komerclikums), Section 9(2)
evidence.quote: After making amendments to the documents of incorporation of a capital company (memorandum of association, articles of association), the text of the amendments and also the full text of the document as amended must be submitted to the Commercial Register.
evidence.note: Establishes that articles of association are filed with the Commercial Register (applies to both SIA and AS as capital companies).

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice portal — Business registers in Latvia
evidence.quote: The Register also issues copies of documents from the registration file of a legal entity
evidence.note: Articles are part of the registration file; public-part documents are free and immediate.

caveats: Section 144 (articles of an SIA) was not fetchable verbatim; the filing/public status is grounded in the general Section 9(2) capital-company rule plus the register's document-issuance practice.

summary: The AS is entered in the Enterprise Register, which issues an official extract of its registered data (name, registration number, legal form, registered office, share capital, board and council members). This extract is the base register document. Note it does not contain the shareholder list.

evidence.basis: Enterprise Register (ur.gov.lv) / EU e-Justice portal
evidence.quote: Documents from the public section of the registration file ... may be obtained online from the Enterprise Register's information website free of charge and immediately.
evidence.note: Same public register and extract mechanism applies to all capital companies including the AS.

caveats: Verbatim statutory wording for the AS extract was not fetched; grounded in the register's general practice.

summary: The SIA is entered in the Enterprise Register (Uzņēmumu reģistrs), which issues an official extract of its registered data (name, registration number, legal form, registered office, share capital, board members and, since the 2021 reform, its members). Documents and open data from the public part of the register are available online free of charge and immediately. This extract is the base register document.

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice portal — Business registers in Latvia
evidence.quote: Documents from the public section of the registration file ... may be obtained online from the Enterprise Register's information website free of charge and immediately.
evidence.note: Confirms the public register issues data and documents for registered entities.

evidence.basis: Enterprise Register (ur.gov.lv) — official site
evidence.note: Register maintains and publishes company data (legal form, seat, capital, representatives); current and historical data plus documents can be requested from the public part.

caveats: Local label 'Izziņa no Uzņēmumu reģistra' is the standard extract term; verbatim statutory wording for the extract was not fetched (likumi.lv English page truncates before the capital-company sections).

documentLocal: annual return (Seventh Schedule, Part 3 — List of Past and Present Members)
summary: CONFIRMED: Malta's annual return IS a full shareholder list and IS public. Art.184 requires every company, on each anniversary of registration, to file an annual return in the form of the Seventh Schedule. Part 3 is the 'List of Past and Present Members' giving each member's name, address and number of shares held. It is a document kept by the Registrar and thus inspectable/obtainable by any person under art.424. The founding shareholders are additionally named as subscribers in the memorandum (art.69). A separately-ordered document, not part of the base extract, hence 'additional'.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 184(1)
evidence.quote: Every company shall, after 1st January 2004, upon each anniversary of its registration, make a return in the form set out in the Seventh Schedule showing the matters therein specified and made up to the date of such anniversary

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), Seventh Schedule, Part 3 (Article 184)
evidence.quote: 3. List of Past and Present Members. List of persons holding shares or stock in the company on the date to which this return is made up, and of persons who have held shares or stock therein at any time since the date of the last return, or in the case of the first return, of the registration of the company.
evidence.note: Columns: Names / Addresses / Number of shares held by existing members.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 424 (public access)
evidence.quote: Any person may - (a) inspect the documents kept by the Registrar ... (b) obtain ... a copy or extract of any other document, and require it to be certified by the Registrar as a true copy.

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice Portal (official) — documents obtainable from MBR
evidence.quote: This documentation includes, inter alia, all the notifications submitted and registered by the Registrar for every company, the status of the company, annual accounts as well as annual returns.

caveats: STALENESS: the annual return is filed only once a year (within 42 days of the registration anniversary), so at any given moment it may be up to ~13 months out of date and will not reflect share transfers since the last filing.

The company-held register of members (art.123) is the live record but is kept at the registered office and open to inspection only by members without charge (art.125) — NOT a Registrar/MBR document. The public MBR proof is the annual return.

Do not confuse with the beneficial-ownership register: BO is a separate filing whose public access was curtailed after the CJEU Nov-2022 ruling; the annual-return member list is unaffected and remains public.

documentLocal: annual return (Seventh Schedule, Part 3 — List of Past and Present Members)
summary: The art.184 annual-return obligation and the Seventh Schedule Part 3 'List of Past and Present Members' apply to ALL companies, with no exemption for public companies (the only carve-out from Parts 2 and 3 is for investment companies with variable share capital / SICAVs, art.84(8)). So a plc's annual return also legally lists every member and their shareholding, and it is public under art.424. Founding subscribers are additionally in the memorandum (art.69).

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 184(1)
evidence.quote: Every company shall, after 1st January 2004, upon each anniversary of its registration, make a return in the form set out in the Seventh Schedule showing the matters therein specified and made up to the date of such anniversary

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 84(8) — scope of exemption (only SICAVs excused)
evidence.quote: an investment company with variable share capital shall not be obliged to give any of the details in Parts 2 and 3 of the form of annual return set out in the Seventh Schedule, other than the share capital of the company and the number of shares issued.
evidence.note: Confirms a plain plc has NO such exemption — it must list members.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 424 (public access)
evidence.quote: Any person may - (a) inspect the documents kept by the Registrar ...

caveats: STALENESS: same annual, up-to-~13-months lag as the Ltd — the list IS complete but is a yearly snapshot.

NOMINEE/LISTED RISK: for a plc whose shares are admitted to trading, the registered members shown in the annual return are frequently nominees, custodians or a CSD, not the ultimate beneficial holders, and registered holdings turn over continuously; the document proves the register of members, not beneficial ownership.
BO-register public-access restriction (post-CJEU 2022) does not affect the annual-return member list.

summary: A Maltese company's constitution is its memorandum of association (art.69) plus its articles of association (art.75). Both are delivered to and registered by the Registrar (art.76) and held as the company's 'statute' at the MBR; any person may obtain a certified copy under art.424. Treated as an additional (separately-ordered) document rather than the base extract.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 69(1)
evidence.quote: The memorandum of every company shall state: (a) whether the company is a public company or a private company; (b) the name and residence of each of the subscribers thereto; (c) the name of the company; (d) the registered office in Malta ...; (e) the objects of the company; (f) the amount of share capital ...

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 75(1)
evidence.quote: There may be registered with the memorandum, articles of association (hereinafter referred to as "articles") which shall be signed by the subscribers to the memorandum and prescribing regulations for the company.
evidence.note: If no articles are registered, the model articles in the First Schedule apply (art.75(2)).

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 76(1)
evidence.quote: The memorandum and articles, if any, shall be delivered for registration to the Registrar who, being satisfied that all the requirements ... have been complied with, shall register them.

caveats: A private company may register bespoke articles OR rely on the First Schedule model articles (art.75(2)).
The MBR-held copy reflects the memorandum and articles as last amended and registered; unregistered/pending amendments would not appear.

summary: Same constitutional framework as the Ltd: memorandum (art.69, stating it is a public company) and articles (art.75), both registered with the Registrar (art.76) and obtainable as certified copies by any person (art.424). A public company must additionally annex a formation-costs/special-advantages statement (art.69(2)), likewise a registered document.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 69(1)(a) and 69(2)
evidence.quote: The memorandum of every company shall state: (a) whether the company is a public company or a private company ... (2) In the case of a public company, there shall be annexed to the memorandum a document providing: (a) the total amount or an estimate of all the costs payable by the company or chargeable to it by reason of its formation ... and (b) a description of any special advantage granted prior to the time the company is authorized to commence business ...

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 76(1)
evidence.quote: The memorandum and articles, if any, shall be delivered for registration to the Registrar who, being satisfied that all the requirements ... have been complied with, shall register them.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 424 (public access)
evidence.quote: Any person may - (a) inspect the documents kept by the Registrar ... (b) obtain ... a copy or extract of any other document, and require it to be certified by the Registrar as a true copy.

caveats: The MBR copy reflects the constitution as last registered; any unregistered amendment would not be evidenced.
Model-articles fallback (art.75(2)) applies as for any company where articles are not separately registered.

summary: The Malta Business Registry (MBR) holds the statutory register and issues a certified company extract and a certificate of registration. Under Companies Act art.424 any person may inspect documents kept by the Registrar and obtain a certified copy or extract. Core identifying data (name, number, legal form, registered office, incorporation date, status, share capital, officers) is publicly available; the extract is the functional equivalent of a German Registerauszug.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 424
evidence.quote: Any person may - (a) inspect the documents kept by the Registrar ... (b) obtain a copy of the certificate of the registration of any commercial partnership or a copy or extract of any other document, and require it to be certified by the Registrar as a true copy.
evidence.note: General public-access provision covering all Registrar-held documents.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 77(2)
evidence.quote: A certificate of registration given in respect of a company is conclusive evidence that the requirements of this Act in respect of registration and of matters precedent and incidental to it have been complied with and that the company is duly registered as a public or private company under this Act.

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice Portal (official) — Malta business register
evidence.quote: company names and registration numbers, their registered address, date of incorporation, whether it is a company or not, share capital, and the identity of the company officials.
evidence.note: Free public information tier; further documents available for a charge.

caveats: INFERENCE: Malta has no statutory document literally called a 'Registerauszug'; the MBR-issued company extract / certificate of registration is the functional equivalent.
The free online extract is a data snapshot; a certified extract must be ordered and carries a fee.

summary: Identical basis to the Ltd: art.424 makes all Registrar-held documents inspectable and obtainable as certified copies/extracts, and art.77(2) makes the certificate of registration conclusive evidence of due registration as a public company. The MBR issues a company extract that functions as the register extract.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 424
evidence.quote: Any person may - (a) inspect the documents kept by the Registrar ... (b) obtain a copy of the certificate of the registration of any commercial partnership or a copy or extract of any other document, and require it to be certified by the Registrar as a true copy.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 77(2)
evidence.quote: A certificate of registration given in respect of a company is conclusive evidence that the requirements of this Act in respect of registration and of matters precedent and incidental to it have been complied with and that the company is duly registered as a public or private company under this Act.

caveats: INFERENCE: same mapping caveat as the Ltd — 'Registerauszug' is our label for the MBR company extract.

documentLocal: company search / register extract; annual return (Tenth Schedule)
summary: Current shareholders of a domestic company are registry-held and publicly obtainable: shareholder particulars are lodged at incorporation (s.23(2)(d)) and the annual return filed with the Registrar must list every shareholder's name/address and shares held (s.223 + Tenth Schedule para 11). Mauritius is comparatively transparent and public register access under s.14 covers these particulars, so shareholders are proved from the registry.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius), s.91(1) and (3) — share register contents
evidence.quote: A company shall maintain a share register which shall record the shares issued by the company
evidence.note: s.91(3) (literal items): the share register shall state, with respect to each class of shares, '(i) the names, in alphabetical order, and the last known address of each person who is, or has within the last 7 years been, a shareholder' and '(b) the number of shares of that class held by each shareholder within the last 7 years'. This register is company-held. Replaces an earlier evidence entry whose quote could not be matched verbatim in this source.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius), s.223(1) and (5) — annual return filed with the Registrar
evidence.quote: every company shall, once in every year, file with the Registrar for registration, an annual return
evidence.note: s.223(5) (literal): 'The annual return shall contain the matters specified in the Tenth Schedule'. Replaces an earlier evidence entry whose quote ('within the prescribed time after the end of each accounting period') could not be matched verbatim in this source.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius), Tenth Schedule, Part I, para 11 — shareholder contents of the annual return
evidence.quote: the following information relating to past and present shareholders of the company
evidence.note: Para 11 items (literal, with typographic soft hyphens in the source removed): (a) the names and addresses (other than residential) of all the shareholders of the company; (c) 'the number of shares held by each shareholder'; plus the names/addresses of all persons who ceased to be shareholders since the last annual return and the shares transferred. Exception: companies within s.223(8) (public companies with more than 500 members providing reasonable access to their register); listed companies additionally report the 10 largest holders per class (para 10).

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius), s.14(1)-(3) — public inspection and certified copies/extracts of Registrar-held documents
evidence.quote: a person may, on payment of the prescribed fees and during such time as the Registrar may decide, inspect
evidence.note: Inspectable items (literal): '(a) any document in a register kept by the Registrar'. Under s.14(2)(b) any person may apply for 'a copy of, or extract from, a document in a register kept by the Registrar', which the Registrar shall issue on payment of the prescribed fee (s.14(3)); Registrar-certified copies/extracts are admissible in evidence to the same extent as the original (s.14(5)). This makes the filed annual return (with its Tenth Schedule shareholder list) publicly obtainable from the registry.

caveats: The registry-held shareholder evidence is the ANNUAL return (Tenth Schedule) — a snapshot as at the return date, not a live register; the s.91 share register (which also captures nominee/beneficial-owner detail) remains company-held.

Exemptions: s.223(1A)/(1B) — qualifying small companies (e.g. small private companies with annual turnover not exceeding 20 million rupees) need not file an annual return unless there is a change in shareholding or board composition; s.223(8) with Tenth Schedule para 11 — public companies with more than 500 members providing reasonable access to their register need not list all shareholders (listed companies report the 10 largest holders per class).
Source is the MauritiusLII consolidation as at 30 June 2017 (eng@2017-06-30); later amendments are not reflected in the verified text.

legalFormLocal: Company holding a Global Business Licence (Global Business Company)
summary: Although a GBC files shareholder particulars with the CBRD (incorporation particulars and annual return, Tenth Schedule para 11), those documents are not disclosable to the public: s.14(7) restricts access to shareholders/officers/management company/registered agent, and s.14(8) limits third-party disclosure to name, registered office and registered agent. Current shareholders of a GBC cannot be proved from the registry by an outside KYC party.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.14(7)
evidence.quote: This section shall not apply to a private company holding a Global Business Licence or an Authorised Company unless the person is a shareholder, officer, management company or registered agent of that company.
evidence.note: Shareholder particulars on the register/annual return are not publicly obtainable for a GBC.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) Tenth Schedule para 11 (via s.223(5))
evidence.quote: the following information relating to past and present shareholders of the company-- (a) the names and addresses (other than residential) of all the shareholders of the company; ... (c) the number of shares held by each shareholder;
evidence.note: GBCs file this annual return too, but s.14(7) makes it non-public.

caveats: The LEGAL shareholder list IS filed with CBRD; it is withheld from third parties by s.14(7) — an insider can obtain it.
Beneficial owners of a GBC sit with the FSC and the licensed management company; GBC shares are frequently held via nominees, so any registered shareholder may itself be a nominee.
Public-facing disclosure is capped at name + registered office + management company/registered agent (s.14(8)).

summary: Where a company has a constitution, a certified copy is lodged with the incorporation application (s.23(1)(c)(vii)) and becomes a registered document obtainable from the Registrar under s.14. It is a separate/additional document from the register extract. However, a private company need not have a constitution: absent one it is governed by the model constitution in the Second Schedule (s.42(1)(b)), in which case no bespoke registry document exists.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius), s.39 (constitution)
evidence.quote: Any company may, but does not need to, have a constitution
evidence.note: Confirms the constitution is a separate, optional instrument — hence an additional document rather than part of the base registry record; where absent, the Act's default provisions apply.

caveats: A Mauritius company may have no constitution at all, in which case there is no such document to obtain.

legalFormLocal: Company holding a Global Business Licence (Global Business Company)
summary: A GBC's constitution is lodged with the CBRD at incorporation (s.23(1)(c)(vii)) and GBCs almost always adopt a bespoke constitution, but as a registered document it falls under the s.14(7) restriction: only a shareholder, officer, management company or registered agent may obtain copies/extracts. It is not obtainable from the registry by an outside KYC party (s.14(8)).

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.14(7)
evidence.quote: This section shall not apply to a private company holding a Global Business Licence or an Authorised Company unless the person is a shareholder, officer, management company or registered agent of that company.
evidence.note: The GBC's registered constitution is not publicly obtainable.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.23(1)(c)(vii) and s.42(1)(a)
evidence.quote: where the proposed company is to have a constitution, a document certified by at least one applicant that the document is the company's constitution.
evidence.note: Constitution is lodged at incorporation (GBCs typically have one), but disclosure is blocked by s.14(7).

caveats: The constitution EXISTS at the registry for most GBCs but is withheld from third parties (s.14(7)); an insider can obtain it.
Assumes GBC is a private company (standard case).

summary: The Registrar of Companies (CBRD) keeps the company register and, on payment of the prescribed fee, issues a copy of or certified extract from the registered documents; the CBRD also runs a free online company search (CBRIS). For an ordinary domestic company this register extract is publicly obtainable and is the base document.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.14(2)(b) and s.14(3)
evidence.quote: A person may ... apply to the Registrar for-- ... (b) a copy of, or extract from, a document in a register kept by the Registrar ... On an application under subsection (2), the Registrar shall, on payment by the applicant of the prescribed fee, issue the document, particulars or copy or certified copy applied for.
evidence.note: General public right to obtain an extract from the register for a domestic company.

evidence.basis: CBRD official site (companies.govmu.org) — online services
evidence.quote: Search Online (free of Charge)
evidence.note: Free CBRIS online company search; certified extracts obtainable via 'Request of Documents'.

caveats: The free online CBRIS search returns data on screen; the actual DOCUMENT for KYC is the certified extract issued by the Registrar on payment (s.14(3)).
INFERENCE: exact label/format of the CBRD register extract not verified from a live extract (search UI is a JS app); classification by statute, confidence high.

legalFormLocal: Company holding a Global Business Licence (Global Business Company)
summary: A GBC is incorporated/registered under the Companies Act 2001 and is on the CBRD register, but s.14(7) disapplies the public inspection-and-copies regime to a private company holding a Global Business Licence unless the requester is a shareholder, officer, management company or registered agent. To the general public (KYC third party) the Registrar discloses only company name, registered office, and the management company/registered agent (s.14(8)). A full register extract is not obtainable from the registry by an outside party.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.14(7)
evidence.quote: This section shall not apply to a private company holding a Global Business Licence or an Authorised Company unless the person is a shareholder, officer, management company or registered agent of that company.
evidence.note: Removes the general public right to inspect/obtain copies or extracts of a GBC's registered documents.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.14(8)
evidence.quote: a person may, on payment of the prescribed fee, request the Registrar to provide, in relation to a private company holding a Global Business Licence or an Authorised Company-- (a) the name of the company and the address of its registered office; and (b) the name and address of any management company or registered agent appointed by the company
evidence.note: Defines the ONLY registry data a third party can obtain for a GBC — no full extract.

caveats: GBC IS on the CBRD register (a company under Companies Act 2001 holding a Global Business Licence from the FSC); the block is on PUBLIC disclosure, not on registration.
An insider (shareholder/officer/management company/registered agent) CAN obtain the extract; beneficial-ownership data sits with the FSC and the licensed management company.
Assumes the GBC is a private company (near-universal case).

legalFormLocal: Sociedad de Responsabilidad Limitada de Capital Variable
documentLabel: Partners and their parts sociales as stated in the constitutive deed (and registered amendments)
documentLocal: Acta constitutiva / escritura pública (socios y partes sociales), más escrituras de cesión/modificación inscritas en el RPC; el libro especial de socios (art. 73 LGSM) lo lleva la sociedad, no el registro
summary: Unlike the S.A., the partners (socios) and their parts sociales are a mandatory content of the escritura constitutiva (LGSM art. 6), which is a base registered instrument, so the founding membership is provable from the registry. Admission or transfer of partners requires partner consent and is typically effected by a notarised amendment that is inscribed in the RPC, so changes can be evidenced by registered additional deeds. However, the authoritative current membership is the company-held libro especial de socios (art. 73), which is kept 'al cuidado de los administradores' and is not a registry document, so the registry cannot guarantee a fully current list on its own.

evidence.basis: Ley General de Sociedades Mercantiles, art. 6, fracción I
evidence.quote: Los nombres, nacionalidad y domicilio de las personas físicas o morales que constituyan la sociedad
evidence.note: The escritura/acta constitutiva must state the founding socios; that instrument is inscribed in the Registro Público de Comercio, so the base registry document evidences the founding partners.

evidence.basis: Ley General de Sociedades Mercantiles, art. 5
evidence.quote: Las sociedades se constituirán ante notario y en la misma forma se harán constar con sus modificaciones.
evidence.note: Constitution and its modifications (including share/partner cessions) are formalised before a notary and recorded — the mechanism by which registered ownership changes reach the RPC.

evidence.basis: Ley General de Sociedades Mercantiles, art. 73
evidence.quote: La sociedad llevará un libro especial de los socios, en el cual se inscribirá el nombre y el domicilio de cada uno, con indicación de sus aportaciones, y la transmisión de las partes sociales.
evidence.note: The up-to-date statutory members list (libro especial de socios) is kept BY THE COMPANY, not by the registry — confirming the current-holdings list is company-held.

caveats: The base registry document (escritura constitutiva + inscribed modifications) proves founding socios and registered transfers; the current holder list (libro especial de socios, art. 73) is company-held, not at the RPC.

summary: For a sociedad anónima the shareholders are recorded only in the company-held libro de registro de acciones (LGSM art. 128), not in the public registry; the company treats as owner whoever appears in that book and records transfers on request (art. 129). Only the founding shareholders named in the acta constitutiva reach the RPC. Therefore a current shareholder list cannot be proved from the registry.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 128
evidence.quote: Las sociedades anónimas tendrán un registro de acciones que contendrá: I.- El nombre, la nacionalidad y el domicilio del accionista, y la indicación de las acciones que le pertenezcan
evidence.note: The share register is a company book, not a registry filing.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 129
evidence.quote: La sociedad considerará como dueño de las acciones a quien aparezca inscrito como tal en el registro...A este efecto, la sociedad deberá inscribir en dicho registro, a petición de cualquier titular, las transmisiones que se efectúen.
evidence.note: Ownership and transfers are tracked in the company-held book, off-registry.

caveats: The founding shareholders do appear in the acta constitutiva (registered), but that is a snapshot at incorporation, not a maintained current shareholder list.
The authoritative current list (libro de registro de acciones) is held by the company and is not a public-registry document.

legalFormLocal: Sociedad de Responsabilidad Limitada de Capital Variable
documentLocal: Acta constitutiva / escritura pública con estatutos sociales (copia certificada), inscrita en el Registro Público de Comercio
summary: The constitution is the escritura constitutiva (acta constitutiva) with the estatutos sociales, granted before a notary and inscribed in the RPC (LGSM arts. 2, 6, 7). It is the base registered instrument and its certified copy is the registry's core proof of the company's charter.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 6
evidence.quote: El importe del capital social.
evidence.note: The escritura constitutiva embeds the estatutos and required capital clauses.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 7
evidence.quote: Si el contrato social no se hubiere otorgado en escritura ante Notario...cualquier socio podrá demandar en la vía sumaria el otorgamiento de la escritura correspondiente.
evidence.note: Constitution granted in escritura pública before a notary and then inscribed.

caveats: Later amendments to the estatutos are separate notarised, inscribed deeds; the original acta's certified copy may not reflect the current bylaws.

documentLocal: Acta constitutiva / escritura pública con estatutos sociales (copia certificada), inscrita en el Registro Público de Comercio
summary: The constitution is the escritura constitutiva (acta constitutiva) containing the estatutos sociales, granted before a notary and inscribed in the RPC (LGSM arts. 5-7). It is the base registered instrument and its certified copy evidences the company's charter. This is the registry's core deliverable.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 6
evidence.quote: Los nombres, nacionalidad y domicilio de las personas físicas o morales que constituyan la sociedad
evidence.note: Escritura constitutiva must state founders and capital; it embeds the estatutos.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 7
evidence.quote: Si el contrato social no se hubiere otorgado en escritura ante Notario...cualquier socio podrá demandar en la vía sumaria el otorgamiento de la escritura correspondiente.
evidence.note: The constitution is granted in escritura pública before a notary.

caveats: Later amendments to the estatutos are separate notarised deeds that must also be inscribed; a single certified copy of the original acta may not reflect amended bylaws.

legalFormLocal: Sociedad de Responsabilidad Limitada de Capital Variable
documentLabel: Commercial register extract — certification of the electronic mercantile folio
documentLocal: Certificación / boleta de inscripción del folio mercantil electrónico (Registro Público de Comercio, vía SIGER)
summary: As with any mercantile company, the S. de R.L. de C.V. acquires legal personality by inscription of its escritura constitutiva in the Registro Público de Comercio (LGSM art. 2) on an electronic mercantile folio in SIGER. The registry evidences registered particulars via the certification/boleta of that folio. Mexico issues no consolidated German-style current-particulars extract; the folio certifies inscribed instruments.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 2
evidence.quote: Las sociedades mercantiles inscritas en el Registro Público de Comercio, tienen personalidad jurídica distinta de la de los socios.
evidence.note: Inscription in the RPC is the constitutive registry act for all mercantile forms. (EN: Mercantile companies registered in the Public Registry of Commerce have legal personality distinct from that of the partners.)

evidence.basis: Código de Comercio art. 18 — RPC registers mercantile acts; operated by the Secretaría de Economía with the state registry authorities
evidence.quote: En el Registro Público de Comercio se inscriben los actos mercantiles, así como aquellos que se relacionan con los comerciantes y que conforme a la legislación lo requieran.
evidence.note: EN: Mercantile acts, and acts relating to merchants that legally require it, are recorded in the Public Registry of Commerce. Source: official Cámara de Diputados consolidated text hosted on gob.mx (Última Reforma DOF 02-05-2017).

evidence.basis: Código de Comercio art. 21 — one electronic folio per company (folio mercantil electrónico)
evidence.quote: Existirá un folio electrónico por cada comerciante o sociedad, en el que se anotarán: I.- Su nombre, razón social o título.
evidence.note: Art. 21(V) adds: 'Los instrumentos públicos en los que se haga constar la constitución de las sociedades mercantiles, así como los que contengan su transformación, fusión, escisión, disolución y liquidación'. EN: There shall be an electronic folio for each merchant or company recording its name, and (V) the public instruments evidencing incorporation and structural changes.

evidence.basis: Código de Comercio art. 21 bis, II(d) — boleta de inscripción issued for each registration
evidence.quote: Emisión de una boleta de inscripción que será entregada física o electrónicamente.
evidence.note: EN: Issuance of a registration slip (boleta de inscripción) delivered physically or electronically — confirms the 'boleta' as a registry-issued deliverable.

evidence.basis: Código de Comercio art. 30 — public consultation of the databases and certifications of folio entries
evidence.quote: Los particulares podrán consultar las bases de datos y, en su caso, solicitar las certificaciones respectivas, previo pago de los derechos correspondientes.
evidence.note: Second paragraph: 'Las certificaciones se expedirán previa solicitud por escrito que deberá contener los datos que sean necesarios para la localización de los asientos sobre los que deba versar la certificación y, en su caso, la mención del folio mercantil electrónico correspondiente.' EN: Private parties may consult the databases and request the corresponding certifications upon payment of fees; certifications are issued on written request identifying the entries and the electronic mercantile folio. This is the statutory register-extract (certificación) route.

caveats: The RPC certifies inscriptions (asientos) on the folio mercantil (CCom art. 30); it does not synthesise a German-style consolidated current extract.
Capital-variable changes are often not re-registered, so the folio may lag actual capital.
'SIGER' is the Secretaría de Economía's brand name for the 'programa informático' of CCom art. 20; the name SIGER itself does not appear in the code text.

Edition note: quotes taken from the official Cámara de Diputados consolidated PDF hosted on gob.mx (Última Reforma DOF 02-05-2017); diputados.gob.mx itself was unreachable this session. Arts. 18-30 were not amended by later reforms as far as verified.

documentLabel: Commercial register extract — certification of the electronic mercantile folio
documentLocal: Certificación / boleta de inscripción del folio mercantil electrónico (Registro Público de Comercio, vía SIGER)
summary: A mercantile company acquires legal personality by inscription of its escritura constitutiva in the Registro Público de Comercio (LGSM art. 2), kept as an electronic mercantile folio (folio mercantil electrónico) in SIGER. The registry evidences the registered particulars through a certification/boleta of that folio, which is the closest analogue to a German Registerauszug. Note that Mexico issues no consolidated German-style 'current particulars' printout — the folio certifies the inscribed instruments rather than a synthesised current state.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 2
evidence.quote: Las sociedades mercantiles inscritas en el Registro Público de Comercio, tienen personalidad jurídica distinta de la de los socios.
evidence.note: Inscription in the RPC is the constitutive registry act. (EN: Mercantile companies registered in the Public Registry of Commerce have legal personality distinct from that of the partners.)

evidence.basis: Código de Comercio art. 18 — RPC registers mercantile acts; operated by the Secretaría de Economía with the state registry authorities
evidence.quote: En el Registro Público de Comercio se inscriben los actos mercantiles, así como aquellos que se relacionan con los comerciantes y que conforme a la legislación lo requieran.
evidence.note: EN: Mercantile acts, and acts relating to merchants that legally require it, are recorded in the Public Registry of Commerce. Source: official Cámara de Diputados consolidated text hosted on gob.mx (Última Reforma DOF 02-05-2017).

evidence.basis: Código de Comercio art. 21 — one electronic folio per company (folio mercantil electrónico)
evidence.quote: Existirá un folio electrónico por cada comerciante o sociedad, en el que se anotarán: I.- Su nombre, razón social o título.
evidence.note: Art. 21(V) adds: 'Los instrumentos públicos en los que se haga constar la constitución de las sociedades mercantiles, así como los que contengan su transformación, fusión, escisión, disolución y liquidación'. EN: There shall be an electronic folio for each merchant or company recording its name, and (V) the public instruments evidencing incorporation and structural changes.

evidence.basis: Código de Comercio art. 21 bis, II(d) — boleta de inscripción issued for each registration
evidence.quote: Emisión de una boleta de inscripción que será entregada física o electrónicamente.
evidence.note: EN: Issuance of a registration slip (boleta de inscripción) delivered physically or electronically — confirms the 'boleta' as a registry-issued deliverable.

evidence.basis: Código de Comercio art. 30 — public consultation of the databases and certifications of folio entries
evidence.quote: Los particulares podrán consultar las bases de datos y, en su caso, solicitar las certificaciones respectivas, previo pago de los derechos correspondientes.
evidence.note: Second paragraph: 'Las certificaciones se expedirán previa solicitud por escrito que deberá contener los datos que sean necesarios para la localización de los asientos sobre los que deba versar la certificación y, en su caso, la mención del folio mercantil electrónico correspondiente.' EN: Private parties may consult the databases and request the corresponding certifications upon payment of fees; certifications are issued on written request identifying the entries and the electronic mercantile folio. This is the statutory register-extract (certificación) route.

caveats: The RPC does not issue a single consolidated 'current registered particulars' extract like the German Handelsregister; it certifies the inscriptions (asientos) recorded on the folio mercantil (CCom art. 30).
Under capital variable, changes to the variable capital are frequently not re-registered, so the folio may lag the company's actual capital.
'SIGER' is the Secretaría de Economía's brand name for the 'programa informático' of CCom art. 20; the name SIGER itself does not appear in the code text.

Edition note: quotes taken from the official Cámara de Diputados consolidated PDF hosted on gob.mx (Última Reforma DOF 02-05-2017); diputados.gob.mx itself was unreachable this session. Arts. 18-30 were not amended by later reforms as far as verified.

documentLabel: Register of Members / Annual Return (s.68); for a listed Berhad: Record of Depositors (Bursa Malaysia Depository / CDS)
documentLocal: Daftar Ahli / Penyata Tahunan; Rekod Pendeposit (Bursa Malaysia)
summary: A Berhad keeps a register of members (s.50) and lodges an annual return (s.68), from which member particulars can be obtained as documents from SSM — so a shareholder list is provable, but generally via a document additional to the base profile rather than from the profile itself. For a LISTED Berhad, legal/beneficial shareholdings are held in the central depository system operated by Bursa Malaysia Depository, and the authoritative member list is the Record of Depositors sourced from the depository (not an SSM extract); SSM outputs for listed companies may show only substantial shareholders.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2016 (Act 777), s.50(1) — every company keeps a register of members
evidence.quote: Every company shall keep a register of its members
evidence.note: s.50(1)(a)-(b) require the register to record names/addresses/ID numbers of members and a statement of the shares held by each member. Quoted from the official SSM consolidated PDF (as at 1.8.2022).

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2016, s.68(1) and s.68(3)(i) — the annual return lodged with the Registrar contains the list of members
evidence.quote: A company shall lodge with the Registrar an annual return for each calendar year not later than thirty days from the anniversary of its incorporation date.
evidence.note: s.68(3) enumerates the annual return's contents, including verbatim item '(i) the list of its members; and' plus '(f) in the case of a company with a share capital, the summary of its shareholding structure, including debentures'. This is the statutory channel by which the member list reaches SSM as an obtainable filed document — supporting proved_by_additional_document.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2016, s.68(7) — public-company exception: >500 members with inspection facilities may file only the twenty largest holders per class
evidence.quote: A public company which has more than five hundred members and provides reasonable opportunities and facilities for a person to inspect and take copies of its list of members and its particulars of shares transferred need not comply with the requirement under paragraph (3)(i)
evidence.note: The exception requires instead, verbatim, 'a list showing the prescribed particulars of the twenty largest holders of each class of equity shares.' — so for a large/listed Berhad the SSM annual return does not carry the full member list.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2016, s.51(1) and s.51(3) — changes in the register of members are notified to the Registrar within 14 days, EXCEPT for exchange-quoted companies
evidence.quote: This section is not applicable to a company whose shares are quoted on a stock exchange.
evidence.note: s.51(1), verbatim in the same source: 'A company shall notify the Registrar of the changes in the particulars in the register within fourteen days'. s.51(3) (quoted) removes listed companies from that notification duty — grounding the listed/unlisted split: SSM shareholder data is maintained current for unlisted companies only.

evidence.basis: SSM e-Info service — purchasable registry products include shareholder information
evidence.quote: Particular of Shareholders
evidence.note: The e-Info price list offers 'Particular of Shareholders' (RM25.40, CTC RM40.80) and 'Company Profile (ROC)' as purchasable products, confirming shareholder particulars are obtainable from SSM as a registry output additional to the base profile.

evidence.basis: Securities Industry (Central Depositories) Act 1991 — central depository / Record of Depositors (for listed public companies)
evidence.note: For listed Berhad, shares are in scripless CDS form; the Record of Depositors (Bursa Malaysia Depository) is the operative member list, sourced outside SSM. UNVERIFIED verbatim.

caveats: Listed vs. unlisted Berhad matters: unlisted -> register of members / annual return at SSM (s.50, s.51, s.68(3)(i)); listed -> excluded from s.51 notification (s.51(3)) and may file only the twenty largest holders per class in the annual return (s.68(7)); the operative member list is the Record of Depositors via Bursa Malaysia Depository, not SSM.
SICDA/Record of Depositors text not verified verbatim this session (SSM statute and e-Info product are verified; the depository mechanics rest on established practice).

documentLabel: SSM Company Profile (shareholders section) / Register of Members
documentLocal: Profil Syarikat (bahagian pemegang saham) / Daftar Ahli
summary: A Sdn Bhd is a private company that keeps a register of members (s.50) and must lodge changes to that register with the Registrar within 14 days (s.51), so SSM holds current shareholder data. The SSM company profile therefore already lists shareholders and their holdings, so the base extract itself proves the member list. A standalone register of members and the s.68 annual return (of a company having share capital) are additionally obtainable as documents.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2016 s.50 (register of members) and s.51 (notification of change in the register of members lodged with the Registrar)
evidence.note: SSM legal-framework page lists the statutory form for notifying changes to the register of members (s.51), confirming SSM captures member changes for private companies.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2016 s.68 (annual return of a company having share capital)
evidence.note: SSM legal-framework page lists the 'Annual Return of company having share capital' form (s.68).

caveats: That the standard SSM profile prints the full shareholder list (vs. a summary) is well-established for private companies but was not verbatim-verified via fetch (INFERENCE).
If the base profile is treated as showing only a summary, the register of members / annual return serves as the additional document — the member list remains registry-provable either way.

documentLabel: Constitution (formerly Memorandum & Articles of Association) — only if adopted
summary: Under s.14/s.31 of the Companies Act 2016, a public company limited by shares (Berhad) may but need not have a constitution; the former mandatory M&A was abolished for companies limited by shares. If a Berhad adopts a constitution it must be lodged with the Registrar (s.32/s.36) and can then be purchased from SSM as a separate document. If none is adopted, the document does not exist. (Public companies adopt a constitution more often than private ones, but it remains optional; only a company limited by guarantee must have one under s.38.)

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2016 s.14/s.31 (constitution optional for a company limited by shares) and s.32 (lodgement of adopted constitution)
evidence.note: SSM legal-framework page lists the 'Lodgement of constitution' form (s.32).

caveats: Constitution is OPTIONAL for a Berhad limited by shares — may not exist for a given company (then not_provable for that company).
Only a company limited by guarantee is statutorily required to have a constitution (s.38) — not in scope here.
That MyData-SSM sells the lodged constitution as a document is standard practice but not directly verified (INFERENCE).

documentLabel: Constitution (formerly Memorandum & Articles of Association) — only if adopted
summary: Under s.14/s.31 of the Companies Act 2016, a company limited by shares may but need not have a constitution; the former mandatory Memorandum & Articles of Association was abolished. If a Sdn Bhd adopts a constitution it must lodge it with the Registrar (s.32/s.36), after which it can be purchased from SSM as a separate document. Where no constitution has been adopted (common for Sdn Bhd relying on the Act's default provisions), no such document exists.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2016 s.14/s.31 (constitution optional) and s.32 (registration/lodgement of adopted constitution)
evidence.note: SSM legal-framework page lists the statutory form 'Lodgement of constitution' (s.32), confirming an adopted constitution is filed with SSM and thereby obtainable as a document.

caveats: Constitution is OPTIONAL under CA 2016 — a Sdn Bhd may have none, in which case the document does not exist (would be not_provable for that specific company).
That MyData-SSM sells the lodged constitution as a document image is standard practice but not directly verified via fetch (INFERENCE).

summary: As for a Sdn Bhd, SSM issues an official company profile/extract for a Berhad (public company limited by shares) via e-Search / MyData-SSM, containing registration number, name, status, registered office, nature of business, directors, secretary and share capital. This issued document is the Registerauszug equivalent and is the base search product.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2016 (Act 777) — Registrar-held company register; SSM e-Info / MyData-SSM company profile product
evidence.note: Same SSM base product applies to public companies. Verbatim statute not fetchable.

caveats: Verbatim statutory text could not be fetched; quotes empty.
Exact profile fields not verifiable via fetch (INFERENCE from established practice).

summary: SSM (Suruhanjaya Syarikat Malaysia) issues an official company profile/extract via its e-Search / MyData-SSM portal for every registered company. For a Sdn Bhd it contains registration number, name, status, registered office, nature of business, directors, secretary, share capital and shareholder particulars. This issued document is the direct equivalent of a Registerauszug and is the base search product.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2016 (Act 777) — incorporation and Registrar-held company register; SSM e-Info / MyData-SSM company profile product
evidence.note: SSM's legal-framework page confirms SSM issues company information products; MyData-SSM is the e-Search portal for buying the company profile. Verbatim statute text not fetchable (AGC/CommonLII returned 403).

evidence.basis: MyData-SSM / SSM e-Search portal (product listing)
evidence.note: Company profile is the base purchasable product. Portal is a JS app; exact on-screen menu not directly readable via fetch (UNVERIFIED layout).

caveats: Verbatim statutory text could not be fetched; quotes left empty per instruction.
Exact fields printed on the SSM profile were not verifiable via fetch (MyData is a SPA) — treated as well-established practice (INFERENCE).

summary: Only a SOLE shareholder is registered. A multi-shareholder BV publishes nothing about its shareholders.

evidence.basis: art. 22 lid 1 sub e Handelsregisterbesluit 2008
evidence.note: Requires registration of personal data (excluding signature) of 'de houder van alle aandelen' of an NV or BV — i.e. the sole shareholder only. With two or more shareholders, nothing is registered.

evidence.basis: KVK — uittreksel
evidence.quote: In een uittreksel zie je bijvoorbeeld wie eigenaar, enig aandeelhouder, bestuurder of gevolmachtigde is van een bedrijf.

evidence.basis: art. 2:85 BW — aandeelhoudersregister
evidence.quote: Niet iedereen mag het aandeelhoudersregister inzien
evidence.note: Internal register kept by the board at the company address. Inspection limited to shareholders, pledgees, usufructuaries and certificate holders with meeting rights.

caveats: Partial fallback: the oprichtingsakte/statuten deposited with KvK is orderable by anyone (online only if deposited after 1 June 2013) and names founders + shares at incorporation — a snapshot, never updated for transfers.
UNVERIFIED: KvK screens deposited documents for sensitive information; whether the founder section always survives redaction is unconfirmed.
The planned Centraal Aandeelhoudersregister (CAHR) would also NOT be public.
UBO register closed to the public since CJEU WM/Sovim.

documentLabel: Names of participants (shown on the register extract)
summary: Unlike an AS/ASA, the OWNERS of an ANS — the participants (deltakere) with personal liability — are registered in Foretaksregisteret and MUST appear on the firmaattest. So current owners are evidenced by the base register document itself.

evidence.basis: Forskrift om registrering av foretak §13 (firmaattestens innhold), Lovdata
evidence.quote: Navn på deltakerne i ansvarlig selskap og EØFG.
evidence.note: The firmaattest for an ansvarlig selskap must state the names of the participants (deltakere).

caveats: This evidences the personally-liable participants (deltakere), the ANS analogue of shareholders — a genuine distinction from AS/ASA. Ownership percentages/interest splits may not be fully detailed on the firmaattest; the selskapsavtale gives the detailed shares.

summary: There is NO shareholder-evidencing document issued by the commercial register. Brønnøysundregistrene holds no shareholder data. The aksjeeierbok (shareholder register, aksjeloven §4-5) is kept by the company itself and is open to inspection at the company (§4-6), but it is not a registry-issued document. Separately, shareholder data is reported annually to Skatteetaten's aksjonærregisteret; anyone may request an extract, but it is delivered as a caveated .csv data file (available only in May of the following year), not an authoritative certified document.

evidence.basis: Brønnøysundregistrene – The shareholder register
evidence.quote: The Brønnøysund Register Centre does not keep any record of shareholders.
evidence.note: brreg explicitly disclaims holding shareholder data; directs to the company / Tax Administration.

evidence.basis: Aksjeloven §4-5 / §4-6 (Lovdata)
evidence.note: §4-5: the board must keep an updated aksjeeierbok; §4-6: anyone has the right of inspection into the aksjeeierbok — but this is inspection AT THE COMPANY, not a registry document.

evidence.basis: Skatteetaten – Aksjonærregisteret (deling)
evidence.quote: Alle kan få tilgang til opplysninger om aksjeeiere i aksjonærregisteret
evidence.note: Public access exists but delivery is a downloadable .csv data extract, not a certified document; opens ~May of the year after the tax year.

evidence.basis: Skatteetaten – Aksjonærregisteret (deling)
evidence.quote: Det kan forekomme feil eller ufullstendige opplysninger
evidence.note: Skatteetaten itself flags the data as possibly erroneous/incomplete — not authoritative proof.

caveats: CONFLICT of concepts: distinguish (a) the company-kept aksjeeierbok — a real document but held by the company, inspectable under §4-6, NOT obtainable from any registry; from (b) the tax aksjonærregisteret — publicly accessible but delivered as raw .csv DATA that Skatteetaten warns may be wrong/incomplete and is ~4 months stale. Neither is a commercial-register proof document.
INFERENCE: framed strictly as 'a document is the proof, parsed data is not a substitute', the honest status is not_provable_from_registry.

documentLocal: aksjeeierregister i verdipapirsentral (Euronext Securities Oslo/VPS) / aksjonærregisteret (Skatteetaten)
summary: Not provable from the commercial register. For an ASA the shareholder register (aksjeeierregister) is held in a securities depository — Euronext Securities Oslo (VPS) — under allmennaksjeloven §4-4, not at Brønnøysundregistrene. It is open to anyone (§4-5) but through the VPS/company, not as a Brønnøysund document. Shareholder data is also reported to Skatteetaten's aksjonærregisteret and obtainable as a caveated .csv extract — data, not a certified document.

evidence.basis: Allmennaksjeloven §4-4 / §4-5 (Lovdata)
evidence.note: ASA shareholder register (aksjeeierregister) must be established in a securities depository (VPS/Euronext Securities Oslo); §4-5 makes it open to anyone — held at the depository, not the commercial register.

evidence.basis: Brønnøysundregistrene – The shareholder register
evidence.quote: The Brønnøysund Register Centre does not keep any record of shareholders.

evidence.basis: Skatteetaten – Aksjonærregisteret (deling)
evidence.quote: Det kan forekomme feil eller ufullstendige opplysninger
evidence.note: Tax-route data explicitly flagged as possibly erroneous/incomplete.

caveats: CONFLICT of concepts: the authoritative live shareholder register for an ASA sits in the VPS/Euronext Securities Oslo, NOT in Brønnøysundregistrene and NOT the tax aksjonærregisteret. Neither the VPS register nor the tax .csv is a commercial-register proof document.

documentLabel: Names of participants (shown on the register extract)
summary: As with an ANS, the participants (deltakere) of a DA are registered in Foretaksregisteret and must appear on the firmaattest, so current owners are evidenced by the base register document.

evidence.basis: Forskrift om registrering av foretak §13 (firmaattestens innhold), Lovdata
evidence.quote: Navn på deltakerne i ansvarlig selskap og EØFG.
evidence.note: 'Ansvarlig selskap' in the forskrift covers both ANS and DA.

caveats: Evidences the participants; the pro-rata liability shares / detailed ownership splits are in the selskapsavtale rather than always fully on the firmaattest.

summary: An ANS has a selskapsavtale (partnership agreement, required under selskapsloven), not vedtekter. It is filed at registration and obtainable as a copy of a case document (saksdokument) via the brreg webshop.

evidence.basis: Selskapsloven (lov 21. juni 1985 nr. 83) § 2-3 første ledd (Lovdata)
evidence.quote: I selskap som ikke er indre selskap skal det opprettes en datert skriftlig selskapsavtale som skal undertegnes av samtlige deltakere, unntatt stille deltaker.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'In companies other than internal partnerships, a dated written partnership agreement shall be drawn up and signed by all participants, except silent participants.' § 2-3 second paragraph further requires minimum content including — verbatim — 'selskapets foretaksnavn' and 'navn og bosted for samtlige deltakere unntatt stille deltaker' (name and residence of all participants). So every registrable ANS mandatorily has a written, dated, signed selskapsavtale naming the partners.

evidence.basis: Brønnøysund Register Centre — product/document ordering page
evidence.quote: Certificates of registration and register transcripts in English do not normally include information about the objective as laid down in the articles of association.
evidence.note: The register transcript/certificate (base document) does not carry the agreement/articles content; the page lists Partnership agreements among documents that can be ordered (Copies of documents from the Central Coordinating Register for Legal Entities, fee NOK 241), i.e. the selskapsavtale is obtained as a separately-ordered additional document.

caveats: Mandatory existence of the selskapsavtale for every ANS (other than an indre selskap, which is not registered) is now verbatim-confirmed (selskapsloven § 2-3).

The filing link — foretaksregisterloven's requirement that the selskapsavtale be held by/attached for the Foretaksregisteret (§§ 3-4, 4-4) — could not be fetched verbatim (lovdata URLs for the act returned 404 this session); registry availability rests on brreg's own product listing of orderable partnership agreements.

documentLabel: Articles of association / Memorandum of association
summary: Vedtekter (articles) are a mandatory attachment for AS registration and, together with the stiftelsesdokument (memorandum of association), are filed with Foretaksregisteret. They are not part of the standard firmaattest but are separately obtainable as copies of case documents (saksdokumenter) via the brreg webshop.

evidence.basis: Brønnøysundregistrene – Information about our products (copies of case documents)
evidence.quote: Copies of case documents are documents that have been submitted to us in connection with registrations and registrations of legal rights at the Brønnøysund Register Centre.
evidence.note: Examples include 'Memorandum of association' (stiftelsesdokument); vedtekter for AS are attached to/part of the stiftelsesdokument and filed at registration.

evidence.basis: Brønnøysundregistrene – Attachment requirements for limited companies
evidence.note: AS registration requires stiftelsesdokument and vedtekter to be attached.

caveats: Obtained as a copy of the filed case document, not a live register printout — reflects the version on file, which may pre-date later amendments unless the latest amendment was also filed.

documentLabel: Articles of association / Memorandum of association
summary: Vedtekter and stiftelsesdokument are mandatory registration attachments filed with Foretaksregisteret and separately obtainable as copies of case documents via the brreg webshop, same as for an AS.

evidence.basis: Brønnøysundregistrene – Information about our products (copies of case documents)
evidence.quote: Copies of case documents are documents that have been submitted to us in connection with registrations and registrations of legal rights at the Brønnøysund Register Centre.
evidence.note: Examples include 'Memorandum of association'.

caveats: Copy of the filed version; may pre-date later amendments unless amendments were also filed.

summary: A DA has a selskapsavtale (partnership agreement under selskapsloven), filed at registration and obtainable as a copy of a case document via the brreg webshop.

evidence.basis: Selskapsloven (lov 21. juni 1985 nr. 83) § 2-3 første ledd (Lovdata)
evidence.quote: I selskap som ikke er indre selskap skal det opprettes en datert skriftlig selskapsavtale som skal undertegnes av samtlige deltakere, unntatt stille deltaker.
evidence.note: English gloss: 'In companies other than internal partnerships, a dated written partnership agreement shall be drawn up and signed by all participants, except silent participants.' A DA (ansvarlig selskap med delt ansvar) is a selskap under selskapsloven, so the same mandatory written selskapsavtale applies; § 2-3 second paragraph requires minimum content including 'navn og bosted for samtlige deltakere unntatt stille deltaker' (verbatim).

evidence.basis: Brønnøysund Register Centre — product/document ordering page
evidence.quote: Certificates of registration and register transcripts in English do not normally include information about the objective as laid down in the articles of association.
evidence.note: Same registry basis as ANS: the base transcript/certificate excludes the agreement content, while Partnership agreements are listed among separately-orderable documents (NOK 241) — supporting the selskapsavtale as an additional document.

caveats: Mandatory existence of the selskapsavtale for every DA (other than an indre selskap, which is not registered) is now verbatim-confirmed (selskapsloven § 2-3).

The filing link — foretaksregisterloven's requirement that the selskapsavtale be held by/attached for the Foretaksregisteret (§§ 3-4, 4-4) — could not be fetched verbatim (lovdata URLs for the act returned 404 this session); registry availability rests on brreg's own product listing of orderable partnership agreements.

summary: An ANS (general partnership, full joint liability) is registered in Foretaksregisteret; the firmaattest / registerutskrift is the base register document.

evidence.basis: Brønnøysundregistrene – Information about our products
evidence.quote: Certificates of registration from the Register of Business Enterprises and Register transcripts from the Central Coordinating Register for Legal Entities show that an organisation is registered and who is responsible for it.

summary: An AS is registered in Foretaksregisteret; the base register document is the firmaattest (a registerutskrift), issued by Brønnøysundregistrene and showing org number, name, address, board/managing director, signature/procuration and share capital. Orderable as a digital PDF (free online).

evidence.basis: Brønnøysundregistrene – Information about our products
evidence.quote: Certificates of registration from the Register of Business Enterprises and Register transcripts from the Central Coordinating Register for Legal Entities show that an organisation is registered and who is responsible for it.
evidence.note: Official English brreg product page.

caveats: The firmaattest evidences the entity, its officers and share capital, but NOT the identity of shareholders.

summary: An ASA is registered in Foretaksregisteret; the base register document is the firmaattest / registerutskrift issued by Brønnøysundregistrene, same product as for an AS.

evidence.basis: Brønnøysundregistrene – Information about our products
evidence.quote: Certificates of registration from the Register of Business Enterprises and Register transcripts from the Central Coordinating Register for Legal Entities show that an organisation is registered and who is responsible for it.

caveats: Evidences the entity and its officers, not shareholders.

summary: A DA (partnership with divided/pro-rata liability) is registered in Foretaksregisteret; the firmaattest / registerutskrift is the base register document. Same rules as ANS except liability is split by fraction.

evidence.basis: Brønnøysundregistrene – Information about our products
evidence.quote: Certificates of registration from the Register of Business Enterprises and Register transcripts from the Central Coordinating Register for Legal Entities show that an organisation is registered and who is responsible for it.

summary: Current shareholders and their exact shareholdings are shown FREE on the public Companies Register as part of the company details page (base extract). Each shareholder's name, address and number/percentage of shares held is displayed, and a shareholder view gives a record of all the shareholdings and how they are allocated. Shareholdings must be kept current — updated on share allocations/transfers and confirmed each year via the mandatory annual return. No separate shareholder-list document is needed; the register page itself evidences current shareholders.

evidence.basis: Companies Office official help-centre — Searching the companies register
evidence.quote: the number of shares, the names of shareholders and how many shares they hold
evidence.note: Shareholder link shows 'a record of all the shareholdings for that company and how they are allocated'; shareholder record shows 'the number or percentage of shares they hold'.

evidence.basis: Companies Office official help-centre — Completing an annual return
evidence.quote: the total number of your company's shares, each shareholder's full legal name and address, and the number of shares they hold.
evidence.note: The annual return confirms/updates shareholders so the register is correct. NZ requires an annual return each year.

evidence.basis: Companies Office official help-centre — Updating a shareholder's details
evidence.quote: These changes must be recorded on your own share register, and included in your next company annual return.
evidence.note: The company maintains its own share register (Companies Act 1993 s.87); changes flow to the public register via the annual return.

caveats: Public register shareholdings are kept current but between filings may lag actual changes until the next allocation filing or annual return; the definitive record remains the company's own share register (s.87).
Companies Act 1993 section numbers (s.87 share register; s.214 annual return) cited from knowledge only — legislation.govt.nz returned HTTP 403 this session (INFERENCE).

summary: A NZ limited company MAY be incorporated with or without a constitution. If it has one, a copy is filed with the Registrar and appears among the company's registered documents on the free public register, downloadable as a document. If the company has no constitution, none exists to evidence and it is governed by the default provisions of the Companies Act 1993. So the constitution is provable as a separate registered document only when the company actually has one.

evidence.basis: Companies Office official help-centre — Adding, amending or removing a company constitution
evidence.quote: You can incorporate a New Zealand company with or without a company constitution.
evidence.note: A constitution is optional, not mandatory.

evidence.basis: Companies Office official help-centre — Adding, amending or removing a company constitution
evidence.quote: NZ companies that don't have a constitution are governed by the Companies Act 1993.
evidence.note: Where no constitution is filed, the Act's default provisions apply and there is no constitution document to obtain.

evidence.basis: Companies Office official help-centre — Searching the companies register
evidence.quote: All registered company documents
evidence.note: The company details page lists all registered documents; a filed constitution is among them and downloadable free.

caveats: Conditional existence: a constitution exists on the register ONLY if the company adopted one; a company on the Act's default provisions has no constitution document — for such companies this kind is not_provable_from_registry.
Exact statutory wording (Companies Act 1993 s.26-27) cited from knowledge only — legislation.govt.nz returned HTTP 403 this session (INFERENCE).

documentLocal: company register (Companies Office) / company details page
summary: The NZ Companies Register is free and public. Searching a company returns its full details page (name, company number, NZBN, status, registered office and other addresses, incorporation date, directors, and shareholders/shareholdings) plus all registered documents. The page can be printed or saved as a PDF at no cost, serving as the current register extract. No paywalled certificate is needed for the current extract; the free public page is the document.

evidence.basis: Companies Office official help-centre — Searching the companies register
evidence.quote: the number of shares, the names of shareholders and how many shares they hold
evidence.note: The company details page also shows current/previous addresses, directors, and 'All registered company documents'. Results can be saved 'as a PDF'.

evidence.basis: Companies Office homepage (register scale / transparency mission)
evidence.quote: Building trust and confidence in our economy, and making New Zealand a transparent, fair and easy place to do business.
evidence.note: Register held 756,821 companies as at 30 June 2026; search is free and public.

caveats: The free public page is a self-generated print/PDF of the live register record, not a Registrar-certified/sealed extract; a formally certified extract may require a separate request. UNVERIFIED whether a certified version carries a fee.
Statutory basis (Companies Act 1993 s.360 Registrar's registers) cited from knowledge only — legislation.govt.nz returned HTTP 403 this session (INFERENCE).

summary: BLUNT: shareholders of a Panamanian SA are NOT recorded in the Registro Público. Ley 32 de 1927 requires the company itself to keep a book 'Registro de Acciones' at its office listing nominative shareholders; bearer (al portador) shares are not even named there. Nominative-share transfers bind the company only upon entry in that COMPANY-held book, not any public register. No public shareholder register, so no register-derived proof of current shareholders.

evidence.basis: Ley 32 de 1927, Art. 36 (Registro de Acciones)
evidence.quote: La sociedad estará obligada a tener en su oficina en la República, o en cualquier otro lugar que el pacto social o los estatutos determinen, un libro que se llamará "Registro de Acciones", en el que se anotarán, salvo en el caso de acciones emitidas al portador, los nombres de todas las personas que son accionistas de la compañía
evidence.note: The share register is a private company book, not a Registro Público filing; bearer holders expressly excluded from being named.

evidence.basis: Ley 32 de 1927, Art. 29 (transfer of nominative shares)
evidence.quote: Las acciones nominativas serán transferibles en los libros de la compañía ... Pero en ningún caso la transmisión obligará a la sociedad sino desde su inscripción en el Registro de Acciones.
evidence.note: Ownership changes are recorded in the company book, never in a public registry.

caveats: Bearer/nominative shares are held privately; the share register is company-held.
