legalFormLocal: Company holding a Special Licence (Companies (Special Licences) Act 2003)
summary: Current shareholders of a CSL cannot be proved from the registry by a third party. Shareholder names/addresses are supplied to the FSA at application (s.5) and changes notified within 14 days (s.18D), but s.22 prohibits the Authority/Registrar from disclosing such information to third parties without consent or court order. Public inspection is confined by s.15(6) to the annual return and audited accounts of a CSL that has derived assessable income in Seychelles — which most CSLs (foreign-source income vehicles) do not. No reliable public shareholder-list document.

evidence.basis: Companies (Special Licences) Act 2003 (consolidated to 6 Aug 2021), s.22(1)
evidence.quote: the Authority, the Registrar of Companies and an officer, employee or agent of the Authority or the Registrar of Companies shall not disclose to a third party any information or documents acquired in the performance of their functions under this Act.
evidence.note: Statutory non-disclosure — shareholder information filed under the Act cannot be released to third parties, so it is not obtainable via the registry.

evidence.basis: Companies (Special Licences) Act 2003, s.15(6)
evidence.quote: Any person may inspect during office hours the any annual return and audited accounts of a relevant company which has derived any assessable income in Seychelles.
evidence.note: Public inspection is limited to the annual return and audited accounts (and only where assessable income arises in Seychelles) — there is no public members register/extract.

evidence.basis: Companies (Special Licences) Act 2003, s.5(2)(b)
evidence.quote: names and addresses of shareholders
evidence.note: Shareholder details are filed with the Authority at incorporation (and changes notified under s.18D), but held confidentially, not as a public list.

caveats: Shareholder names/addresses are filed with the FSA at incorporation and changes notified (s.18D), but disclosure to third parties is barred by s.22; no public shareholder register or extract is available.

summary: No publicly obtainable document evidences the current members of a Seychelles IBC. Every IBC keeps a register of members at its registered office in Seychelles (with its registered agent) (s.104); inspection is restricted by law to a director or member only (s.107). Filing a copy with the Registrar is OPTIONAL (s.349) and not routinely done. Seychelles is a secrecy jurisdiction on ownership: current shareholders cannot be proved from the registry.

evidence.basis: IBC Act 2016 (Seychelles) s.104(1) — Register of members
evidence.quote: every company shall keep at its registered office in Seychelles a register to be known as a register of members
evidence.note: Agent-held, not filed with the Registrar.

evidence.basis: IBC Act 2016 (Seychelles) s.107(1) — Inspection of register of members
evidence.quote: A director or member of a company, in person or by attorney, is entitled without charge to inspect the company's register of members.
evidence.note: Right of inspection limited to directors/members; no public right.

evidence.basis: IBC Act 2016 (Seychelles) s.349(1) — Optional registration of specified registers
evidence.quote: A company may elect to file for registration by the Registrar a copy of any or all of the following -- (a) its register of members; ... (c) its register of beneficial owners
evidence.note: Filing is optional ('may elect'); not a reliable public source.

caveats: Register of members is registered-agent-held and inspectable only by directors/members; not public.
A separate register of beneficial owners (Beneficial Ownership Act 2020) is held by the registered agent and accessible to authorities/FIU, but likewise NOT public.
The only way to obtain a current shareholder list is directly from the company / registered agent with the company's cooperation — off-registry, not registry-provable.

legalFormLocal: Company holding a Special Licence (Companies (Special Licences) Act 2003)
summary: A CSL's constitution is filed: the memorandum and articles are submitted on application (s.5(2)(c)) and the Registrar of Companies retains and registers them under the Companies Act 1972 (s.7). As a document on the domestic register, the M&A is in principle obtainable as the base constitutional document. However CSL confidentiality (s.22) may restrict third-party access without company consent, so it is less freely available than for an IBC.

evidence.basis: Companies (Special Licences) Act 2003, s.5(2)(c)
evidence.quote: the memorandum and articles of association duly signed and dated
evidence.note: The M&A must accompany the incorporation application — mandatory constitutional document.

evidence.basis: Companies (Special Licences) Act 2003, s.7(1)
evidence.quote: The Registrar of Companies may retain and register the memorandum and articles of a company forwarded under section 6 if he is satisfied that they comply with the provisions of the Companies Act 1972.
evidence.note: The M&A is retained and registered by the Registrar, confirming it is the base registry constitutional document.

caveats: The M&A is registered by the Registrar but the registry is confidential (s.22); copies are obtainable by the company itself or with its consent, not freely from a public register.

summary: The constitution IS provable from the registry. Incorporation requires filing a memorandum and articles with the Registrar (s.9(1)(a)), registered on incorporation (s.10(1)). The M&A is a 'qualifying document' open to inspection (s.347) and available as a certified/uncertified copy on payment (s.348). The base document evidencing the company's constitution.

evidence.basis: IBC Act 2016 (Seychelles) s.9(1)(a) — Application to incorporate
evidence.quote: an application may be made to the Registrar for the incorporation of a company under this Act by filing with the Registrar -- (a) a memorandum and articles which comply with the requirements of this Act

evidence.basis: IBC Act 2016 (Seychelles) s.10(1) — Incorporation of a company
evidence.quote: the Registrar shall upon receipt of the documents filed under section 9(1), -- (a) register the documents; (b) allot a unique registration number to the company; and (c) issue a certificate of incorporation

evidence.basis: IBC Act 2016 (Seychelles) s.348(1) — Copies of documents filed
evidence.quote: a person may request, and the Registrar shall provide on payment of the specified fee ... a certified or uncertified copy ... (b) of any qualifying document or any part of any qualifying document filed with the Registrar.
evidence.note: The registered M&A is a qualifying document (required to be filed under s.9).

caveats: Amendments to the M&A must also be filed, so the Registrar copy should reflect the current constitution; confirm the copy is the latest amended/consolidated version.
The M&A is one of the very few IBC documents that IS public — contrast with the register of members and register of directors, which are not.

legalFormLocal: Company holding a Special Licence (Companies (Special Licences) Act 2003)
summary: A CSL is a domestic company (Companies Act 1972) holding a Special Licence from the FSA. A Certificate of Good Standing is obtainable: under s.18B the Registrar of Companies issues it once the FSA confirms fees are paid and the company complies with the CSL Act. As a company on the Registrar of Companies' domestic register, a register-extract-type document is in principle available — a base document proving existence and standing.

evidence.basis: Companies (Special Licences) Act 2003, s.18B
evidence.quote: The Registrar of Companies shall not issue a certificate of good standing under the Companies Act unless he has received written confirmation from the Authority that — (a) the relevant company has paid all fees and penalties due and payable under this Act; and (b) the relevant company is in compliance with this Act.
evidence.note: Statutory basis that a Certificate of Good Standing is issued by the Registrar for a CSL/relevant company — the base registry document evidencing existence and standing.

caveats: The Certificate of Good Standing evidences existence/compliance only; it does not disclose shareholders or constitutional content.

documentLabel: Certificate of Official Search / Certificate of Good Standing
documentLocal: Certificate of Official Search / Certificate of Good Standing
summary: The Registrar maintains a public Register of IBCs and, on payment of the fee, issues a Certificate of Official Search (s.352) and a Certificate of Good Standing (s.351) to any person; the public register and qualifying filed documents are inspectable (s.347). Closest thing to a Registerauszug. CRITICAL LIMIT: the official-search extract lists only name, registration number, prior names, incorporation date, registered office, registered agent, annual-fee due date, status and charge counts — NOT shareholders, and in practice not directors. It proves existence/standing/agent/office, not ownership or management.

evidence.basis: IBC Act 2016 (Seychelles) s.351(1) — Certificate of good standing
evidence.quote: The Registrar shall, upon request by any person and on payment of the fee specified in Part II of the Second Schedule, issue a certificate of good standing under the Official Seal in the approved form certifying that a company is in good standing

evidence.basis: IBC Act 2016 (Seychelles) s.352(1) — Certificate of official search particulars
evidence.quote: the name and registration number of the company; ... the date of its incorporation or continuation in Seychelles; ... the address of its registered office; ... the name and address of its registered agent; ... the status of the company
evidence.note: List (a)-(i) contains NO directors and NO members.

evidence.basis: IBC Act 2016 (Seychelles) s.347(1) — Inspection of documents filed
evidence.quote: a person may during ordinary office hours on payment of the specified fee ... (a) inspect the Registers maintained by the Registrar under section 346(1); and (b) inspect any qualifying document filed with the Registrar.

caveats: The register extract does NOT evidence current shareholders (register of members is agent-held, not filed) and in practice does NOT disclose directors: s.346(2)(g) is 'subject to subsection (4)', and s.152(5) prohibits the Registrar from disclosing the filed register of directors except by court order / law enforcement / registered agent / authorised third party. The Certificate of Official Search omits directors.
Use the extract only as proof of existence, good standing, registered office and registered agent.

summary: Bolagsverket does NOT issue any document evidencing current shareholders of an AB. The aktiebok (share register) is created and kept BY THE COMPANY under ABL chapter 5 and is never filed with Bolagsverket. It is nonetheless public: for a non-avstämningsbolag (which private ABs are), ABL 5 kap. 10 § requires the company to keep the aktiebok available at the company for anyone who wants to see it. So a current-shareholders document exists and is obtainable AS A DOCUMENT — but only from the company/its board, not from the registry. For KYC purposes it is not provable from Bolagsverket.

evidence.basis: Aktiebolagslagen (2005:551) 5 kap. 10 § (bolag som inte är avstämningsbolag)
evidence.quote: aktieboken skall hållas tillgänglig hos bolaget för alla som vill ta del av den
evidence.note: Obligation rests on the company (styrelsen), not Bolagsverket; the register is kept 'hos bolaget'.

evidence.basis: Bolagsverket / statute — no shareholder document filed with or issued by the registry
evidence.note: Confirmed against ABL ch. 5: the aktiebok is maintained by the company; Bolagsverket registers company name, board, signatories and share capital but not the list of shareholders.

caveats: The document (aktiebok) IS obtainable, but from the company itself, not from Bolagsverket — the registry issues nothing evidencing shareholders.
INFERENCE: private ABs are non-avstämningsbolag, so 5 kap. 10 § governs; not independently verified per company.

summary: Bolagsverket issues no shareholder document for a publikt AB either. Which ABL rule governs depends on whether the company is an avstämningsbolag (typically listed/Euroclear), NOT on private-vs-public status. If an avstämningsbolag, ABL 5 kap. 19 § requires a printout of the aktiebok (max three months old, shareholders alphabetical, holders of at most 500 shares omitted) kept available at the company and at the securities central (Euroclear). If not, ABL 5 kap. 10 § applies. In every case the document comes from the company or the securities central, never from Bolagsverket.

evidence.basis: Aktiebolagslagen (2005:551) 5 kap. 19 § (avstämningsbolag)
evidence.quote: en utskrift eller annan framställning av aktieboken hålls tillgänglig hos bolaget för alla som vill ta del av den. I en sådan utskrift eller framställning ska aktieägarna och förvaltarna tas upp i alfabetisk ordning. Utskriften eller framställningen får inte vara äldre än tre månader.
evidence.note: 'En aktieägare ska inte tas upp ... om hans eller hennes aktieinnehav uppgår till högst 500 aktier.' Register kept by Euroclear, not Bolagsverket.

evidence.basis: Aktiebolagslagen (2005:551) 5 kap. 10 § (publikt AB that is not an avstämningsbolag)
evidence.quote: aktieboken skall hållas tillgänglig hos bolaget för alla som vill ta del av den
evidence.note: Fallback rule where a publikt AB is not an avstämningsbolag.

caveats: CONFLICT-OF-CATEGORY: the split is avstämningsbolag vs non-avstämningsbolag, which does NOT map 1:1 to public vs private; avstämningsbolag status is set in the bolagsordning.
For avstämningsbolag the printout omits holders of <=500 shares (5 kap. 19 §), so even the company/Euroclear document is not a complete shareholder list for small holders.
In all cases Bolagsverket issues nothing evidencing shareholders.

documentLabel: Partners named in the register extract
summary: Unlike an AB, a handelsbolag has no aktiebok. Its partners (bolagsmän) are themselves registered facts in Handelsregistret and appear on the registreringsbevis. Bolagsverket notifies each person when registered/deregistered as a bolagsman. Therefore the current owners/partners ARE evidenced by the registry's base document.

evidence.basis: Bolagsverket, 'Bolagsmän i handelsbolag' and 'Ändra bolagsmän i handelsbolag'
evidence.note: Bolagsmän are registered in Handelsregistret; changes to partners must be registered, so the register/registreringsbevis reflects the current partners.

caveats: The register evidences the identity of partners (bolagsmän); it does not express fractional ownership/profit shares, which live in the (unfiled) bolagsavtal.

documentLabel: Partners named in the register extract
summary: No aktiebok exists. The partners of a kommanditbolag — the general partner(s) (komplementär) and limited partner(s) (kommanditdelägare) — are registered in Handelsregistret and appear on the registreringsbevis, together with the registered limited-partner contribution (insats). The current partners are thus evidenced by the registry's base document.

evidence.basis: Handelsregisterlagen (1974:157) § 4 — introductory rule and handelsbolag particulars (riksdagen.se)
evidence.quote: Handelsregistret skall innehålla följande uppgifter om:
evidence.note: English gloss: 'The trade register shall contain the following particulars about:'. Under the handelsbolag head (a kommanditbolag is a form of handelsbolag) item 4 requires — verbatim — 'bolagsmännens fullständiga namn, personnummer eller organisationsnummer och postadresser' (the partners' full names, personal or organisation numbers and postal addresses). The partners are thus statutory register content.

evidence.basis: Handelsregisterlagen (1974:157) § 4 — kommanditbolag-specific particular (riksdagen.se)
evidence.quote: om bolaget utgör kommanditbolag, vilken eller vilka av bolagsmännen som är kommanditdelägare och beloppet av varje sådan bolagsmans utfäst insats
evidence.note: English gloss: 'if the company is a kommanditbolag, which of the partners is/are kommanditdelägare (limited partners) and the amount of each such partner's committed contribution (utfäst insats)'. So the register distinguishes komplementär from kommanditdelägare and records each limited partner's contribution — directly supporting the partner list (with roles and insats) as base register data appearing on the registreringsbevis.

evidence.basis: Bolagsverket, 'Bolagsmän i kommanditbolag'
evidence.note: Both komplementär and kommanditdelägare are registered as bolagsmän; changes must be registered, so the register/registreringsbevis reflects current partners.

caveats: Register evidences partner identity, roles (komplementär vs kommanditdelägare) and the limited partner's utfäst insats, not internal profit/ownership shares, which live in the unfiled bolagsavtal.

summary: The bolagsordning must be filed with Bolagsverket at registration and every amendment must be registered to take effect. As a document that has 'kommit in' to the authority it is an allmän handling (public document) and anyone may order a copy from Bolagsverket. It is a separate registered document from the registreringsbevis, hence proved_by_additional_document.

evidence.basis: Bolagsverket, 'Bolagsordning för aktiebolag' + 'Allmänna handlingar'
evidence.quote: En handling är allmän om den förvaras hos en myndighet och om den har kommit in till myndigheten eller upprättats där. Du har rätt att begära ut allmänna handlingar från Bolagsverket enligt tryckfrihetsförordningen
evidence.note: A copy of the bolagsordning must be submitted at registration and all changes must be registered; being filed, it is orderable from Bolagsverket.

evidence.basis: Bolagsverket, 'Bolagsordning för aktiebolag'
evidence.note: A copy of the articles of association must be submitted with the application for registration; all changes must also be registered with Bolagsverket.

summary: Identical to private AB: the bolagsordning is filed with Bolagsverket at registration, every amendment must be registered, and it is a public document (allmän handling) obtainable as a copy from Bolagsverket.

evidence.basis: Bolagsverket, 'Allmänna handlingar' + 'Bolagsordning för aktiebolag'
evidence.quote: En handling är allmän om den förvaras hos en myndighet och om den har kommit in till myndigheten eller upprättats där. Du har rätt att begära ut allmänna handlingar från Bolagsverket enligt tryckfrihetsförordningen
evidence.note: A copy of the bolagsordning must be submitted at registration and all changes registered; being filed, it is orderable.

summary: A handelsbolag has no bolagsordning. Its constitutive agreement is the bolagsavtal (partnership agreement), a private document NOT filed with or registered by Bolagsverket. Bolagsverket therefore holds no articles-equivalent document and can issue none.

evidence.basis: Bolagsverket, 'Bolagsmän i handelsbolag' / partnership-formation guidance
evidence.note: The bolagsavtal is not a public document and is not registered with Bolagsverket; a written agreement should exist by registration but stays with the partners.

caveats: No verbatim Swedish primary quote captured for the 'bolagsavtal not filed' rule; based on Bolagsverket guidance and consistent secondary sources — UNVERIFIED at statute-quote level but consistent across sources.

summary: A kommanditbolag has no bolagsordning; its constitutive document is the bolagsavtal (partnership agreement), which is private and NOT filed with or registered by Bolagsverket. Bolagsverket holds and can issue no articles-equivalent document.

evidence.basis: Bolagsverket, 'Bolagsmän i kommanditbolag' / partnership-formation guidance
evidence.note: The bolagsavtal is not registered with Bolagsverket; it remains a private document with the partners.

caveats: No verbatim Swedish primary quote captured for the 'bolagsavtal not filed' rule; based on Bolagsverket guidance and consistent secondary sources — UNVERIFIED at statute-quote level but consistent across sources.

summary: Bolagsverket issues the registreringsbevis for a private AB. It is the current register extract and evidences company name, org. number, registration date, address, representatives (företrädare), signatory rules (firmateckning) and business description.

evidence.basis: Bolagsverket, official page 'Registreringsbevis på svenska och engelska'
evidence.quote: Ett registreringsbevis innehåller bland annat företagsnamn, organisationsnummer, datum för registrering, adress till företaget, företrädare, firmateckning och verksamhetsbeskrivning.
evidence.note: Ordered via Bolagsverket's company-information search or downloaded via Mina sidor.

summary: Same as private AB: Bolagsverket issues the registreringsbevis as the current register extract, showing name, org. number, registration date, address, representatives, firmateckning and business description. The 'publikt' status itself is recorded in the register/bolagsordning.

evidence.basis: Bolagsverket, 'Registreringsbevis på svenska och engelska'
evidence.quote: Ett registreringsbevis innehåller bland annat företagsnamn, organisationsnummer, datum för registrering, adress till företaget, företrädare, firmateckning och verksamhetsbeskrivning.
evidence.note: Applies to aktiebolag generally, including publikt aktiebolag.

summary: A handelsbolag is registered in Handelsregistret at Bolagsverket, which issues a registreringsbevis. It is the current register extract for the partnership.

evidence.basis: Bolagsverket, 'Bolagsmän i handelsbolag' / 'Set up a trading partnership'
evidence.note: Handelsbolag must be registered in Handelsregistret before starting business; it thereby receives an org. number and a registreringsbevis.

summary: A kommanditbolag (a form of handelsbolag) is registered in Handelsregistret at Bolagsverket and receives a registreringsbevis and org. number — the current register extract.

evidence.basis: Bolagsverket, 'Bolagsmän i kommanditbolag' / 'Set up a limited partnership'
evidence.note: After registration the kommanditbolag receives its registreringsbevis and organisationsnummer.

documentLocal: ACRA Business Profile / ACRA Registers (official list of members)
summary: For a private company the current members (shareholders) are on ACRA's record: ACRA maintains the electronic register of members for private companies (from 3 Jan 2016), and sells a dedicated 'Registers' product providing the official list of members. Current shareholders are therefore provable from the registry; historically the Business Profile also itemised shareholders and their shareholdings.

evidence.basis: ACRA — Types of ACRA Information Products ('Registers' product)
evidence.quote: Getting official lists of key people involved in a company, such as members, directors, CEOs, secretaries or auditors
evidence.note: ACRA (the registry) issues an official Register of members as a purchasable product, so the member list is obtainable directly from the registry base record.

caveats: For a public/listed company, shares held via CDP/nominees mean the ACRA register may not reflect beneficial holders; the confirmed case is the Pte Ltd.

summary: For a public company ACRA does not maintain the authoritative register of members: the company (or its share registrar; for listed companies the depository/CDP) keeps the register under the Companies Act. ACRA holds only limited shareholder information (e.g. substantial-shareholder disclosures), so a complete current list of shareholders is not reliably provable from the ACRA registry. The ACRA-maintained electronic register of members applies to private companies only.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1967 (Singapore), s.190(1) — the register of members of a PUBLIC company is kept by the company itself
evidence.quote: Every public company must keep a register of its members and enter therein
evidence.note: s.190(1)(a)-(d) require names/addresses, shareholdings, entry and cessation dates. The authoritative member register of a public company is company-held, not Registrar-held. Fetched from Singapore Statutes Online (sso.agc.gov.sg).

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1967, s.196A(1) — the Registrar-kept electronic register of members exists for PRIVATE companies only
evidence.quote: On and after 3 January 2016, the Registrar must, in respect of every private company, keep and maintain an electronic register of members of that company containing such information notified to the Registrar on or after that date.
evidence.note: The ACRA-maintained electronic register of members is expressly limited to private companies; there is no equivalent Registrar-kept member register for public companies — supporting not_provable_from_registry for a public company.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1967, s.19(6)(a) — on incorporation, subscribers of a public company are entered in the COMPANY-kept register (vs the Registrar-kept register for private companies)
evidence.quote: in the case of a public company — in the register of members kept by the public company under section 190
evidence.note: s.19(6)(b), same source: 'in the case of a private company — in the electronic register of members kept by the Registrar under section 196A.' The statute draws the public/private split explicitly.

evidence.basis: ACRA official page — Registers product (lists 'members' but for public/listed companies ACRA does not hold the full membership)
evidence.quote: Getting official lists of key people involved in a company, such as members, directors, CEOs, secretaries or auditors
evidence.note: The 'members' list is authoritative for private companies (ACRA e-register); for public companies the full register is kept by the company/share registrar, not ACRA.

caveats: Only substantial shareholders (typically >=5%) are disclosed to ACRA/SGX channels; not a full member list.
For listed public companies, beneficial holdings sit with the Central Depository (CDP), not ACRA.
NOMINEE HOLDINGS: registered holder vs beneficial owner distinction applies; RORC largely non-public.

summary: Every Singapore company must have a constitution, lodged with the Registrar as part of incorporation. The filed constitution is held by ACRA and retrievable via ACRA's 'Extracts' product (copies of past documents filed by the entity).

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1967, s.19(1)(a) — the constitution is submitted to the Registrar to incorporate
evidence.quote: submit to the Registrar the constitution of the proposed company and such other documents as may be prescribed
evidence.note: s.19(3), same source, verbatim: 'the Registrar must, subject to this Act, register the company by registering its constitution.' The constitution is the filed base document of incorporation, held by ACRA.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1967, s.26(2) — after any alteration, a copy of the constitution as adopted or altered is lodged with the Registrar
evidence.quote: lodge with the Registrar a copy of such resolution or other document or a copy of such order together with (unless the Registrar dispenses therewith) a copy of the constitution as adopted or altered, as the case may be.
evidence.note: s.26(3), same source: 'The Registrar must register every resolution, order or other document lodged with the Registrar under this Act that affects the constitution of a company'. So the current constitution (as altered) is on the ACRA file, subject to the dispensation proviso.

evidence.basis: ACRA official page — Types of ACRA information products (Extracts product)
evidence.quote: Getting copies of past documents filed by businesses, such as annual returns or audited accounts
evidence.note: Extracts (from $11) = copies of past FILED documents; the constitution is filed at incorporation (s.19) and on alteration (s.26(2)).

caveats: A company may have adopted a prescribed Model Constitution — s.26(1AB) permits adoption 'by reference to the title of the model constitution' and the resolution 'need not set out the text of the whole or part of the model constitution to be adopted' (verbatim, same source), so the filed record may reference the model rather than a bespoke document.

s.26(2) lets the Registrar dispense with lodging the consolidated altered constitution; in that case the currently effective version must be assembled from the original constitution plus registered amendment resolutions.
The ACRA 'Extracts' product page does not name the constitution explicitly; its retrievability is inferred from 'copies of past documents filed' plus the statutory filing duty (s.19/s.26).

summary: A public company, like any Singapore company, must have a constitution lodged with the Registrar at incorporation. The filed constitution is held by ACRA and retrievable via the 'Extracts' product (copies of past documents filed by the entity).

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1967, s.19(1)(a) — the constitution is submitted to the Registrar to incorporate (applies to every company, public included)
evidence.quote: submit to the Registrar the constitution of the proposed company and such other documents as may be prescribed
evidence.note: s.19(3), same source, verbatim: 'the Registrar must, subject to this Act, register the company by registering its constitution.'

evidence.basis: Companies Act 1967, s.26(2)-(3) — altered constitutions are lodged with and registered by the Registrar
evidence.quote: lodge with the Registrar a copy of such resolution or other document or a copy of such order together with (unless the Registrar dispenses therewith) a copy of the constitution as adopted or altered, as the case may be.
evidence.note: Constitutional amendments by special resolution (s.26(1)) reach the ACRA file, keeping the filed constitution reconstructable.

evidence.basis: ACRA official page — Extracts product
evidence.quote: Getting copies of past documents filed by businesses, such as annual returns or audited accounts
evidence.note: Extracts = copies of past FILED documents; constitution is filed at incorporation and on alteration.

caveats: Listed public companies file constitutional amendments by special resolution; the currently effective constitution may require the original plus amendments (s.26(2) allows the Registrar to dispense with lodging a consolidated altered copy).
The ACRA 'Extracts' product page does not name the constitution explicitly; retrievability is inferred from 'copies of past documents filed' plus the statutory filing duty (s.19/s.26).

summary: ACRA's Business Profile is an on-demand paid extract generated live from ACRA's register, giving current registered particulars (UEN, entity name, date of registration/incorporation, entity type, status, registered address, principal activities and position holders/officers). The Singapore analogue of a current register extract (Registerauszug).

evidence.basis: ACRA official page — Types of ACRA information products
evidence.quote: Getting basic entity information such as UEN, entity name, date of registration, and information about position holders, business owners or partners
evidence.note: Business Profile, $5.50; scope description.

evidence.basis: ACRA official page — Business Profile
evidence.note: Confirms Business Profile is a purchasable official record for entity verification; generated from Bizfile.

caveats: Business Profile reflects information as filed/updated in ACRA's register; currency depends on the company having filed changes (e.g. annual return) on time.

summary: As for a private company, the ACRA Business Profile is a live paid extract of the public company's current registered particulars (UEN, name, incorporation date, type, status, registered address, principal activities, officers). The Singapore register-extract equivalent, applying to all company types.

evidence.basis: ACRA official page — Types of ACRA information products
evidence.quote: Getting basic entity information such as UEN, entity name, date of registration, and information about position holders, business owners or partners
evidence.note: Same Business Profile product regardless of company type.

caveats: Currency depends on the company's filing compliance.

summary: For a d.d., current shareholders are not held in the court register. Shares are dematerialised and the share register (delniška knjiga) is maintained centrally by KDD (Centralna klirinško depotna družba), which is not publicly open. The court register and the founding documents show only the founders (ustanovitelji) at incorporation, which do not evidence current ownership. No registry document proves the current shareholder list.

evidence.basis: ZGD-1 — d.d. shares; dematerialised securities held in the central register maintained by KDD; court register records founders only
evidence.note: KDD central register is not a public source of a shareholder list; only founders appear at incorporation.

caveats: INFERENCE/UNVERIFIED verbatim: KDD-held share register and founders-only court register are established features of Slovenian d.d. law; article text not fetched.
Founders listed at incorporation are typically stale and do not prove current shareholders.

documentLabel: Members shown within the court register extract
documentLocal: Družbeniki (navedeni v izpisku iz sodnega registra)
summary: Slovenia is distinctive: the members (družbeniki) of a d.o.o. are entered directly into the public court register together with their business shares (poslovni delež), and changes of membership must be registered. The register extract therefore itself evidences the current members, so no separate shareholder-list document is required. The identity of members is public.

evidence.basis: ZGD-1 (Companies Act) — d.o.o. members and their business shares are registered facts; changes require entry in the court register
evidence.note: Article numbers (registration of d.o.o. and of transfers of business shares) not verified verbatim; membership is a registered, public datum shown on the extract.

caveats: The proof is the register extract itself (same base document as REGISTERAUSZUG); there is no distinct filed 'seznam družbenikov' needed for d.o.o.
INFERENCE/UNVERIFIED verbatim: members-in-register is a well-established feature of Slovenian d.o.o. law but exact ZGD-1 article text was not fetched.
The registered members reflect the register; beneficial/indirect ownership is not shown.

summary: The constitution of a d.d. is the statut (statute). It is filed with the court register on incorporation and on amendment and is kept in the public collection of documents (zbirka listin), generally downloadable free through the e-Sodni register. It is a separate registry document from the extract and therefore proved by an additional document.

evidence.basis: ZSReg (Zakon o sodnem registru), 7. člen — javnost sodnega registra (Uradni list RS 13/94)
evidence.quote: V sodni register vpisani podatki so javni in jih sme vsakdo pregledovati, prepisovati ali zahtevati, naj se mu izda overjeni izpisek iz sodnega registra. Pregledovati in prepisovati se smejo tudi listine, na katerih temeljijo vpisi v sodni register.
evidence.note: Register data are public and anyone may inspect/copy them or demand a certified extract; the DOCUMENTS underlying the entries (incl. the statut) may likewise be inspected and copied — direct statutory support that the filed constitution is obtainable from the registry.

evidence.basis: ZSReg, 2. člen (3. odstavek) — zbirka listin
evidence.quote: Listine, na podlagi katerih je bil opravljen vpis posameznega pravno pomembnega dejstva, ali za katere zakon določa, da se predložijo registrskemu sodišču, se vložijo v zbirko listin.
evidence.note: Documents on which entries are based, or which must by law be submitted to the register court, are filed in the collection of documents (zbirka listin) — the statut sits there.

evidence.basis: ZSReg, 28. člen (1. odstavek) — priloge k predlogu za vpis
evidence.quote: Predlagatelj mora predlogu priložiti listine, na katerih temeljijo podatki, ki se vpisujejo v sodni register. ter druge listine, če zakon tako določa, v izvirniku oziroma overjenem prepisu.
evidence.note: The applicant must attach the documents underlying the registered data (for a d.d. incorporation: the statut) in original or certified copy — the constitution is necessarily on file.

evidence.basis: AJPES — official documentation of eObjave / informatizirana zbirka listin sodnega registra
evidence.quote: Listine, ki so podlaga za vpis v sodni register, sklepi sodišč in zbirka listin se objavlja od 1. 2. 2008 dalje
evidence.note: AJPES confirms that documents underlying court-register entries and the zbirka listin have been published electronically since 1 Feb 2008; the same page states verbatim 'Vsi objavljeni podatki in listine so brezplačno dostopne.' (all published data and documents are accessible free of charge).

evidence.basis: ZGD-1 — the d.d. is governed by its statut (constitutive document)
evidence.note: Definitional basis that the d.d.'s constitution is the statut. ZGD-1 text NOT fetched verbatim — pisrs.si is a JavaScript application that could not be rendered this session.

caveats: The filed statute may lag the latest consolidated amendments.

ZSReg quotes are from the original 1994 text (Uradni list RS 13/94); the act has since been amended, but current practice (electronic publication of the zbirka listin, free access) is confirmed by AJPES's own documentation.

AJPES notes that only documents whose publication the law prescribes are publicly accessible online; for other filed documents (which may contain personal data) one must apply to the competent register court.
ZGD-1's definitional provision (d.d. statut) was not fetched verbatim (pisrs.si unrenderable); the filing-and-publicity chain is verbatim from ZSReg and AJPES.

documentLabel: Articles of association / act of establishment
documentLocal: Družbena pogodba (oz. akt o ustanovitvi za enoosebno d.o.o.)
summary: The constitutive document of a d.o.o. — the družbena pogodba (multi-member) or akt o ustanovitvi (single-member) — is filed with the court register and kept in the public collection of documents (zbirka listin). It is a separate document from the register extract but is publicly accessible, generally downloadable free of charge through the e-Sodni register. It is therefore proved by an additional registry document.

evidence.basis: ZSReg (Zakon o sodnem registru), 7. člen — javnost sodnega registra (Uradni list RS 13/94)
evidence.quote: V sodni register vpisani podatki so javni in jih sme vsakdo pregledovati, prepisovati ali zahtevati, naj se mu izda overjeni izpisek iz sodnega registra. Pregledovati in prepisovati se smejo tudi listine, na katerih temeljijo vpisi v sodni register.
evidence.note: Register data are public and the DOCUMENTS underlying the entries (incl. the družbena pogodba / akt o ustanovitvi) may be inspected and copied — direct statutory support that the filed constitution is obtainable from the registry.

evidence.basis: ZSReg, 2. člen (3. odstavek) — zbirka listin
evidence.quote: Listine, na podlagi katerih je bil opravljen vpis posameznega pravno pomembnega dejstva, ali za katere zakon določa, da se predložijo registrskemu sodišču, se vložijo v zbirko listin.
evidence.note: Documents on which entries are based are filed in the zbirka listin — the d.o.o. constitution sits there.

evidence.basis: ZSReg, 28. člen (1. odstavek) — priloge k predlogu za vpis
evidence.quote: Predlagatelj mora predlogu priložiti listine, na katerih temeljijo podatki, ki se vpisujejo v sodni register. ter druge listine, če zakon tako določa, v izvirniku oziroma overjenem prepisu.
evidence.note: The applicant must attach the documents underlying the registered data (for a d.o.o. incorporation: the družbena pogodba or akt o ustanovitvi) in original or certified copy.

evidence.basis: AJPES — official documentation of eObjave / informatizirana zbirka listin sodnega registra
evidence.quote: Listine, ki so podlaga za vpis v sodni register, sklepi sodišč in zbirka listin se objavlja od 1. 2. 2008 dalje
evidence.note: AJPES confirms electronic publication of the zbirka listin since 1 Feb 2008; the same page states verbatim 'Vsi objavljeni podatki in listine so brezplačno dostopne.' (all published data and documents are accessible free of charge).

evidence.basis: ZGD-1 — the d.o.o. is founded by družbena pogodba / akt o ustanovitvi
evidence.note: Definitional basis that the d.o.o.'s constitution is the družbena pogodba (multi-member) or akt o ustanovitvi (single-member). ZGD-1 text NOT fetched verbatim — pisrs.si is a JavaScript application that could not be rendered this session.

caveats: The filed constitution may be an older amended version; it need not reflect the very latest consolidated text.

ZSReg quotes are from the original 1994 text (Uradni list RS 13/94); the act has since been amended, but current practice (electronic publication of the zbirka listin, free access) is confirmed by AJPES's own documentation.

AJPES notes that only documents whose publication the law prescribes are publicly accessible online; for other filed documents (which may contain personal data) one must apply to the competent register court.
ZGD-1's definitional provision (družbena pogodba / akt o ustanovitvi) was not fetched verbatim (pisrs.si unrenderable); the filing-and-publicity chain is verbatim from ZSReg and AJPES.

summary: As for a d.o.o., a d.d. has a public court register extract (izpisek) issued by AJPES via the e-Sodni register. It is the base document evidencing existence, seat, activity, share capital, management/supervisory bodies and representatives, and is freely retrievable online.

evidence.basis: ZSReg — public court register and free extract; AJPES e-Sodni register
evidence.note: Standard public base document; verbatim text not fetched.

caveats: Verbatim statutory text not fetched (SPA sources, search budget exhausted).

summary: The Slovenian court register (sodni register), operated by AJPES via the e-Sodni register, issues a public register extract (izpisek) for every registered company. For a d.o.o. this extract is the base register document evidencing legal existence, seat, business activity, share capital, representatives and members. It is freely retrievable online.

evidence.basis: ZSReg (Zakon o sodnem registru) — public court register and free extract; AJPES e-Sodni register
evidence.note: Extract (izpisek) is the standard public base document; article text not fetched verbatim (sources are JS SPAs).

evidence.basis: AJPES homepage description of PRS
evidence.note: Confirmed live: PRS is a 'public database of all business entities with headquarters in the Republic of Slovenia.'

caveats: Verbatim statutory text (ZSReg) could not be fetched; primary sites are SPAs and web-search budget was exhausted.
INFERENCE: the register extract being the free public base document is standard EU/AJPES practice and consistent with prior jurisdiction findings.

summary: An a.s. shareholder list is not obtainable from the commercial register. The register records the founders (zakladatelia) as at incorporation and the statutory/supervisory bodies, but not the ongoing body of shareholders. Current shareholders of book-entry shares (zaknihované akcie) are held in the register of holders maintained by the Central Securities Depository (Centrálny depozitár cenných papierov, CDCP); for physical registered shares (listinné akcie na meno) the list of shareholders (zoznam akcionárov) is kept privately by the company itself. Neither is a public register document.

evidence.basis: Obchodný zákonník 513/1991, § 156 — evidence of registered shares (register of holders kept by the securities depository / list of shareholders kept by the company); Act 566/2001 on Securities, on CDCP registers
evidence.note: Shareholders evidenced outside the commercial register; verbatim not captured.

evidence.basis: Centrálny depozitár cenných papierov SR (CDCP)
evidence.note: Holder register for book-entry shares; not public per-issuer document

caveats: Verbatim statute text UNVERIFIED — asserted from domain knowledge.
Exception: a single-member a.s. (jediný akcionár) — the sole shareholder IS recorded in the commercial register, so for that narrow case the extract does evidence the (sole) shareholder.
The register's founder (zakladatelia) entry reflects incorporation only and is not updated for later share transfers, so it is not a current shareholder list.
CDCP / company shareholder lists are access-restricted, not open public documents.

summary: For an s.r.o. the members (spoločníci) are a mandatory register entry: each member is recorded with the amount of their contribution (výška vkladu) and the extent of its payment (rozsah splatenia). They appear on the public výpis, so the register extract itself is the authoritative member list — no separate Gesellschafterliste document is needed or exists. This is the key difference from an a.s.

evidence.basis: Act 530/2003 Coll., § 2 — for a s.r.o. the register records each spoločník together with výška vkladu and rozsah jeho splatenia; Obchodný zákonník 513/1991, § 105–106
evidence.note: Members are recorded in the register and shown on the extract; verbatim not captured.

caveats: Verbatim statute text UNVERIFIED — asserted from domain knowledge, not quoted from a fetched source.
The extract reflects the currently registered members; changes take effect only on registration, so a very recent transfer may not yet appear.

documentLabel: Founding deed and articles of association
documentLocal: Zakladateľská zmluva / zakladateľská listina a stanovy
summary: The constitution of an a.s. comprises the founding instrument — zakladateľská zmluva (multiple founders) or zakladateľská listina (single founder) — together with the stanovy (articles of association). Both are filed in the collection of deeds (zbierka listín) of the commercial register, which is public, and are retrievable as separate documents beyond the standard extract. Amended stanovy are deposited as a consolidated wording.

evidence.basis: Act 530/2003 Coll., § 3 — zbierka listín (founding instrument and stanovy and their úplné znenie); Obchodný zákonník 513/1991, § 162–163 (zakladateľská zmluva/listina, stanovy)
evidence.note: Founding documents deposited in and publicly accessible from the zbierka listín; verbatim not captured.

evidence.basis: Obchodný register SR — zbierka listín access

caveats: Verbatim statute text UNVERIFIED — asserted from domain knowledge.
Single-founder a.s. uses a zakladateľská listina instead of a zakladateľská zmluva; the stanovy are the substantive constitution in either case.
Latest consolidated stanovy depend on the company having filed them; older filings may be scanned images.

documentLocal: Spoločenská zmluva (alebo zakladateľská listina, ak je jediný spoločník)
summary: The constitution of an s.r.o. is the spoločenská zmluva, or a zakladateľská listina where there is a single founder. It is filed in the collection of deeds (zbierka listín) of the commercial register, which is public, and is retrievable as a separate (additional) document beyond the standard extract. Amendments are deposited as a consolidated wording (úplné znenie).

evidence.basis: Act 530/2003 Coll., § 3 — zbierka listín contents (spoločenská zmluva / zakladateľská listina and their úplné znenie); Obchodný zákonník 513/1991, § 110
evidence.note: Founding document deposited in and publicly accessible from the zbierka listín; verbatim not captured.

evidence.basis: Obchodný register SR — zbierka listín access
evidence.note: Collection of deeds browsable per company

caveats: Verbatim statute text UNVERIFIED — asserted from domain knowledge.
Single-founder s.r.o. uses a zakladateľská listina rather than a spoločenská zmluva.
Availability of the latest consolidated wording depends on the company having filed it; older filings may be scanned images.

summary: The base register document for an a.s. is likewise the výpis z obchodného registra from Obchodný register SR. It publicly shows company name, seat, IČO, activities, statutory body (predstavenstvo), supervisory board (dozorná rada) and registered capital / share structure. It is the direct equivalent of a German Registerauszug.

evidence.basis: Act 530/2003 Coll., § 2 and § 11; Obchodný zákonník 513/1991, § 154 et seq.
evidence.note: Extract is the public base document produced by ORSR; verbatim not captured.

evidence.basis: Obchodný register SR public portal

caveats: Verbatim statute text UNVERIFIED — asserted from domain knowledge, not quoted from a fetched source.

summary: The base register document for an s.r.o. is the výpis z obchodného registra issued by the Obchodný register SR (orsr.sk / ru.justice.sk). It is the standard, publicly retrievable extract carrying company name, seat, IČO, business activities, statutory body and registered capital. It is the direct equivalent of a German Registerauszug.

evidence.note: Verbatim not captured (Slov-Lex is JS-rendered; not fetchable). Extract is the public base document produced by ORSR.

evidence.note: Výpis available online per company

legalFormLocal: บริษัทจำกัด
documentLocal: บัญชีรายชื่อผู้ถือหุ้น (บอจ.5)
summary: Thailand is a notable case: shareholders ARE obtainable from the registry. The CCC obliges the directors to send the DBD Registrar, at least once a year within 14 days after the ordinary (annual) general meeting, a copy of the full list of shareholders (form BOJ.5), and the list is also filed at incorporation. A DBD-certified copy of the BOJ.5 is therefore an additional registry document that evidences the shareholders. It is separate from the base affidavit, which does not carry shareholders.

evidence.basis: Civil and Commercial Code, Section 1139 (second paragraph)
evidence.quote: It shall be the duty of the directors to send once at least in every year to the Registrar, and not later than on the fourteenth day after the ordinary meeting, a copy of the list of all shareholders at the time such meeting and those who have ceased to be shareholders since the date of the last ordinary meeting. Such list shall include all particulars specified in the foregoing section.
evidence.note: 'The foregoing section' is Section 1138 (names, addresses, occupations, shares held, amounts paid). The filed form is BOJ.5 (บอจ.5).

evidence.basis: Civil and Commercial Code, Section 1138 (particulars the list must contain)
evidence.quote: The names and addresses, and occupations, if any, of the shareholders ... the amount paid or agreed to be considered as paid on the shares of each shareholder.

caveats: The BOJ.5 on file reflects the position as at the last AGM (updated at least annually); intra-year transfers may not yet be reflected.
Product/form label 'BOJ.5' is established DBD practice; not fetched verbatim from dbd.go.th.

legalFormLocal: บริษัทมหาชนจำกัด
documentLabel: List of Shareholders as at the AGM date (form BorMorJor.006)
documentLocal: บัญชีรายชื่อผู้ถือหุ้น (บมจ.006)
summary: A public company keeps a register of shareholders and must submit to the DBD Registrar a copy of the list of shareholders as at the date of the annual general meeting (form BorMorJor.006 / บมจ.006). A DBD-certified copy is therefore obtainable as an additional registry document. For SET-listed companies the live register is maintained by the Thailand Securities Depository (TSD), but the AGM-date snapshot is still filed with the DBD.

evidence.basis: Public Limited Companies Act B.E. 2535 (1992), Section 64 — annual filing of the shareholder list with the Registrar
evidence.quote: Section 64. The company shall file a list of persons who are shareholders on the date of the annual ordinary meeting which includes the particulars required under section 39 paragraph two to the Registrar within one month as from the date of completion of the meeting.
evidence.note: Direct statutory basis for the AGM-date shareholder-list filing with the DBD Registrar (form BorMorJor.006 in practice). English translation of the Thai statute; extraction whitespace normalized.

evidence.basis: Public Limited Companies Act B.E. 2535, Section 39 paragraph two — shareholder-list particulars filed at registration
evidence.quote: In applying for registration under paragraph one, the board of directors shall simultaneously submit the articles of association and the list of shareholders which indicate names, nationalities, addresses, number of shares held and share certificate serial numbers, including the minutes of the statutory meeting.
evidence.note: Defines the particulars (names, nationalities, addresses, shares held) that the Section 64 annual list must also contain; the initial list is filed with the Registrar at company registration.

evidence.basis: Public Limited Companies Act B.E. 2535, Section 10 — public inspection and certified copies of Registrar-held documents
evidence.quote: Any person who has paid the prescribed fee shall be entitled to inspect or to copy the contents of the register or the documents kept by the Registrar or to request the Registrar to make copies or photocopies of any documents together with a certification by the Registrar that they are correct or to request the Registrar to issue a certificate of any registered particulars.
evidence.note: Any person can obtain Registrar-certified copies of filed documents — making the filed shareholder list obtainable as a registry document.

caveats: The Section 64 filing is an annual snapshot as at the AGM date; for SET-listed companies the live register sits with the Thailand Securities Depository (TSD), so the DBD copy can lag current holdings.

The company-held register of shareholders (Sections 61-62) is the live record; Section 63 inspection rights over it belong to shareholders, not the public — public access runs through the Registrar-filed copies (Sections 39/64 + Section 10).
Quotes are from an English translation of the Thai statute (AustLII-hosted PDF; PDF text extraction whitespace normalized); the Thai Government Gazette text is authoritative.

legalFormLocal: บริษัทจำกัด
documentLabel: Memorandum of Association (BOJ.2) + Articles of Association / company regulations
documentLocal: หนังสือบริคณห์สนธิ (บอจ.2) และข้อบังคับของบริษัท
summary: The constitution is filed with the DBD. The memorandum of association (หนังสือบริคณห์สนธิ, form BOJ.2) is registered before incorporation, and the company's regulations (articles of association), 'if any', are filed together with the registration application. DBD-certified copies of both are obtainable as additional registry documents.

evidence.basis: Civil and Commercial Code, Section 1111 (paragraph on accompanying documents)
evidence.quote: The application must be accompanied by the copy of the regulations, if any, and of the proceedings of the statutory meeting, both certified by the signature of at least one director.
evidence.note: 'Regulations' = articles of association. Filed with the Registrar and retrievable as a certified copy.

evidence.basis: Civil and Commercial Code, Section 1098 (memorandum contents)
evidence.quote: The name of the proposed company, which must always end with the word 'limited'. ... The objects of the company. ... A declaration that the liability of the shareholders shall be limited. ... The amount of share capital with which the company proposes to be registered, and the divisions thereof into shares of a fixed amount.

caveats: Articles ('regulations') are filed only 'if any' — a company that adopts the CCC's default statutory regulations may have no separately filed AOA; the memorandum (BOJ.2) is always filed.
MOA and AOA are two distinct documents; together they form the German-sense Gesellschaftsvertrag.

legalFormLocal: บริษัทมหาชนจำกัด
documentLabel: Memorandum of Association + Articles of Association
documentLocal: หนังสือบริคณห์สนธิและข้อบังคับของบริษัทมหาชนจำกัด
summary: Under the Public Limited Companies Act B.E. 2535 the promoters register the memorandum of association with the DBD Registrar, and the articles of association are adopted and filed as part of incorporation. DBD-certified copies of both are obtainable as additional registry documents.

evidence.basis: Public Limited Companies Act B.E. 2535, Section 19 — registration of the memorandum of association
evidence.quote: The memorandum shall be signed by all the promoters and shall be applied for registration to the Registrar.
evidence.note: The memorandum of association (nangsue borikonsonthi) is registered with the DBD Registrar; Section 18 prescribes its minimum contents (name, objects, registered capital, head office, promoters).

evidence.basis: Public Limited Companies Act B.E. 2535, Section 39 paragraph two — articles of association filed at company registration
evidence.quote: In applying for registration under paragraph one, the board of directors shall simultaneously submit the articles of association and the list of shareholders which indicate names, nationalities, addresses, number of shares held and share certificate serial numbers, including the minutes of the statutory meeting.
evidence.note: The articles of association (kho bangkap) are filed with the Registrar as part of the company registration.

evidence.basis: Public Limited Companies Act B.E. 2535, Section 31 paragraph two — registration of amendments
evidence.quote: For amending the memorandum of association or the articles of association of the company, the company shall apply to register the amendment within fourteen days as from the date on which the resolution was passed at the meeting.
evidence.note: Amendments to MOA/AOA must be registered within 14 days, keeping the Registrar-held constitutional documents current.

evidence.basis: Public Limited Companies Act B.E. 2535, Section 10 — public inspection and certified copies of Registrar-held documents
evidence.quote: Any person who has paid the prescribed fee shall be entitled to inspect or to copy the contents of the register or the documents kept by the Registrar or to request the Registrar to make copies or photocopies of any documents together with a certification by the Registrar that they are correct or to request the Registrar to issue a certificate of any registered particulars.
evidence.note: Any person can obtain Registrar-certified copies of the filed MOA and AOA.

caveats: MOA and AOA are separate documents; both are DBD-held for public companies (memorandum registered under Section 19, articles filed under Section 39 paragraph two).
Quotes are from an English translation of the Thai statute (AustLII-hosted PDF; PDF text extraction whitespace normalized); the Thai Government Gazette text is authoritative.

legalFormLocal: บริษัทจำกัด
documentLabel: Company Affidavit / Certificate of the Registrar (DBD)
documentLocal: หนังสือรับรองบริษัท (หนังสือรับรอง)
summary: A private limited company is registered with the DBD Registrar, who delivers a certificate of registration and, on request, issues the standard company affidavit (หนังสือรับรอง). This affidavit is the DBD's official register extract: it states the registration number, company name, registered capital, registered office, objects, directors and their signing authority. It is the base registry document and is obtainable as a DBD-certified document.

evidence.basis: Civil and Commercial Code, Title XXII, Ch. IV, Section 1111 (final paragraph)
evidence.quote: A certificate of registration shall be delivered to the company.
evidence.note: The DBD affidavit (หนังสือรับรอง) is the certified extract of these registered particulars; the DBD website was not fetchable for a verbatim product label.

evidence.basis: Civil and Commercial Code, Section 1111(1)-(8) (contents of the register entry)
evidence.quote: The application and entry in the register must contain, in conformity with the decisions of the statutory meeting, the following particulars: ... 5. The names, occupations and addresses of the directors. 6. If the directors have power to act separately, their respective powers and the number or names of the directors whose signature is binding on the company. ... 8. The address of the principal of business office and of all branch offices.
evidence.note: These registered particulars are what the affidavit certifies.

caveats: The DBD affidavit lists directors, capital, objects and signing authority, but NOT shareholders (shareholders sit on the separate BOJ.5 list).
The exact English product label used by DBD ('Company Affidavit' / 'Certificate') could not be fetched verbatim from dbd.go.th (site returned 404 to WebFetch); label given is established practice.

legalFormLocal: บริษัทมหาชนจำกัด
documentLabel: Company Affidavit / Certificate of the Registrar (DBD)
documentLocal: หนังสือรับรองบริษัทมหาชนจำกัด (หนังสือรับรอง)
summary: A public limited company is incorporated by registration with the DBD Registrar under the Public Limited Companies Act B.E. 2535. The DBD issues the company affidavit (หนังสือรับรอง) as the official register extract, stating registration number, name, registered capital, registered office, objects, directors and signing authority. It is the base registry document.

evidence.basis: Public Limited Companies Act B.E. 2535 (registration of the company with the DBD Registrar)
evidence.note: INFERENCE/UNVERIFIED verbatim: PLCA English text (krisdika/SEC PDFs) returned 403/self-signed-cert and could not be fetched; DBD affidavit for public companies is well-established DBD practice, structurally identical to the private-company affidavit.

caveats: Verbatim PLCA provision not fetched (source PDFs blocked); status rests on established DBD practice and the PLCA registration regime.
As with the private company, the public-company affidavit does not list shareholders.

documentLocal: Pay defteri (şirket içi hisse defteri — ticaret siciline tescil edilmez)
summary: For an Anonim Şirket the shareholders (pay sahipleri) are NOT registered in the Trade Registry nor published in the Gazette. Under TTK art. 499 the company records its shareholders (name, title, address) in its own private share ledger (pay defteri), and for company purposes only the person entered in that ledger counts as shareholder. The pay defteri is an internal company book, not a registry document, so current shareholders are not provable from the registry. EXCEPTIONS registered/gazetted: the sole shareholder where the shareholder count drops to one (art. 338(2)), and the founders (kurucular) named in the esas sözleşme at incorporation.

evidence.basis: Türk Ticaret Kanunu (Law 6102) art. 499(1) and (4)
evidence.quote: Şirket, senede bağlanmamış pay ve nama yazılı pay senedi sahipleriyle, intifa hakkı sahiplerini, ad, soyad, unvan ve adresleriyle, pay defterine kaydeder. ... Şirketle ilişkilerde, sadece pay defterinde kayıtlı bulunan kimse pay sahibi ve intifa hakkı sahibi olarak kabul edilir.
evidence.note: Shareholders live in the internal pay defteri, not the trade registry.

evidence.basis: Türk Ticaret Kanunu (Law 6102) art. 354(1)(a)-(h)
evidence.quote: Şirket esas sözleşmesinin tamamı, ... ticaret siciline tescil ve Türkiye Ticaret Sicili Gazetesinde ilan olunur. ... g) Yönetim kurulu üyeleriyle şirketi temsile yetkili kimselerin ad ve soyadları, unvanları, yerleşme yerleri ve vatandaşlıkları.
evidence.note: The registered/gazetted particulars list board members and representatives — NOT the shareholders.

evidence.basis: Türk Ticaret Kanunu (Law 6102) art. 338(2)
evidence.quote: ... şirketin tek pay sahipli bir anonim şirket olduğunu tescil ve ilan ettirir. ... tek pay sahibinin adı, yerleşim yeri ve vatandaşlığı da tescil ve ilan edilir.
evidence.note: EXCEPTION: a sole shareholder is registered and gazetted; likewise founders at incorporation.

caveats: EXCEPTION: sole shareholder (art. 338(2)) and incorporation founders are registry-provable; the general body of shareholders is not. Multi-shareholder AŞ shareholder proof requires the internal pay defteri / hazirun (attendance) list, which are not registry documents.

documentLocal: Türkiye Ticaret Sicili Gazetesi tescil ilanı / ticaret sicili kayıt örneği (ortaklar)
summary: For a Limited Şirket the partners (ortaklar) and each partner's capital share ARE registered particulars and are published in the Trade Registry Gazette. TTK art. 587(1)(e) lists each real-person partner's name and residence, each legal-person partner's title/seat, and every partner's undertaken capital share among the matters registered and gazetted. Share transfers (pay geçişi) must be registered under art. 598, so the registry/Gazette reflects the CURRENT partners. Proof therefore exists as a public registry document (Gazette announcement / registry record), not merely as parsed data.

evidence.basis: Türk Ticaret Kanunu (Law 6102) art. 587(1) and (1)(e)
evidence.quote: Şirket sözleşmesinin tamamı, ... şirketin merkezinin bulunduğu yer ticaret siciline tescil ve Türkiye Ticaret Sicili Gazetesinde ilan olunur. ... e) Gerçek kişi ortağın adı ve soyadı, yerleşim yeri, tüzel kişi ortakların unvanı, merkezleri ve her ortağın üstlendiği esas sermaye payları.

evidence.basis: Türk Ticaret Kanunu (Law 6102) art. 598(1)
evidence.quote: Esas sermaye paylarının geçişlerinin tescil edilmesi için, şirket müdürleri tarafından ticaret siciline başvurulur.
evidence.note: Share transfers are registered, keeping the registered partner list current.

caveats: The registered/gazetted particulars capture partners as of incorporation and each registered transfer; the current partner set is derived from the founding sözleşme plus subsequent art. 598 transfer registrations. There is no single standalone statutory 'shareholder list' document — proof is the Gazette announcement(s) / certified registry record.

documentLocal: Esas sözleşme (Türkiye Ticaret Sicili Gazetesinde ilan / ticaret sicilinde saklanan)
summary: The entire articles of association (esas sözleşme — the constitution of an Anonim Şirket) are registered with the Trade Registry and published in full in the Türkiye Ticaret Sicili Gazetesi under TTK art. 354(1); underlying documents are archived and available as certified copies under art. 35. The constitution is directly provable as a registry-published base document.

evidence.basis: Türk Ticaret Kanunu (Law 6102) art. 354(1)
evidence.quote: Şirket esas sözleşmesinin tamamı, ... şirketin kuruluşunu izleyen otuz gün içinde şirketin merkezinin bulunduğu yer ticaret siciline tescil ve Türkiye Ticaret Sicili Gazetesinde ilan olunur.

caveats: The Gazette publishes the founding esas sözleşme plus later amendment announcements; a consolidated current text may require combining announcements or requesting the certified copy from the registry (art. 35).

documentLocal: Şirket sözleşmesi (Türkiye Ticaret Sicili Gazetesinde ilan / ticaret sicilinde saklanan)
summary: The entire company contract (şirket sözleşmesi — the constitution / articles of a Limited Şirket) is registered with the Trade Registry and published in full in the Türkiye Ticaret Sicili Gazetesi under TTK art. 587(1), and the underlying documents are archived and available as certified copies under art. 35. The constitution is directly provable as a registry-published base document.

evidence.basis: Türk Ticaret Kanunu (Law 6102) art. 587(1)
evidence.quote: Şirket sözleşmesinin tamamı, kurucuların imzalarının ticaret sicili müdürlüğünde yetkilendirilmiş personelin huzurunda imzalandığı tarihi izleyen otuz gün içinde, şirketin merkezinin bulunduğu yer ticaret siciline tescil ve Türkiye Ticaret Sicili Gazetesinde ilan olunur.

evidence.basis: Türkiye Ticaret Sicili Gazetesi (official gazette portal)
evidence.note: Gazette (art. 37 TTK) archives certified copies of published announcements (incl. company contracts) from 1957 to date.

caveats: The Gazette publishes the full sözleşme at incorporation and amendments as gazetted; a fully consolidated current text may require combining the founding text with later amendment announcements or requesting the certified copy held at the registry (art. 35).

documentLocal: Ticaret Sicili Tasdiknamesi / Faaliyet Belgesi (ticaret sicili kayıt örneği)
summary: Same registry regime as for the Ltd Şti: the Trade Registry issues a certified extract/record of the AŞ's registered particulars. TTK art. 35 guarantees public inspection of registry content and certified copies of filed documents; the current-extract documents in practice are the Ticaret Sicili Tasdiknamesi and the Chamber's Faaliyet Belgesi. Registry-issued base document.

evidence.basis: Türk Ticaret Kanunu (Law 6102) art. 35(2) and (4)
evidence.quote: Herkes ticaret sicilinin içeriğini ve müdürlükte saklanan tüm senet ve belgeleri inceleyebileceği gibi giderini ödeyerek bunların onaylı suretlerini de alabilir.

caveats: As with the Ltd Şti, TTK defines no single named 'extract'; provability of a current registry extract is high, exact document label (Tasdikname / Faaliyet Belgesi) is by administrative practice.

documentLocal: Ticaret Sicili Tasdiknamesi / Faaliyet Belgesi (ticaret sicili kayıt örneği)
summary: The Trade Registry (Ticaret Sicili Müdürlüğü, coordinated via MERSIS) issues a certified extract/record of the registered particulars. TTK art. 35 guarantees public access to registry content and to certified copies (onaylı suret) of the filed documents; the practical current-extract documents are the Ticaret Sicili Tasdiknamesi and the Chamber's Faaliyet Belgesi. This is a registry-issued base document.

evidence.basis: Türk Ticaret Kanunu (Law 6102) art. 35(2) and (4)
evidence.quote: Herkes ticaret sicilinin içeriğini ve müdürlükte saklanan tüm senet ve belgeleri inceleyebileceği gibi giderini ödeyerek bunların onaylı suretlerini de alabilir. Bir hususun sicilde kayıtlı olup olmadığına dair onaylı belge de istenebilir.
evidence.note: art. 35(4): 'İlan, Türkiye genelinde sicil kayıtlarının ilanına özgü Türkiye Ticaret Sicili Gazetesi ile yapılır.' Public register + certified copies are the legal basis for a register extract.

caveats: TTK does not define a single named 'register extract'; in practice the current extract is the Ticaret Sicili Tasdiknamesi (registry) or Faaliyet Belgesi (Chamber of Commerce). Provability is high; the exact document label is by administrative practice.

summary: For a 股份有限公司 the shareholders are recorded in the shareholder register (股東名簿) which Company Act Art 169 requires the company to keep itself; it is not filed with or issued by the MOEA. The articles (Art 129) do not list individual shareholders, and the registry only shows promoters, directors and supervisors (Art 393(2) items 4-5). Ordinary shareholders therefore cannot be evidenced by any MOEA/GCIS document; only the company's internal 股東名簿 (or, for listed firms, TDCC records) evidences them.

evidence.basis: Company Act (公司法) Art 169
evidence.quote: 股東名簿應編號記載下列事項：一、各股東之姓名或名稱、住所或居所。二、各股東之股數；發行股票者，其股票號數。三、發給股票之年、月、日。四、發行特別股者，並應註明特別種類字樣。
evidence.note: The shareholder register is maintained by the company, not the registry.

evidence.basis: Company Act (公司法) Art 393, para 2 items 4-5
evidence.quote: 執行業務或代表公司之股東。
evidence.note: Only representative shareholders (unlimited-type) and directors/supervisors (item 5) are disclosed; no general shareholder list for a company limited by shares.

caveats: Only promoters (發起人, in incorporation documents) and directors/supervisors reach the registry; the ongoing shareholder body does not.
The company-held 股東名簿 (Art 169) is the actual shareholder list but is not a registry document; obtaining it requires the company's cooperation (or, for TWSE/TPEx-listed companies, TDCC 集保 records).

documentLabel: Company articles of incorporation (which by statute list every shareholder and each shareholder's capital contribution)
summary: For a 有限公司 the shareholders and their capital contributions are not a separate freely-public register item, but Company Act Art 101 requires the articles of incorporation (章程) to state each shareholder's name and each shareholder's capital contribution, and Art 111(3) confirms any change of shareholder/contribution is effected by amending those provisions of the 章程. The 章程 is a filed document that MOEA discloses under Art 393(2) item 10, obtainable by any person on application (online only with company consent). Thus the shareholder list is provable, but via the additional 章程 document rather than the base extract.

evidence.basis: Company Act (公司法) Art 101, para 1 — mandatory contents of a limited company's articles of incorporation
evidence.quote: 公司章程應載明下列事項：一、公司名稱。二、所營事業。三、股東姓名或名稱。四、資本總額及各股東出資額。
evidence.note: The articles (章程) of a 有限公司 MUST state each shareholder's name (item 3) and each shareholder's capital contribution (item 4) — so the filed 章程 is a complete shareholder list. Fetched verbatim from the official MOJ law database.

evidence.basis: Company Act (公司法) Art 111, para 3
evidence.quote: 前二項轉讓，不同意之股東有優先受讓權；如不承受，視為同意轉讓，並同意修改章程有關股東及其出資額事項。
evidence.note: Confirms the 有限公司 articles (章程) contain the 'shareholders and their capital contributions' matters, updated on any transfer.

evidence.basis: Company Act (公司法) Art 393, para 2 item 10 + para 3
evidence.quote: 前項第一款至第九款，任何人得至主管機關之資訊網站查閱；第十款，經公司同意者，亦同。
evidence.note: Item 10 = 公司章程. Disclosable to any person; online display of the 章程 requires company consent, but application-based 查閱/抄錄/複製 is available regardless.

caveats: The separate at-company shareholder roster under Art 103 (股東名簿) is NOT a registry document — it is held by the company; the registry-provable proof is the 章程.

INFERENCE: classified as additional (not base) because the full shareholder roster is not on the free public extract; if the MOEA full registration file / 登記事項卡 is treated as showing all shareholders and 出資額, it could instead be read as proved_by_base_document.
Online availability of the 章程 on the MOEA information website depends on company consent per Art 393(3); offline any-person application under Art 393(2) is the reliable route.

summary: A 股份有限公司's articles of incorporation (章程) are filed in registration and enumerated as a publicly-disclosable item under Company Act Art 393(2) item 10; any person may inspect, transcribe or copy them on application to MOEA (online display requires company consent per Art 393(3)). This is a distinct document from the base register extract, so it is an additional registry document.

evidence.basis: Company Act (公司法) Art 393, para 2 item 10
evidence.quote: 公司章程。
evidence.note: Articles of incorporation are an enumerated publicly-disclosable registration item for all company types.

evidence.basis: Company Act (公司法) Art 393, para 3
evidence.quote: 前項第一款至第九款，任何人得至主管機關之資訊網站查閱；第十款，經公司同意者，亦同。
evidence.note: Online display of the 章程 requires company consent; application-based any-person access remains under para 2.

caveats: Free online access to the 章程 requires the company's consent (Art 393(3)); guaranteed any-person access is via application to MOEA under Art 393(2).
For a 股份有限公司 the articles state authorized/issued capital and share structure but do NOT list individual shareholders (contrast Art 101 for 有限公司).

summary: A 有限公司's articles of incorporation (章程) are a document filed in the registration process. Company Act Art 393(2) item 10 lists 公司章程 among the items the MOEA must disclose, and any person may inspect, transcribe or copy it on application (online display requires company consent per Art 393(3)). This is a distinct document from the base register extract, so it is an additional registry document.

evidence.basis: Company Act (公司法) Art 393, para 2 item 10
evidence.quote: 公司章程。
evidence.note: The articles of incorporation are an enumerated publicly-disclosable registration item.

evidence.basis: Company Act (公司法) Art 393, para 3
evidence.quote: 前項第一款至第九款，任何人得至主管機關之資訊網站查閱；第十款，經公司同意者，亦同。
evidence.note: Item 10 (章程) is viewable online only with company consent; application-based any-person access remains available under para 2.

caveats: Free online access to the 章程 requires the company's consent (Art 393(3)); guaranteed any-person access is via 查閱/抄錄/複製 application to MOEA under Art 393(2).

documentLabel: MOEA company registration transcript / GCIS public registration record
documentLocal: 公司登記事項 (抄錄本) / 商工登記公示資料
summary: The MOEA/GCIS registration system issues a registration transcript that is the register extract for a 股份有限公司, showing name, business scope, address, capital, and directors/supervisors (with their shareholding). Under Company Act Art 393(2)-(3) these items are publicly disclosed and any person may view items 1-9 on the MOEA website. This is the base document equivalent to the German Registerauszug.

evidence.basis: Company Act (公司法) Art 393, para 2 item 5
evidence.quote: 董事、監察人姓名及持股。
evidence.note: Directors' and supervisors' names and shareholding are publicly-disclosed registered items for a company limited by shares.

evidence.basis: MOEA GCIS company registration public query (商工登記公示資料查詢服務)
evidence.note: Public registration search portal issuing the register extract for a 股份有限公司.

caveats: The extract reflects directors/supervisors and promoters, not the general body of shareholders.

documentLabel: MOEA company registration transcript / GCIS public registration record
documentLocal: 公司登記事項 (抄錄本) / 商工登記公示資料
summary: The MOEA/GCIS company registration system issues a registration transcript that constitutes the register extract for a 有限公司, showing company name, business scope, address, capital, and the director(s). Under Company Act Art 393(2)-(3) these registered particulars are publicly disclosed and any person may inspect, transcribe or copy them (items 1-9 freely on the MOEA website). This is the equivalent of the German Registerauszug and is the base document.

evidence.basis: Company Act (公司法) Art 393, para 3
evidence.quote: 前項第一款至第九款，任何人得至主管機關之資訊網站查閱；第十款，經公司同意者，亦同。
evidence.note: Registered items 1-9 (name, business, address, directors/supervisors, capital, etc.) are freely inspectable by anyone on the MOEA information website.

evidence.basis: MOEA GCIS company registration public query (商工登記公示資料查詢服務)
evidence.note: Public registration search portal that issues the register extract for all company types including 有限公司.

caveats: The base free public extract shows the director(s) and capital, not the full non-director shareholder roster (that is treated separately under GESELLSCHAFTERLISTE).

summary: Delaware does NOT hold or publish any list of shareholders. Ownership is recorded in the corporation's private stock ledger, administered by/for the corporation, not the state. Access is limited to stockholders exercising a statutory inspection right (proper purpose), not the public and not via the Division of Corporations. No document proving current shareholders is obtainable from the registry.

evidence.basis: Delaware General Corporation Law, Title 8, § 219(c) (stock ledger definition)
evidence.quote: the stock ledger shall be the only evidence as to who are the stockholders entitled by this section to examine the list required by this section or to vote in person or by proxy
evidence.note: § 219(c) defines the stock ledger as records administered by/for the corporation.

evidence.basis: Delaware General Corporation Law, Title 8, § 220(b) (inspection of books and records)
evidence.quote: the corporation's stock ledger, a list of its stockholders, and its other books and records
evidence.note: Held at the corporation, disclosed only on a qualifying stockholder demand, never filed with the state.

caveats: Shareholders are entirely private in Delaware; no state document evidences them. Ownership proof must come from the company (stock ledger, cap table) — outside the registry.
