summary: Stockholders of a Delaware corporation are not filed with the state. Ownership is recorded in the corporation's own stock ledger. No document from the Division of Corporations evidences the shareholder list.

evidence.basis: 8 Del. C. § 219/§ 224 (stock ledger, DGCL)
evidence.note: The stock ledger is a corporate record maintained by the company, not a state filing; cite unverified verbatim.

evidence.basis: 8 Del. C. § 102 (certificate contents, DGCL)
evidence.note: The certificate of incorporation lists authorized shares, not the identities of stockholders.

caveats: Shareholders are held only in the company's stock ledger, never filed with the state.
UNVERIFIED: statutory verbatim not fetched for §§ 219/224/102.

summary: Members of a Delaware LLC are not filed with the state. The certificate of formation does not list members, and membership is recorded only in the company's own limited liability company agreement and internal records. No registry document evidences the member list.

evidence.quote: The certificate of formation shall be filed in the office of the Secretary of State and set forth:
evidence.note: The enumerated contents cover name and registered office/agent; members are not among the required particulars.

evidence.quote: A limited liability company agreement shall be entered into or otherwise existing either before, after or at the time of the filing of a certificate of formation
evidence.note: Membership lives in the private LLC agreement, which is not filed.

caveats: Members are held in the company's internal records/operating agreement, never filed with the Secretary of State.

summary: Delaware does NOT hold any list of LLC members. The Certificate of Formation is not required to name members or managers, and membership is governed by the private limited liability company (operating) agreement, which may be written, oral or implied and is never filed. No document proving current members is obtainable from the registry.

evidence.basis: Delaware Limited Liability Company Act, Title 6, § 18-201(a) (contents of certificate of formation)
evidence.quote: Any other matters the members determine to include therein
evidence.note: Required contents are only the LLC name and registered office/agent; naming members/managers is NOT required, so the public filing does not evidence ownership.

evidence.basis: Delaware Limited Liability Company Act, Title 6, § 18-101(9) (definition of LLC agreement)
evidence.quote: "Limited liability company agreement" means any agreement (whether referred to as a limited liability company agreement, operating agreement or otherwise), written, oral or implied, of the member or members as to the affairs of a limited liability company and the conduct of its business.
evidence.note: The document defining membership is private (may even be oral/implied) and not filed with the state.

caveats: Members are entirely private in Delaware; no state document evidences them. Ownership proof must come from the company (operating agreement, member ledger) — outside the registry.

summary: Stockholders of a Nevada corporation are not filed with the state. The NRS 78.150 Annual List discloses the president, secretary, treasurer and all directors — officers and directors only, not stockholders. Shareholdings live in the corporation's own stock ledger, so no registry document evidences the shareholder list.

evidence.quote: The names and titles of the president, secretary and treasurer, or the equivalent thereof, and of all the directors of the corporation
evidence.note: Confirms the public list is directors/officers, expressly not stockholders.

caveats: The Annual List is a common false friend — it evidences officers/directors, never shareholders.

summary: Shareholders of a New York business corporation are not filed with the Department of State. The certificate of incorporation states authorized shares, not shareholder identities, and ownership is kept in the corporation's own record of shareholders. No registry document evidences the shareholder list.

evidence.basis: N.Y. Bus. Corp. Law § 402
evidence.quote: The name of the corporation.
evidence.note: § 402 contents cover name, purpose, county, and authorized shares — not shareholder identities.

evidence.basis: N.Y. Bus. Corp. Law § 624 (books and records of account)
evidence.note: The record of shareholders is a corporate book maintained by the company, not a state filing; verbatim not fetched.

caveats: Shareholders are held only in the company's own record of shareholders, never filed with the Department of State.
UNVERIFIED: verbatim of § 624 not fetched.

summary: Members of a Wyoming LLC are not filed with the state. The articles of organization require only the LLC name and its registered office/agent (with the agent's consent) — no member list. Membership is held in the company's own operating agreement and records, so no registry document evidences it.

evidence.quote: One (1) or more persons may act as organizers to form a limited liability company by signing and delivering to the secretary of state for filing articles of organization.
evidence.note: The enumerated required contents are name and registered office/agent only; members are not required.

caveats: Members are held in the company's internal operating agreement/records, never filed with the state.
Wyoming is a privacy-oriented filing state; members are deliberately not part of the public record.

documentLabel: Certified copy of the Certificate of Incorporation (the charter)
summary: For a Delaware corporation the constitutive/charter document is the Certificate of Incorporation, which IS filed with the Secretary of State and obtainable as a certified copy — the correct analog of a Gesellschaftsvertrag/Satzung. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: the bylaws are a SEPARATE internal governance document, NOT filed with the state and NOT provable from the registry. So the charter is provable; the bylaws are not.

evidence.basis: statute (Delaware General Corporation Law, 8 Del. C. § 106)
evidence.quote: Upon the filing with the Secretary of State of the certificate of incorporation...the incorporator or incorporators...shall...be and constitute a body corporate
evidence.note: The corporation comes into existence by filing the certificate of incorporation; it is the base constitutional document.

evidence.basis: statute (8 Del. C. § 102(a))
evidence.quote: The name of the corporation...The address...of the corporation's registered office...The nature of the business or purposes...the total number of shares...The name and mailing address of the incorporator
evidence.note: § 102(a) enumerates the mandatory contents of the certificate of incorporation.

caveats: The § 102/106 fragments are joined with ellipses marking omitted words; consult the full sections for continuous wording.

documentLabel: Certified copy of the Certificate of Incorporation
summary: The Certificate of Incorporation is filed with the Delaware Division of Corporations, is public, and a certified copy is obtainable — it constitutes the corporation and proves its charter. The bylaws, which govern internal affairs like a Gesellschaftsvertrag, are adopted internally and not filed, so they cannot be obtained from the registry.

evidence.basis: 8 Del. C. § 106 (DGCL)
evidence.quote: Upon the filing with the Secretary of State of the certificate of incorporation
evidence.note: Filing the certificate constitutes the body corporate; the document is public.

evidence.basis: 8 Del. C. § 109 (bylaws, DGCL)
evidence.note: INFERENCE: § 109 provides for adoption/amendment of bylaws by the corporation but imposes no requirement to file them with the Secretary of State.

caveats: Only the Certificate of Incorporation is provable; bylaws are NOT filed and not provable from the registry.
INFERENCE: 'bylaws not filed' rests on the absence of any filing requirement in § 109, not on an affirmative prohibition.

documentLabel: Certified copy of the Certificate of Formation
summary: The Certificate of Formation is filed with and public at the Delaware Division of Corporations, and a certified copy is obtainable — it is the foundational constitutional filing and proves the entity's charter. However, the operating (LLC) agreement, which contains most of what a German Gesellschaftsvertrag governs, is a private document that is not filed and cannot be obtained from the registry.

evidence.quote: The certificate of formation shall be filed in the office of the Secretary of State and set forth:
evidence.note: The charter document is public and certifiable.

evidence.quote: A limited liability company agreement shall be entered into or otherwise existing either before, after or at the time of the filing of a certificate of formation
evidence.note: The LLC agreement exists independently of the filing and is not lodged with the state.

caveats: Only the Certificate of Formation is provable; the operating agreement (the substantive constitution) is NOT filed and not provable from the registry.

documentLabel: Certified copy of the Certificate of Formation (the charter)
summary: The constitutive/charter document filed with the state for a Delaware LLC is the Certificate of Formation, which IS filed and obtainable as a certified copy — the provable base document. CRITICAL: it is minimal (only the LLC name and registered office/agent). The document that actually functions as the Gesellschaftsvertrag in substance — the operating agreement governing members, capital and management — is PRIVATE and NOT provable from the registry.

evidence.basis: statute (Delaware LLC Act, 6 Del. C. § 18-201)
evidence.quote: In order to form a limited liability company, 1 or more authorized persons must execute a certificate of formation.
evidence.note: Formation is effected by executing and filing the certificate of formation.

evidence.basis: statute (6 Del. C. § 18-201)
evidence.quote: The certificate of formation shall be filed in the office of the Secretary of State and set forth: (1) The name of the limited liability company; (2) The address of the registered office and the name and address of the registered agent for service of process required to be maintained by § 18-104 of this title; and (3) Any other matters the members determine to include therein.
evidence.note: Verbatim, contiguous statutory text defining the certificate of formation and its required contents.

caveats: The certificate of formation is the base public filing; the LLC's fuller constitutional terms sit in the private limited liability company (operating) agreement, which is not on the public register.

documentLabel: Certified copy of the Articles of Incorporation
summary: The Articles of Incorporation are filed with the Nevada Secretary of State, are public, and a certified copy is obtainable — the foundational charter document. The bylaws, adopted by the directors under NRS ch. 78, are internal and not filed, so they cannot be obtained from the registry.

evidence.basis: NRS 78.030 / 78.035 (articles of incorporation)
evidence.note: INFERENCE: articles are executed and filed with the Secretary of State and become the public charter; verbatim not fetched.

evidence.basis: NRS 78.120 (bylaws adopted by directors)
evidence.note: INFERENCE: directors adopt bylaws; the chapter imposes no requirement to file them with the state.

caveats: Only the Articles of Incorporation are provable; bylaws are NOT filed and not provable from the registry.
UNVERIFIED: verbatim text of NRS 78.030/78.035/78.120 not fetched (leg.state.nv.us blocked to WebFetch).

documentLabel: Certified copy of the Certificate of Incorporation
summary: The Certificate of Incorporation is filed with the NY Department of State, is public, and a certified copy is obtainable — the foundational charter. The by-laws, adopted by the shareholders or board under BCL § 601, are internal and carry no filing requirement, so they cannot be obtained from the registry.

evidence.basis: N.Y. Bus. Corp. Law § 402
evidence.quote: The name of the corporation.
evidence.note: § 402 sets out what the filed certificate of incorporation must contain; the document is public.

evidence.basis: N.Y. Bus. Corp. Law § 601(a)
evidence.quote: by-laws may be adopted, amended or repealed by a majority of the votes cast by the shares at the time entitled to vote in the election of any directors
evidence.note: By-laws are adopted internally; § 601 imposes no requirement to file them with the Department of State.

caveats: Only the Certificate of Incorporation is provable; by-laws are NOT filed and not provable from the registry.
INFERENCE: 'by-laws not filed' rests on the absence of any filing requirement in § 601.

documentLabel: Certified copy of the Articles of Organization
summary: The Articles of Organization are filed with the Wyoming Secretary of State, are public, and a certified copy is obtainable — the foundational charter filing. The operating agreement, which governs the LLC's internal affairs like a Gesellschaftsvertrag, is private and not filed, so it cannot be obtained from the registry.

evidence.quote: One (1) or more persons may act as organizers to form a limited liability company by signing and delivering to the secretary of state for filing articles of organization.
evidence.note: The charter document is public and certifiable.

evidence.basis: Wyo. Stat. § 17-29-110 (operating agreement)
evidence.note: INFERENCE: the operating agreement is recognized as a private governance document and is not required to be filed with the state.

caveats: Only the Articles of Organization are provable; the operating agreement is NOT filed and not provable from the registry.
UNVERIFIED: verbatim text of § 17-29-110 not fetched.

documentLabel: Certificate of Good Standing (a.k.a. Certificate of Status)
summary: Delaware has no 'register extract' equivalent to a Handelsregisterauszug. The official state-issued document evidencing existence, exact registered name, incorporation date and current good standing is the Certificate of Good Standing from the Delaware Secretary of State / Division of Corporations; a certified copy of the Certificate of Incorporation with subsequent filings is also orderable. Closest official base document proving registration.

evidence.basis: Delaware Division of Corporations, official 'About Agency' page
evidence.quote: The Division of Corporations now offers online Name Reservations, Entity Status and General Information.
evidence.note: Same page lists 'Order a Good Standing Certificate' as a service.

evidence.basis: Delaware General Corporation Law, Title 8, § 106 (commencement of corporate existence)
evidence.quote: Upon the filing with the Secretary of State of the certificate of incorporation, executed and acknowledged in accordance with § 103 of this title, the incorporator or incorporators who signed the certificate ... shall, from the date of such filing, be and constitute a body corporate.
evidence.note: The charter is a filed public record establishing existence, the substrate for a good-standing certificate.

caveats: The Certificate of Good Standing evidences existence and status only; it does NOT list directors, officers, shareholders, or capital.
Delaware issues no periodic 'extract' document; equivalence to a REGISTERAUSZUG is a functional mapping, not a like-for-like document.

documentLocal: Certificate of Good Standing (Delaware Division of Corporations)
summary: Delaware issues no consolidated current-particulars extract. On request the Division of Corporations provides a Certificate of Good Standing evidencing the corporation's existence and standing, optionally listing filed documents (long form), but not a full snapshot of current officers/directors or capital. It is an ordered additional document, not a base register printout.

evidence.basis: 8 Del. C. § 106 (DGCL)
evidence.quote: Upon the filing with the Secretary of State of the certificate of incorporation
evidence.note: Fragment; the state holds the certificate of incorporation and filed amendments, not a live particulars register.

evidence.basis: Delaware Division of Corporations — 'Accessing Corporate Information' (official registry documentation): Certificate of Status / Certificate of Good Standing ordered on request
evidence.quote: You may order a Certificate of Status or Certificate of Good Standing by submitting a request in writing to the Division of Corporations. The fee is $50.00 per certificate for a short form certificate of status (includes the name of the entity and the status at the time the certificate is issued) or $175.00 for a Long Form certificate of Good Standing (states all documents that have been filed, including the dates and times and any name changes that occurred along with the status at the time the certificate is issued).
evidence.note: Directly confirms: (i) the certificate is an ordered, paid additional document, and (ii) even the long form only lists filed documents and status — no consolidated current-particulars extract (officers/directors/capital) exists.

evidence.basis: Delaware Division of Corporations — FAQ: contents of the free public entity search
evidence.quote: The entity information provided free of charge, includes the entity name, file number, incorporation/formation date, registered agent name, address and phone number along with the entity kind, type, and residency.
evidence.note: The free public record is minimal (no officers/directors/shareholders/capital), reinforcing that status evidence comes from the ordered certificate.

caveats: A Good Standing certificate is not a true Registerauszug — it proves existence/status (short form: name + status only; long form adds the list of filed documents), not consolidated current particulars such as officers, directors or capital.

documentLocal: Certificate of Good Standing (Delaware Division of Corporations)
summary: The US has no consolidated 'current particulars' register extract equivalent to a German Registerauszug. On request the Delaware Division of Corporations issues a Certificate of Good Standing, which proves the LLC's existence and standing but NOT its legal form details, registered address history, managers or members. It is an ordered additional document, not an automatically available base register printout.

evidence.basis: 6 Del. C. § 18-201 (Delaware LLC Act)
evidence.quote: The certificate of formation shall be filed in the office of the Secretary of State and set forth:
evidence.note: Only the certificate of formation — not a running register of particulars — is held by the state.

evidence.basis: Delaware Division of Corporations — 'Accessing Corporate Information' (official registry documentation): Certificate of Status / Certificate of Good Standing ordered on request (applies to all entity types administered by the Division, including LLCs)
evidence.quote: You may order a Certificate of Status or Certificate of Good Standing by submitting a request in writing to the Division of Corporations. The fee is $50.00 per certificate for a short form certificate of status (includes the name of the entity and the status at the time the certificate is issued) or $175.00 for a Long Form certificate of Good Standing (states all documents that have been filed, including the dates and times and any name changes that occurred along with the status at the time the certificate is issued).
evidence.note: Directly confirms the certificate is an ordered, paid additional document and that even the long form lists only filed documents and status — no consolidated current-particulars extract (managers/members) exists.

evidence.basis: Delaware Division of Corporations — FAQ: contents of the free public entity search
evidence.quote: The entity information provided free of charge, includes the entity name, file number, incorporation/formation date, registered agent name, address and phone number along with the entity kind, type, and residency.
evidence.note: The free public record is minimal — no managers or members — reinforcing that status evidence comes from the ordered certificate.

caveats: A Good Standing certificate is, at best, a weak substitute for a Registerauszug — it evidences existence/status (short form: name + status; long form adds the list of filed documents), not the consolidated current particulars (legal form, address, representatives, members) a German extract carries.

documentLabel: Certificate of Good Standing (a.k.a. Certificate of Status)
summary: As with corporations, Delaware issues no register extract for LLCs. The official state document evidencing existence, exact registered name, formation date and current good standing is the Certificate of Good Standing from the Division of Corporations; a certified copy of the Certificate of Formation with subsequent filings is also orderable. Base document proving registration.

evidence.basis: Delaware Division of Corporations, official 'About Agency' page
evidence.quote: The Division of Corporations now offers online Name Reservations, Entity Status and General Information.
evidence.note: Same page/FAQ offer 'Order a Good Standing Certificate' for entities including LLCs.

evidence.basis: Delaware Limited Liability Company Act, Title 6, § 18-201(b) (formation on filing)
evidence.quote: The certificate of formation shall be filed in the office of the Secretary of State
evidence.note: The filed Certificate of Formation is the public record underlying the good-standing/status certificate.

caveats: The Certificate of Good Standing evidences existence/status only; it lists no members, managers, or capital.
No periodic 'extract' document exists; equivalence to a REGISTERAUSZUG is a functional mapping.

documentLabel: Certificate of Existence / Good Standing (plus Annual List)
documentLocal: Certificate of Existence with Status; Annual List of Officers and Directors
summary: Nevada issues no consolidated current-particulars extract. The Secretary of State provides a Certificate of Existence/Good Standing (existence and status), and — uniquely among these forms — a publicly filed Annual List of the officers and directors under NRS 78.150 can supplement it as to representation. Together these are ordered additional documents, still short of a full German-style register extract, and they do not evidence shareholders.

evidence.basis: statute (NRS 78.150)
evidence.quote: The corporation shall annually thereafter, on or before the last day of the month in which the anniversary date of incorporation occurs in each year, or, if, pursuant to subsection 12, the corporation has selected an alternative due date for filing the list required by subsection 1, on or before the last day of the month in which the anniversary date of the alternative due date occurs in each year, file with the Secretary of State, on a form furnished by the Secretary of State, an annual list containing all of the information required in subsection 1.
evidence.note: Confirms the mandatory Annual List filed with the Secretary of State (the register content for officers/directors).

caveats: The quoted annual list covers officers and directors, not shareholders.
The Certificate of Existence with Status is a separate administrative certificate not quoted in NRS 78.150.

documentLocal: Certificate of Status (NY Department of State, Division of Corporations)
summary: New York issues no consolidated current-particulars extract. The Department of State provides a Certificate of Status (also called Certificate of Existence) evidencing that the corporation exists and is active, but not its officers, directors, capital or shareholders. It is an ordered additional document, not a base register printout.

evidence.basis: registry (NY Department of State, Division of Corporations)
evidence.quote: A Certificate of Status evidencing the existence of a corporation or other business entity
evidence.note: The page adds it is 'also referred to as a Certificate of Good Standing or Certificate of Existence.'

evidence.basis: statute (NY Business Corporation Law § 402)
evidence.quote: A certificate, entitled 'Certificate of incorporation of ...... (name of corporation) under section 402 of the Business Corporation Law', shall be signed by each incorporator, with his name and address included in such certificate and delivered to the department of state.
evidence.note: Confirms the NY Department of State is the register of record where the corporation is filed.

caveats: INFERENCE/JUDGMENT: a Certificate of Status is not a true Registerauszug — existence/status only.
UNVERIFIED: no verbatim quote fetched for certificate contents.

documentLocal: Certificate of Good Standing (Wyoming Secretary of State)
summary: Wyoming issues no consolidated current-particulars extract. The Secretary of State provides a Certificate of Good Standing on request, evidencing existence and standing but not legal form details, managers or members. It is an ordered additional document, not a base register printout.

evidence.basis: W.S. 17-29-208(a) — Certificate of existence or authorization, furnished by the Secretary of State on request and payment
evidence.quote: The secretary of state, upon request and payment of the requisite fee, shall furnish to any person a certificate of existence for a limited liability company if the records filed in the office of the secretary of state show that the company has been formed under W.S. 17-29-201 and the secretary of state has not filed articles of dissolution pertaining to the company.
evidence.note: s.208(a)(i)-(viii) limits the certificate's contents to: name; due formation and date; whether fees/taxes/penalties are paid; whether the latest annual report is filed; administrative dissolution; articles of dissolution; and other facts of record on request — not managers or members. Official statute PDF (Title 17), Wyoming Legislature.

evidence.basis: W.S. 17-29-208(b) — evidentiary effect
evidence.quote: Subject to any qualification stated in the certificate, a certificate of existence or certificate of authorization issued by the secretary of state is conclusive evidence that the limited liability company is in existence.

evidence.basis: W.S. 17-29-201(e)(i) — formation upon effectiveness of articles of organization
evidence.quote: A limited liability company is formed when the articles of organization become effective, unless the articles state a delayed effective date pursuant to W.S. 17-29-205(c)
evidence.note: Filing articles of organization with the Secretary of State places the LLC on the register from which the certificate of existence is issued. Replaces the earlier FindLaw citation with the official wyoleg.gov statute text.

caveats: Naming: the statutory document is the 'certificate of existence or authorization' (W.S. 17-29-208); 'Certificate of Good Standing' is the Secretary of State's product name for it. The SoS ordering page (wyobiz.wyo.gov) returned an empty response this session, so the product-name styling was not verbatim-verified.
The certificate evidences existence, fee/annual-report standing and dissolution facts (s.208(a)) — not legal form details, managers or members.

summary: The register of members is kept by the company at the office of its registered agent (s.96(1)(b), s.41(1)); it is NOT filed with the Registrar by default and is NOT publicly available. There is no registry-issued shareholder list. A company MAY elect under s.231(1) to file a copy of its register of members, which would then be obtainable via s.233 — but this is optional and rare. Absent that election, current members cannot be proved from the registry; the only proof is the registered-agent-held register (or a registered-agent Certificate of Incumbency, not a registry document).

evidence.basis: BVI Business Companies Act 2004 (as amended), s.41(1)
evidence.quote: A company shall keep a register of members
evidence.note: Company-kept register; no filing obligation to the Registrar.

evidence.basis: BVI Business Companies Act 2004 (as amended), s.96(1)
evidence.quote: A company shall keep the following documents at the office of its registered agent: ... (b) the register of members maintained in accordance with section 41
evidence.note: Register of members held at the registered agent, not the Registry.

evidence.basis: BVI Business Companies Act 2004 (as amended), s.231(1)
evidence.quote: A company may elect to file for registration by the Registrar a copy of either or both of the following: (a) its register of members; (b) its register of directors.
evidence.note: Optional (permissive 'may elect') filing; rarely used.

caveats: EXCEPTION: if the company made the OPTIONAL s.231 election to file a copy of its register of members, that copy becomes a registry document obtainable under s.233 (in that case, proved_by_additional_document). Uncommon.
Register of members contents are not verified by the Registrar even when filed; the filed copy binds the company (s.231(4)) but reflects only what was filed.

summary: The memorandum and articles are filed by the registered agent on incorporation (s.6), registered by the Registrar who issues the certificate of incorporation (s.7), retained as a qualifying document, expressly available to the public from the Registrar (s.32(1)(a)), and obtainable by ANY person as a certified or uncertified copy/extract under s.233(1). This is the BVI constitution and the base registry document proving it.

evidence.basis: BVI Business Companies Act 2004 (as amended), s.7(1)
evidence.quote: the Registrar shall ... (a) register the documents ... (c) issue a certificate of incorporation to the company in the approved form.
evidence.note: M&A filed on incorporation (s.6) and registered.

evidence.basis: BVI Business Companies Act 2004 (as amended), s.32(1)(a)
evidence.quote: any document relating to a company, including the memorandum and articles ... (a) is available to the public from the Registrar;
evidence.note: Statute expressly treats M&A as available to the public from the Registrar.

evidence.basis: BVI Business Companies Act 2004 (as amended), s.233(1)
evidence.quote: require a certified or uncertified copy or extract ... of any document ... of which he has custody, to be certified by the Registrar
evidence.note: Any person may obtain a certified copy of the filed M&A.

caveats: Amendments to the M&A must be filed with the Registrar (s.13); the Registry copy reflects the version last filed, so it may lag a board/member-adopted amendment not yet filed. For the current constitution ensure the filed copy is the latest restated/amended version.

summary: The BVI Registry of Corporate Affairs maintains only a Register of Companies, a Register of Foreign Companies and a Register of Charges (s.230(1)); it does NOT issue a German-style consolidated register extract listing directors, members and capital. The registry's official current-status document, obtainable by ANY person, is the Certificate of Good Standing (s.235(1)). A certified/uncertified copy of the certificate of incorporation is likewise obtainable under s.233(1). These are the only registry-issued current-status documents.

evidence.basis: BVI Business Companies Act 2004, Section 235(1) (Certificate of good standing)
evidence.quote: The Registrar shall, upon request by any person, issue a certificate of good standing under his hand and seal certifying that a company is of good standing if the Registrar is satisfied that (a) the company is on the Register of Companies; and (b) the company has paid all fees, annual fees and penalties due and payable.
evidence.note: The Registrar-issued Certificate of Good Standing is the base register document evidencing that the BC is on the Register of Companies and current on fees.

caveats: The Certificate of Good Standing evidences existence/standing only; under s.41 the register of members is company-kept and only optionally filed (s.231), so a BC's shareholder list is not part of this base register document.

legalFormLocal: Công ty trách nhiệm hữu hạn
summary: For a multi-member LLC the members (thành viên) and their capital contributions are registered particulars: the List of Members is a mandatory component of the registration dossier filed with the Business Registration Office under Article 21, and a change of member/capital contribution must be re-notified as a change to enterprise registration content. The list is a distinct filed document, not part of the ERC itself, hence an additional document. A single-member LLC has no member list because the sole owner is otherwise identified.

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises No. 59/2020/QH14, Article 21 (Registration dossier for a limited liability company)
evidence.quote: Article 21. Registration dossier for a limited liability company 1. A written request for enterprise registration. 2. Company charter. 3. List of members.
evidence.note: English translation (LuatVietnam) of Dieu 21 Luat Doanh nghiep 2020; 'List of members' = danh sach thanh vien. The member list is a mandatory, distinct filed component of the LLC registration dossier — supports proof via an additional registry document.

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises No. 59/2020/QH14, Article 25 (contents of the list of members)
evidence.quote: The list of members of a limited liability company or partnership or the list of founding shareholders and shareholders being foreign investors of a joint stock company must contain the following principal details: 1. Full names, signatures, citizenship and contact addresses of individual members, for a limited liability company or partnership
evidence.note: Article 25 also requires (item 4) 'Contributed capital amounts and their value, holding rate of contributed capital amounts' per member — the filed list identifies each member and their stake.

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises No. 59/2020/QH14, Article 28(3) (contents of the enterprise registration certificate)
evidence.quote: full name, contact address, citizenship, and serial number of the legal paper of each individual member; or name, identification number and head office address of the institutional member, for a limited liability company.
evidence.note: Supporting: even the base ERC itself carries LLC member identities, reinforcing that LLC membership is a registered particular kept current via change registration (Articles 30-31).

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises No. 59/2020/QH14, Article 33 (Provision of information about contents of enterprise registration)
evidence.quote: Organizations and individuals may request the state management agency in charge of business registration and the business registration agency to provide information stored on the national information system on business registration and shall pay charges in accordance with law.
evidence.note: Any organization or individual can obtain registered enterprise information from the business registration authority for a fee — the route to actually obtain the registered member information.

caveats: Applies to multi-member LLCs; a single-member LLC (mot thanh vien) has no member list (the sole owner is identified directly in the registration).
The NBRP publicly exposes only basic company data; obtaining the actual filed List of Members as a document typically requires an Article 33 information request to the Business Registration Office.
Quotes are from the LuatVietnam English translation of Law 59/2020/QH14; the Vietnamese original is authoritative (official portals vbpl.vn / vanban.chinhphu.vn were unreachable this session).

summary: For a JSC, only the founding shareholders (cổ đông sáng lập) are a registered particular — the List of Founding Shareholders is filed with the incorporation dossier under Article 22. Ordinary and subsequent shareholders are recorded only in the company's internal Register of Shareholders (sổ đăng ký cổ đông) under Article 122, which is held by the company and is neither filed with nor published by the Business Registration Office. A complete, current shareholder list is therefore not provable from the registry; the founding-shareholder list captures only the incorporation snapshot and becomes stale after transfers.

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises No. 59/2020/QH14, Article 22 (Registration dossier for a joint stock company)
evidence.quote: Article 22. Registration dossier for a joint stock company 1. A written request for enterprise registration. 2. Company charter. 3. List of founding shareholders; list of shareholders being foreign investors.
evidence.note: English translation (LuatVietnam) of Dieu 22. Only FOUNDING shareholders (danh sach co dong sang lap) and foreign-investor shareholders are filed — not the full shareholder body.

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises No. 59/2020/QH14, Article 122 (Register of shareholders)
evidence.quote: A joint stock company shall make and keep a register of shareholders from the date it is granted the enterprise registration certificate.
evidence.note: The complete current shareholder record (so dang ky co dong) is a company-made and company-kept register, not a registry filing.

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises No. 59/2020/QH14, Article 122(3) (custody of the register of shareholders)
evidence.quote: The register of shareholders shall be kept at the head office of the company or at an organization having the function of keeping the register of shareholders.
evidence.note: The register sits at the company (or a registrar organization), never at the Business Registration Office — hence a complete current shareholder list is not obtainable from the registry.

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises No. 59/2020/QH14, Article 31(1)(b) (notification of changes)
evidence.quote: b/ Founding shareholder(s) and shareholder(s) being foreign investor(s), for a joint stock company, except listed companies;
evidence.note: From the list of registration contents whose changes must be notified: for a JSC the registry tracks only founding and foreign-investor shareholders (and not even those for listed companies) — ordinary shareholder turnover never reaches the registry.

caveats: A List of Founding Shareholders exists in the registry file as an additional document, but it covers only founders at incorporation (plus foreign-investor shareholders) and does not reflect current ownership — hence it is not a Gesellschafterliste-equivalent.

The complete current shareholder list lives in the company's Article 122 register (or with a registrar organization); shareholders' inspection rights under Article 122(3) do not extend to the general public.
Quotes are from the LuatVietnam English translation of Law 59/2020/QH14; the Vietnamese original is authoritative.

legalFormLocal: Công ty trách nhiệm hữu hạn
summary: The company charter (Điều lệ công ty) is a mandatory component of the LLC registration dossier under Article 21 of the Law on Enterprises 2020 and is filed with the Business Registration Office at incorporation. It is a filed constitutional document distinct from the ERC, so it is proved by an additional document rather than the base extract. The NBRP does not publish the charter publicly.

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises No. 59/2020/QH14, Article 21 (Registration dossier for a limited liability company)
evidence.quote: Article 21. Registration dossier for a limited liability company 1. A written request for enterprise registration. 2. Company charter. 3. List of members.
evidence.note: English translation (LuatVietnam); 'Company charter' = Dieu le cong ty. The charter is a mandatory filed component of the LLC registration dossier — a filed constitutional document distinct from the ERC.

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises No. 59/2020/QH14, Article 24 (Company charter)
evidence.quote: The company charter means the charter upon enterprise registration and the charter that is revised in the process of operation.
evidence.note: Article 24(1); Article 24(2) then enumerates mandatory contents (name, business lines, charter capital, members and their contributed capital for an LLC, governance, etc.) — the charter is a concrete constitutional document.

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises No. 59/2020/QH14, Article 33 (Provision of information about contents of enterprise registration)
evidence.quote: Organizations and individuals may request the state management agency in charge of business registration and the business registration agency to provide information stored on the national information system on business registration and shall pay charges in accordance with law.
evidence.note: Statutory route for third parties to obtain registered enterprise information from the business registration authority.

caveats: The filed charter is the incorporation version; charter revisions in the course of operation (Article 24(1) 'charter that is revised') are not consistently re-filed at or published by the registry, so the on-file copy may be outdated.
Retrieval generally requires a request to the Business Registration Office or the company; the charter is not downloadable from the NBRP public portal.
Quotes are from the LuatVietnam English translation of Law 59/2020/QH14; the Vietnamese original is authoritative.

summary: The company charter (Điều lệ công ty) is a mandatory component of the JSC registration dossier under Article 22 of the Law on Enterprises 2020 and is filed with the Business Registration Office at incorporation. It is a filed constitutional document distinct from the ERC, so it is proved by an additional document. The NBRP does not publish the charter publicly.

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises No. 59/2020/QH14, Article 22 (Registration dossier for a joint stock company)
evidence.quote: Article 22. Registration dossier for a joint stock company 1. A written request for enterprise registration. 2. Company charter.
evidence.note: English translation (LuatVietnam); 'Company charter' = Dieu le cong ty, a mandatory filed component of the JSC registration dossier — a filed constitutional document distinct from the ERC.

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises No. 59/2020/QH14, Article 24 (Company charter)
evidence.quote: The company charter means the charter upon enterprise registration and the charter that is revised in the process of operation.
evidence.note: Article 24(1); Article 24(2) enumerates mandatory contents including, for a JSC, charter capital, total shares, types and par value of shares, and founding shareholders.

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises No. 59/2020/QH14, Article 33 (Provision of information about contents of enterprise registration)
evidence.quote: Organizations and individuals may request the state management agency in charge of business registration and the business registration agency to provide information stored on the national information system on business registration and shall pay charges in accordance with law.
evidence.note: Statutory route for third parties to obtain registered enterprise information from the business registration authority.

caveats: The filed charter is the incorporation version; revisions are not consistently re-filed at or published by the registry, so the on-file copy may be outdated.
Retrieval generally requires a request to the Business Registration Office or the company; the charter is not downloadable from the NBRP public portal.
Quotes are from the LuatVietnam English translation of Law 59/2020/QH14; the Vietnamese original is authoritative.

legalFormLocal: Công ty trách nhiệm hữu hạn
documentLocal: Giấy chứng nhận đăng ký doanh nghiệp
summary: The Enterprise Registration Certificate (ERC) is the base registry document issued to every enterprise by the Business Registration Office under the Ministry of Planning and Investment. Under Article 28 of the Law on Enterprises 2020 it carries the enterprise name and ID number, head-office address, the legal representative's identity, and charter capital. It is the direct functional equivalent of a Registerauszug for an LLC. Company registration data is also publicly retrievable via the National Business Registration Portal.

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises 2020 (Law No. 59/2020/QH14), Article 28 (Contents of the enterprise registration certificate)
evidence.note: Verbatim text could not be fetched (all hosting sources returned 403/404/redirect). Article 28 lists: enterprise name and code; head-office address; legal representative identity; charter capital.

evidence.basis: National Business Registration Portal (dangkykinhdoanh.gov.vn), operated by the Business Registration Management Agency, MPI
evidence.note: Public company-information lookup; ERC is the authoritative issued extract.

caveats: Verbatim statutory text could not be retrieved — Article numbers and contents are stated from established knowledge of the LOE 2020 and should be treated as INFERENCE/UNVERIFIED.
The ERC identifies only the legal representative, not the member list.

documentLocal: Giấy chứng nhận đăng ký doanh nghiệp
summary: As for all enterprises, the joint-stock company's base registry document is the Enterprise Registration Certificate issued by the Business Registration Office under Article 28 of the Law on Enterprises 2020, carrying the company name and ID number, head-office address, legal representative, and charter capital. It is the direct equivalent of a Registerauszug. Registration data is also publicly retrievable via the National Business Registration Portal.

evidence.basis: Law on Enterprises 2020, Article 28 (Contents of the enterprise registration certificate)
evidence.note: Verbatim text could not be fetched. Same ERC content applies to JSCs.

evidence.basis: National Business Registration Portal (dangkykinhdoanh.gov.vn)
evidence.note: Public company-information lookup.

caveats: The ERC does not list shareholders (only the legal representative and charter capital).
Verbatim statutory text unverified (sources blocked) — INFERENCE/UNVERIFIED.

summary: For a public company, shares are typically held (often in uncertificated form) and recorded in the company's securities register / uncertificated securities register administered by the company or a CSD participant (s50) — not filed with CIPC. The annual return (s33) does not include a shareholder list. No shareholder/member list is obtainable from the registry for a public (Ltd) company; a public company's uncertificated register is even inspectable via the CSD, not CIPC.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 71 of 2008, s50(1)
evidence.quote: Every company must-- (a) establish or cause to be established a register of its issued securities in the prescribed form; and (b) maintain its securities register in accordance with the prescribed standards.
evidence.note: Company-held register; for uncertificated securities a record is kept by a participant/CSD (s50(3)), still not CIPC.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 71 of 2008, s33(1)
evidence.quote: Every company must file an annual return in the prescribed form ... including in that return-- (a) a copy of its annual financial statements ...; and (b) any other prescribed information.
evidence.note: No shareholder list in the annual return.

caveats: For listed public companies the shareholder register is administered via CSD participants (s50(3)); still outside CIPC. Beneficial-interest disclosure rules (s56) may reveal some holders but do not produce a registry shareholder list.

summary: Shareholders of a profit company are not filed with CIPC. The company itself must establish and maintain a securities register in the prescribed form (s50(1)), which is company-held and 'sufficient proof of the facts recorded in it' (s50(4)). The annual return (s33) carries financial statements and prescribed information, not a shareholder list. Therefore no shareholder/member list is obtainable from the registry for a (Pty) Ltd.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 71 of 2008, s50(1)
evidence.quote: Every company must-- (a) establish or cause to be established a register of its issued securities in the prescribed form; and (b) maintain its securities register in accordance with the prescribed standards.
evidence.note: Register is kept by the company, not lodged with CIPC.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 71 of 2008, s33(1)
evidence.quote: Every company must file an annual return in the prescribed form with the prescribed fee ... including in that return-- (a) a copy of its annual financial statements, if it is required to have such statements audited in terms of section 30(2)(a); and (b) any other prescribed information.
evidence.note: Annual return content is FS + prescribed info; no shareholder list required.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 71 of 2008, s24(4)(a)
evidence.quote: In addition to the requirements of subsection (3), every profit company must maintain-- (a) a securities register or its equivalent, as required by section 50
evidence.note: Confirms the securities register is a company record obligation.

caveats: A current shareholder list can only be obtained directly from the company's own securities register (s50) / via a s26 access request, or reconstructed from share-transfer documents — none of which is a registry document.

summary: A public company's MOI is likewise filed with CIPC as a copy accompanying the Notice of Incorporation (s13(2)(b)) and endorsed/held by CIPC (s14). It is registry-retrievable but as a separately-ordered document, not a field on the standard extract — an additional document.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 71 of 2008, s13(2)
evidence.quote: The Notice of Incorporation of a company must be-- (a) filed together with the prescribed fee; and (b) accompanied by a copy of the Memorandum of Incorporation, subject to any declaration contemplated in section 6(14)(b).
evidence.note: Applies to all company categories including public companies.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 71 of 2008, s14(1)
evidence.quote: As soon as practicable after accepting a filed Notice of Incorporation ... endorse the Notice of Incorporation, and, if applicable, the copy of the Memorandum of Incorporation filed with it, in the prescribed manner
evidence.note: CIPC retains the filed MOI copy.

caveats: Classed 'additional' because the MOI is ordered separately from the standard extract.
INFERENCE: mapping 'Gesellschaftsvertrag' to the MOI.

summary: The MOI is the founding constitutional document and a copy is filed with CIPC accompanying the Notice of Incorporation at registration (s13(2)(b)); CIPC endorses and holds it (s14). It therefore exists in the registry, but as a separately-lodged document that must be ordered on request rather than a field on the standard disclosure certificate — hence an additional document.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 71 of 2008, s13(2)
evidence.quote: The Notice of Incorporation of a company must be-- (a) filed together with the prescribed fee; and (b) accompanied by a copy of the Memorandum of Incorporation, subject to any declaration contemplated in section 6(14)(b).
evidence.note: MOI copy is filed with CIPC at incorporation.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 71 of 2008, s14(1)
evidence.quote: As soon as practicable after accepting a filed Notice of Incorporation ... endorse the Notice of Incorporation, and, if applicable, the copy of the Memorandum of Incorporation filed with it, in the prescribed manner
evidence.note: CIPC retains/endorses the filed MOI copy, making it registry-retrievable.

caveats: Classed 'additional' because the MOI is ordered separately (not part of the standard extract). A company adopting the standard-form MOI (Table CoR15.1A/B) may have limited unique content on file.
INFERENCE: mapping German 'Gesellschaftsvertrag' to the MOI (the company's constitution).

summary: As for a private company, CIPC maintains the register and issues an electronic disclosure certificate showing registered particulars and directors for a public (Ltd) company. Directors are a registered record (s24(5)). The extract is the base register document; shareholders are not shown.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 71 of 2008, s24(5)
evidence.quote: A company's record of directors must include, in respect of each director, that person's-- (a) full name, and any former names; (b) identity number
evidence.note: Applies to every company including a public company.

evidence.basis: CIPC fees schedule (electronic disclosure certificate)
evidence.quote: Issuing an electronic disclosure certificate
evidence.note: CIPC issues the certificate for companies generally.

caveats: Exact certificate field list not captured verbatim from CIPC (fetch exposed only the fee table); treated as established domain knowledge corroborated by the Act.
INFERENCE: mapping 'Registerauszug' to the CIPC disclosure certificate/company extract.

summary: CIPC (Companies and Intellectual Property Commission) maintains the register of companies and issues an electronic disclosure certificate / company extract showing the company's registered particulars (registration number, name, status, registered address, financial year end) and its directors. Directors' details are a registered/filed record (s24(5)), so the extract is the base register document for a (Pty) Ltd. Shareholders do not appear on it.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 71 of 2008, s24(5)
evidence.quote: A company's record of directors must include, in respect of each director, that person's-- (a) full name, and any former names; (b) identity number
evidence.note: Directors are a statutorily recorded particular; CIPC holds current director data and reflects it on the disclosure certificate.

evidence.basis: CIPC fees schedule (electronic disclosure certificate)
evidence.quote: Issuing an electronic disclosure certificate
evidence.note: Confirms CIPC issues an electronic disclosure certificate for a company (R30). CIPC page did not expose the certificate's exact field list to the fetch.

caveats: The exact field list of the CIPC disclosure certificate (that it shows directors + registered particulars but not shareholders) could not be captured verbatim from a CIPC page — the fetched page only exposed the fee table. This is treated as established domain knowledge, corroborated by the Act's structure (directors filed under s24(5); shareholders not filed).
INFERENCE: mapping the German 'Registerauszug' to the CIPC disclosure certificate/company extract.
